En: For heaven's sake, he thinks, this has gone too far. Don't make it worse -- just get back to questioning the kid.
Zh: 看在老天爷的份上，他想，这真是太过分了。不要把事情弄得更糟了——还是继续询问那个孩子吧。

En: He gets it, you lost it. Just don't dig the hole any deeper.
Zh: 他赢了，你输了。只是别在给自己挖坑了。

En: It's safe to say he *did not* establish dominance over you.
Zh: 可以肯定的说，他*并没有*压制住你。

En: If this act was about him trying to establish dominance over you, it's safe to say things didn't go as he'd planned.
Zh: 如果这个行为是他在试图压制住你，那么可以肯定的说他并没有如愿。

En: The creature behind the fence has fallen ominously silent. Only her eyes are alive, jumping from actor to actor.
Zh: 围栏后面的生物诡异地沉默了。只有她的眼睛是活动的，从一个人跳到另一个人身上。

En: She understands: The battle for the control over Cuno is a battle of madness against madness. For the time being, her madness has proven less potent.
Zh: 她明白了：争夺坤诺控制权的斗争就是疯狂与疯狂的斗争。目前为止，她的疯狂已经被证实并没有那么严重。

En: Interesting. This tug of war has probably happened between her and Cuno's violent father too.
Zh: 有意思。这场拉锯战很可能也发生在她与坤诺残暴的父亲之间。

En: Ouch, my arm... and my fucking *knee*...
Zh: “嗷，我的胳膊……该死的还有我的*膝盖*……”

En: Yeah, I deserved this.
Zh: “是啊，我自找的。”

En: Stop laughing!
Zh: “别笑了！”

En: Kim, help me up!
Zh: “金，扶我起来！”

En: No.
Zh: “不要。”

En: The creature behind the fence makes it a point to laugh even harder.
Zh: 围栏后面的家伙笑的更厉害了。

En: Someone call an ambulance, I think I really hurt myself!
Zh: “快叫救护車，我感觉我是真的受伤了！”

En: Officer, you need to get up. This is turning into a farce.
Zh: “警官，你得站起来。这已经变成一场闹剧了。”

En: That's what you get if you fuck with the Cuno. You get fucked up.
Zh: “这就是你挑衅坤诺的下场，一顿痛揍。”

En: It's a dry laugh. Mirthless. She's not even actually enjoying this. She only wants to hurt you.
Zh: 一声干笑。阴郁。她甚至并不喜欢这样。她只是单纯地想伤害您。

En: Cuno made you his fuck-gimp! He bursts into a violent fit of laughter. Sounds like a flock of seagulls taking flight.
Zh: “坤诺让你成为了他的手下败将！”他突然大笑起来，仿佛一群海鸥原地起飞了。

En: The lieutenant isn't laughing. At all.
Zh: 金没有一丝笑意。

En: Pig, Cuno thought you had this. What happened? Cuno can do *anything* now. He's writhing with joy, like the power you gave him is too much to take.
Zh: “猪猡，坤诺以为你能搞定呢。怎么了？坤诺现在可以做*任何事*了。”他浑身洋溢着喜悦，就像你给他的力量有些超过负荷了似的。

En: Get a hold of yourself. At least the previous horror is over. At least they're no longer...
Zh: 控制一下自己。至少之前的恐惧已经过去了，至少他们不再……

En: It's not as bad as it feels. You've maybe pulled a joint; the indignity makes it worse.
Zh: 其实也没有感觉上的那么糟。你可能是拉伤了关节；侮辱让它更严重了。

En: Try really, *really* hard to hold it together.
Zh: 非常，*非常*努力地控制自己。

En: Pig's tryin' not to cry. Cuno can't believe this shit! It's like he's now realized he has super-powers. "Can *no one* stop the Cuno?!"
Zh: “猪猡忍着不让自己哭出来，坤诺真是不敢相信！”就好像他突然意识到自己有超能力似的。“难道*没有人*能阻止坤诺了吗？！”

En: Pig's biting down on his knuckles. Cuno can't believe this shit! It's like he's now realized he has super-powers. "Can *no one* stop the Cuno?!"
Zh: “猪猡在咬自己的猪蹄了，坤诺真是不敢相信！”就好像他突然意识到自己有超能力似的。“难道*没有人*能阻止坤诺了吗？！”

En: Stay with me, the biting is having *some* kind of effect, the pain is receding...
Zh: 听我说，这样咬是有*某种*效果的，疼痛正在消退……

En: Yeah, you deserve this. Trying to show your dick to Cuno. Cuno was scared.
Zh: “是啊，是你自找的。想让坤诺看你的老二，坤诺吓坏了。”

En: Oh god, do something quick, they're gonna start *that* again!
Zh: 哦天呐，赶快做点什么，他们又要*开始*了！

En: Now how about we go and do something worth the public's time?
Zh: “不如我们现在去做一些不会浪费大家时间的事情如何？”

En: He gets it. You lost. Now walk away with what remains of your dignity.
Zh: 他赢了，你输了。现在带着你残存的尊严离开吧。

En: You lost. Now walk away with what remains of your dignity.
Zh: 你输了。现在带着你残存的尊严离开吧。

En: Look, f*g... Cuno whispers, even softer than before, vanishingly silent...
Zh: “听着，娘炮……”坤诺低声细语，甚至比以前更加轻柔，逐渐趋于沉静……

En: Look, f*g... Cuno whispers as you retreat, vanishingly silent...
Zh: “听着，娘炮……”坤诺趁你后退时低声细语道，逐渐趋于沉静……

En: I know you wanted to hit me... He lets it linger. "You got that 'I'm gonna fuck that Cuno up' look that Cuno's dad gets. The murder look... the rage look..."
Zh: “我知道你想对我动手……”他踌躇了一会儿。“你脸上那种‘我要狠狠揍坤诺一顿’的表情跟坤诺的爸爸一模一样。杀人犯的表情……愤怒的表情……”

En: Yes, how do I get back to it? I wanna go back to that thought!
Zh: “没错，我要怎么回到那种状态呢？我要恢复那种状态！”

En: Yes, but I didn't act on it. It was just a fantasy.
Zh: “没错，但我并没有采取行动，只是个幻想罢了。”

En: How do you know that? You can't see inside my head. (Whisper) "Can you read my mind, Cuno?"
Zh: “你是怎么知道的？你是看不透我的内心的。”（低语）“你会读心吗，坤诺？”

En: No!
It's good you said that -- here it is! Here we go... Cuno likes this... He starts prancing around again, tongue lolling out of his mouth...
Zh: “你能这么说真是太好了——就在这！来吧……坤诺很喜欢这样……”他又开始蹦来蹦去，舌头从嘴巴里伸了出来……

En: Yes. No. I've changed my mind again.
Zh: “是的。不要，我又改变主意了。”

En: Relax, he can't read your mind. He doesn't know you were thinking that.
Zh: 放松，他并不会读心，他也不知道你有这种想法。

En: There's a dead body, remember? That's what you were doing here. You're a cop on a case.
Zh: 这里还有一具尸体呢，记得吗？这正是你来这里的原因，你是个正在办案的警察。

En: Why don't we get back to the interview, Cuno.
Zh: “为什么我们不继续询问呢，坤诺。”

En: I know what you thought: 'I'm gonna fuck that Cuno up, I'm gonna shut that shit down...' You know what? You should've, because now... He raises he's voice again.
Zh: “我知道你在想什么：‘我要狠狠揍坤诺一顿，我要让他闭嘴……'知道吗？你确实应该这么做，因为现在……”他又提高了嗓门。

En: For emphasis. A *ghost* is saying this. A shit-eating psychopathic ghost with an ace up his sleeve.
Zh: 强调一下。是*鬼魂*在说话。一个自有锦囊妙计的吃屎精神病幽灵。

En: You're NOTHING! You're a joke to Cuno. Cuno LAUGHS at you! He spreads his arms, taking dominion over the yard. "KING CUNO!"
Zh: “你什么都不是！对坤诺来说就是个笑话！坤诺要嘲笑你！”他张开双臂，宣告着对这个院子的主权。“王者坤诺！”

En: Cuno turned you into his prison bitch! You're gonna be *in* this shit with Cuno...
Zh: “坤诺把你变成了他的手下败将！你要和坤诺永远*待在*这里……”

En: No. The lieutenant almost rolls his eyes. "You're not. We can just leave..."
Zh: “不。”警督几乎要翻白眼了。“你不会的。我们可以直接离开……”

En: Bitch, you're gonna be in this shit with Cuno *forever*! The pipo-headed critter doesn't let him finish.
Zh: “你会和坤诺*永远*待在这里的傻逼！”带着毛绒帽的小鬼并没有让他说完。

En: A pipo is a type of hat by the way.
Zh: 顺便一提，那是一种帽子。

En: Whatever. I had some questions about the crime scene.
Zh: “随你怎么说。我对犯罪现场还有一些疑问。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I can. His voice is so silent, it seems the words are echoing in your head, not coming from his lips. "Cuno can smell that violence shit. I know what you were thinking..."
Zh: “我会。”他的声音是如此低沉，似乎这些话是直接回响在你的脑海中，而非从他的嘴里说出来的。“坤诺能闻到暴力的气味，我知道你在想什么……”

En: 'I'm gonna fuck that Cuno up. I'm gonna shut his shit down...' You know what? You should have hit the Cuno, because NOW... He raises his voice again.
Zh: “‘我要狠狠揍坤诺一顿，我要让他闭嘴……’知道吗？你确实应该这么做，因为现在……”他又提高了嗓门。

En: Nice pipo.
Zh: “毛绒帽挺漂亮的。”

En: You don't talk to me about my fucking pipo! You don't know where I come from. You're just Cuno's bottom bitch.
Zh: “别他妈对我的毛绒帽评头论足！你根本不知道我从何而来，区区一个坤诺的手下败将罢了。”

En: It's safe to say he has established dominance over you.
Zh: 可以肯定的说，他已经压制住了你。

En: The whole charade was about establishing dominance over you. It's safe to say he has succeeded.
Zh: 整场闹剧都是为了压制住你，可以说他已经成功了。

En: The whole charade was about establishing dominance over you. You backing up means he's succeeded.
Zh: 整场闹剧都是为了压制住你，你的退缩意味着他已经成功了。

En: Backing up was a bad idea, now he thinks he's established dominance over you.
Zh: 退缩并不是个好主意，现在他认为自己已经压制住你了。

En: Okay, Cuno is kind to his bitch. Ask your questions, but remember. He taps at his temporal lobe. "This *changes* shit."
Zh: “好吧，坤诺总是善待输家。问你的问题吧，但是记住，“他敲了敲自己的颞叶。”这*改变*不了什么。”

En: *Click*, *click*, *click*, *click* goes the little pipo-wearing asshole behind the fence.
Zh: “*咔哒*，*咔哒*，*咔哒*，*咔哒*”围栏后面那个戴着毛绒帽的小混蛋说着。

En: Meaning, there's been a change in some kind of evil clockwork. There will be *consequences*.
Zh: 意味着，某种邪恶的发条装置发生了变化，必定会带来一些*后果*。

En: Oh no, you pussied out again. Shouldn't have done that, Cuno's gonna bitch you out now. Cuno's gonna *sub let* you... He raises his voice again.
Zh: “哦不，你又退缩了。你不应该这么做的，坤诺要让你滚蛋了，坤诺要*驱逐*你了……”他又提高了嗓门。

En: Pass you around like Candy, fat boy! Pass your fat ass AROUND! He spreads his arms, taking dominion over the yard. "KING CUNO!"
Zh: “像糖果一样滚下去吧，肥猪！滚下去！”他张开双臂，宣告着对这个院子的主权。“王者坤诺！”

En: Don't listen to the blind fuck, piggo, you're doing the right thing here, trying to get Cuno to like you again. He pauses to think.
Zh: “别听那个瞎子的，猪猡。你这么做是对的，让坤诺重新喜欢上你。”他停下来思索着。

En: I've come to ask questions -- I still have a case to solve.
Zh: “我是来问问题的——我还有个案子要解决呢。”

En: Look, C, it's the fucking war-pig! Try not to knock yourself out, f****t!
Zh: “看啊，坤妮，这还是一头斗猪呢！别把自己撞晕了，傻逼！”

En: Have you come to make your offering to Cuno?
Zh: “你是来跟坤诺开价的吗？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Look, you're on my crime scene. I can't have uncooperative civilians here.
Zh: “听着，你出现在我的犯罪现场，这里不欢迎不配合的平民。”

En: Whoah-whoah! The boy hovers in your face. "What's this psycho shit? You gonna pretend like nothing happened?"
Zh: “哇-哇哦！”男孩在你面前晃来晃去。“这算什么鬼？你想装作什么都没有发生吗？”

En: Whatever then. [Leave.]
Zh: “那随便吧。”[离开。]

En: Whatever then.
Zh: “那随便吧。”

En: A material offering is implied. He wants *stuff*. Only stuff can make it okay again.
Zh: 他在暗示你实质性的补偿，他想要*物品*，只有物品才能让一切恢复如常。

En: Cool what? What the fuck is wrong with you?
Zh: “好什么好？你他妈是怎么回事儿？”

En: I think you broke his brain when you fucked him up, Cuno.
Zh: “我觉得你可能把他的脑子打坏了，坤诺。”

En: You lost your shit in the booth, tried to beat a confession out of Cuno... and pissed yourself?
Zh: “你在卡座那里发了疯，想逼坤诺招供……还喝得大醉？”

En: That's right, Cuno. You're right. The sycophantic pipo-wearing pup behind the fence agrees.
Zh: “没错，坤诺，你说得对。”围栏后面那只戴着毛绒帽的小舔狗赞同道。

En: What, do you think Cuno's fucking *eighty years old*? Tryin'a bring me bum juice... make the Cuno paranoid!
Zh: “干嘛，你觉得坤诺他妈的*八十岁*了吗？想给我灌迷魂汤……让坤诺发昏！”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: You don't like alcohol?
Zh: “你不喜欢喝酒吗？”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: Just because you get high off that shit doesn't mean Cuno has to fuck himself with that fermented, -- he points at the bottle -- "piss and shit mix."
Zh: “不要以为自己有酒瘾，就意味着坤诺也会为此发疯”——他指着瓶子——“恶心的混合物。”

En: Tryin'a get a get a twelve year old hooked on the shit-shake... He looks like he can't believe your depravity.
Zh: “想让一个十二岁的小孩迷上这玩意……”他看上去难以相信你会堕落至此。

En: Hmh... the lieutenant mumbles optimistically. "Alcohol use *has* been declining in the under 16 bracket this past decade..."
Zh: “嗯……”警官乐观地咕哝着。“过去的十年间，十六岁以下人群的饮酒量一直在下降……”

En: This pleases him.
Zh: 这让他很愉悦。

En: The lieutenant's opinion of you has *definitely* lowered.
Zh: 警督对你的评价*绝对*降低了。

En: You don't need to. He doesn't know anything.
Zh: “你没必要这么做，他什么都不知道。”

En: Oh, Cuno knows. Cuno knows shit, he squints at you. "Expensive shit. Mysterious shit. *Killer* shit."
Zh: “哦，坤诺知道，坤诺当然知道，”他眯着眼睛看你。“昂贵的玩意，神秘的玩意，*杀手*的玩意。”

En: He does not know *killer* related shit, sire. It's a falseness. He *may* know mysterious shit, however... it's hard to tell.
Zh: 他才不知道跟*杀手*有关的玩意，大人。这就是个错误。不过他*可能*知道一点神秘的玩意吧……很难说。

En: See? He could know something. So how about it, Cuno? (Shake the pack of cigarettes at the boy.)
Zh: “你看？他可能确实知道点什么。你怎么说，坤诺？”（朝着男孩摇晃那包香烟。）

En: Historical revisionism has taken place.
Zh: 历史修正主义上演了。

En: The wide grin above his clenched jaw agrees. His heavy breathing, his dilated pupils, it all speaks of one thing -- this was a really good idea!
Zh: 他紧咬牙关的嘴咧着笑意，表示同意。他那沉重的呼吸，长大的瞳孔都说明了一件事——这真是一个好主意！

En: He *really* likes this. This motor lorry shit makes Cuno happy. You could say you interfaced with his good side.
Zh: 他*真的*很喜欢这个，这辆货車让坤诺很高兴。可以说你与他的关系往好的方面发展了。

En: Check out this bizarre drug I found. (Show him the pyrholidon.)
Zh: “看看我发现的神奇的药。”（向他展示吡嚯浣酮。）

En: No, you don't. He stops you. "No one is *checking out* anything. Put that away."
Zh: “不，你没有。”他阻止了你。“没有人*要看*任何东西，把它放到一边去。”

En: Not this *dreamer* shit again... Cuno slaps his face. "Why do Cuno's bitches always try and bring Cuno that lame sha-la-la-la-bang shit? Cuno's not a fucking witch doctor!"
Zh: “别又是这个*梦幻*玩意了……”坤诺扇了自己一耳光。“为什么坤诺的手下败将总是想把这种土鳖的玩意拿给坤诺呢？坤诺他妈的又不是巫医！”

En: No, I didn't... The adolescent is momentarily disoriented. "It's fucking *Cuno*. Say it right, bitch, say it like it's said."
Zh: “不，我没有……”这个少年暂时失去了方向感。“他妈的是*坤诺*。说啊，傻逼，像这样说出来。”

En: It was only once... The little rat isn't having it. "And you fucking asked me to nick it, *Kuuno*!"
Zh: “只有一次……”这是小老鼠并没有拿。“你他妈的居然要我去偷，*库诺*！”

En: Kuuno. That's an Oranjese name.
Zh: 库诺，那是个奥兰治名字。

En: Anyway. He mentally props himself back up. "Cuno doesn't do that radioactive shit. Makes Cuno's dick fall off. Cuno's got a huge dick."
Zh: “总之，”他精神上强撑着自己。“坤诺不用那种有辐射的玩意，会让坤诺的丁丁掉下来，坤诺可是有个大丁丁。”

En: A pretty *lame* oranjese name. Nowhere near *Cuno*... The thought crosses your mind and then it's gone. Perhaps something for later?
Zh: 一个很*土鳖*的奥兰治名字，完全不像*坤诺*……这个想法在你的脑海里转瞬即逝，以后再说吧？

En: Bitches slapping each other, fighting for Cuno's attention. Cuno likes this pole dancing shit -- but it's not good enough. He crosses his hands.
Zh: “菜鸡们又在互啄了，想争夺坤诺的注意。坤诺还挺喜欢这场钢管舞的——但还不够好。”他双手交握。

En: What a fucking pooper... Cuno points at his heart. "Bitch doesn't have the pump for it. Old bitch, pump's bust. Cuno has the pump for it. Cuno has a giant pump."
Zh: “该死的混球……”坤诺指着他的心脏。“贱人没有那么大的能耐。老东西，心脏快要爆炸了吧。坤诺的心脏好着呢，坤诺有一个大心脏。”

En: Don't ruin it for Cuno. Can't you see Cuno's pump can still take it? Can still pump the good.
Zh: 别坏了坤诺的好事。你看不出来坤诺的心脏还能承受吗？还好着呢。

En: What the *fuck* are you talking about? Those are barely words. Anyway... this didn't work.
Zh: 你*他妈*说什么呢？甚至都不算一句话。总而言之……这行不通。

En: Nothing, lieutenant. I don't want to get into this. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么，警督。我不想扯上关系。”[离开。]

En: This is probably too lame for you, Cuno, but maybe you know someone... (Hold out the *Hjelmdallerman* book.)
Zh: “或许这对你来说有点土鳖了，坤诺，但或许你知道有人……”（拿出*来自赫姆达尔的男人*。）

En: This is probably too lame for *you*, but maybe you know someone who'd dig this... (Hold out the *Man from Hjelmdall* book.)
Zh: “或许这对*你*来说有点土鳖了，但或许你知道有人……”（拿出*来自赫姆达尔的男人*。）

En: Uh... Cuno doesn't fucking read! He quickly glances at the fence. "Fucking book shit, fucks with your mind..."
Zh: “呃……坤诺他妈的不识字！”他飞快地瞥了一眼围栏。”该死的书，你脑袋有问题吧……”

En: Cuno knows this four-eyed fuck, totally hooked on the book, can't get enough of this shit. Fucking sad really... Cuno could make mad money off him, exploit his disease.
Zh: “坤诺知道一个四眼混球，每日沉迷书本，对那玩意欲罢不能。真他妈的悲哀……坤诺会利用他的毛病，从他身上大赚一笔。”

En: Who are you talking about, Cuno?
Zh: “你在说谁，坤诺？”

En: A guy Cuno knows! He shouts at the fence. "Martin. From out of town. From Graad or some shit."
Zh: “坤诺认识的人！”他冲着围栏喊道。“马丁。从城外来的，是格拉德还是哪里。”

En: This boy of twelve does not know anyone from Graad, sire. We suspect the boy himself might be this mystery 'Martin'.
Zh: 这个十二岁的男孩根本不认识什么格拉德人，大人。我们怀疑这个男孩就是那个神秘的‘马丁’本人。

En: You don't know anyone from Graad. You want it for yourself, don't you?
Zh: “你根本不认识格拉德人。是你自己想要，不是吗？”

En: So you want the book?
Zh: “你想要这本书？”

En: Fine... Cuno's gonna take it off your fat ass. At least he's gonna make some paper off this shit... He eagerly pockets the book. "Now, what do you want?"
Zh: “好吧……坤诺就从你的猪蹄上取过来好了。至少他还能用这玩意赚点钱……”他急切地把书装进口袋。“现在，你想要什么？”

En: Fine... Cuno's gonna take it off your fat ass. At least he's gonna make some paper off this shit... He eagerly pockets the book. "Now, what do you want?"
Zh: “好吧……坤诺就从你的猪蹄上取过来好了。至少他还能用这玩意赚点钱……”他急切地把书装进口袋。“现在，你想要什么？”

En: The fuck do you know what Cuno wants or knows, huh? The kid lashes out at you. "Shove that book-hook in your fat ass and find something worthy to bring to the Cuno!"
Zh: “你他妈怎么知道坤诺想要什么或者认识什么人，哈，”这孩子嘲讽道。“把书塞进你的肥屁股吧，去给坤诺找点值钱的东西！”

En: Shove that shit in your fat ass and stop wasting Cuno's time if you ain't got anything to offer to the Cuno.
Zh: “把那玩意塞进你的肥屁股吧。如果你不给坤诺找来值钱的东西，就别再浪费坤诺的时间了。”

En: Actually I wanna keep the book.
Zh: “说实话我想留下这本书。”

En: He's disappointed by your change of heart.
Zh: 你临时变卦让他很失望。

En: You can still see the top of her hat from behind the fence.
Zh: 你仍然可以从围栏后方看到她的帽子。

En: She continues to be *here* -- all due to you being a coward and not shooting her. The contempt is thick enough to cut through with a knife.
Zh: 她还在*这里*——都因为你是个懦夫，没有朝她开枪。那种轻蔑之情浓郁的需要用尖刀才能穿透。

En: This kid is way, way worse than the other one.
Zh: 这个孩子远比，远比另一个要难搞得多。

En: Her knuckles have turned white, holding on to that fence. She's been here for days, seething.
Zh: 她抓着围栏的指关节都变成了白色，她已经在这里怒火中烧好几天了。

En: Logic error! She's not sleeping right now.
Zh: 逻辑错误！她现在可没有睡着。

En: Apparently, she doesn't like people standing behind her back.
Zh: 很明显，她不喜欢有人站在她的身后。

En: If anything, you're afraid she'll take *you* out one day...
Zh: 如果真有什么的话，那就是你害怕有一天她会把*你*带走……

En: The girl is ignoring you. It's like you don't exist at all as far as she's concerned.
Zh: 女孩无视了你。就好像在她看来，你根本不存在。

En: A breeze whistles slightly through the fence. "Pig broke out of the pigpen. Fuck off before you shit on everything!"
Zh: 一阵风轻轻吹过围栏。“猪从猪圈里跑出来了。在你把事情全搞砸之前赶紧给我滚！”

En: Is it you? Are you the Child of Communism the wind told me to find?
Zh: “是你吗？风让我去寻找的康米主义之子就是你吗？”

En: Is it you? Are you the Child of the Big Sea the wind told me to find?
Zh: “是你吗？风让我去寻找的大海之子就是你吗？”

En: Pig wants to know who I am? I'm the biggest FUCK YOU of your entire life -- go on and get, piggy!
Zh: “猪猡也想知道我是谁？我操你妈——滚蛋吧，猪猡！”

En: I'll die before I squeal, pig. She hides behind the fence.
Zh: “我死也不会告密的，猪猡。”她躲到了围栏的后面。

En: Listen, child... That corpse made me puke twice, and I was wondering why you don't...
Zh: “听着，孩子……那具尸体都让我吐了两次了，我很好奇为什么你不会……”

En: Listen, child. The smell is so bad that I threw up, and I was wondering...
Zh: “听着，孩子。那股味道难闻到我都吐了，我很好奇……”

En: Child, converse with me.
Zh: “孩子，跟我说说吧。”

En: Cuno, the pig's getting pretty close to me, she hisses. "Come to snuff my shit out, I think."
Zh: “坤诺，猪猡离我太近了，”她轻蔑到。“该不是来刺杀我的吧。”

En: Looks like it's time for me to go, Cuno. Pig's come to take me in.
Zh: “看来我该离开了，坤诺。猪猡来抓我了。”

En: You there, behind the fence!
Zh: “你过来，围栏后面那个。”

En: Hey, kid!
Zh: “嘿，小孩！”

En: Walk away. [Leave.]
Zh: 走开。[离开。]

En: What's this... kid shit? Fucking mind games... She puts her finger to her temple. "I'd rather die than squeal!"
Zh: “这算什么……小孩子的把戏？该死的心理游戏……”她的手指按在太阳穴上。“我死都不会说的！”

En: You don't wanna fuck with me -- I got my hands bloody. The hat drops even lower. "I'm not here, pig. You're not seeing this."
Zh: “你不会想招惹我的——我的手可是沾过血的。”帽子被压得更低了。“我没来过这儿，猪猡。你什么都没看见。”

En: Stay away from me, pig -- you don't wanna see what happens when you corner me.
Zh: “离我远一点，猪猡——如果你逼得太紧，后果将不堪设想。”

En: Murder was the case, was the case, they gave me... She has almost vanished behind the fence. Only the top of her hat remains.
Zh: “谋杀情况属实，情况属实，他们给我……”她几乎要消失在围栏后面，只露出一顶帽子尖。

En: Can't believe you missed... We were so close, you and I... fucking noodle fingers! Can't you see I'm *IMMORTAL* now!
Zh: “真不敢相信你竟然失手了……我们离得那么近，你和我……真他妈的笨手笨脚！你看不出我现在*死不掉*了吗！”

En: Can't believe you didn't take the shot. Fucking coward... waved your gun like a clown. Next time you point that shit at me, you better take the shot.
Zh: “真不敢相信你竟然没开枪。该死的懦夫……像个小丑似的挥着抢。下次你再用那玩意指着我，最好扣下扳机。”

En: Are you trying to sneak up on me? she whispers. "Come to slit my throat? In my sleep?"
Zh: “你打算偷袭我吗？”她低语到。“想割断我的喉咙？趁我睡着的时候？”

En: No, why would you even say that?
Zh: “没有，你怎么会这么说？”

En: Are you sleeping right now?
Zh: “你正在睡觉吗？”

En: Pig's come to take me out? Her voice becomes almost inaudible. "Trying to snuff me out?"
Zh: “猪猡要来带我走吗？”她的声音几乎听不见了。“想干掉我吗？”

En: Then why are you sneaking up on me like that? It's not a good idea to scare me, pig. Not a good idea *at all*.
Zh: “那你为什么要这么鬼鬼祟祟地接近我？吓到我的后果是很严重的，猪猡，*相当*严重。”

En: I'm sorry for sneaking up on you. [Leave.]
Zh: “抱歉这样接近你。”[离开。]

En: Fägäri.... she whispers as you're sneaking away. "Look at the ass on that."
Zh: “基佬…….”在你悄悄离去时，她低声说道。“去查查那个吧。”

En: Take one step closer.
Zh: 上前一步。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: I just want to ask you some questions. (Take another step closer.)
Zh: “我只想问你几个问题。”（再上前一步。）

En: I'm going away for a long, long time, Cuno. Going away for life!
Zh: “我要离开一段时间了，坤诺。有一生那么长的时间。”

En: She means it. The fear is real.
Zh: 她所言非虚，那种恐惧是真实的。

En: What's going on there? Fuck are you trying to pull, pig?
Zh: “这是怎么回事儿？你想拉她吗，猪猡？”

En: Get the fuck out of her face! You got something, talk to me!
Zh: “快从她面前消失！有什么事直接跟我说！”

En: I'm never getting out. She touches her face and then her body. "I'm in it for life now. I'm doing life with you pigs! Fucking sweaty pigs. Fucking fisher-people."
Zh: “我永远出不去了。”她摸了摸自己的脸，和自己的身体。“我要永远留在这里了，我要跟你们这群猪猡耗上一辈子了！该死的咸湿猪猡。该死的渔民。”

En: What are those strange words you use, girl?
Zh: “你这用词怎么这么奇怪啊，小姑娘？”

En: I come from the woods, *kyttä vittu*... You don't wanna go there with me... you don't wanna see what I've seen.
Zh: “我来自森林，*警察*……你不会想要跟我一起过去的……也不会想看我所见到的东西。”

En: Don't be traumatizing her. Get the fuck out of here!
Zh: “别再折磨她了，快他妈的滚！”

En: What do you mean *fisher-people*?
Zh: “*渔民*是什么？”

En: Come. We need to go now. The lieutenant puts his hand on your shoulder.
Zh: “来吧，我们得走了。”警督把手搭在你的肩上。

En: Fucking *näkki*...
Zh: “该死的*女水妖*……”

En: No, Kim! I wanna hear about the *näkki*!
Zh: “不，金！我想听听关于*女水妖*的事！”

En: Okay, okay... I'm good. We're done here. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，好吧……可以了，我们走吧。[离开。]

En: Merrow...
Don't get fucking clever with me, pig... you think you're so clever... She hisses.
Zh: “别跟我耍花招，猪猡……你以为自己很聪明吗……”她轻蔑地说。

En: What does *näkki* mean?
Zh: “*女水妖*是什么意思？”

En: Okay, okay... we're done here. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，好吧……我们走吧。[离开。]

En: Trying to sneak up on me again? Trying to snuff me out? Get away, pig!
Zh: “又想偷偷接近我了？又想杀掉我吗？滚远点，猪猡！”

En: Merrow...
So it's a girl... interesting.
Zh: 原来是个女孩……有趣。

En: Yeah Cuno, fuck him up!
Zh: “是啊坤诺，干掉他！”

En: Oh yeah, *napakymppi* Cuno!
Zh: “噢耶，*正中靶心*坤诺！”

En: Right in the dick, Cuno! Get him right in the dick!
Zh: “瞄准他下面，坤诺！瞄准他的下面！”

En: Fuck him up, Cuno. Yeah.
Zh: “干掉他，坤诺，耶！”

En: Yeah, Cuno, yeah!
Zh: “好耶，坤诺，耶！”

En: Right in the mouth-hole!
Zh: “正中嘴巴！”

En: Got fucked... fucked real bad.
Zh: “去死吧……下地狱去吧。”

En: Break him, Cuno!
Zh: “打烂他，坤诺！”

En: Nice one, Cuno!!
Zh: “漂亮，坤诺！！”

En: You should throw the pig at him, Cuno!
Zh: “你应该把猪猡也扔过去，坤诺！”

En: Cuno, throw the pig!
Zh: “坤诺，扔那个猪猡！”

En: Cuno's got this.
Zh: “交给坤诺吧。”

En: Fucker shit himself.
Zh: “该死的混蛋。”

En: Cuno knows how to fucking throw!
Zh: “坤诺他妈的知道怎么扔！”

En: Over here, baconator! Time for launch.
Zh: “看招，终结者！着陆时间到了。”

En: I'm bored, Cuno!
Zh: “我好无聊啊，坤诺！”

En: Let's get out of here, Cuno!
Zh: “我们走吧，坤诺！”

En: Booooring!
Zh: “太无聊啦！”

En: Snap out of it, Cuno!
Zh: “快点走吧，坤诺！”

En: What are we doing, Cuno?
Zh: “我们在干什么，坤诺？”

En: Cuno's fucking electricity! 2000 volts. Cuno-volts.
Zh: “坤诺他妈的能通电！2000伏特！坤诺伏特。”

En: Cuno's gonna have a fucking heart attack!
Zh: “坤诺他妈的要犯心脏病了！”

En: Speedtrain: Cuno -- leaving station!
Zh: “高速列車：坤诺号——离开車站！”

En: Cuno's little shitshow...
Zh: “坤诺的小把戏……”

En: This shit ain't nothing to Cuno.
Zh: “这玩意对坤诺来说不算什么。”

En: Cuno likes this brain-shit. Thinking-shit.
Zh: “坤诺很喜欢动脑子，思考什么的。”

En: Sitting at a desk, Lieutenant Kitsuragi fiddles with a pen, then writes something on the paper in front of him. He shouldn't be doing that, he should be here discussing the footprints.
Zh: 曷城警督坐在办公桌后，摆弄着一支笔，接着在面前的纸上写下了什么东西。那不是他该做的事，他应该在这里和你讨论足迹的事。

En: 1) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 46.
Zh: 1）标准工作靴，配钢趾帽，46码。

En: Eight pairs of boots have shuffled back and forth in the mud. Where else have we seen a gang of men in work boots? That's right, the Hardie boys in the mess hall of Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: 一共八双靴子在泥地里踏来踏去。我们还能在什么地方看到一群穿着工作靴的人？对了，褴褛飞旋食堂里的哈迪兄弟。

En: Maybe more than 12? No. Eight pairs of boots have shuffled back and forth in the mud.
Zh: 也许超过12个？不。只有八双靴子在泥里走来走去。

En: Go over them one by one.
Zh: 逐一清点。

En: Not now. [Leave.]
Zh: 现在不行。 [离开。]

En: 2) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 44.
Zh: 2）标准工作靴，配钢趾帽，44码。

En: 3) Hobnailed work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 43.
Zh: 3）平头钉工作靴，配钢趾帽，43码。

En: 4) Standard work boot, no 45 or 46?
Zh: 4）标准工作靴，45或者46码？

En: Wait, which is it?
Zh: 等等，是哪个？

En: Count more.
Zh: 继续清点。

En: You don't know. It's a miracle you can tell the prints apart as it is. The cold must have preserved them.
Zh: 你不知道。光是能把这些足迹区分开就已经堪称奇迹了。多亏寒冷的天气保留了现场。

En: 5) Another standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 44.
Zh: 5）另一双标准工作靴，配钢趾帽，44码。

En: 6) An aberration -- light as air. Even pace. Same make of boot, but no 41.
Zh: 6）反常的足迹——轻若无物。步伐平稳。相同款式的靴子，但尺码只有41。

En: Male or female?
Zh: 是男性还是女性？

En: Count the rest.
Zh: 清点其它足迹。

En: Impossible to tell. Could also have been an adolescent. The gait is undeveloped.
Zh: 无法判断。也可能是青少年，步态尚未发育完全。

En: 7) The glowing outline of a standard work boot, no 46. But the imprints are *twice* as deep as the others -- the weight exceeds 200 kilograms.
Zh: 7）一双标准工作靴留下了特别明显的轮廓，46码。但鞋印的深度是其他鞋印的*两倍*——重量超过200公斤。

En: 8) And yet another standard work boot, no 44. There's an aberration in the pattern of the sole, however. The right sole is smoother, more worn.
Zh: 8）又一双标准工作靴，44码。但鞋底留下的印迹有些异常。右脚鞋底更光滑，磨损更严重。

En: How many? The lieutenant has been tracking your eyes' movements.
Zh: “有多少人？”警督一直在追踪你的视线。

En: Eight.
Zh: “八个。”

En: Four hundred million.
Zh: “四亿人。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Mhm. He looks to the holes in the mud. "I counted twenty. I take it it's less than that."
Zh: “唔。”他盯着泥里的坑洞。“我数出了二十人。应该比这个少。”

En: This is not a man easily toyed with.
Zh: 这并不是一个好糊弄的角色。

En: I never got the hang of it. Hyperopia. He points to his glasses. "Do you see anything out of the ordinary?"
Zh: “我一直没能掌握这个的窍门。因为远视。”他指了指自己的眼镜。“你发现什么不寻常的事了吗？”

En: I was pretty off then. I counted 20.
Zh: “那我肯定是数错了。我数出了20人。”

En: The same guys are going back and forth.
Zh: “是同一群人在这里来回走动。”

En: Way off. *Waaaaaay*.
Zh: “错大发了。*大——发——了*”

En: I assumed you were counting.
Zh: “我还以为你刚才是在清点足迹呢。”

En: I'm not.
Zh: “我什么都没看出来。”

En: Very well.
Zh: “很好。”

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi does not appreciate you withholding case specific information.
Zh: 曷城警督并不认同你拒绝透露与案件密切相关的信息这种做法。

En: Okay, there are eight.
Zh: “好吧，一共有八个人。”

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 不告诉他。

En: Silence. The lieutenant takes his glasses off and cleans them.
Zh: 警督沉默不语，摘下眼镜仔细擦拭了一番。

En: (Point.) "Light step. Number 41 shoe."
Zh: （指向足迹。）“脚步很轻。41码的靴子。”

En: (Point.) "A heavy one. Two hundred kilogram imprint."
Zh: （指向足迹。）“很重的家伙。需要200公斤才能留下这种脚印。”

En: (Point.) "An aberration. One sole is smoother than the other."
Zh: （指向足迹。）“一对异常的脚印。一个鞋底比另一个更光滑。”

En: We've been through all of it. [Leave.]
Zh: “我们已经都说过一遍了。”[离开。]

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: A woman or a kid?
Zh: “是女人还是孩子？”

En: Two hundred? He thinks for a moment. "This could be the combined weight of two people, one carrying the other who's tied up. Let's say, a heavily built worker carrying a similarly built, armoured man? Maybe it was the fat Hardie boy, the one sitting in the middle?"
Zh: “200？”他思索了一会儿。“会不会是两个人的重量呢，一个人把另一个绑住扛起来了？比如说，一个身强力壮的工人扛着另一个差不多体格的，穿着盔甲的男人？或许是哈迪兄弟中的胖子，坐在中间的那个？”

En: Two hundred? He thinks for a moment. "This could be the combined weight of *two* people, one carrying the other. Let's say: a heavily built worker carrying a soon to be dead man who's tied up?"
Zh: “200？”他思索了一会儿。“会不会是*两个人*的重量呢，一个人把另一个扛起来了？比如说：一个身强力壮的工人扛着另一个差不多体格的，被绑起来的即将死去的男人？”

En: Two hundred? He thinks for a moment. "Could it be the combined weight of *two* people, one carrying the other? Let's say: a heavily built worker carrying a heavily built corpse -- with a bullet in his head?"
Zh: “200？”他思索了一会儿。“会不会是*两个人*的重量呢，一个人把另一个扛起来了？比如说：一个身强力壮的工人扛着另一个差不多体格的——脑袋上中了一枪的尸体？”

En: Two hundred? He thinks for a moment. "Could it be the combined weight of two people, one carrying the other who's tied up? Let's say, a heavily built worker carrying a similarly built, soon-to-be-dead man?"
Zh: “200？”他考虑了片刻。“有没有可能是两个人的体重之和，一个扛着被绑起来的另一个？比如说，一个身强力壮的工人扛着一个体格接近，奄奄一息的男人？”

En: Interesting. Let's name it the Odd-Sole. I wouldn't be surprised if this was the missing Hardie boy... wonder who he is?
Zh: “真有趣，我们就叫它奇异鞋底吧。如果这就是那个失踪的哈迪兄弟，我可不会有一丝惊讶……真想知道他是谁？”

En: Interesting -- let's name it the Odd-Sole.
Zh: “有意思——暂且把它命名为怪鞋底吧。”

En: You can almost *feel* the association taking form in your frontal lobe:
Zh: 你几乎可以*感受*到这种关联正在自己的大脑额叶中成形。

En: You're right, the fat guy from the booth was carrying the victim.
Zh: “你说得对，正是吧台旁的那个胖子扛起了受害人。”

En: One of them was carrying him over.
Zh: “其中一人把受害者扛了过来。”

En: Maybe it was a giant?
Zh: “也有可能是个巨人？”

En: It could have been one extremely obese person.
Zh: “也有可能是个极度肥胖的人。”

En: It could have been an extremely obese person.
Zh: “可能是个特别胖的人。”

En: I can't see any prints fitting the armoured boots the victim was wearing, can you? Someone had to carry him. Are any of the *other* prints deep enough?
Zh: “我没看到与受害者穿着的装甲靴相符的足迹，你看到了吗？肯定有人扛着他。你看到*别的*更深的脚印了吗？“

En: Possibly, yes. The lieutenant marks something down in his notebook.
Zh: “确实，有可能。”警督在他的笔记本上做了某种记录。

En: No.
Zh: “我没骗你。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “我说谎了。”

En: Which ones?
Zh: “哪几个？”

En: An obese person is becoming less likely.
Zh: “胖子的可能性变得越来越低了。”

En: Point at random prints.
Zh: 随便指向几个足迹。

En: Point at the pine tree.
Zh: 指向松树。

En: None of them.
Zh: “哪个都不是。”

En: That's a tree. I think we're close to a conclusion here.
Zh: “那是一颗树。就这个问题，我们马上就能得出结论了。”

En: I don't know... The lieutenant squints, trying to assess their depth: "Are you sure? I could swear you're *lying* to me."
Zh: “我不确定……”警督眯起眼睛，试图估算足迹的深度：“你确定吗？我敢肯定，你绝对是在*骗*我。”

En: It would take more subterfuge to trick the lieutenant.
Zh: 想要骗过警督还需要更高明的花招。

En: Mhm. I think we have arrived at a conclusion here.
Zh: “唔，就这个问题，我们已经得出结论了。”

En: But why?
Zh: 但是为什么？

En: Maybe it *wasn't*?
Zh: “也许*不是那样*？”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: I do. The lieutenant marks something in his notebook. "One of them carried him over."
Zh: “我知道。”警督在他的笔记本上记录着。“其中一个人把他扛了过来。”

En: I was thinking it was the Giant of Koko Nur.
Zh: “我还以为是库库诺尔的巨人呢。”

En: But maybe it *was* -- just imagine it, a giant man at least two and a half meters tall.
Zh: “但也许*正是那样*——想象一下，一个身高至少两米半的巨人。”

En: You're right, it probably wasn't.
Zh: “你是对的，也许没那回事。”

En: There is real, palpable excitement in his voice -- at the prospect of it *not* being a giant.
Zh: 他的声音里流露出一股真诚、坦然的兴奋——只是因为想到那*不是*巨人。

En: Mhm, he nods enthusiastically. "And I was thinking it *wasn't* the Giant of Koko Nur."
Zh: “唔，”他激动地点着头。“在我看来，那*并不是*库库诺尔的巨人。”

En: It could have been -- but if it *wasn't*... He waits for you to finish the sentence.
Zh: “有这个可能——但如果*不是*的话……”他在等着你把这句话说完。

En: Mmmhm... The thought pleases him.
Zh: “嗯哼……”这个念头令他感到愉悦。

En: You're thinking: *why* did they have to carry him?
Zh: “你在考虑的是：他们*为什么*要扛着他？”

En: Let it be.
Zh: 多说无益。

En: There are eight pairs of tracks in the mud. Three stick out.
Zh: 泥里有共八对足迹。其中有三对非常显眼。

En: Yes, they could have used a makeshift stretcher or just marched him up to the gallows.
Zh: “是的，他们原本可以用临时担架，或者直接把他拖上绞刑架。”

En: Maybe the victim wasn't conscious.
Zh: “也许被害者当时已经失去意识了。”

En: Our reading of these prints could be wrong.
Zh: “我们对这些足迹的分析可能有误。”

En: Even easier to carry on a stretcher -- or between two men. Anyway, it's for future consideration. What else can you see?
Zh: “用担架抬着反而更容易——或者由两人架着。总之，这个问题就留到以后考虑吧。你还有别的发现吗？”

En: I'm not assuming it isn't. But it's still helpful -- is there anything else?
Zh: “我无意反对。这是合理的推测——还有其他发现吗？”

En: Maybe there was a physically impressive strongman in their midst. Someone who wanted to impress their peers.
Zh: 也许他们之中有一位体格出众的大力士。此人想向同伴炫耀自己的力气。

En: Could he have been showing off? Maybe the carrier wanted to impress his peers?
Zh: “他会不会是在炫耀呢？也许那个搬运者想在他的同伴面前表演一番？”

En: Maybe the carrier wanted to impress their peers with a display of physical might?
Zh: “也许负责搬运的人想通过这种做法向同伴炫耀自己的力气？”

En: You mean a display of athletic ability meant to belittle the victim? A local champion carrying the intruder alone? I can see it. Anyway -- the others?
Zh: “你的意思是他通过展示体能来贬低受害者？本地的勇士单独扛起入侵者？我明白了。话说回来——有其他发现吗？”

En: I don't think there's any way to be sure.
Zh: “我觉得这是没办法确认的。”

En: Could be a woman?
Zh: “可能是女人？”

En: Could be a kid?
Zh: “可能是小孩？”

En: Understood. Anything else?
Zh: “明白了。还有其他发现吗？”

En: Okay. How do you know?
Zh: “好吧。你是怎么知道的？”

En: He knows it's hard to discern sex from a person's gait.
Zh: 他知道很难通过人的步态来辨明性别。

En: I don't.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: I just do.
Zh: “我无所不知。”

En: I'm just saying random things while looking at holes in the mud, I have no idea where any of this is coming from.
Zh: “我只是一边看着泥里的坑一边胡言乱语罢了，我根本就看不懂这些到底是怎么回事。”

En: That's okay, he nods, "go on."
Zh: “好吧，”他点点头，“继续。”

En: A *driver* would wear down their right shoe before the left -- the accelerator is on the right.
Zh: “*司机*右脚鞋子的磨损会比左脚的更严重——油门在右脚边。”

En: Do you have any ideas, lieutenant?
Zh: “有何高见，警督？”

En: Mhm. He takes his glasses off and cleans them.
Zh: “唔。”他摘下眼镜擦拭着镜片。

En: A normal human being like Lieutenant Kitsuragi here needs to pause before they know whose shoe this is. Not you. You literally *feel* the association taking shape in your *torque* bent frontal lobe.
Zh: 曷城警督这样的普通人在确认鞋子的归属之前需要思考一段时间。而你不需要。你可以直接了当地*感觉*到其中的关联在大脑额叶中*扭转*成型。

En: Someone operating a work bench -- with a pedal? Like a joiner at the harbour? He thinks for a second. "Or maybe a drummer..."
Zh: “某个工作台操作员——那种有踏板的工作台？比如在港口工作的细木工人？”他思考了片刻。“也可能是一个鼓手……”

En: He regrets it the moment he says it.
Zh: 他刚开口就后悔了。

En: A drummer? That's stupid.
Zh: “鼓手？太蠢了吧。”

En: So one of the people we are looking for is a drummer?
Zh: “你的意思是，在我们要找的人里有一个鼓手？”

En: Don't say anything, just nod.
Zh: 什么都不说，默默点头。

En: A drummer only uses their right foot for the kick drum... He explains, then stops and looks at the hole in the mud. "You're right, it's stupid."
Zh: “一个只用右脚踩底鼓的鼓手……”他解释到一半，停下来继续观察泥里的坑。“没错，这确实很蠢。”

En: So maybe one of them wasn't a dockworker but a *driver*? His eyes narrow. "That traffic jam in front of the harbour gates -- I wonder if it's lasted as long as the strike?"
Zh: “也就是说，他们中的一员不是码头工人，而是*司机*？”他眯起双眼。“港口大门前的那场交通堵塞——持续时间是不是和罢工一样长了？”

En: Check out the big brains on whatever your name is!
Zh: 姓名什么的无关紧要，快发动你那发达的大脑吧！

En: No, it's not. Forget I said it, we're not looking for a drummer.
Zh: “不，没这回事。忘掉我刚才说的吧，我们不用找鼓手。”

En: I don't know why I said that. We're not looking for a drummer, we're looking for a group of dockworkers.
Zh: “我不知道自己为什么要说那种话。我们不用找鼓手，我们要找的是一群码头工人。”

En: The lieutenant clearly appreciates the chance to clear up the drummer issue himself.
Zh: 警督显然很高兴能有机会亲自澄清鼓手的问题。

En: I just blew this shit right open.
Zh: “我令真相大白。”

En: Case solved.
Zh: “结案。”

En: Behold my brain the golden throne of my consciousness. In here I am seated. Shackled. From here I police the land.
Zh: “见识一下我的大脑吧，这可是意识的黄金王座。我拱手垂裳，披枷带锁。在此治理这片土地。”

En: I have a name and it's god damn Raphaël, how many times do I have to say it?!
Zh: 我行不更名，坐不改姓，鼎鼎大名唤作拉斐尔，还要我重复多少次？！

En: Never again, Mr. Costeau. Everyone knows what your name is -- Raphaël A. Costeau. From here on it's what they will call you.
Zh: 不用重复了，库斯托先生。您的大名无人不知——拉斐尔·A·库斯托。从今往后，人人都要这样称呼您。

En: Unless they were a machine worker, or a drummer I guess.
Zh: “除非这是专门操作机械的工人，也可能是鼓手。”

En: Yes... a drummer. I don't know about a drummer. Seems tangential. However.
Zh: “是啊……鼓手。我不怎么了解鼓手。但这听起来有些沾边。”

En: Yes, you are after all, The Law. We are the law of the land.
Zh: “是啊，毕竟您就是法律嘛。我们就是这片土地上的法。”

En: The joy dissipates down your spinal column like a grounding effect. Glorious intellect at work!
Zh: 一股愉悦沿着你的脊柱消散，宛如地面效应一般。辉煌的才智大显神威！

En: We should keep our eyes open around the traffic jam, see whether anyone strikes out as a potential suspect. Seems prudent, no?
Zh: “我们应该留意一下交通堵塞的状况，看看有没有嫌犯露出马脚。你觉得这个主意够严谨吗？”

En: The case is not solved, he says without a speck of laughter. "However."
Zh: “不过，”他面无一丝笑意。“案子还没解决呢。”

En: Wide open, he says without a speck of laughter. "The expression is *blew the shit wide open*."
Zh: “大白于天下。”他面无一丝笑意。“俗话说的是*令真相大白于天下*。”

En: Beneath the mirthless exterior the lieutenant is amused. Although he does not actually think you blew it wide open.
Zh: 在沉闷的外表之下，警督觉得你的说法还挺有趣的。虽然他并不真的认为你揭示了真相。

En: We are the fucking Law.
Zh: “我们就是你大爷的法。”

En: FUCK WITH US AND GET FUCKED.
Zh: “谁敢惹老子，老子就干你全家。”

En: How should we lay down the next Law?
Zh: “我们该如何制定下一部律法呢？”

En: You and him are on the same page now -- about The Law and being it. This has affected your relationship positively.
Zh: 你与警督就法律与人法合一达成了共识。这对你们之间的关系产生了积极的影响。

En: Yes. Prudent.
Zh: “很好，很严谨。”

En: I'm not sure. We don't want to attract too much attention.
Zh: “不好说。我们不该吸引过多注意。”

En: Mhm. The lieutenant writes the information down in his notebook, then reverts to the tracks in the mud.
Zh: “嗯。”警督在自己的笔记本上记录了相关信息，又转过身去盯着泥里的足迹了。

En: Yes, it would seem we are -- both -- from the police.
Zh: “是的，看起来你我——都是——警察。”

En: What do you see? The lieutenant cleans his glasses, squinting at the shapes in the mud.
Zh: “你发现什么了？”金擦拭着他的眼镜，眯起眼镜盯着泥里的足迹。

En: How old do you think these tracks are?
Zh: “你觉得这些足迹是多久之前的？”

En: I pulled last week's forecast for coastal Revachol. Seven days below freezing. The day before -- the day of his hanging -- was the last warm day.
Zh: “我调查了瑞瓦肖沿海地区上周的天气预报。最近七天都处于零度以下。他被吊死的那天之前——是最后一个暖和的日子。”

En: A week maybe? Seven days would fit the time frame provided to us by the caller, who reported the hanging.
Zh: “也许是一周之前？七天正好符合报案者向我们提交的时间范围。”

En: It is not impossible.
Zh: 这不是不可能的事。

En: How do you know?
Zh: “你是怎么知道的？”

En: There are eight pairs of tracks in the mud. Three stick out, but...
Zh: 泥里有共八对足迹。其中有三对非常显眼，但是……

En: Correct again. Sub-zero temperatures would preserve the tracks in a good state. The commotion here *could* have taken place a week ago.
Zh: 正确的判断。零度以下的气温能够完好地保存足迹。骚乱*可能*是一周之前在这里发生的。

En: What do you think happened here?
Zh: “你认为这里发生了什么？”

En: What do I think? He tilts his head. "A mob of people brought someone to the tree. Not by dragging -- probably by carrying him."
Zh: “我认为？”他歪着头。“一群暴徒把某个人带到了树下。不是拖过来——可能是扛过来的。”

En: Indeed, they all stood in a row here and looked at the tree.
Zh: 确实，他们曾在这里站成一排，盯着这棵树。

En: Yes! Everything fits so well. Carried him over, hoisted him up, watched him hang -- this is easy!
Zh: 很好！一切都对上了。把人扛过来，把人吊起来，再看着他被吊死——显而易见！

En: Isn't it strange to have your assumptions confirmed like this? This is what someone whispering *suggestions* in your ear would like you to feel.
Zh: 以这种方式证实自己的推测不是很奇怪吗？你有这种想法，正顺了那种在你耳边提供*建议*的家伙的意。

En: He might be right. Two hundred kilograms of living weight *is* unlikely.
Zh: 也许他是对的。重达200公斤的人*确实*不多见。

En: From here, the boots the victim wears... The lieutenant stops mid sentence. A sudden change in wind direction blows the stench of rotting meat right in your face. He tries to continue...
Zh: “被害者穿的靴子，在这里……”警督话说到一半就停住了。风向突然改变，迎面吹来一阵尸体的腐臭。他试图继续说下去……

En: It's bad. Very bad. Before you can heave the wind changes direction again, the greenhouse plastic flapping in it's gust.
Zh: 恶心。恶心极了。在你想吐之前，风向就再次改变，温室的塑料大棚在阵风中扑腾着。

En: He appears to be wearing some kind of armoured boots. I can't see any exotic prints here... His face muscles twitch. "Someone had to carry him. Are any of the other prints deep enough?"
Zh: “他似乎穿着某种装甲靴。但这里没有那种外来的足迹……”他的面部肌肉抽动着。“一定是有人扛着他。这里有足够深的足迹吗？”

En: My god... what was that?
Zh: “天呐……怎么回事儿？”

En: Wind keeps changing... are any of the prints deep enough in your opinion?
Zh: “风向一直在变……你觉得这里有足够深的足迹吗？”

En: What do I think? A mob of people brought something heavy to the tree. One of them was carrying the victim. They shuffled around, especially under the tree.
Zh: “我认为？一群暴徒把某个重物带到了树下。其中一人扛着受害者。他们在这里走来走去，尤其集中在这棵树的周围。”

En: Then after hoisting him up, they stood in a semicircle facing his direction. At first glance, this appears to be a lynching.
Zh: “在将他吊起来之后，他们站成半圆形的队伍，看着他被吊死。乍看起来就是一场私刑。”

En: Isn't it strange when all your assumptions are confirmed like this? Stood in a semicircle, hung him, carried him over... like you're supposed to be feeling clever. And content.
Zh: 以这种方式证实自己的全部推测不是很奇怪吗？站成半圆形的队伍，吊死这个人，把他扛过来……仿佛有人想让你自我感觉判断准确。并满足于这样的结论。

En: We should withhold being content.
Zh: “我们应该戒骄戒躁。”

En: Say: "Everything fits. We should celebrate by getting absolutely *UNDER THE HAMMER* in this yard."
Zh: 说吧：“一切正如我所料。我们应该在这个院子里庆祝一番，*醉生梦死*。”

En: That's not going to happen, I'm not letting him make it into one of his tasks. Know your limits, tie-creature!
Zh: 我不会让你称心如意的，这必定不会成为他的任务之一。你讲话要有分寸，领带怪物！

En: The lieutenant is saying something, but you can't hear it... Suddenly it seems like your tie is alive again, whispering -- no, *screaming* -- into your ear:
Zh: 警督正在说着什么，但你听不到……突然之间，你的领带仿佛又恢复了生机，在你的耳畔轻声低语——不，是*嘶吼*：

En: Live a little! Everything on the god damn crime scene fits like a glove, you should get *SHITFACED* on this *GOD DAMN CRIME SCENE*!
Zh: 及时行乐吧！这个该死的犯罪现场的一切都与你的假设严丝合缝，你应该在这个*该死的犯罪现场**喝得烂醉*！

En: Say: "I'm experiencing a mild malfunction, lieutenant. Something to do with my tie. It'll pass, can you hold on for a moment?"
Zh: 直说：“我正在经历一场轻微的机能失常，警督。这和我的领带有关。很快就会过去的，你能稍等一会儿吗？”

En: This is not how vivid I want my inner life to be. I just want to get back to solving the crime now.
Zh: 我可不希望自己的精神生活变得如此栩栩如生。我只想回去继续破案。

En: I'll find a way to get drunk on this scene, I swear to god I will.
Zh: 我一定会想方设法在此情此景下大醉一场的，我向天发誓。

En: I like how vivid my interior is, but could we maybe do something that doesn't involve getting drunk?
Zh: 我很享受鲜活的内心世界，但我们能不能做点不用酗酒的事？

En: REGARDEZ LA RETARD! He wants to have fun *without* alcohol. That won't be fun, that'll be boring and sad.
Zh: 瞅瞅这二货！他指望滴酒*不沾*就能快活。那可快活不起来，你只会感到无聊和悲伤。

En: The lieutenant looks at you curiously. A beat passes in silence, the wind blowing, the cargo belt squeaking.
Zh: 警督好奇地观察着你。沉默只持续了一瞬，风声呼啸，货物绑带咯咯作响。

En: I agree, he clears his throat. "Our assumptions could be wrong. Better not to have them confirmed just yet. Do you see anything else?"
Zh: “我同意。”他清了清嗓子。“我们的推测可能有误。最好不要立刻下定论。你还有其他发现吗？”

En: Uhm... His face is one of disbelief. The words coming out of your mouth are filled with such sincerity.
Zh: “嗯……”他露出了难以置信的表情。虽然你的发言充满了真情实意。

En: Yes, it was naive of me to congratulate us. Assumptions are dangerous. He looks around. "Do you see anything else?"
Zh: “是的，现在就庆祝的话也未免太天真了。推测是很危险的。”他环顾四周。“你还有其他发现吗？”

En: The lieutenant doesn't understand what's happening right now. You're twisting at your tie, looking sweaty even by your standards. He needs to say something.
Zh: 警督无法理解眼下到底是什么情况。他只看到你在大汗淋漓地揪着自己的领带，出汗量就算是以你的标准衡量都算离谱。在这种情况下，他总得说点什么。

En: Do you think there's something we're missing?
Zh: “你觉得我们遗漏了什么吗？”

En: SHUT UP, FUCK-MIDGET!
Zh: 闭嘴，傻逼侏儒！

En: I have no idea what's going on here, but you should not do anything that the horrific necktie tells you to do.
Zh: 我不明白这究竟是什么情况，但你绝不应该听从那条可怕的领带。

En: Yes. We should have another look at the tracks.
Zh: “可能有，我们应该再检查一遍这些足迹。”

En: No. Everything fits *too* well.
Zh: “没有遗漏。一切都与推测*完全一致*。”

En: We should just generally withhold being content. With life. It's a philosophical disposition we should have.
Zh: “人的一生应当戒骄戒躁。这是我们应有的处世哲学。”

En: His eyebrows arch. They are the liveliest part of his otherwise quite inexpressive face:
Zh: 他扬起了眉毛。在总是面无表情警督脸上，眉毛可以算是最生动的部分了。

En: Is that so? Well, okay I guess. If it helps with the police-work, by all means. Do you see anything else?
Zh: “这样吗？那好吧。如果这有助于警务的话，就悉听尊便吧。你还有其他发现吗？”

En: It does. He looks around. "I shouldn't have assumed in the first place. The caller could have lied, the whole thing could be misreported. Should we take another look? Or go?"
Zh: “确实。”他环顾四周。“我一开始就不该预设什么的。报案者可能说了谎，整个案件都可能是谎报的结果。我们要再检查一遍吗？还是离开这里？”

En: Mhm. If it fits too well, we might be missing something. What do you see?
Zh: “唔。如果一切都与推测完全一致，说明我们可能遗漏了什么。你觉得呢？”

En: I think we have a firm understanding of what happened here.
Zh: “我认为我们已经切实了解了在这里发生过的一切。”

En: The lieutenant's eyes narrow. He's thinking to himself.
Zh: 警督眯起了眼睛。独自思考着。

En: A doubt perhaps? It would be unprofessional of him to be sure of himself at such an early stage of the investigation.
Zh: 也许他对此抱有疑问？毕竟在调查初期阶段就下这种结论显得非常不专业。

En: Either way... he concludes. "What else?"
Zh: “无论如何……”他总结道：“还有别的发现吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: The lieutenant looks around, tracing your eyes on the tracks.
Zh: 警督环顾四周，追踪着你观察足迹的视线。

En: There are several footprints in the mud, left by work boots: anywhere from 6 to 12 pairs have walked here.
Zh: 泥里保留着几串足迹，是工作靴留下的：大约有6至12人从这里走过。

En: What kind of boots?
Zh: 什么类型的靴子？

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Heavy worker's boots with reinforced toes and hobnails. All over the yard.
Zh: 重体力劳动者的工作靴，配有钢趾帽和平头钉。足迹遍布整个院子。

En: Isn't this something an industrial worker would wear?
Zh: 这不是体力劳动者常穿的靴子吗？

En: Get an exact count.
Zh: 清点出确切的数字。

En: Counted the Hardie boys.
Zh: 计算哈迪兄弟的人数。

En: Hardies said they hanged the victim.
Zh: 哈迪兄弟说是他们吊死了受害人。

En: Viscal said go do it.
Zh: 见微知著说去做吧。

En: Her prints near Video Revachol...
Zh: 她的足迹就在瑞瓦肖录像店旁边……

En: What do you think you are, a super-detective? You're hung over, these are just dents in the mud. No pattern emerges for the time being.
Zh: 你以为自己是什么，超人警探吗？你还没从宿醉里恢复过来呢，这些只是泥里的凹陷而已。目前根本看不出个所以看。

En: Eight pairs of boots have shuffled back and forth in the mud.
Zh: 共有八双靴子在泥里走在走去。

En: Mhm.
As I said, I pulled last week's forecast for coastal Revachol. Seven days below freezing. The day before his hanging was the last warm day.
Zh: “正如我之前所说，我调查了瑞瓦肖沿海地区上周的天气预报。七天都处于零度以下。他被吊死的那天之前是最后一个暖和的日子。”

En: No horizontal bootprints. Whoever walked in the Whirling's pinball workshop didn't walk here...
Zh: “没有横纹靴印。无论谁穿着它走进了褴褛飞旋的弹球工坊，都没有来过这里……”

En: No unusual horizontal bootprints, like the ones I saw in the Whirling's pinball workshop...
Zh: “没有异常的横向靴印，就是我在褴褛飞旋的弹珠机工坊里见过的那种……”

En: I don't know... He inspects the odd sole more closely. "They're about the same size. Not the same *boot*, no -- but they *could* be the same person."
Zh: “这不好说……”他靠近观察着怪鞋底的足迹。“这些足迹的尺寸接近。不是同样的*靴子*——但*有可能*是同一个人。”

En: No. These prints are pretty standard -- the ones in the dust looked custom. Or maybe they're just a foreign design? It's a bootprint, whatever the case.
Zh: “不，这些鞋印都很标准——灰尘里的那些看上去像是定制款。或者它们就是些外国货？无论如何，这是靴子的痕迹。”

En: No. These prints are pretty standard.
Zh: “不。这些靴印都很标准。”

En: It still doesn't rule Ruby out. People change shoes, after all. But this isn't an argument for her guilt either.
Zh: “这不能排除露比的可能性。毕竟每个人都可能换鞋。但这也不是为她定罪的依据。”

En: So. Our Odd Sole wasn't in the back of the Whirling?
Zh: “也就是说，怪鞋底没出现在褴褛飞旋楼上？”

En: You're right. Let's keep a low profile. The lieutenant writes something down in his notebook, then reverts to the tracks in the mud.
Zh: “你说得对。我们应该保持低调。”警督在笔记本上做着记录，又转过身去盯着泥里的足迹了。

En: And the last one?
Zh: 最后那个呢？

En: I should have gotten this earlier.
Zh: 我真应该早点做到这种事的。

En: Better late than never, detective. The whole world is dark and the tracks burn in it with strange beauty.
Zh: 大器晚成总要好过废柴一生，警探。整个世界陷入黑暗，只有这片足迹以一种奇妙而美丽的方式燃烧着。

En: Note to self: this would be a good question to ask Titus -- where's the eighth man?
Zh: 切记：这个问题非常适合用来质问提图斯——哈迪兄弟的第八人在哪儿？

En: A leader like Titus doesn't let one of his guys out of sight easily -- especially at a time like this.
Zh: 提图斯这样的领袖是不会纵容手下轻易消失的——尤其是在这种时候。这里一定有什么重要的内情。

En: You mean the rat-faced one?
Zh: “你指那个长得像老鼠的家伙？”

En: Yes, well. The lieutenant thinks for a moment. "He *did* look a bit like a rat, you're right. Do you think those prints belong to him?"
Zh: “嗯，是的。”警督稍微考虑了一下。“有道理，他*确实*有点像老鼠。你觉得那些足迹是他留下的？”

En: Check out the big brains on Harry Du Bois!
Zh: 见识下哈里·杜博阿的超凡智慧吧！

En: There's no denying it. Ruby really is running a *complex operation* out of her lorry.
Zh: “这是不可否认的。露比确实在用自己的货車里开展*复杂的业务*。”

En: She is. And now we have concrete evidence binding everything together. We should ask Titus about this.
Zh: “是的。现在我们拿到了能把所有线索联系在一起确凿的证据。我们应该去问问提图斯。”

En: Lieutenant -- workers' boot tracks. (Point to them.)
Zh: “警督——这是工作靴留下的足迹。”（指向足迹。）

En: Noted. The lieutenant takes out his little notebook.
Zh: “收到。”警督掏出了他的小号笔记本。

En: He doesn't seem to hear you, looking south toward the traffic jam instead. The machines are silent, the engines are all turned off...
Zh: 他好像没在听你说话，而是望向了南方的交通堵塞。那里的机械一片死寂，引擎都关闭了……

En: Perhaps it could be a *driver?* He raises his index finger. "A driver would wear out their right shoe before the left. The accelerator is on the right."
Zh: 他竖起食指：“也许这人是个*司机*？司机右脚的鞋子会比左脚的磨损更严重。因为油门在右脚边。”

En: Probably yes. This would also fit with the victim being dead from a previous gunshot wound. They had to carry him, because he could no longer walk.
Zh: “有可能。这还与受害者死于枪伤的发现相符。他们不得不扛着死者，因为尸体无法行走。”

En: We're liking this. The pieces fit.
Zh: 我们喜欢这样。零散的拼图终于完整了。

En: He makes a note in his blue binder: "Is there anything else that's noteworthy here?"
Zh: 他在蓝色的活页上做着记录：“这里还有其他值得留意的地方吗？”

En: But we *know* the victim had a bullet in his head. A more precise way to put it is: it was *made* to look like a lynching.
Zh: “但我们*知道*受害者的头部中了一枪。更确切的说法是：这个现场是*被设计为*看似一场私刑的。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't understand what's happening right now. You're twisting at your tie, looking sweaty even by your standards. He needs to say something.
Zh: 警督无法理解眼下到底是什么情况。他只看到你在大汗淋漓地揪着自己的领带，出汗量就算是以你的标准衡量都算离谱。在这种情况下，他总得说点什么。

En: I'm pretty good at this, ain't I?
Zh: 我还挺擅长这一行的，对吧？

En: You're not bad. It's as if the whole world darkens, everything else has a thin film of unimportance on it -- and the tracks burn in the middle of it, in a strange, beautiful way.
Zh: 你做得不错。如同整个世界都陷入黑暗之中，周围的一切都蒙上了一层标示着无足轻重的薄膜——唯有位于中央的那片足迹，在以一种奇妙而美丽的方式燃烧着。

En: A morbidly obese 250 kilogram man? Let's say it is a very *low* probability. I'm thinking... He looks around. "Maybe there's a more *commonplace* explanation."
Zh: “一个病态肥胖，重达250公斤的人？只能说这种可能性很*低*。我在想……”他环顾四周。“也许还有更*普通*的解释方式。”

En: Like the Coalition official said... surreal like a *play*.
Zh: “正如那位联盟官员所说……离奇得如同一出*戏*。”

En: We've been purposefully mislead?
Zh: “难道我们被故意误导了？”

En: Exactly. They had to perform it to whoever was looking -- the whole neighbourhood, I suppose. He adds: "And us too."
Zh: “正是如此。他们不得不为目击者——也就是左邻右舍，上演一出戏。”他补充道：“当然，也是为我们而演戏。”

En: By these tracks, yes.
Zh: “是的，正是通过这些足迹。”

En: OH YEAH BABY! FUCK THIS PLACE UP!
Zh: 绝了宝贝儿！把这地方操翻天吧！

En: ...you won't get far without the lieutenant here to bounce thoughts off of -- where could he be right now? The asphalt passing underneath the front wheels of his motor carriage at a high speed...
Zh: ……如果不和警督交流意见，你独自一人无法取得多少进展——他人在何方？你想象着他驾驶着高速行驶的汽車，前轮掠过柏油路面……

En: Perhaps it could be a *driver*? A driver would wear out their right shoe before the left. The accelerator is on the right.
Zh: “也许这人是个*司机*？司机右脚的鞋子会比左脚的磨损更严重。因为油门在右脚边。”

En: Interesting. If only I had come up with that idea.
Zh: “有意思。如果我能想到这点就好了。”

En: I was actually thinking the exact same thing.
Zh: “其实我也有同样的想法。”

En: Nod thoughtfully.
Zh: 若有所思地点头。

En: The fat guy from the mess hall isn't *that* obese, he's more like a farm boy -- definitely not 250 kilograms on his own. You have to admit, he's the best fit we've got.
Zh: “餐厅里的胖子还没胖到*那种程度*，他更像是农家子弟——体重绝对到不了250公斤。你得承认，他已经是我们见过的最接近的人了。”

En: I'm guessing that's the skinny Hardie boy, the one with his front teeth missing.
Zh: “我猜这个足迹是瘦小的哈迪兄弟留下的，就是那个缺了门牙的人。”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: Probably not.
Zh: “也许没错。”

En: I could still be wrong, but... I'm probably not.
Zh: “我可能猜错了，但是……也可能没错。”

En: Hmm, maybe you're right and it's someone else... although I doubt it.
Zh: “嗯，也许你是对的，我们要找的另有其人……虽然我觉得未必如此。”

En: Go over them one by one.
Zh: 逐一清点。

En: 1) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 46. Just like Titus was wearing in his booth. This is the big dick -- Titus Hardie. The one with the ball cap on his head.
Zh: 1）标准工作靴，配钢趾帽，46码。与提图斯在包间里穿的那双靴子相符。这是他们当中最屌的家伙——提图斯·哈迪。头上戴着一顶棒球帽。

En: 2) Standard work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 44. Either the blond muscular guy, Glen, or the young guy with a plectrum around his neck.
Zh: 2）标准工作靴，配钢趾帽，44码。可能是那个金发肌肉男，格伦，也可能是那个脖子上挂着一片拨片的青年。

En: 3) Hobnailed work boot, steel reinforced toes, no 43. The inked banger perhaps?
Zh: 3）平头钉工作靴，配钢趾帽，43码。也许是那个有纹身的帮派成员？

En: 4) Standard work boot, no 45 or 46... Theo, the old smoker. You think you even see a tiny fleck of cigarette ash inside the print...
Zh: 4）标准工作靴，45或者46码……是西奥，那个老烟枪。你几乎能从足迹里看到一丝烟灰……

En: 5) Another standard work boot, reinforced toes, no 44. Same as before -- either the musician Eugene, or the muscle-bound blond Glen.
Zh: 5）另一双标准工作靴，配钢趾帽，44码。和2号一样——要么是音乐家尤金，要么是金发肌肉男格伦。

En: 6) Light as air. Same make of boot, but no 41. Small like a rat? Shanky!
Zh: 6）轻若无物的足迹。相同款式的靴子，但尺码只有41。像耗子一样小的家伙？一定是小刀！

En: 7) The glowing outline of a standard work boot, no 46. The imprints are *twice* as deep as the others -- the weight exceeds 200 kilograms. Fat-Angus -- carrying something?
Zh: 7）一双标准工作靴留下了特别明显的轮廓，46码。但鞋印的深度是其他鞋印的*两倍*——重量超过200公斤。是胖子安格斯——还扛着什么东西？

En: 8) Another standard work boot, no 44. There's an aberration in the pattern of the sole. The right sole is smoother, more worn. Curious... A missing *eighth* Hardie boy.
Zh: 8）又一双标准工作靴，44码。但鞋底留下的印迹有些异常。右脚鞋底更光滑，磨损更严重。奇了怪了……这是失踪的*第八个*哈迪兄弟。

En: I never got the hang of it. Hyperopia. He points to his glasses. "Any of them look familiar? Are these the Hardies?"
Zh: “我一直没能掌握这个的窍门。因为远视。”他指了指自己的眼镜。“这些足迹看起来眼熟吗？是哈迪兄弟留下的吗？”

En: What else?
Zh: 还有什么？

En: I'm pretty good at this, ain't I?
Zh: 我还挺擅长这一行的，对吧？

En: Count the rest.
Zh: 清点其它足迹。

En: You're not bad. It's as if the whole world darkens -- and the tracks burn in it with strange beauty.
Zh: 你做得不错。如同整个世界都陷入黑暗之中——唯有那片足迹在以一种奇妙而美丽的方式燃烧着。

En: Maybe the carrier wanted to impress his peers... demonstrate his physical might?
Zh: 也许负责搬运的人是想向他的同伴炫耀……展示自己力大无穷？

En: I do. The lieutenant marks something in his notebook. "The fat guy, I think I heard them call him Angus -- most likely to have carried the victim over."
Zh: “在我看来……”警督在他的笔记本上做着记录。“那个胖子，好像是叫安格斯——很可能是他把受害者扛过来的。”

En: Interesting.
Zh: 有意思。

En: Continue counting.
Zh: 继续清点。

En: Is it? They didn't even bother to change boots. Putting them on the scene is easy; maybe even *too* easy.
Zh: 是吗？他们甚至懒得把靴子换掉。很容易就能把他们和现场的足迹一一对应上，这也未免*过于*容易了。

En: Seven sets of tracks, right? The lieutenant has been tracking your eyes' movements. "The Hardie boys were here..."
Zh: “七对足迹，是吗？”警督一直在追踪着你的视线。“当时在这里的一定是哈迪兄弟……”

En: Eight, actually.
Zh: “实际上，有八对足迹。”

En: That so? Interesting. Then one of them seems to be missing. Anything else out of the ordinary?
Zh: “这样吗？有意思。也就是说他们中有人不见了。还有别的异常之处吗？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Check out the big brains on Harrier Du Bois!
Zh: 见识下哈里尔·杜博阿的超凡智慧吧！

En: Check out the big brains on Harry Whatever-Your-Last-Name-Is!
Zh: 见识下哈里·姓氏不详的超凡智慧吧！

En: A *driver* would wear down their right shoe before the left -- the accelerator is on the right. And remember that abandoned lorry cabin we found?
Zh: “*司机*右脚鞋子的磨损会比左脚的更严重——油门在右脚边。你还记得我们发现的那个废弃的货車驾驶舱吗？”

En: With a piece of sandpaper glued to the throttle... The lieutenant adjusts his glasses as his eyes light up with excitement.
Zh: “油门上贴着砂纸的那个……”金调整着他的眼镜，双眼因兴奋而熠熠生辉：

En: Which means that the missing lady driver was also present at the lynching? That's it, she's the odd-sole!
Zh: “这就意味着失踪的女司机还出现在了私刑现场？这就对了，留下怪鞋底足迹的就是她！”

En: She's also the one running the drug trade... Interesting.
Zh: “她同时还兼营毒品交易……有意思。”

En: This looks like proof the missing eighth person at the lynching was Ruby... Do you think that Hardie and his 'boys' could also be involved in the drug operation?
Zh: “看来这个可以证明私刑现场不见的第八个人就是露比……你觉得哈迪和他的‘兄弟们’会不会也参与了贩毒活动？”

En: Now we know who's the missing eighth person at the lynching. Do you think that Hardie and his boys could also be involved in the drug operation?
Zh: “既然我们已经查出了私刑现场的第八人身份。你觉得哈迪和他的兄弟们是否也参与了毒品交易？”

En: Not necessarily. The lady driver could have kept the drug trade a secret.
Zh: “未必。女司机可能对他们隐瞒了毒品交易的事。”

En: Women... (Shake your head.) "Always *deceitful*."
Zh: “女人啊……”（摇头。）“总是*谎话连篇*。”

En: I think the entire Union is involved. Maybe even all of Martinaise... (Look around suspiciously.)
Zh: “我认为整个工会都参与了。甚至整个马丁内斯都……”（警惕地东张西望。）

En: Those jerks? Definitely.
Zh: “那群混蛋？绝对参与了。”

En: This would fit what Joyce told us, but I don't want to make any assumptions.
Zh: “这与乔伊斯的说法一致，但我不想做任何假设。”

En: First the drug smuggling, now this... How deep does this rabbit hole go?
Zh: “先是贩毒，接着又是这种事……这浑水到底有多深？”

En: Yes, well... He quickly disregards your statement as another one of your outbursts.
Zh: “这个嘛……”他立刻就无视了你的说法，把这当成了你的又一次因冲动而口不择言。

En: We've identified one of the footprints, but there are still seven to go... He writes something in his little blue notebook.
Zh: “我们辨认出了一对足迹，但还有七对待查……”他在黑色的小笔记本上做着记录。

En: Understood. We should still go and see what Titus Hardie has got to say on the matter -- this might throw them off, work in our advantage... He writes something down into his little notebook. "So, what else?"
Zh: “明白。我们还得去找提图斯·哈迪，听听他对此有什么说法——这也许能杀他们一个措手不及，让形势变得对我们有利……”他在小笔记本上写着什么。“完毕，还有别的发现吗？”

En: You're right. But we should still go and see what Titus Hardie has got to say on the matter -- this might throw them off, work in our advantage... He writes something down into his little notebook. "So, what else?"
Zh: “你说得对。但我们还得去找提图斯·哈迪，听听他对此有什么说法——这也许能杀他们一个措手不及，让形势变得对我们有利……”他在小笔记本上写着什么。“完毕，还有别的发现吗？”

En: We should start with Titus Hardie, see what he's got to say on the matter -- this might throw them off, work in our advantage... He writes something down into his little notebook. "So, what else?"
Zh: “我们应该从提图斯·哈迪入手，听他对此有什么说法——这也许能杀他们一个措手不及，让形势变得对我们有利……”他在小笔记本上写着什么。“完毕，还有别的发现吗？”

En: We should see what Titus Hardie has got to say on the matter -- this might throw them off, work in our advantage... He writes something down into his little notebook. "So, what else?"
Zh: “我们应该听听提图斯·哈迪对此有什么说法——这也许能杀他们一个措手不及，让形势变得对我们有利……”他在小笔记本上写着什么。“完毕，还有别的发现吗？”

En: Oh, this one's easy. Way too easy. It's a driver.
Zh: 哦，这个简单。实在是太简单了。这是个司机嘛。

En: Wow. Still not doing it. You're *bad* at this.
Zh: 哇哦。还是做不到。你实在是*不擅长*这种事。

En: This seems *really* important. Tracks on the scene. And I can't read them at all, lieutenant!
Zh: “这个看起来*非常*重要。都是犯罪现场留下的足迹。但我什么都看不出来，警督！”

En: Swallow it. Just go on.
Zh: 忍气吞声，以后再说。

En: What else is there to do?
Zh: 还有什么可以做的？

En: (Whisper to yourself) "Why is this so complicated? I keep failing..."
Zh: （自言自语）“为什么这种事这么复杂？我总是失败……”

En: Don't beat yourself down -- neither can I. We'll have another look later.
Zh: “别丧气——我也做不到。我们可以稍后再来看看。”

En: Maybe you keep failing because you *suck*?
Zh: 也许你总是失败的原因是你太*烂*了？

En: So far so good -- only one thing missing. He looks at the tracks, face lighting up from the realization.
Zh: “迄今为止一切顺利——只有一处遗漏。”他望着地上的足迹，因调查的进展而面露喜色。

En: I know. Eight tracks, seven Hardie boys.
Zh: “我知道。足迹有八对，但哈迪兄弟只有七人。”

En: Let him have his moment of joy.
Zh: 让他好好享受片刻的快乐。

En: Of course, there were eight tracks! But there are only *seven* Hardie boys.
Zh: 当然了，足迹有八对！但哈迪兄弟只有*七人*。

En: There's one pair missing from the Union box. The eighth pair. I'm going to say it was our odd-sole.
Zh: “工会的包间里少了一个人。留下第八对足迹的人不在那里。我认为就是这个人留下了被我们称为怪鞋底的足迹。”

En: Mhmh. Which one is missing do you think? He quickly adds: "I'm going to say it was our odd-sole."
Zh: “嗯。你觉得不在场的人是哪个？”他马上补充道：“我认为是留下怪鞋底足迹的人。”

En: He's not going to let you steal his thunder twice.
Zh: 他可不会让你连续两次抢走他的风头。

En: So, the odd-sole was present at the lynching, but isn't in the mess hall right now.
Zh: “也就是说，怪鞋底出现在了私刑现场，但此人如今不在餐厅里。”

En: Yes. He corrects his glasses. "I doubt the Hardies are going to tell us much, but we should still confront them about the possible drug trade connection."
Zh: “好吧。”他扶正了眼镜。“我怀疑哈迪兄弟不会向我们透露多少信息，但我们还是应该就与贩毒相关的可能性与他们正面对峙。”

En: Yes. He corrects his glasses. "I doubt the Hardies are going to tell us who this person is. For now it's best if we just keep our eyes open -- I'm sure our investigation will eventually lead us to the odd-sole."
Zh: “好吧。”他扶正了眼镜。“我怀疑哈迪兄弟不会向我们透露此人的身份。就目前的情况来看，我们最好对此保持警惕——我确定，接下来的调查最终还是会把我们引向怪鞋底。”

En: Maybe the victim refused to walk to his own lynching.
Zh: “也许是因为受害者拒绝走向为自己准备的刑场。”

En: You have a point there. He clicks his pen, thinking for a moment. "Anyway, it's something to consider. What else can you see?"
Zh: “有道理。”他来回按动着自己的笔，考虑了片刻。“无论如何，这是值得考虑的。你还有其他发现吗？”

En: Yes. He corrects his glasses. "I doubt the Hardies are going to tell us much, but we should still confront them about the possible drug trade connection."
Zh: “好吧。”他扶正了眼镜。“我怀疑哈迪兄弟不会向我们透露多少信息，但我们还是应该就与贩毒相关的可能性与他们正面对峙。”

En: What, *WITHHOLD BEING CONTENT*?! What are you, mild to moderately disabled? That doesn't feel like something you should do.
Zh: 扯什么，*戒骄戒躁*？！你是怎么回事，轻微至中等残疾了吗？这可不是你该做的事。

En: Uhm... detective? He's staring at you as you clutch your necktie.
Zh: “呃……警探？”他盯着紧紧攥着领带的你。

En: The corpse looks at you with bulging white eyes. The face around them does not look human, it's swollen and ready to burst. His lips are fishlike and his tongue like a ball gag in his mouth.
Zh: 尸体那双凸起的白眼凝视着你。他肿胀到即将爆开的脸已经不成人形。他的嘴唇变得像鱼一样，而舌头则鼓了起来，好像在嘴里含着一个口球。

En: There he still is -- looking right through you with his white eyes. The body below is entirely dedicated to that corpse smell. Emitting it is *all* it does now.
Zh: 他仍在原地——一双白眼紧盯着你。散发尸臭已经成为了整具尸体存在的*全部*意义。

En: You seem to be holding your breath.
Zh: 你似乎在屏住呼吸。

En: Look down.
Zh: 向下看。

En: Turn away. [Leave.]
Zh: 转身。[离开。]

En: A cargo belt twists his neck at an unnatural angle. The body below appears stiff. It's letting out an ungodly rot, the smell seeps in even through your clenched nostrils.
Zh: 一条货物绑带将他的脖子扭到了异常的角度。尸体僵硬，散发着一股令人无法忍受的腐臭，你捏紧鼻子也无法避开这无孔不入的臭气侵袭。

En: God... what is that? Why is it so bad?
Zh: “天呐……那是怎么回事？怎么会这么臭？”

En: Turn away. [Leave.]
Zh: 转身。[离开。]

En: Let go of your nose without throwing up.
Zh: 松开鼻子，忍住不吐。

En: Has ammonia.
Zh: 有氨。

En: Shit compressed.
Zh: 粪便压缩过了。

En: Cunofied Nonvomitor.
Zh: 坤诺呕吐抑制大法。

En: You've waited long enough.
Zh: 你已经等的够久了。

En: Already smelled death recently.
Zh: 最近已经闻到过死亡的味道。

En: Active decay. The lieutenant raises a white piece of linen to his nose. "It's okay to throw up, officer. No one is judging."
Zh: “腐烂得太严重了，”警督用一块白色亚麻布盖住鼻子。“想吐就吐吧，警官。没人会说三道四的。”

En: The corpse looks at you with mute, liquefying eyes. A dark froth-like liquid has gathered on his lips. It's sickening.
Zh: 尸体那寂静无声，逐渐液化的双眼凝视着你。一团泡沫状的液体凝聚在他的嘴唇上。真是令人作呕。

En: As you breathe in, the odour comes over you. It's a spell of the mind telling you to run -- and your stomach to wring itself empty. With your hands at your sides and your eyes squinting you stand in it.
Zh: 你的每次呼吸都会被那股恶臭淹没。仿佛有一道操纵意识的魔咒在命令你逃跑——同时还在命令你的胃把自己挤干。你的双手捂住身体两侧，双眼斜视着站在原地的自己。

En: It's a puzzle -- what's hanging in front of you is a puzzle of decaying flesh, tattoos, and tendons.
Zh: 这是一张拼图——挂在你面前的是一张由腐肉、纹身与肌腱组成的拼图。

En: Do they always do that?
Zh: “尸体总会这样吗？”

En: Step closer.
Zh: 靠近一步。

En: They do after seven days, yes. We're deep in decomposition here.
Zh: “尸体在七天之后总会变成这样的。我们面对的腐烂实在是太严重了。”

En: The man before you is naked, but for a pair of underpants and enameled boots. His skin is greenish, marbled with decaying veins and blotched by lividity. A fading web of tattoos covers his chest and shoulders. The cargo belt used to fasten him to the branch above appears industrial in strength.
Zh: 你面前的男人几乎赤身裸体，只穿着内裤和一双有搪瓷涂层的靴子。他的皮肤发绿，腐烂的血管构成了大理石状的纹路，遍布尸斑。尸体的胸口与肩膀覆盖着一组褪色的网状纹身。把他吊在树枝上的绑带看起来非常结实，应该是工业用品。

En: Inspect the boots.
Zh: 检查靴子。

En: Inspect the belt.
Zh: 检查绑带。

En: Inspect the tattoos.
Zh: 检查纹身。

En: Squint and take a step back.
Zh: 眯着眼睛后退一步。

En: Look him in the eye.
Zh: 注视他的双眼。

En: So, how do we get him down?
Zh: “那么，咱们怎么才能把他放下来？”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The smell is repulsive. It pushes in from your mouth, more instant and more familiar than anything you'd expected; more fever than odour. It fills your mind, flushing you from within.
Zh: 这股气味令人作呕。它强行闯入你的口中，效果立竿见影，令你措手不及，而这种感觉居然出人意料地熟悉；与其说这是一股恶臭，倒不如说这是一场高烧。它占据了你的意识，从内部冲刷着你的思绪。

En: You try to -- very professionally -- look at the corpse, but your stomach contracts. You manage to keep it in once...
Zh: 你试图——从非常专业的角度——观察这具尸体。但你的胃抽搐不止。这一次，你强行忍住没吐……

En: Ha-ha-ha! AGAIN! The corpse laughs at you, puss dripping from its mouth. You will *never* be able to hold it in. It's *always* too much...
Zh: 哈哈哈！又来了！尸体嘲笑着你，嘴里流下了腐水。你*永远*不可能控制得住，*实在*太夸张了……

En: The corpse appears to laugh at you, black froth bubbling on it's lips. Every molecule in this body reeks of death. It's *still* too much, you feel it coming up again...
Zh: 尸体似乎是在嘲笑你，他的嘴唇上泛着黑色的泡沫。整具尸体的每个分子都散发着死亡的气息。这*依旧*恶心过头，你又一次感受到了那股呕吐的冲动……

En: He's about to blow! The cop's gonna blow, Cuno!
Zh: “他要吐了！条子要吐了。坤诺！”

En: Try to walk away.
Zh: 试图走开。

En: Let it out.
Zh: 吐出来。

En: Too late. It's impossible to keep it in. Your body curls and pushes it out. Burst by burst...
Zh: 来不及了。憋住不吐是不可能的。你蜷成一团，一口接一口地吐个没完……

En: You feel a great force wringing you from the stomach. Your body curls and pushes it out. Burst by burst...
Zh: 你感觉胃部传来一股几乎要将你绞成人干的巨大力量。你只能蜷成一团，一口接一口地吐个没完……

En: The smell of Commodore Red rises from the pool. Among it, distilled spirit and bits of shish-kebab.
Zh: 呕吐物中传来一股红色海军准将的酒味。还掺杂着蒸馏酒和一点烤肉串的味道。

En: The corpse looks at no particular direction with its bulging eyes. Its mouth is silent.
Zh: 尸体凸起的双眼没有明确的焦点。他的口中吐出一片死寂。

En: God...
I'm sorry.
Zh: “我很抱歉。”

En: Fucking corpse.
Zh: “该死的尸体。”

En: Keep it. The lieutenant hands you a blue checkered handkerchief.
Zh: 警督递给你一条蓝格纹手帕。“拿着吧，”他说。

En: Thanks. (Wipe your mouth.)
Zh: “谢啦。”（擦嘴。）

En: I don't need your pity.
Zh: “我不需要你的同情。”

En: It's not *pity*. You should wipe your mouth after vomiting. He pockets the handkerchief.
Zh: “这不是*同情*。你吐完了总该擦擦嘴的。”他把手帕装回了口袋里。

En: It's okay, happens to everyone. The lieutenant hands you his white handkerchief: "Keep it."
Zh: “这是正常反应，在所难免。”警督将他的白手帕递给了你：“拿着吧。”

En: You think ammonia would help?
Zh: “你觉得氨水会有用吗？”

En: If you can handle the headache, he nods. "Some officers use it to deal with cadaverine odour."
Zh: “只要你能忍受头疼就行，”他点了点头。“有些警官会用氨水来抵御尸氨的恶臭。”

En: I don't need that shit.
Zh: “我不需要那玩意儿。”

En: Sure you do. You just threw up *a lot*.
Zh: “你当然需要，你刚刚吐了*好久*。”

En: That young woman, the gardener, mentioned she used salts for the smell. He nods toward the plaza.
Zh: “那位年轻的女园丁提到过，她会用盐来对抗这股尸臭。”他朝广场的方向点了点头。

En: There's Frittte nearby, east of the hostel. They usually have a small apothecary. If they don't... He points to the greenhouse.
Zh: “附近有家弗莱特，就在旅馆东侧。店里通常会有个小药柜。如果没有的话……”他指了指温室。

En: There's a greenhouse here -- and a gardener with a wheelbarrow on the corner of the Whirling-in-Rags. If she works here she might have something for the smell.
Zh: “这里有座温室——褴褛飞旋门口的角落还有位推着独轮車的园丁。如果她在这里工作的话，也许会有什么抵御尸臭的方法。”

En: God... this sucks.
Zh: “天呐……真是糟透了。”

En: What now?
Zh: “接下来呢？”

En: Fucking corpse.
Zh: “该死的尸体。”

En: If the lieutenant were here he would have an idea what to do. But he isn't. He's somewhere filling paperwork. Looks like you'll just have to work it out on your own.
Zh: 如果警督在场的话，他可能会知道该怎么做。但他不在。此时此刻，他正在别处整理工作报告。看来你只能孤军奋战了。

En: The second time -- not so much. When the vomiting is done your cheeks are wet with tears.
Zh: 再吐一次——已经吐不出那么多东西了。吐完之后，你已经以泪洗面了。

En: I think I don't wanna be a cop anymore...
Zh: “我再也不想做条子了……”

En: The ammonia didn't help at all...
Zh: “氨水根本没用……”

En: Spit and wipe your mouth with the handkerchief.
Zh: 啐一口，用手帕把嘴擦干。

En: Spit and say nothing.
Zh: 啐一口，沉默不语。

En: Get a hold of yourself. You feel the lieutenant pat your back, rhythmically.
Zh: “控制住情绪。”你感到警督正在有节奏地拍着你的后背。

En: The weight is reassuring. Like a crenel on solid fortification. Pat pat pat...
Zh: 这种力度非常令人安心。如同坚固防御工事上的一处垛口。拍拍拍……

En: Nor does the wind right now... You feel the lieutenant pat your back, rhythmically.
Zh: “这阵风也够要命的……”你感到警督正在有节奏地拍着你的后背。

En: I've seen strong men turn themselves inside out for hours. You're facing tough odds here. Alcohol withdrawal makes it considerably harder.
Zh: “我见识过身强体壮的男人昏天黑地地连着吐了几个小时的惨状。眼下的考验相当艰难。而戒酒导致难度更高了。”

En: Are you okay, officer? You feel the lieutenant pat you on the back. Heavy, rhythmic pats.
Zh: “你没事吧，警官？”你感觉警督正在用力，有节奏地拍着你的后背。

En: The weight is reassuring. Like a crenel on solid fortification. Pat pat pat...
Zh: 这种力度非常令人安心。如同坚固防御工事上的一处垛口。拍拍拍……

En: You're facing tough odds here. It's aggravated further by alcohol withdrawal.
Zh: “眼下的考验相当艰难。而戒酒导致难度加剧了。”

En: I've seen *captains* puke their guts out. It never gets easier, you never get used to the smell. Every Monday is cadaver day -- throw up, investigate, throw up, initial autopsy, throw up, bag it... He pats your back again.
Zh: “我见识过*不少队长*吐得一塌糊涂的惨状。这种事总是这么艰难，没人能习惯这种臭味。每个周一都要与死尸相伴——呕吐，调查，呕吐，初步尸检，呕吐，收尸……”他又拍了拍你的后背。

En: I failed *AGAIN*!
Zh: “我*又*失败了！”

En:
Why can't I keep it in if I've been a cop my whole life?	“我都干了一辈子警察了，怎么会忍不住呢？”
0x010000040000E1A3	Alternate1	YARD / HANGED MAN	You	Seriously, this isn't fun. I don't wanna be a policeman anymore."
Zh: “说真的，这一点都不好玩。我不想再做警察了。”

En: This is *bullshit*. I don't wanna do this anymore. I don't wanna be a cop.
Zh: “真他妈*扯淡*。老子不想干了。老子不想做条子了。”

En: Can't we do something else? I think I want to solve something else now.
Zh: “咱们不能去干别的吗？我想去破别的案子了。”

En: You seem to be fine.
Zh: “你看上去还好。”

En: Okay.
Zh: “好的。”

En: Then drive to the station, maybe throw up on the way there if you didn't bag the thing tight enough.
Zh: “那就开車回警局吧，如果你没把尸袋密封好，可能半路上还是要吐的。”

En: I think I've lost my sense of smell. There's a pause.
Zh: “我觉得我已经失去了嗅觉。”他停顿了一下。

En: A white lie.
Zh: 一个善意的谎言。

En: Not being hungover helps too.
Zh: “我没有宿醉，这也有助于与尸臭对抗。”

En: Monday -- make sure you bring a handkerchief.
Zh: “周一——记得随身携带手帕。”

En: Do it without me. I just can't keep it down.
Zh: “别管我了，你自己上吧。我怎么都忍不住。”

En: Okay. You've said it. You needed to say it, and now that you have... He withdraws his hand from your back and looks you in the eye:
Zh: “好吧。你都这么说了。只要说出来就好了，既然如此……”他缩回放在你背上的手，看向你的眼睛：

En: You need to get your shit together.
Zh: “你必须得振作起来。”

En: That's probably a good idea. Clear our heads. But before we can do that... He withdraws his hand from your back and looks you in the eye:
Zh: “这可能是个好主意。清醒一下头脑，不过在那之前……”他缩回放在你背上的手，看向你的眼睛：

En: No. This is a two-man assignment, because it needs two officers to complete. I need your help. He withdraws his hand and looks you in the eye:
Zh: “不行。这是一项双人任务，需要两名警官才能完成。我需要你的帮助。”他缩回手，看向你的眼睛：

En: You are being weak. This is weakness -- and it cannot go on much longer, or you will LOSE.
Zh: 你太软弱了，这就是弱点——不能再这样下去了，否则你会输的。

En: Give me a moment. I got this. Let's try *again*.
Zh: “再给我一点时间，我可以的。我们*再*试一次吧。”

En: Say nothing -- just nod.
Zh: 沉默不语——仅是点头。

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: An appropriate attitude. He withdraws his hand and looks you in the eye:
Zh: “态度很好。”他缩回手，看向你的眼睛：

En: But I don't wanna get my shit together!
Zh: “可是我不想振作起来！”

En: But my shit already *is* together!
Zh: “我*已经*很振作了！”

En: No, it's not officer.
Zh: “不，并没有，警官。”

En: Then the world will turn away from you, and leave you behind.
Zh: “然后整个世界都会背你而去，把你抛在身后。”

En: (Shake your head.) "No."
Zh: （摇摇头。）“不行。”

En: No? The lieutenant's expression is stern and impenetrable. He points at you: "You *will* get your shit together."
Zh: “不行？”警督的表情严厉又令人费解，他指着你：“你*会*振作起来的。”

En: You're sure this has not affected his impression of you in a good way.
Zh: 你很肯定这并不会让他对你的印象所有改善。

En: We should go talk to the locals, find something else to do while the wind changes. It's pretty bad right now.
Zh: “我们应该去跟当地人谈谈，趁风向改变之时找点别的事做。现在真的很糟糕。”

En: Give it half an hour, get yourself together, then come back and have another go.
Zh: “给你半个小时，振作起来，然后回来再试一次。”

En: WHAT IS HAPPENING TO YOU?!?! Are you going to CRY now, son?
Zh: 你怎么了？！？！是要哭出来了吗，孩子？

En: You heard the coach! This is weakness. It cannot go on much longer, or you will LOSE.
Zh: 听到教练的话了吗？这就是弱点，不能再这样下去了，否则你会输的。

En: For a moment, the hanged man appears to smile. His lips are red like a clown's.
Zh: 有那么一瞬间，被吊起来的男人似乎微笑了。他的嘴唇就像小丑一样红。

En: Every time it happens it gets worse and worse. There's nothing more to throw up now. All that's left is crying and convulsing dryly at the same time.
Zh: 每一次呕吐，情况就变得越来越糟。现在你已经没有任何东西可吐，唯有哭泣和麻木地抽搐。

En: It's especially painful this time. You've thrown up so much there's nary anything left down there. Hot tears of pain streak down your face -- you're crying too. Crying and convulsing at the same time.
Zh: 这次尤其痛苦。你吐得如此剧烈，胃里什么都没剩下。痛苦的热泪顺着你的脸颊滚落——你哭了出来。一边哭泣，一边抽搐着。

En: Just cry.
Zh: 放声哭泣。

En: The lieutenant is clearly at a loss.
Zh: 警督显然不知所措了。

En: Little black bubbles pop on the corpse's lips, decay liquid like tar. It's like he's gurgling.
Zh: 尸体的嘴唇上冒出了黑色的小小气泡，腐尸的体液如同焦油，他好像在咯咯笑一样。

En: What do I do now?
Zh: “接下来我该怎么做？”

En: Officer, you just need to be stronger. Learn to keep it in long enough for us to work. There's nothing else *to* do.
Zh: “警官，你只需要变得再坚强些。坚持到我们能够完成工作，没有别的*选择*了。”

En: Your muscles tense up. The vision in your dead angle darkens. You feel the sudden urge to push your hand into his soft gut.
Zh: 你的肌肉紧绷，视线死角的景象变得昏暗起来。你感觉到了一股突如其来的，想要把手伸进他柔软的肚子中去的冲动。

En: The spell has no effect. You stand eye to eye with it, the odour washing through you.
Zh: 这个咒语并不灵验，你站着与他四目相对，那股气味笼罩了你。

En: This delicate machine, ready for picking... Instinctively you crack your fingers.
Zh: 这台脆弱的机器，随时都可以被人拿走……你本能地扳了扳手指。

En: The material appears to be ceramic. Its clean white stands in stark contrast to the decaying flesh above the knee. The man wore thick polymer socks, probably for padding. A fine array of interlocking plates covers them.
Zh: 似乎是陶瓷制成的。它无暇的纯白与膝盖上方腐烂的肉块形成了鲜明的对比。这个男人穿着厚厚的化纤袜子，可能是为了填满他的腿与靴子内壁之间的空隙。一排精致的连锁板包裹着它们。

En: Knock on the boot.
Zh: 敲一下靴子。

En: Pull the boot off.
Zh: 将靴子脱下。

En: A small bell-like sound fills the air. Like tapping on the side of a porcelain cup.
Zh: 一声铃铛似的声音回荡在空气里。就像敲击瓷杯杯壁的声音。

En: Delicate and fragile, they feel alien to the world around you. Out of place somehow.
Zh: 精致而脆弱，与你周遭的世界是那样格格不入，有些突兀。

En: The material looks out of place here.
Zh: “这种材质看上去有些突兀。”

En: It is. It's expensive. The lieutenant draws a line in the condensation on the ceramic -- with his index finger.
Zh: “确实，它很昂贵。”警督用食指在陶瓷上的冷凝物上——划了一道线。

En: We've requested similar material for our tactical units for years now. The Constabulary's deemed it too costly. In that time, we've lost six men to semi-automatics.
Zh: “多年来，我们一直要求为我们的机动部队配发类似材质的护甲，但警队认为它们太贵了。在那段时间里，我们有六名战友倒在了半自动武器的枪口之下。”

En: How much are we talking about?
Zh: “大概要多少钱呢？”

En: For a full set -- about four years of wages.
Zh: “一整套的话——大约要四年的薪水。”

En: For the Northwest Region of Revachol, an officer's average yearly income is 5,500 reál (unadjusted for rank).
Zh: 在瑞瓦肖的西北部，一名警官的年均收入是5500雷亚尔。（未根据警阶调整的状况下）。

En: Wait -- my yearly pay is 5,500 reál?
Zh: “等等——我的年薪是5500雷亚尔？”

En: Ka-ching, baby! (Nod to the boots.)
Zh: “发财啦，宝贝儿！”（对着靴子点点头。）

En: Not too much, yes.
Zh: “是的，确实不多。”

En: By *ka-ching* do you mean... He tilts his head. "'Let's not log them as evidence, let's steal them'?"
Zh: “你说的*发财了*是指……”他歪着头。“不要把它当做证据存档，而是偷偷自己留着？”

En: The only difference is a barely audible *click* underneath. Sounds like the end of a long dice roll.
Zh: 唯一的区别就是底下传来几乎听不见的*咔哒*声。像是骰子滚动很长时间之后停下来的声音。

En: These aren't just boots, are they? This is the armour he was stripped of.
Zh: “这些不仅仅是靴子，对吗？这是他被剥去的盔甲。”

En: These aren't just boots, are they -- they're armour.
Zh: “这些不仅仅是靴子，对吗——它们是盔甲。”

En: No. That's not what I meant.
Zh: “不，我不是那个意思。”

En: If this *Constabulatory* is dragging their feet why not start ourselves, before more good men die?
Zh: “如果这个*警队*一直在拖后腿，为什么不自己行动起来呢，在更多的战友牺牲之前？”

En: What was that about the Constabulatory before? You need to push some buttons if you want to recruit the lieutenant in a conspiracy.
Zh: 之前那个警队是怎么回事？如果你想说服警督相信这个阴谋论，还需要更强的攻势。

En: It's enough.
Zh: “这么多足够了。”

En: Pathetic. I need to hustle more and hate less.
Zh: “太可悲了。我需要多些努力，少点仇恨。”

En: Sounds puny. No wonder I can't pay my hostel bill.
Zh: “听起来太微薄了，难怪我连旅社的钱都付不起。”

En: I have no idea what that means.
Zh: “我不知道那是什么意思。”

En: The lieutenant nods, wiping his finger on his sleeve.
Zh: 警督点点头，用袖子擦着手指。

En: It's a sorry situation, I agree.
Zh: “确实很遗憾，我同意。”

En: Swing the prybar at the boot.
Zh: 用撬棍瞄准靴子打去。

En: Sounds fragile.
Zh: “听起来很脆弱。”

En: Punch it harder.
Zh: 用力重击。

En: Suddenly your biceps coil up. Your elbow is sharp and cocked for a punch.
Zh: 突然你的肱二头肌隆起来了，手肘为了这一拳而高高扬起。

En: The prybar in your hand is itching for some action.
Zh: 你手中的撬棍已经蠢蠢欲动了。

En: The metal connects with the same *ding*. The sound does not appear to get louder...
Zh: 金属之间的撞击迸发出了相同的*叮*的声音，声音似乎并没有变大……

En: The lieutenant cups his ear: "Did you hear that? A click?"
Zh: 警督拢起了他的耳朵：“你听到了吗？咔哒的声音？”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是指？”

En: Yes, like dice rolling.
Zh: “是的，像是掷骰子的声音。”

En: This material is a kinetic re-distributor. It spreads kinetic energy horizontally. From plate to plate. When the plates connect there's a click. He points to the toe. "See these lines?"
Zh: “这是一种动力再分配装置，它能水平地传播动能，在一个又一个的陶瓷板上游走。当两个板块连接起来的时候，就会有咔哒一声。”他指向了脚趾部位。“看到这些线条了吗？”

En: This is a kinetic re-distributor. It spreads the incoming energy horizontally, from plate to plate. When the plates connect, there's a click. That's the sound you heard. He points to the toe. "See these lines?"
Zh: “这是一种动力再分配装置，它能水平地传播吸收到的能量，在一个又一个的陶瓷板上游走。当两个板块连接起来的时候，就会有咔哒一声。那就是你听到的声音。”他指向了脚趾部位。“看到这些线条了吗？”

En: These are clearly not boots, they're *armour*. Possibly part of a larger set.
Zh: 这显然不是靴子，它们是*盔甲*。很可能是一个套装中的一部分。

En: Ceramic plate. Zirconium dioxide most likely. This is where the make would be...
Zh: “陶瓷板，极有可能是二氧化锆。生产序列号或许就在……”

En: Indeed... With his notebook under his arm the lieutenant crouches to inspect the soles: "Technically speaking these are *sabatons*, not boots."
Zh: “确实……”警督将笔记本夹到腋下，蹲下身去检查鞋底：“严格意义上说，这是*铠靴*，而非普通的靴子。”

En: Oh! The lieutenant uses a Mnemotechnique A6 -- that's not just any notebook, it's a classic!
Zh: 哦！警督使用的是记忆法A6——那可不是一般的笔记本，而是一个经典！

En: Thanks, super useful to know.
Zh: 谢谢，真是帮了大忙了呢。

En: Forget about the notebook -- tell me about the armour!
Zh: 别管什么笔记本了——跟我说说盔甲的事。

En: It's clearly some manner of super-armour. Or future armour? Super-future armour? I'm useless.
Zh: 这显然是某种超级盔甲，或是未来盔甲？超级未来盔甲？我尽力了。

En: It's all you, baby.
Zh: 这都是为了你，宝贝。

En: What kind of armour is this exactly?
Zh: “这究竟是什么盔甲？”

En: Where?
Zh: “哪里？”

En: Under the heel. Fairweather... He turns the boot slightly. "Fairweather model T-500 / VE. I'm guessing that's 'vitreous enamel'. This is advanced stuff."
Zh: “脚后跟下面。费尔韦瑟……”他轻轻地转动着靴子，”费尔韦瑟T-500/VE款。我猜这一款是‘珐琅质’的，这可是高级货。”

En: The sabatons dangle off the man's decaying form, ageless and synthetic.
Zh: 铠靴悬挂在那个男人腐败的身体上，成为一种人为的永恒。

En: The locals probably scavenged it -- it would be odd if he had more on after *seven* days.
Zh: “很可能是当地人取走了它们……要是过了*七天*他的身上还能留着更多东西的话，那才奇怪呢。”

En: He nods: "Piece by piece. He's been out here for seven days -- it would be odd if they didn't."
Zh: 他点头：“应该是一块块拿走的。他已经在这里挂了七天了——如果他们不拿才是怪事呢。”

En: No. He must have worn something precious underneath his clothes. They've removed all his clothes to get to it -- they did not just strip him for the putrid rags.
Zh: “不。他一定在衣服里穿了什么贵重的东西。他们扒光了他的衣服去找——并不是为了他身上的破布。”

En: What if they told him to strip before they hung him? To demean him?
Zh: “如果他们命令他在临死前脱光了呢？为了羞辱他？”

En: *They* usually hang them completely naked for that. La Puta Madre, the Mazda, the Besmerties, and the like. This one still has his underpants.
Zh: “为了那样的话*他们*通常会选择把人全裸吊起来，就像马德雷教父、马兹达和贝斯莫蒂之流的人。但是这位还穿着内裤呢。”

En: Then where are these clothes -- have you seen any around?
Zh: “那他的衣服去哪儿了——你看见了吗？”

En: Someone could have cleaned the yard. But that's a question for... the red-haired thing.
Zh: “或许有人打扫了院子，但这就要问问……那个红毛小鬼了。”

En: I hear you, f****t cop. Talking shit 'bout the Cuno. Come here and say it to Cuno's face!
Zh: “我可听到了，该死的条子，又在对坤诺叽叽歪歪。有种过来当着坤诺的面说啊！”

En: Cuno this boot-shit is super boring and the guys are total *vittupää's*.
Zh: “坤诺，靴子的事可真是太无聊了，根本就是一群*混蛋*。”

En: What are you tryin'a ignore me now, fuck face?
Zh: “你现在是准备无视我吗，傻逼？”

En: Fuck you, Cuno!
Zh: “该死的，坤诺！”

En: You wanna fuck me, huh? You wanna FUCK the Cuno?
Zh: “你想挑衅我，嗯？你想要挑衅坤诺吗？”

En: Where's the rest of it? Scavenged by the locals?
Zh: “剩下的盔甲呢？都被当地人捡走了吗？”

En: What happened to the rest of it?
Zh: “剩下的部分去哪儿了？”

En: Maybe he was just wearing these boots and there is no rest of the armour?
Zh: “也许他只穿了这双靴子，根本没穿其余的盔甲呢？”

En: Of course. He nods.
Zh: “当然。”他点点头。

En: The lieutenant nods often. It's part of his un-plastic expression range, communicating both professionalism and sarcasm. This time, the latter.
Zh: 警督常常点头。这是他独特情感表达的一部分，既传达了专业精神，也暗含了讽刺意味。这一次，属于后者。

En: So they don't get shot in the foot? I think our *extremities* are fine without a dead man's boots.
Zh: “这样他们的脚部就不会中枪了？我想我们的*四肢*即使没有死者的靴子也很好。”

En: For a full set of armour I wouldn't blame you. But these boots are hardly worth a disciplinary hearing. There are better ways to pay a hostel bill. Besides... He taps the boot.
Zh: “如果是全套的盔甲我倒不会责怪你，但这双靴子甚至不值得一场纪律听证会，还有更好的办法来付旅社费用的。另外……”他轻敲着靴子。

En: Honestly -- a whole set of ceramics: the breastplate, the pauldrons... I wouldn't blame you. But these two? Not worth bringing Internal Affairs into your life over. Besides... He taps the boot.
Zh: “说实话——一整套陶甲：胸牌，肩甲……我倒不会责怪你。但这两个？都不值得让内部事务的人过来调查。另外……”他轻敲着靴子。

En: Uhm...
Yeah.
Zh: “是啊。”

En: Oops. Well, no one said it would work. Back to detecting now.
Zh: 哎呀，好吧，没人保证过它能起什么作用。现在继续侦查吧。

En: That's a lot, I take it?
Zh: “我猜一定很贵？”

En: As a wage, it's regrettably small. But for a piece of hardware -- yes, that's a lot.
Zh: “作为薪水的话，它确实少得可怜。但作为一件装备来说——没错，确实很贵。”

En: (Point to it.) "Looks like there's a serial number on the right sabaton."
Zh: （指向它。）“右脚铠靴上好像有一串序列号。”
