En: Good. Can you read it to me? He tips the drying ball point of his pen on his tongue.
Zh: “很好，你能念给我听吗？”他把干燥的笔尖放在舌头上舔了舔。

En: We have a make and a number -- that's something. We can use the radio in my Kineema to run it later. Either station can chase it for us.
Zh: 警督点点头。“我们有生产商和序列号了——也算小有进展。之后我们可以用我的锐影里的无线电查一下，任何一个警察局都能找到它。”

En: True. It didn't have the cadence and logic of a real manufactured item.
Zh: 正确。它不具备一件真正的产品的韵律和逻辑。

En: Officer, the decomposing corpse has temporarily suspended my sense of humour. Let's continue in a less playful manner. He pauses to think. "Anyway..."
Zh: “警官，腐烂的尸体使我暂时失去了幽默感，我们就以一种不那么有趣的方式继续吧。”他停下思索了一会儿。“无论如何……”

En: We have a make and a number -- that's something. We can use the radio in my Kineema when we're done. Either station can chase it for us.
Zh: “我们有生产商和序列号了——也算小有进展。结束后我们可以用我的锐影上的无线电查一下，任何一个警察局都能找到它。”

En: That doesn't sound like a serial number. Where's the structure and logic of a line of manufactured products... He pauses to think. "No matter. We should run it anyway."
Zh: “那听上去可不像是序列号。生产线的结构和逻辑在哪里……”他停下思索了一会儿。“不管了，无论如何我们都应该查一查。”

En: Understood.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Your fist connects to the boot with the same *ding*. The sound does not appear to get louder.
Zh: 伴随着相同的*叮*的一声，你的拳头落在了靴子上，声音似乎没有变大。

En: It's anything but. This material is a kinetic re-distributor. It spreads kinetic energy horizontally, from plate to plate. Dissipating it entirely. He points to the boots. "See?"
Zh: “不是这样的。这是一种动力再分配装置，它能水平地传播吸收到的能量，在一个又一个的陶瓷板上游走，直至完全消散。”他指着靴子。“看到了？”

En: Faint, organic lines cover the plates where they separate into smaller ones. These plates then divide into smaller plates, until there are hundreds of them altogether...
Zh: 细微的机体线条覆盖着这些陶瓷板，并把它们分隔成更小的板块。然后这些小板块又被分成更小的板块，直至数以百计的陶板汇聚在一起……

En: How could this man afford such expensive hardware?
Zh: “这个人怎么能负担得起如此昂贵的装备？”

En: That's for us to find out. He checks his notes. "Mr... Garte implied he was security personnel for the harbour company. This confirms my own assumptions."
Zh: “这就要靠我们去发现了。”他检查着自己的笔记。“加尔特……先生曾暗示他是港口公司的安保人员，这也证实了我自己的推测。”

En: That's for us to find out. My initial report on the area suggests he was a security guard for the harbour company -- but that's just hearsay.
Zh: “这就要靠我们去发现了。我在现场的初步调查显示，他是港口公司的一名安保人员——也不过是道听途说罢了。”

En: These look pretty advanced for a security guard.
Zh: “这对于安保人员来说，有点过于先进了吧。”

En: Okay. Makes sense.
Zh: “好吧，有道理。”

En: No. He shakes his head. "Your first instinct was correct. This equipment is way beyond what a security guard can afford."
Zh: “不，“他摇摇头。”你的第一反应是对的。这套装备远远超出安保人员所能负担的范围了。”

En: I agree. This equipment is *way* beyond what a guard can afford.
Zh: “我同意。这套装备*远远超出*一名安保人员所能负担的范围了。”

En: What kind of boots are these?
Zh: “这是什么样的靴子？”

En: Back off.
Zh: 后退。

En: They're armour, not boots. With his notebook under his arm the lieutenant crouches to inspect the soles: "Technically speaking these are *sabatons*."
Zh: “它们是盔甲，不是靴子。”警督将笔记本夹到腋下，蹲下身去检查鞋底：“严格意义上说，这是*铠靴*。”

En: Okay then -- what kind of *armour* is this?
Zh: “好吧——这是什么样的*盔甲*？”

En: The cadaver slowly twists on the cargo belt, his torso covered in tattoos and extremities blotched pink and blue.
Zh: 运货带上的尸体缓缓地晃动着，他的躯干上刺青密布，四肢则满是粉蓝色的斑点。

En: It's hard to get a read on the lieutenant. What would he do if you stole them? Would he report you, or not? Surprisingly, you feel like he probably wouldn't.
Zh: 警督的表情很难读懂，如果你偷走了靴子他会怎么做？他会举报你吗，或是不会？奇怪的是，你有一种他不会举报的感觉。

En: (Back off and look at the corpse.)
Zh: （退后并观察尸体。）

En: The stench fills your nostrils. As you push downward, an ominous creaking sound comes from above.
Zh: 恶臭涌向你的鼻孔。当你向下推的时候，一种不祥的吱吱声从上面传来。

En: Stop! The lieutenant's voice is sharp. He looks at you, with the boot under your arm.
Zh: “住手！”警督的声音很刺耳。他看着你，而你的腋下还夹着靴子。

En: What?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: Lieutenant, you seem distressed -- why?
Zh: “警督，你看上去很忧虑——怎么了？”

En: Oh! This is a bad idea, isn't it?
Zh: “哦！这真是个坏主意，不是吗？”

En: You're going to pull his head off.
Zh: “你要把他的头扯下来了。”

En: Do what?
Zh: “做什么？”

En: I'm gonna pull his head off, right?
Zh: “我会把他的头扯下来的，是不是？”

En: Pig's gonna pull his head off.
Zh: “猪猡要把他的脑袋扯下来了。”

En: Brutal.
Zh: “野蛮。”

En: Yes, that's what I said. You'll compromise the coroner's case if you do. So please *don't*.
Zh: “没错，正如我所说。如果你这么做了，会给验尸官添乱的。请千万*不要*这么做。”

En: You're about to pull his head off.
Zh: “你快要把他的头扯下来了。”

En: Even the mongrels can see you're about to pull his head off.
Zh: “就连小杂种们都能看出你快要把他的头扯下来了。”

En: Do it, homo!
Zh: “快点儿，基佬！”

En: Indeed. From this angle it does look like the neck isn't going to take much more. Being dead for a week has all but liquefied his muscles.
Zh: 没错。从这个角度来看，颈部恐怕支撑不了多久了。死了一个星期之久，足以使他的肌肉液化了。

En: I'm not a homo.
Zh: “我不是基佬。”

En: Stop obsessing about your sexuality officer, you're about to seriously compromise the coroner's case.
Zh: “别再纠结你的性取向了警官，你就要给验尸官添大麻烦了。”

En: *Pull his head off*. There's no point performing an autopsy if you do, we'll have compromised the coroner's case.
Zh: “*把他的头扯下来*。如果你这么做了，也就没有验尸的必要了，我们会给验尸官添乱的。”

En: What are you trying to achieve anyway, why are you hanging on to that boot?
Zh: “你的目的又是什么呢，为什么总是对那双靴子恋恋不舍的？”

En: Let go of the boot.
Zh: 放开靴子。

En: What's wrong with you, asshole? Why's he letting go, Cuno?
Zh: “你有什么毛病，混蛋？他为什么放手了，坤诺？”

En: I don't know, baby, Cuno shrugs. "I don't know why he's such a f*g."
Zh: “我不知道宝贝，”坤诺耸耸肩，“我不知道他为什么这么娘炮。”

En: Officer, if I may ask -- what were you trying to achieve by pulling on the deceased foot?
Zh: “警官，冒昧请问——你去扯那个死人的脚的目的是什么呢？”

En: These boots would go super well with my bell-bottom pants.
Zh: “这双靴子跟我的喇叭裤是绝配。”

En: This is advanced enemy technology. We should conduct research into their weaknesses.
Zh: “这是敌人的先进技术，我们应该针对他们的弱点进行研究。”

En: Are we not detectives? There may be *clues* inside the boot.
Zh: “我们不是警探吗？靴子里很可能有*线索*。”

En: You said they're expensive. I thought I could pawn them, earn some *lucre*.
Zh: “你说过它们很昂贵的，我想着可以把它们当掉，赚点*外快*。”

En: They look fancy. I thought I could pawn them, earn some *lucre*.
Zh: “它们看起来可高档了。我想着可以把它们当掉，赚点*外快*。”

En: It's that Constabulatory thing I said before. With the dead guys. I wanna *protect* them.
Zh: “就是我之前提过的警队的事儿，那些牺牲的同伴，我想*保护*他们。”

En: I'm sorry. I didn't even want to take them off, I just thought I should try.
Zh: “抱歉，我根本不想把它们脱下来，就是觉得自己应该试试。”

En: This is not the *enemy*, this is the deceased. The victim. In a murder investigation.
Zh: “这并不是*敌人*，这是死者，是被害人，在一场凶杀案调查中。”

En: Is he now? A sense of danger emanates from the remains of the person in front of you.
Zh: 是吗？一种危机感从你面前的遗体身上散发出来。

En: ...like whorls of floorboards. The design looks organic, influenced by highly resistant wood materials, like Lignum Vitae and ebony perhaps.
Zh: ……如同回旋的地板。设计看起来是有机的，受高度耐腐蚀的木材的影响，比如癒创树和黑檀木之类的。

En: For a quarter of a second he appears to be looking at you. The stench of death makes your eyes well up.
Zh: 有那么一瞬间，他似乎在看着你。死亡的恶臭使你的眼睛溢满了泪水。

En: I feel as though we may cross paths with him yet. One way or another.
Zh: “感觉我们似乎还会与他相遇，无论以何种方式。”

En: You're right, that makes sense.
Zh: “你说得对，很有道理。”

En: Better safe than sorry.
Zh: “还是小心为上。”

En: There's no way you're getting them off. All the organic matter in his body has been flowing down into the boots. For *seven* days. They're *fused* to his feet now -- why do you think they're the only thing left on him?
Zh: “你不可能脱得下来的，他体内所有的有机物都流入了靴子里。整整*七天*，它们都*粘*在他的脚上了——不然你以为怎么就只剩下这个了？”

En: You do understand the boots are steeped in cadaverine odour, don't you?
Zh: “你知道靴子已经染上尸臭了，对吧？”

En: So are my pants.
Zh: “我的裤子也是啊。”

En: Can't I just wash them?
Zh: “我可以把它们洗干净呀？”

En: You're right. I didn't think this through. It was an impulse thing.
Zh: “你说得对。是我没想明白，这只是一时冲动。”

En: There's no way you're getting them off. All the organic matter in his body has been flowing down into the boots. They're *fused* to his feet now -- why do you think the locals haven't scavenged them yet?
Zh: “你不可能脱得下来的，他体内所有的有机物都流入了靴子里。它们都*粘*在他的脚上了——不然你觉得为什么当地人还没把它们捡走？”

En: You'd have to boil them in acid. Besides... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “你得把它们放在酸里煮过。另外……”警督轻敲着靴子。

En: You have a point there, detective. You have a point. But... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “你说得很有道理，警探。有些道理，但……”警探轻敲着靴子。

En: That doesn't mean anything, detective. Besides... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “那并不能说明什么，警探。另外……”警探轻敲着靴子。

En: It *does* make sense, doesn't it? Besides... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “*确实*很有道理，不是吗？另外……”警探轻敲着靴子。

En: One way or *another?* I can't even think of the *one* way... *magic*? *Semenese corpse salt*? Besides... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “无论*以何种方式*？我连*一种*方式都想不出来……*魔法？*，*塞美尼尸盐*？再说了……”警探轻敲着靴子。

En: You're *sure* there's a way to peel them off -- but first the body needs to be down. And second, it would probably be better if the lieutenant wasn't around...
Zh: 你*一定*会有办法把它们脱下来的——不过首先要把尸体放下来。其次，如果警督能不在左右就更好了……

En: How many clues do you need? You already found the number. Besides... He taps on the boot.
Zh: “你还要什么线索，不是已经找到序列号了？另外……”他轻敲着靴子。

En: Maybe we should concentrate on what's outside the boot and leave what's inside to the boys at Processing? Just this once? Besides... He taps on the boot.
Zh: “或许我们应该多多关注靴子外部的情况，就把内部的事情交给分析部门的孩子们处理吧？就这一次？另外……”他轻敲着靴子。

En: Okay. Fine. You want to protect the *feet* of six dead men. That's commendable. But... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “好的，行吧。你想保护这个九泉之下的人的脚，确实值得称赞。但是……”警督轻敲着靴子。

En: I don't think we should *do* anything just for the hell of it. Besides... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “我觉得我们不应该为这该死的东西做任何事情。另外……”警督轻敲着靴子。

En: This feels... dangerous. Are you sure?
Zh: 这感觉……很危险。你确定吗？

En: There might still be a way to peel them off, but first the body needs to be down. And second -- it would probably be better if the lieutenant wasn't around...
Zh: 也许还有别的方法把它们脱下来，但首先要把尸体放下来。其次——如果警督不在就更好了……

En: Sounds like a plan. (Accept.)
Zh: 听上去值得一试。（接受。）

En: Steal a dead man's boots? But that would be *DISHONOURABLE*! (Refuse -- for honour.)
Zh: 偷死人的靴子？但这么做很不*光彩*！（拒绝——为了荣誉。）

En: The anticipation makes you crack your fingers. Feels nice. Nice and *cracky*.
Zh: 那股期待让你蠢蠢欲动起来。感觉很好，又好又冲动。

En: All right then, you can pay the hostel bill in *honour* points. How many honour points have you collected?
Zh: 那好吧，你就用*荣誉点*去付旅社账单吧。你现在有多少荣誉点了？

En: There are honour points?
Zh: 还有荣誉点的吗？

En: What's going to happen to the boots then?
Zh: “靴子会如何处理呢？”

En: Okay, got it.
Zh: “好的，明白了。”

En: Of course there aren't, don't be naive.
Zh: 当然没有了，别那么天真。

En: You still feel as though there might be some honour points. And if there are, you've surely earned one.
Zh: 你仍然感觉可能会有荣誉点的存在。如果真的有的话，你肯定已经赢得一分了。

En: Processing will take care of them. With the situation in the morgue, it will yield nothing. But we must pick our fights. He looks toward the boots. "Should we continue?"
Zh: “分析部门的人会处理它们的。鉴于停尸房的状况，也不会有任何结果的。但我们总得试一试。”他看向靴子。“我们要继续吗？”

En: Grab the boot under your arm.
Zh: 把靴子夹在腋下。

En: Better not even try.
Zh: 最好不要尝试。

En: The man before you is naked, but for a pair of underpants and white boots. His skin is marbled with decaying veins and blotched by lividity. On his chest: a fading web of tattoos. The cargo belt used to hang him from the tree looks reinforced.
Zh: 你面前的男人浑身赤裸，只有一条内裤和一双白色的靴子。他的皮肤上布满了斑驳的腐烂血管与点点青斑。他的胸口上：是一片褪色的纹身。用来把他吊在树上的运货带看起来被加固过。

En: Initial report?
Zh: “你是指你的初步报告？”

En: Just something I scraped together from my station. An area report on Martinaise. I'm sure you did the same...
Zh: “只是些我在警局拼凑出来的东西，关于马丁内斯的地区报告。你肯定也这么做了……”

En: He's not actually sure of that. He's just being tactful.
Zh: 他其实并不确定，只是想点到即止地试探你。

En: As you narrow your eyes, the monster before you blurs into a violent mess of green and pink.
Zh: 当你眯起眼睛，眼前的怪物突然模糊成了一团绿色与粉色的形状。

En: Only the lower extremities are pink with a dash of blue: his fatted hands, thighs and his neck, just above the noose. The rest of the corpse appears dark green in the cold spring air.
Zh: 只有下肢是粉色中透露着一点蓝：他肥胖的手、大腿和脖子，就在绞索上面。尸体其余的部分在凌冽的春风中呈现深绿色。

En: Observe.
Zh: 观察。

En: Stop, relax your eyes.
Zh: 停下，放松你的眼睛。

En: His face and hands are pink. Thighs too.
Zh: “他的面部和手都是粉色的，大腿也是。”

En: I'm squinting, Kim. Why am I doing it?
Zh: “我正眯着眼睛，金。我为何要这么做？”

En: How should I know why you're squinting, officer?
Zh: “我怎么知道你为何要眯着眼睛，警官？”

En: (Squint harder) "His face and hands are pink. Thighs too. The rest is greenish."
Zh: （更用力的眯着眼）“他的面部和手都是粉色的，大腿也是。其余的部分都是绿的。”

En: (Stop squinting) "I don't know either. Maybe I should just stop."
Zh: （停止眯眼）“我也不知道。也许我该停下来。”

En: Oh, he snorts. "You're trying to assess *lividity*."
Zh: “哦，”他哼了一声。“你在评估*尸斑*吗？”

En: The monster comes back into focus: an explosion of colour, coursing with dark marbled veins. His stomach appears pregnant with something -- black liquid streams down his thigh and onto his boot.
Zh: 面前的怪物又重新聚焦了：一团爆炸的色块，蔓延着黑色的脂肪纹路。他的肚中仿佛孕育了某样东西——黑色的液体顺延着他的大腿，流到了靴子上。

En: I see it. He adjusts his glasses. "His neck too, the lividity goes right up his chin. We have good, well-pronounced discolouration here..."
Zh: “我看到了。”他调整了自己的眼镜。“他的脖子也是如此，尸斑一直蔓延到了他的下巴。我们面前呈现了非常明显的变色……”

En: (Cover your nose.) "Something is coming out of him."
Zh: （捂住你的鼻子。）“有东西要从他体内出来了。”

En: Should we worry? His stomach looks like it's gonna explode.
Zh: “我们是不是该小心点？他的肚子好像快要爆炸了。”

En: A pool of blood and faeces has eaten into the frozen mud below the man's feet. Purge liquid is dripping into it, drop by drop.
Zh: 一滩血迹与粪便已经侵蚀了这名男子脚下的冻土，便液一滴滴的滴落进去。

En: The victim appears to have contained no more than half a kilogram of digestion at the time of death.
Zh: “死者在死亡时似乎只消化了半公斤不到的食物。”

En: We got lucky.
Zh: “我们很走运。”

En: Maybe he went to the toilet some time before death?
Zh: “也许他死前去过厕所？”

En: God damn right I'm a f****t! (Turn around and yell.)
Zh: “没错，我是个混蛋！”（转身大叫。）

En: Did we? I don't *feel* lucky.
Zh: “你说我们？我可没*觉得*走运。”

En: Maybe. He doesn't *really* want to dwell on it too long.
Zh: “也许吧。”他*真的*不想在这个问题上纠缠太久。

En: F****ts talkin' 'bout shit.
Zh: “混蛋又在胡言乱语了。”

En: The fuck he sayin'?
Zh: “他说什么呢？”

En: Malicious laughter erupts in the yard. Sounds like seagulls.
Zh: 院子里爆发出恶意的笑声，听上去像海鸥一样。

En: The lieutenant's face is made of stone.
Zh: 警督的脸仿佛石头一般。

En: Pig said he's a f****t.
Zh: “猪猡说他自己是混蛋。”

En: I think he was upright immediately after death. Blood has gathered in his hands and feet. And his neck. He points to his fattened chin.
Zh: “我认为他死后就立刻竖直了，血液都汇聚到了他的手脚，还有脖子上。”他指着他肥胖的下巴。

En: Could it still be he was moved after death?
Zh: “还有没有可能是死后被搬运过来的？”

En: Maybe he was strangled by someone?
Zh: “或许他是被人勒死的？”

En: You would still like the hypostasis marks in the neck to be a *bit* more pronounced.
Zh: 你还是希望颈部的尸斑能更明显*一些*。

En: For some reason my brain would like the pink to be more pronounced, especially in the neck.
Zh: “出于某种原因，我的大脑希望粉色，尤其是颈部的粉色能更明显一些。”

En: Maybe it looks faint to you? He leans closer, covering his nose: "It *could* be more pronounced, actually. Dûment noté."
Zh: “也许对你来说有点模糊？”他捂着鼻子更靠近了些：“实际上，它*可以*更明显些。按规定记录。”

En: Yes, there's always a chance we're wrong. We should check for ligament marks on his neck to see if they're in tune with the belt. We'll have to get him *down* first...
Zh: “没错，人总是会犯错的。我们应该检查一下他脖子上的勒痕，看看是否与绑带相匹配。首先得把他*放下来*……”

En: There's always a chance. We should check for ligament marks on his neck to see if they're in tune with the belt. We'll have to get him *down* first...
Zh: “这种可能是存在的。我们应该检查一下他脖子上的勒痕，看看是否与运货带相匹配。首先得把他*放下来*……”

En: (Back off and catch your breath.)
Zh: （退后，喘口气。）

En: But there is no breath to catch -- only the cadaver filling the air and your nostrils. He slowly rotates before you, decomposing.
Zh: 但你根本无法呼吸——只有尸臭味弥漫在空气中和你的鼻孔里。他腐坏的身体慢慢地，在你面前旋转。

En: I don't know what to think. What do you think?
Zh: “我不知道要怎么想了，你觉得呢？”

En: There's a... his eye twitches for a moment, "a strong build-up of gases, yes. Rigor mortis becoming livor mortis. He'll be fully limp by tomorrow. I don't think he'll explode... I *hope* not."
Zh: “有一种……”他的眼睛抽搐了一会儿。“很强烈的气味，没错。死后僵直已经变成了尸斑，到了明天他就彻底腐烂了。我不觉得他会爆炸……*希望*不会。”

En: I've kept an eye on the forecast. It's been below freezing since last Sunday. Today is the first chance to thaw. Things would be... *worse* if it had thawed earlier.
Zh: “我一直在留意天气预报，从上周日以来气温一直低于零点，今天是回暖的第一天。如果提前回暖……情况会变得*更糟*。”

En: As I said it's been below freezing since last Sunday. Today is the first chance to thaw. Things would be... *worse* if it had thawed earlier.
Zh: “就像我说的，自从上周日以来气温一直在零度以下，今天是回暖的第一天。如果提前回暖……情况会变得*更糟*。”

En: The hangman's knot is pulled tight by the weight of the corpse below. Yellow, hard-edged polyester cuts into his neck. Above, a sliding buckle ties the belt to the branch.
Zh: 绞刑结被下方的尸体自重拉得很紧。黄色、边缘坚硬的聚酯纤维扎进了他的颈部。在此之上，一个滑扣把运货带绑在了树枝上。

En: We should have a look at that belt before we even *consider* taking him down. It looks worrisome. (He points to the belt around his neck.)
Zh: “在我们*思考*如何放他下来之前，应该先看看那条绑带。有点令人不安。”（他指着他脖子上的绑带）

En: This is a steel-reinforced cargo lashing belt, big brother of the regular cargo belt. It's used for tying cargo under six-rotor airships.
Zh: 这是钢线加固过的货物系固带，最坚固的一种运货带，用于把货物捆在六旋翼飞艇下。

En: What kind of rope is this?
Zh: “这是哪种绳子？”

En: Don't ask me how I know -- but this is a lashing belt used for airlifting cargo.
Zh: “别问我是怎么知道的——但这是一条用于空运货物的运货带。”

En: Industrial strength. The kind used for tying cargo to lorries.
Zh: “工业级的，用来把货物绑在货車上的那种。”

En: Relax your eyes.
Zh: 放松你的眼睛。

En: For a week he seems *well* preserved actually.
Zh: “就一个星期来说，他看上去被*保存*的很好。”

En: For seven days the cadaver is quite well preserved. Surprisingly so -- there's no deflation, no apparent fly larvae in the abdomen.
Zh: 作为一个死去七天的尸体，它被保存的相当完好。令人惊讶的是——没有巨人观，腹部也没有明显的蝇蛆。

En: He's beaten up -- see the bruises?
Zh: “他被殴打过——看到这些伤口了吗？”

En: I do. Most of them are postmortem. Maybe even all of them. The delinquents have made our jobs harder with their little sport.
Zh: “看见了。大部分，甚至可能全部都是死后造成的。这群暴徒的小动作让我们办案更困难了。”

En: Keep it to yourself.
Zh: 保持沉默。

En: Good. Death does not care for your smart alecking. It reeks and disintegrates in a feast for cadaverine bacteria.
Zh: 很好。死亡并不在乎你的小聪明。它散发着恶臭，在尸体细菌的盛宴上被分解了。

En: Stop talking in riddles, coin slot.
Zh: “别再打哑谜了，眯眯眼。”

En: He means you fucked him up good, Cuno, the girl yells. "Fucked him up brutal-like."
Zh: “他的意思是你把他整惨了，坤诺。”女孩尖叫着。“狠狠地把他搞得很惨。”

En: Did they climb up using the kids' ladder? (Point to the one at the side of the tree.)
Zh: “他们是用了孩子们的梯子爬上去的吗？”（指向树边的梯子。）

En: Oh my, there's something on the belt... a familiar word that speaks to the *thirst* within you.
Zh: 哦天呐，绑带上有东西……一个熟悉的词，表达了你的内心的*渴望*。

En: What was that? A word?
Zh: 那是什么？一个词？

En: VERMILLION, in yellow letters along the length of the twisting cargo belt. Only a deep longing for Vermillion *Golden Spirits* lets you decipher the fading logo of the local brewery.
Zh: 弗米利恩，黄色的字母沿着扭曲的运货带排列着。只有对弗米利恩牌的*金色圣灵*的深切渴望，才让你辨认出了这是当地酿酒厂日渐褪色的标志。

En: I should get a drink.
Zh: “我得去喝一杯。”

En: No. You should *not* get a drink. You should perform an initial inspection of the victim's corpse -- we're on the rope part.
Zh: “不行，你*不能*去喝一杯。你得对被害人的尸体进行初步检验——现在到绳索的部分了。”

En: Airlifting? I thought it was used on lorries -- for strapping cargo to them.
Zh: “空运？我以为是用在货車上的——用来绑货物的。”

En: Apparently this is the reinforced kind. For air transport. My brain tells me so.
Zh: “这明显是加强型的，空运专用。我的大脑是这么说的。”

En: He nods. "The local harbour uses six-rotors to shuffle containers around. I get the sense they used whatever was on hand, without paying much attention to not incriminating themselves."
Zh: 他点点头。“当地的港口会用六旋翼飞艇来运送集装箱。我的感觉是他们直接就地取材了，甚至没注意要掩藏身份。”

En: Then again what do I know? I don't even know what an airship is!
Zh: “不过我又知道什么呢？我甚至都不知道飞艇是什么！”

En: No, you're right. The harbour uses six-rotors to shuffle containers around. They used whatever was on hand here. Without paying attention to not incriminating themselves.
Zh: “不，你说得对。港口会用六旋翼飞艇来运送集装箱，他们直接就地取材了，甚至没注意要掩藏身份。”

En: We're assuming dockworkers from the harbour did it?
Zh: “我们怀疑是港口的工人们干的？”

En: I'm still approaching this as a lynching, yes. Motivated by the ongoing strike. He politely raises an eyebrow: "You?"
Zh: “我更倾向于这是一场私刑，是的。由正在进行的罢工激发的。”他礼貌地扬起了眉毛：“你呢？”

En: Like in a harbour?
Zh: “像是在港口？”

En: In a... I can't come up with anything other than a harbour.
Zh: “在……除了港口，我想不出别的了。”

En: Yes. It looks like they used whatever was on hand, paying no attention to not incriminating themselves.
Zh: “是的。像是他们直接就地取材了，甚至没注意要掩藏身份。”

En: Like in a circus! When the circus leaves town and they tie a black spotted giraffe to the wall of a carry-pen!
Zh: 就像在马戏团似的！每当马戏团离开小镇时，他们总会将一头黑色斑点的长颈鹿栓在货車壁上！

En: Like in a circus, for transporting black spotted giraffes?
Zh: “就像马戏团似的，为了运送黑色斑点的长颈鹿吗？”

En: Uhm... He considers it: "No. More like in a *harbour*. Like the one just east of here. I get the sense they used whatever was on hand, without paying much attention to not incriminating themselves."
Zh: “嗯……”他思考了一会儿：“不对，更像是*港口*，比如东边的那个。我的感觉是他们直接就地取材了，甚至没注意要掩藏身份。”

En: The brief suggested as much. Politically motivated by the ongoing strike... did you not get a briefing?
Zh: “简报也证明了这一点，持续罢工是有政治动机……你没有拿到简报吗？”

En: Uhm, yeah.
Zh: “呃，是啊。”

En: No. They might have forgotten to brief me.
Zh: “没有，他们可能忘记告诉我了。”

En: Then you should ask me the first moment we get.
Zh: “那你当即就应该问我的。”

En: My past has undergone total annihilation. Nothing remains -- my mother, the love of my life. Certainly not a *briefing*.
Zh: “我的过去已经完全湮灭了，什么都没剩下——我的母亲，一生挚爱，当然也没有什么*简报*。”

En: Okay. He blinks. "You should ask me for one the first moment we get."
Zh: “好吧，”他眨眨眼。“那你当即就应该问我的。”

En: Makes sense. Believably mundane.
Zh: “有道理，很可信。”

En: I feel like it was something else.
Zh: “我觉得好像是别的东西。”

En: Don't ask me, I'm just lumbering from one moment to the next.
Zh: “别问我，我只个时刻飘荡的游魂罢了。”

En: No. You *feel* like it was something else -- but what?
Zh: “是的。”他赞同着。“我们从野松公司代表那里得到的简报似乎也很可靠，这看起来就是一场私刑。”不对。你*觉得*好像是别的东西——但是什么呢？

En: Yes. He covers his nose. "It often is. This belt worries me."
Zh: “是的，”他捂住了自己的鼻子。“往往都是，这条绑带令我很不安。”

En: Seventy percent of the cases I get are just filling in the blanks on the initial report. He covers his nose. "This belt worries me."
Zh: “我经手的所有案件中，70%只是在初步调查里填空罢了。”他捂住了自己的鼻子。“这条绑带令我很不安。”

En: We all are. He covers his nose. "This belt worries me."
Zh: “我们都一样。”他捂住了自己的鼻子。“这条绑带令我很不安。”

En: (Back off and look at the corpse.)
Zh: （退后并看向尸体。）

En: They sure wanted him to stay up there. The rope is reinforced with steel wiring.
Zh: “他们肯定希望他能挂在上面，这根绳子是用钢线加固过的。”

En: They sure wanted him to stay there. The polyester seems strong.
Zh: “他们肯定希望他能一直挂在那儿，聚酯纤维似乎很结实。”

En: I was afraid it would be... He rises to inspect the noose. "Thin steel wiring, parallel strands. This makes getting him down more problematic than I had assumed..."
Zh: “我担心有可能是……”他站起来检查绳索。“细钢线，平行丝股法。这样要把他放下来的话，会比我预测的更加困难……”

En: It's not merely polyester -- it's steel reinforced. He rises to inspect the noose. "See these lines? This is where the wires run. I see rabbets for more than twenty strands."
Zh: “不仅是聚酯纤维——是用钢线加固过的。”他站起来检查绳索。“看到这些线了吗？钢线就是穿过这里，我看到了超过20股钢线。”

En: A noose is one of those things that's easier to use one way around... He points to the buckle tying the belt to the branch above.
Zh: “这种情况下使用套索会简单很多……”他指着系在树枝上面的绑带滑扣。

En: That ladder can't carry a grown man. I didn't see any splintering either, did you? I think they lassoed the branch, then pulled on the belt to close the buckle... He makes a pulling motion.
Zh: “那把梯子是承受不住一个成年人的。我也没有看到任何梯子碎片，你呢？我推测他们是用套索捆住了树枝，然后拉上皮搭扣上了滑扣……”他做了一个下拉的动作。

En: I think they lassoed the branch, then pulled on the belt -- buckle closes. He makes a pulling motion. "It's what I would do. Seems easier than climbing up there."
Zh: “我推测他们是用套索捆住了树枝，然后拉上绑带——滑扣扣上。”他做了一个下拉的动作。“我也会这么做的，感觉比爬到上面去简单很多。”

En: Ignore it.
Zh: 忽略它。

En: It's hard, but somehow you manage not to see it. The corpse looks at you sideways, from an unnatural angle.
Zh: 虽然艰难，但不知怎地你设法无视了它。尸体从一个不自然的角度，斜视着你。

En: An intricate web of blue lines stretches across the torso. From the right shoulder to the solar plexus, each time they intersect a small white star is formed in their crossing. Hundreds of fading asterisks riddle his skin, their concentration is highest around his heart.
Zh: 一张错综复杂的蓝色线网覆盖了躯干。从右肩横跨至腹腔神经丛，每当它们相交时都会形成一颗白色的星星。他的皮肤上点缀着数百个褪了色的星号，尤其是心脏周围最为密集。

En: His corpse is marked by stars.
Zh: 他的尸体上布满了星星。

En: What will mine be marked by?
Zh: 我的标记会是什么呢？

En: Alcohol and heartbreak.
Zh: 酒精和心碎吧。

En: It will be marked by bullet holes.
Zh: 会是弹孔吧。

En: Your fist clenches suddenly.
Zh: 你突然握紧了拳头。

En: It will be riddled with disco.
Zh: 一定会布满迪斯科的。

En: Is this a map of the night sky?
Zh: “这是夜空的地图吗？”

En: Is this a micro-electronic system?
Zh: “这是微电子系统吗？”

En: Is this a national pattern?
Zh: “这是一个国家的图案吗？”

En: I have only a cursive knowledge of the science of cybernetics -- I would not know if it were. But it's not *quite* complex enough, is it?
Zh: “我对控制学只有一些粗浅的了解——也不知道是不是这样的。但它还*不够*复杂，不是吗？”

En: Of no nation that I know of. If anything, it reminds me of religious illumination, last or penultimate century. Men who live harsh lives often turn to innocentic worship -- but which one? I see no trace of a humanoid figure.
Zh: “据我所知，没有一个国家是这样的。说起来，它让我想起了上个世纪，或者上上个世纪的宗教启示。生活艰苦的人往往会转向单纯的神明崇拜——但是是哪一个呢？我没有看到任何人形痕迹。”

En: A map of the stars? He turns around to breathe before inspecting it closer. "I do see some similarity to astronomical charts, Great Century Messinian maybe... But this seems more particular. Customized somehow."
Zh: “星空图？”他转过身去喘了口气，然后凑近查看。“我确实看到了一些类似于天文图的东西，也许是伟大时代的梅西纳人……但这似乎更为特殊一点，像是定制的。”

En: As if someone left out most of the night sky, filtering it through personal choice. The principle of this filter remains unknown to you. The thought dissipates and you feel as though you were only half-right.
Zh: 仿佛有人忽略了大部分的夜空，凭借个人喜好做出了选择，选择的标准你尚不知晓。这种想法消散了，你感觉自己只答对了一半。

En: I'm missing something here.
Zh: “我遗漏了某些东西。”

En: So am I. A sudden ringing fills the air as the lieutenant pulls down the zipper of his orange jacket.
Zh: “我也是。”当警督拉下他的橘色夹克拉链时，突然响起了一阵铃声。

En: He takes a thin piece of milled aluminium from his coat pocket and pulls it open. Sounds like a sword being unsheathed. A small lens appears -- some sort of camera.
Zh: 他从上衣口袋掏出一小片研磨过的铝片，将其拉开。听起来就像一把出鞘的剑，一小块透镜出现了——是某种照相机。

En: He wears a wide leather belt around his waist and a gun holster under his arm.
Zh: 他腰间系着一条宽绑带，腋下夹着枪套。

En: A sound, a shrill flash. Followed by the breaking of a small ampoule of glass. You see streams of colour pour onto the thick, glossy piece of paper rolling out.
Zh: 一道声响，一瞬刺眼的闪光，紧接着是一瓶打碎了的安瓿瓶。你看到一串串色彩倾泻在那厚实而有光泽的纸上。

En: In case we need it, the lieutenant says and shakes the paper, letting it dry in the cold wind. On it -- a colour-perfect copy of the dead man's tattooed chest.
Zh: 在上面——是死者胸腔纹身的复刻，色彩近乎完美。“以防万一，“警督说话间摇了摇纸片，让它在寒风中风干。

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: Let the lieutenant work.
Zh: 让警督工作。

En: A Trigat Sunshine. Mini.
Zh: “特里盖特阳光，迷你型。”

En: Trigat is the world's leading manufacturer of intercommunication devices, primarily projectors. The camera before you looks familiar somehow.
Zh: 特里盖特是全球领先的内部通讯设备制造商，主要生产投影仪。你面前的照相机不知怎地看起来很眼熟。

En: An instant colour camera. He produces two metal-capped ampoules and clicks them into place on the side of the apparatus. A thin slot shines there...
Zh: “一台拍立得。”他制作了两个金属瓶盖的安瓿瓶，并将它们放置在仪器的一侧。那里有一条细缝闪闪发光……

En: Shit, Cuno! WHAT THE FUCK IS THAT?!?
Zh: “该死，坤诺！那他妈是什么？！”

En: This is the first time he openly acknowledges the kids' existence.
Zh: 这是他第一次公开承认孩子们的存在。

En: I have only two ampoules, so nobody move -- I don't want to waste one. He points the camera at the corpse, peering into it. The lens needs adjusting. Then:
Zh: “我只有这两个安瓿瓶，所以都不要动——我可不想浪费。”他用照相机对准了尸体，凝视着镜头。镜头需要调整一下。然后：

En: What do we need this photo for?
Zh: “我们要这张照片做什么？”

En: Cool machine.
Zh: “相机不错。”

En: The glassy-eyed corpse looks by, his mouth mute and his skin as colourful as the chemical rainbow on the photo paper, teeming with opportunistic organisms.
Zh: 眼神呆滞的尸体从旁边望过去，他的嘴悄无声息，他的皮肤如同相片纸上的化学彩虹一般色彩斑斓，与蠢蠢欲动的微生物们凑作一团。

En: It contains insight to the victim's person. This was a man of physical violence. The story he wanted his body to tell was important to him. It is his letter -- to us. Now, if we could find someone to decipher it...
Zh: “这包含了对于被害人本人的目击证据。他是一个崇尚暴力的人，他想通过自己的肉体表达的东西非常重要，像是给我们的——一段讯息。现在，如果我们能能找人来破译它的话…….”

En: It contains insight to the victim's person. By his build, I'd say this was a man of physical violence. The story he wanted his body to tell was important to him. It is his letter -- to us. *Someone* should decipher it. We'll need to show it around.
Zh: “这包含了对于被害人本人的理解。从他的体型来看，我觉得他是一个崇尚暴力的人。他想通过自身肉体讲述的故事对他来说非常重要。这是他的一封信——给我们的。应该*有人*能破译它，我们得四处问问。”

En: It doesn't seem familiar from the insides of any radiocomputer your mind can imagine. The somewhat organic lines remind you of old filament memory units, but not quite? No. You're nowhere near right.
Zh: 这跟你大脑中所能想象的任何一台无线电脑都不相同。这些组织线条让你想起了旧时的纤维内存单元，但也不完全是？不，你大错特错了。

En: The pattern does not stir patriotic or religious sentiment in you. What it does is speak to the wounded, limping animal -- the male in particular. Unable to communicate in anything more than grunts, it's impossible to tell if it's advice is right or wrong...
Zh: 这幅图案并不会激起你的爱国主义或宗教情绪。它所表达的就是与受伤的、跛行的动物交流——特别是雄性。咕哝几句之外，无法用任何语言交流，也无法判断这条建议是对还是错……

En: We're missing something here.
Zh: “我们遗漏了一些东西。”

En: I agree. A sudden ring fills the air as the lieutenant pulls down the zipper of his orange jacket.
Zh: “我同意。”当警督拉下他的橘色夹克拉链时，突然响起了一阵铃声。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Can I have it? I should look at it later, without the corpse smell.
Zh: “能把照片给我吗？这样我就能之后在不必闻着尸臭的情况下观察它了。”

En: Sure, just don't lose it. He hands you the piece of rolled up photo paper. It's no larger than a pack of cigarettes.
Zh: “当然，只是不要弄丢了。”他把那张卷起来的相纸递给你，不比一包香烟大了多少。

En: Yes... He slides the camera closed and tucks it away on his belt. "It is pretty *cool*, isn't it?"
Zh: “是的……”他合上相机，将它塞在腰带上。“挺*酷*的，不是吗？”

En: This is a trick. You've done it before. Pink is where the blood settled in the first hours postmortem. You can use it to see if the corpse has been tampered with -- does his position at the time of death match the discolouration?
Zh: 这是个小花招，你之前也这么做过。粉色区域是死后最初几个小时血液沉淀的地方，你可以据此判断尸体是否被动过手脚--他死亡时的位置与他的皮肤颜色相匹配吗?

En: I think he's dead.
Zh: “我觉得他死了。”

En: So -- what do you think?
Zh: “那么——你觉得呢？”

En: I agree.
Zh: “我同意。”

En: There are crows feet in his eyes, he's laughing silently.
Zh: 他的眼角有鱼尾纹，像是在沉默地笑着。

En: What is *dead* anyway?
Zh: “说起来如何定义*死*呢？”

En: This buster's not coming back.
Zh: “这家伙不会回来了。”

En: Totally.
Zh: “绝对的。”

En: Totally dead.
Zh: “绝对死了。”

En: I think he was upright after death. His hands, feet and neck are discoloured.
Zh: “我觉得他是死后就竖直了。他的手、脚和脖子都变色了。”

En: No sirree. He shakes his head.
Zh: “是不会回来了，”他摇着头。

En: Agreed. He points to the belt. "Especially on the neck. The belt acted like a tourniquet keeping the blood in his head. The hypostasis supports a hanging."
Zh: “同意，”他指着绑带。”特别是颈部。绑带就像止血带一样，让血液都集中在了他的头上。尸斑说明了是缢死的。”

En: He is. He points to the corpse.
Zh: “他就是定义，”他指着尸体。

En: Deadady-dead-dead-dead.
Zh: “死咯-死噜-死透啦。”

En: Absolutely.
Zh: “完全正确。”

En: Daba-doop-doop-dead, he says without a hint of melody. Not one muscle in his face moves.
Zh: “死咯-死噜-死透啦。”他毫无起伏地说道，脸上没有一丝表情。

En: The world no longer stores his personality in its composition. There was a time for that -- and it ended seven days ago.
Zh: “这个世界已经不再保有他存在过的痕迹，曾经有过——但在七天前便消失了。”

En: He nods in agreement with this advanced piece of conceptualization. Having mulled it over the lieutenant says: "That sounds about right, yes."
Zh: 他点头认同这一超前的概念，警督思索后开口：“听起来似乎没错，是的。”

En: No, better not to say that. Too heavy.
Zh: 不，还是不说为好，太沉重了。

En: Sure. The corpse looks by, oblivious. Black decay liquid drops from his knee to his boot.
Zh: 当然，尸体明显侧目了过来，黑色的腐烂液体从膝盖滴落至他的靴子上。

En: His eyes are milky white and blind to the world, protruding comically from their sockets. There is no one home, just sub-aquatic terrors there.
Zh: 他的眼睛是乳白色的，对整个世界视而不见，滑稽地从眼窝中凸了出来。眼孔中毫无生气，只有半水深的恐惧。

En: Hmh... The steel-reinforced belt presents a *unique* challenge. I brought chain-cutters, but I don't see a good angle of approach to the belt.
Zh: “嗯……钢线加固的绑带是一项*独特的*挑战。我带了切链钳，但没能找到一个很好的角度插进绑带。”

En: I can cut the belt easily. And I already *have* the chaincutters.
Zh: “我能轻松割断绑带，而且我已经*有*切链钳了。”

En: I can cut the belt easily. Where are those chaincutters you mentioned?
Zh: “我能轻松割断绑带，你说的切链钳呢？”

En: We could bring your motor carriage over -- cut the belt from the roof?
Zh: “可以把你的車开过来——在車顶上割断绑带？”

En: Maybe we could *shoot* him down?
Zh: “或许可以把他*射*下来？”

En: We could saw the branch.
Zh: “可以把树枝锯断。”

En: Underneath the curdled meat there is an expression, not carried on his features but below, inside: an expression of pleasure. This man was experiencing *joy* at the moment of his death.
Zh: 这摊烂肉之下有一种表情，并非流于表面，而是内心的写照：一种快乐的表情。这个男人在临死之际是*愉悦*的。

En: These eyes used to be blue. Baby blue.
Zh: 这双眼睛曾经是蓝色的，天蓝色。

En: Dark brown hair grows on his head, his face is ready to explode from the organic processes inside. The death's head grin has passed, what remains is an unrecognisable mess.
Zh: 他的头上有着深棕色的头发，他的脸因为内部微生物的活动随时可能爆炸。死神咧嘴一笑地悄然离去，只留下这团无法辨认的混乱。

En: (Back off.)
Zh: （退后。）

En: This man enjoyed the moment of death.
Zh: “这个男人在享受死亡的瞬间。”

En: Why do you think so?
Zh: “你为什么这么认为？”

En: It's an intuition.
Zh: “就是一种直觉。”

En: For supra-natural reasons.
Zh: “出于某种超自然的理由。”

En: Okay. The lieutenant marks something in his notebook.
Zh: “好吧。”警督在他的笔记本上做了记录。

En: I am less sceptic about this hunch, he thinks, than I was of the supra-natural premonitions that overtook you at the trash container. He then covers his nose again...
Zh: 我对这个预感倒不那么怀疑了，他想着，相较于在垃圾箱里突如其来的那种超自然的预感来说。然后他又捂住了鼻子……

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Hmm... The lieutenant marks something in his notebook.
Zh: “嗯……”警督在他的笔记本上做了记录。

En: There are no supra-natural reasons, the lieutenant says, marking something in his notebook.
Zh: “不存在什么超自然的理由，”警督说着，在他的笔记本上做了记录。

En: I'm gone.
Zh: 我已经走了。

En: Where have you gone?
Zh: 你去哪儿了呢？

En: Into the wild pale yonder.
Zh: 灰域无垠的彼方。

En: The corpse looks right through you as you distance yourself from its stench, eyes like a shark...
Zh: 你远离那股恶臭越来越远，尸体直直地盯着你，眼睛如同鲨鱼一般……

En: Tell me, who are you, dead man?
Zh: “告诉我，你是谁呢，逝去的人？”

En: Talked in your dream.
Zh: 梦中交流过。

En: His eyes were baby blue.
Zh: 他的眼睛是天蓝色的。

En: Intimate autopsy done.
Zh: 宣告尸检完成。

En: Amber Die with a Mosquito.
Zh: 带蚊子的琥珀骰子。

En: The corpse is dead silent. You have no idea why you just said that.
Zh: 尸体死寂无声，你也不知道自己为何要这么说。

En: Who is he? He is male, forty to fifty. With an athletic build.
Zh: “他是谁？是一名男性，在四十至五十岁之间，体格强壮。”

En: His eyes, they used to be blue.
Zh: “他的眼睛，曾是天蓝色的。”

En: Good. The victim is of Occidental descent, he scribbles in his notebook. "Light brown hair -- blue eyes."
Zh: “很好。被害人是奥西登人，”他在笔记本上记录着。“浅棕色毛发——蓝眼睛。”

En: Occidental?
Zh: “奥西登人？”

En: I mean white.
Zh: “我是指白人。”

En: When he says Occidental he means white. Originally from the Occidental sub-continent on Mundi.
Zh: 当他说奥西登人的时候，就是指白种人。起源于蒙迪西方的次大陆。

En: I told you -- he is male, forty to fifty. With an athletic build.
Zh: “我说过了——他是一名男性，在四十至五十岁之间，体格强壮。”

En: Who is he? He is male, forty to fifty. With an athletic build.
Zh: “他是谁？是一名男性，在四十至五十岁之间，体格强壮。”

En: The lips of the corpse are swollen, preposterously. They are also silent. You have no idea why you just said that.
Zh: 尸体的嘴唇肿胀得有些可笑，同样很沉默。你也不知道自己为何要这么说。

En: Tell me, who are you, dead man?
Zh: “告诉我，你是谁呢，逝去的人？”

En: Talked to him in your dream.
Zh: 在你的梦中与他交流。

En: His eyes were baby blue.
Zh: 他的眼睛是天蓝色的。

En: Intimate autopsy done.
Zh: 宣告尸检完成。

En: Amber Die with a Mosquito.
Zh: 带蚊子的琥珀骰子。

En: Where is that?
Zh: 那是哪儿？

En: In the past. Way out west.
Zh: 在远方，遥远的西方。

En: What is happening?
Zh: 发生什么了？

En: I can see you're gone, but *who* are you?
Zh: 我知道你已经走了，但你是*谁*？

En: I'm a joke. Look at me.
Zh: 我就是个笑话，看看我吧。

En: You are now, but who were you when you were alive?
Zh: 没错，但你生前又是谁呢？

En: There's nothing funny about you.
Zh: 你并不可笑。

En: A killer. A motherfucker and a killer.
Zh: 一个杀手。一个狗杂种兼杀手。

En: There is nothing funny about jokes, either.
Zh: 笑话也没有什么可笑的。

En: Who were you when you were alive?
Zh: 你生前又是谁呢？

En: I have another question for you.
Zh: 我还有一个问题想问你。

En: What was that -- 'pale yonder'? Do you know what the pale is?
Zh: 那是什么——“灰域的彼方”？你知道灰域是什么吗？

En: What is the pale?
Zh: 灰域是什么？

En: The pale, Coppo... it spooks me out.
Zh: 一片灰域，小警察……把我吓坏了。

En: Go ahead, Cobo.
Zh: 继续，小警察。

En: It's the power of your... (Black, frothy liquid starts bubbling on his lips...)
Zh: 是你的力量……（黑色的泡状液体在他的嘴唇上翻腾……）

En: What do you mean?
Zh: 你是什么意思？

En: I'm talking to you.
Zh: 我在跟你说话。

En: Never mind, I wanted to...
Zh: 别介意，我想……

En: Ask me more questions? You fucking *love* questions.
Zh: 问我更多的问题？你真他妈*喜欢*问问题。

En: I hate you. You stink and you're boring.
Zh: 我讨厌你，又臭又无聊。

En: Do I remind you of someone?
Zh: 我让你想起了谁吗？

En: A deep sea creature.
Zh: 一只深海生物。

En: Myself -- in the bathroom mirror.
Zh: 我自己——在浴室镜中的模样。

En: You don't remind me of anyone.
Zh: 你并没有让我想起谁。

En: Why *do* I love questions so much?
Zh: 为什么我*如此*爱问问题呢？

En: Because you're a copparooni. Look at all of them go! Do you want more questions?
Zh: 因为你是个疯警察。看他们都走了！你还想要更多问题吗？

En: No, I'm good.
Zh: 不了，我很好。

En: Yeah, give me questions.
Zh: 是啊，让我问吧。

En: Give me a comical amount of questions.
Zh: 让我问你一大堆问题吧。

En: Comin' right up, copporooni Roonie. This is getting up-beat now!
Zh: 马上就来，疯子警察罗尼。跟上节拍吧！

En: Is my name Roonie?
Zh: 我的名字叫罗尼吗？

En: Why do I feel like I've forgotten something terrible?
Zh: 为什么我感觉自己忘记了某件很可怕的事情？

En: Who killed you?
Zh: 是谁杀了你？

En: Can *you* ask me a question?
Zh: *你*能问我一个问题吗？

En: Fuck no, you're no Roonie.
Zh: 当然不，你才不是罗尼。

En: Here you go, you loonie!
Zh: 来吧，你这个疯子。

En: I do strike myself as a Roonie.
Zh: 但我确实记得自己就是罗尼呀。

En: Roonie is obviously not who I am.
Zh: 罗尼显然不是我。

En: Right, I'm the Tequila Sunset.
Zh: 没错，我是龙舌兰日落。

En: Between you and me, your name is probably Harry.
Zh: 偷偷告诉你，你的名字可能是哈里。

En: No, you don't. Between you and me, your name is probably Harry.
Zh: 不，你不是。偷偷告诉你，你的名字可能是哈里。

En: No, it can't be Harry. I refuse.
Zh: 不，不可能是哈里，我拒绝。

En: Could I really be Harry?
Zh: 我真的会是哈里吗？

En: Yeah, man, don't be *crazy*. Inanimate objects and dead people can't really talk to you, your *wild imagination* is doing this -- ask some more of those questions you love so much!
Zh: 是啊，伙计，别*发疯*了。没有生命的物体和死去的人是不会跟你说话的，是你的*狂野幻想*在作祟——再多问一些你喜欢的问题吧！

En: IMAGINATION.
Zh: 幻想。

En: He *loves* those.
Zh: 他*热爱*那些。

En: Your loss, *coppodoppo*. Would've been so primo...
Zh: 你的损失，*小察察*。本来可以抢先的……

En: Why does it spook you out?
Zh: 为什么会吓到你？

En: All right, I had another question.
Zh: 好吧，我还有另外一个问题。

En: Of course you did, you question-lover.
Zh: 你当然会有，你这个问题狂热者。

En: The corpse is silent. The nose in the middle of his face is swollen, so are his cheeks. His lips are motionless.
Zh: 尸体很沉默，他脸部正中的鼻子，以及两颊都肿了起来，嘴唇纹丝不动。

En: Takes one to know one.
Zh: 彼此彼此。

En: You ain't no Tequila Sunset, you're probably just Harry or something. That's right... *Harry*.
Zh: 你才不是龙舌兰日落，可能就是哈里之类的吧。没错……*哈里*。

En: Good for you.
Zh: 真不错。

En: You can be anything you wanna be, brother Coppo.
Zh: 你可以成为任何你想成为的人，警察兄弟。

En: No, not quite... Be fair now.
Zh: 不，不完全是……公平点儿。

En: There you go -- look at that bright kid! We're birds of a feather, you and I. Soon you will be just like me, just keep drinkin' and havin' a good time. It's a matter of weeks.
Zh: 看吧——看看那个聪明的孩子！我和你，都是长着羽翼的飞鸟。继续饮酒，及时行乐吧，很快你就会像我一样。只有几周的时间了。

En: Feeling nausea? Vomiting? Tenderness or pain around the liver area? Tiny red lines on the skin above waist level? More like *days*, Coppo. The clock is ticking, your liver tells you so.
Zh: 感觉恶心？想吐？肝脏周围有痛感吗？腰部以上的皮肤有红血丝？更像是*几天*时间，小警察。你的肝脏是这么跟你说的。

En: A baby affected with harlequinism.
Zh: 患有丑角综合症的婴儿。

En: A child born with Moller's disease -- Harlequinism -- grown up miraculously.
Zh: 一个天生患有穆勒氏综合征的孩子——丑角综合征——奇迹般的长大成人。

En: You sure wriggled out of that one, Coppolini!
Zh: 你这绝对是在逃避，小察察！

En: Sure I do -- you just don't want to admit it.
Zh: 那是当然——你就是不想承认罢了。

En: Why were you feeling pleasure when you died?
Zh: 你临死之际为什么会感到愉悦？

En: Maybe I was getting my rocks off?
Zh: 或是因为我终于解脱了？

En: First, do you have to speak like that? What dialect is that, anyway?
Zh: 首先，你非得这样说话吗？而且这究竟是哪里的方言？

En: So -- you were feeling sexual arousal when they were hanging you?
Zh: 所以——当他们把你吊起来的时候，你性致盎然了？

En: Forget it.
Zh: 算了吧。

En: It's a mish-mash, Coppobollo. You think I'm Messinian, don't you? For you, this is how people from Messina speak like.
Zh: 天南地北的吧，警小察。你是不是以为我是梅西纳人？对你来说，梅西纳人就是这么说话的。

En: I'm not even sure what Messina is.
Zh: 我甚至不知道梅西纳是什么。

En: Are you? From Messina?
Zh: 你是吗？来自梅西纳？

En: No. My hair is too light a shade of brown. My eyebrows too. Trust your inner racist.
Zh: 不是。我的发色有点太浅了，还带点棕色，我的眉毛也是。相信你内心的种族主义者吧。

En: Well, I'm not *from* Messina, am I? My hair is too light a shade of brown. Trust your inner racist.
Zh: 这个嘛，我并非*来自*梅西纳，是不是？我的发色有点太浅了，还带点棕色。相信你内心的种族主义吧。

En: What are you, racist now too?
Zh: 你怎么回事，也开始种族歧视了吗？

En: I *always* trust my inner racist. He shows me the score.
Zh: 我*总是*相信自己内心的种族主义，他让我看清真相。

En: You think I am. You think I was a racist because this lump looks like military and has tattoos. That's called *profiling*.
Zh: 你觉得我是。你觉得我是种族主义者，就因为这个大块头像个军人，还有纹身。这叫做*剖绘*。

En: Yeah, well, he seems to think I'm also a racist since I have tattoos and a military body build. It's called *profiling*.
Zh: 好吧，这个嘛，他似乎认为我也是一个种族主义者，因为我有纹身和军人的体格。这叫做*剖绘*。

En: Do I look like an erotic auto-asphyxiation type to you?
Zh: 对你来说，我看上去像是一个喜欢性窒息的色情狂吗？

En: Yes.
Zh: 是的。

En: No.
Zh: 不是。

En: I know what this means -- more questions.
Zh: 我知道这意味着什么——更多的问题。

En: What is erotic auto-asphyxiation?
Zh: 性窒息是什么？

En: Captain Coppodromo, I fear we are drifting away, fixating on sexuality again... let's go with a simpler question.
Zh: 小察察警长，恐怕我们又跑题了，关注点又放在了性的方面……还是问个更简单的问题吧。

En: He didn't choke himself. You know it.
Zh: 他并没有扼死自己，你很清楚。

En: Because you *have*.
Zh: 因为你*真忘了*。

En: Love did me in, brother Coppo. It was love all along...
Zh: 是爱情毁了我，小警察。从来都是因为爱情……

En: Sure, Lobo, I can ask you a question: Why are you doing this?
Zh: 当然，大灰狼，我可以问你一个问题：你为什么要做这个？

En: What?
Zh: 做什么？

En: Looking at my face, motionless. Looking into my eyes. Standing here. Why are you investigating my murder?
Zh: 一动不动地盯着我的脸，望着我的眼睛，站在这里。你为什么要调查我的案子？

En: I don't have anything else to do. This case is all I have.
Zh: 我也没有别的事情可做，唯一只剩这个案子了。

En: Maybe this will lead to something? Something indescribable. Unforeseen. Miraculous?
Zh: 或许这能带来新的发现？一些难以形容、无法预见、奇迹般的发现？

En: *I'm* all you have? Then you truly lost it all, brother. You let the world drag it all away from you, and what it left, you pissed away -- and here we are.
Zh: *我*是你的全部？那你真的失去了一切，兄弟。你让世界把一切从你身边带走，而剩下来的，又被你糟蹋了——结果就成了现在这样。

En: Enough.
Zh: 够了。

En: Hah! The clown lips on the corpse appear to smile. The face rotates before you, slowly...
Zh: 哈！尸体滑稽的嘴唇似乎扬起了微笑，他的脸庞慢慢在你面前旋转起来……

En: Something is on its way. Something hidden. It's coming, a miracle -- from the northwest -- and it's almost here. You can feel it in the air on your hands, the cold spring air smoothing them over.
Zh: 有东西要来了，某种隐蔽的东西。就要来了，一个奇迹——从西北方向——就快要来临。你可以从手中的空气感受到它们，冷冽的春风掩盖了它们。

En: I have something I need to know, corpse-man.
Zh: 我有些事需要知道，尸体。

En: Of course! You have questions, don't you? The power of your *imagination* is at your service.
Zh: 当然！你有问题想问，是不是？你的*幻象*之力随时为你效劳。

En: He doesn't actually think the challenge is *unique*. He thinks it's frustrating, annoying and harder than he thought.
Zh: 其实他并不认为这项挑战具有*独特性*。他觉得这很令人沮丧，惹人厌烦且比他想象中的要困难得多。

En: The cadaver is a good 1.2 metres up, neither one of us can reach the belt without assistance. And even if we do, there's the question of cutting it...
Zh: “尸体距离地面足足1.2米高，在没有任何帮助的情况下，我跟你谁也够不着绑带。而且将就算我们够得着，如何割断它也是个问题……”

En: The cadaver is a good 1.2 metres up, neither one of us can reach the belt without assistance. And even if we do, there's the question of cutting the airship-strength material...
Zh: “尸体距离地面足足1.2米高，在没有任何帮助的情况下，我跟你谁也够不着绑带。而且将就算我们够得着，如何割断飞艇强度的材料也是个问题……”

En: There's no *question*. There's only CUTTING. How hard can it be?
Zh: 没有任何*问题*。只是割断绑带而已，能有多难？

En: I don't doubt your physical prowess, officer, but this is a gruelling surgical procedure. I saw twenty steel strands in there -- and we do not have a secure platform for the cutting.
Zh: “我并非是怀疑你的身体素质，警官，但这是一个很费精力的作业过程。我看到里面有二十股钢线——我们也没有安全的切割作业平台。”

En: I don't doubt your physical prowess, officer, but that's aircraft strength material. And we do not have a secure platform to perform the procedure on.
Zh: “我并非是怀疑你的身体素质，警官，但这是飞艇强度的材料。而且我们也没有一个安全的作业平台来执行这项工作。”

En: Seems like a lot of hassle, let's *not* do it.
Zh: “听起来确实很麻烦，还是*不要*勉强了。”

En: Let's just pull his head off.
Zh: “干脆把他的头扯下来吧。”

En: I'm out of ideas. Let's have another look at him.
Zh: “我放弃了，还是再看看他吧。”

En: The lieutenant inspects the belt, his forehead lined with worry.
Zh: 警督检查着绑带，额头上写满了忧虑。

En: Hmmm... The lieutenant falls deep in thought.
Zh: “嗯……”警督陷入了沉思之中。

En: It's tempting. But decapitating him will make it impossible to ascertain whether he was really hung. The damage to the neck would make coronary work pointless.
Zh: “很吸引人，但将他斩首的话就无从得知他是否真的是被绞死的了。颈部的损伤会让验尸官的工作毫无意义。”

En: How about I take you piggyback?
Zh: “我托着你上去如何？”

En: An acrobatic manoeuvre would solve this situation. Nothing can go wrong with a good *manoeuvre*.
Zh: 一个杂技动作就能解决这个问题，一个精彩的*特技动作*准不会出错的。

En: I have thought of that, yes. But the tyre tracks would compromise the scene. Any prints would become illegible. That's *if* we get it through the hole in the fence...
Zh: “我也考虑过，是的。但車轮痕迹会破坏现场。任何痕迹都会变得难以辨认。还有一个前提，*如果*我们能穿过围栏上的那个洞的话……”

En: Also, my MC has a sloped roof, it's a *sports* model. He makes a gesture indicating a harsh degree of sloping. "The roof is slippery -- an all-around bad platform to stand on."
Zh: “更何况，我的汽車是斜顶的，*跑車型*。”他用手势演示了車顶的倾斜程度。“車顶很滑——根本不适合站立。”

En: He could come up with at least four more reasons why not to trample on the roof of his *sports model* motor carriage.
Zh: 他至少还能想出四个理由来解释为什么不能踩在他的*跑車型*車顶上。

En: Could your attachment to your sports model motor carriage be clouding your judgement?
Zh: “你对于跑車的迷恋程度会影响你的判断吗？”

En: Yes, he concedes. "I am attached to it. But it's also unsuitable. We can't bring a supercharged tractor through that tiny gap in the fence, then plough the scene with it. Then fall off the roof. It would sound bad on Channel 8."
Zh: “是的，”他承认到。“我很迷恋車子，但同样很不合适。我们无法让一台超动力拖拉机穿过围栏上的那个小缺口，把犯罪现场犁一遍，然后又从車顶上摔下来。8频道会让我们面上无光的。”

En: We're *not* getting him down already. Not getting him down is a task that's already accomplished. Sadly, it's not our job to *keep* him up there, but to get him down.
Zh: “我们还*没有*把他放下来，‘不把他放下来’这一项任务我们已经完成了。遗憾的是，我们的工作不是让他*吊在*上面，而是要放他下来。”

En: How about I take *you* piggyback? After a moment’s silence, he raises an eyebrow.
Zh: 一阵沉默之后，他扬起了眉毛：“不如我托着*你*上去如何？”

En: The fuck are these pigs talking about?
Zh: “这群猪猡在说什么呢？”

En: Their bank or something. It's fucking boring, Cuno. Wish they'd fuck already.
Zh: “驼背什么的吧。真他妈的无聊，坤诺。希望他们早点玩完。”

En: Fucking SHUT UP!
Zh: “他妈的闭上你们的嘴！”

En: Would you please shut up.
Zh: “能不能请你们闭嘴呢？”

En: Lieutenant, are you sure you could carry my weight though?
Zh: “警督，你确定你能扛得动我吗？”

En: Lieutenant, you meant it rhetorically, right? You're criticizing my idea?
Zh: “警督，你只是在打比方，对吧？在批评我的主意？”

En: Yes. It's not a good idea.
Zh: “的确，这并不明智。”

En: It's a fucking hilarious idea -- you two paskapää's get on top each other and *fuck*!
Zh: “这太他妈搞笑了——两头猪猡*搞*在一起了！”

En: Would you please SUCK MY DICK? The one behind the fence makes a gesture, as if taking out their penis.
Zh: “能不能请你过来跪舔呢？”围栏后面的人做了个手势，好像要掏出老二似的。

En: Fucking *mulkku*...
Zh: “该死的*老二*……”

En: Pig, why you gotta disrespect my girl? This shit makes Cuno *sad*.
Zh: “猪猡，你怎么敢不尊重我的女人？这让坤诺很*难过*啊。”

En: Fuck that *banaanipoika* in the ass Cuno!
Zh: “肛了那个*基佬*，坤诺！”

En: I wasn't actually implying I would. I meant it's a bad idea.
Zh: “我不是在暗示我会这么做，我的意思是这并不是一个好主意。”

En: It'd sound fucking hilarious, do it! Fucking get on top of it and dance, fägäri!
Zh: “听起来太他妈的搞笑了，上吧！快他妈的跳上去跳舞吧，死基佬！”

En: (Give up.) "Okay, how about..."
Zh: （放弃）“好吧，要不然……”

En: Climb up there and... saw the branch?
Zh: “爬到上面去……把树枝锯断？”

En: Yes. Either one of us could do it. We could use the kids' ladder on the side of the tree.
Zh: “是的，我们俩谁都能做。我们可以把孩子们的梯子架在树的旁边。”

En: Yeah... it seems dangerous.
Zh: “是啊……感觉很危险。”

En: There has to be a less risky way. With less falling down off trees.
Zh: “一定有比较低风险的方法，至少不会从树上跌下去。”

En: It seems doable. The kids' ladder is treacherously rotten, but you could use your hands and feet to reach around the tree, then hang from the branch...
Zh: 似乎可行。那些孩子的梯子几乎烂透了，但你可以用自己的手脚够到大树，然后挂在树枝上……

En: Honestly, I prefer a *non-acrobatic* solution to this. He looks around -- then at you.
Zh: 他四下打量着——然后看向你。“说实话，我倾向于一种不那么*杂技*的解决方法。”

En: I could do it. It's well within my repertoire.
Zh: “我可以做，这完全在我的能力范围之内。”

En: I don't trust that ladder. The assailants didn't use it. It's rotten and less sturdy than it looks. And I don't see another good way up there.
Zh: “我可不敢用那把梯子，连暴徒们都没有用。它已经腐烂了，也没有看上去那么结实。我也不觉得爬上去有什么好的。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: *Clown Cops Climb Tree, Fall Down*, he says, furrowing his brow. "*Enraged Cop Assaults Children -- After Falling From Tree*."
Zh: “*小丑警察爬上树，一不小心摔下来*，”他皱着眉头说道。“*摔下树后无颜面——恼羞成怒打小孩*。”

En: *Cretin Cop*... -- they like that word -- ...*Saws The Branch He's Sitting On (Literally)*. *Local Children Report Corpse Mutilation*.
Zh: *白痴警察*……——他们喜欢这个词——锯树枝……*（字面意义上）*。*儿童报案，尸体数目又增加*。”

En: Can you see into the future, Kim?
Zh: “你能预知未来吗，金？”

En: Fuck, yeah! Climb that shit, monkeys!
Zh: “对啊！快爬上去吧，猴子们！”

En: C, you're only making them do those things less.
Zh: “C，你这样只会让他们更不情愿那么做了。”

En: No, I do not see the future. I made those up using two decades in the Revacholian police force as a reference point.
Zh: “不能，我并没有预知未来。这是我根据二十年来在瑞瓦肖的从警经验推断出来的。”

En: I'm not afraid of newspapers. We shouldn't be.
Zh: “我不怕报纸上怎么写，我们都不应该害怕。”

En: Oh, no. He shakes his head slowly. "We should be *very* afraid of newspapers. And the radio stations too. We should be wary of enticing them in *any* way."
Zh: “错了，”他缓缓摇头。“我们应该*相当*害怕报纸，还有无线电台才对。我们应当警惕，*千万*不要招惹它们。”

En: So we're going to just let those kids bully us into not doing our work?
Zh: “所以我们就任由那些孩子欺压我们，妨碍我们工作？”

En: What I'm proposing is -- we save acrobatic tricks as a last resort. After we've tried *everything* else.
Zh: “我的建议是——把杂技当成最后手段，在尝试过其它一切方法之后。”

En: You're right. The risks are too high and there are too many.
Zh: “你说得对，风险太高又太多了。”

En: Yes. We should use acrobatics as the very last option. After we've tried *everything* normal.
Zh: “是的。我们应该把杂技当成最后手段，在尝试过一切*正常*方法之后。”

En: These have got to be the most fucking boring *mulkkupäät* I've ever seen.
Zh: “他们一定是我见过最他妈无聊的*老二*了。”

En: Go ahead, ask me more questions. You fucking *love* questions.
Zh: 来啊，再多问我一些。你真他妈*爱*问问题。

En: I feel like I've been getting a lot of Harry lately...
Zh: 我感觉自己最近真的越来越哈里了……

En: The lieutenant remains unaffected. "How?"
Zh: 警督并未受到影响。“怎么做？”

En: Where the buckle ties the rope to the branch -- that's a good spot to aim.
Zh: 把绳子绑在树枝上的滑扣那里——是个不错的瞄准点。

En: The lieutenant is undecided. On one hand he *wants* to shoot some gun -- on the other, it's an awfully stupid idea.
Zh: 警督犹豫不决，一方面他确实*想*开枪——另一方面，这其实是个非常愚蠢的主意。

En: (Point to it.) "There. The buckle holds the belt together."
Zh: （指向它。）“那里。绑带是用滑扣系在一起的。”

En: I don't know, just, you know -- shoot up there maybe? (Point towards the branch.)
Zh: “我也不知道，只是，你也明白——要不对着它开几枪吧？”（指向树枝。）

En: Just shoot the belt, the bullet will break it.
Zh: “对绑带开枪就行了，子弹会打断它的。”

En: Where? He corrects his glasses. "Ah, yes. I see. If the shot hits that there *might* be a chance to release the belt..."
Zh: “对着哪里？”他调整着自己的眼镜。“啊，是的，我明白了。如果子弹击中了那儿，就有*一定*几率松开绑带……”

En: It absolutely will not, officer. That's not how physics work. It will maybe cut *one* thread loose.
Zh: “这是绝对不可能的，警官。那并不符合物理学的原理，子弹*可能*勉强打断*一根*线的几率太低了。”

En: Yes... He corrects his glasses. "The buckle -- where it ties the cargo belt to the tree. If the shot hits that then there might be a small chance to release the belt..."
Zh: “是的……”他调整着自己的眼镜。“滑扣——它把运货带系在了树上。如果子弹击中了那儿，就有很小的几率松开绑带……

En: Yeah, now we're talkin'. Entertain the Cuno with some shit.
Zh: “没错，这样才对嘛。来给坤诺找点乐子吧。”

En: They'll miss. The pigs will miss, Cuno!
Zh: “他们打不中的。猪猡们是瞄不准的，坤诺！”

En: I don't know how. Maybe it's better not to... (Back off.)
Zh: “我不知道怎么做。也许还是不要……”（退后。）

En: What else can we do?
Zh: “我们还能怎么做？”

En: Take the shot, lieutenant -- what's the worst thing that could happen?
Zh: “开枪吧，警督——最糟的结果会是如何呢？”

En: Actually -- don't. It has *bad idea* written all over it.
Zh: “说实话——不要这么做。它浑身上下都写满了*坏主意*几个大字。”

En: Wait. Let *me* try.
Zh: “等等，让*我*试试。”

En: Yeah! The enthusiasm is unrestrained. "Bang bang time, pigs! Shoot his head off!"
Zh: “好啊！”他掩藏不住那股兴奋了。“枪击时间到了，猪猡们！射穿他的脑袋！”

En: The lieutenant's brow is furrowed. He appears too deep in concentration to even notice what you said.
Zh: 那个男人皱着眉头，他似乎太过于专注了，根本没注意你说了什么。

En: I'll blow his head off.
Zh: “我会射穿他的头。”

En: Say nothing -- let him choose.
Zh: 沉默不语——让他选择。

En: Take it! TAKE THE SHOT!
Zh: “开枪吧！快开枪！”

En: Yeah, take the shot, Cuno wants some of that shit.
Zh: “是啊，开枪吧，坤诺很期待哦！”

En: Silence. With his elbows sharp, the lieutenant unzips his jacket...
Zh: 沉默。他抬起手肘，拉开了皮夹克的拉链……

En: ...and produces a lightweight firearm. He drops a paper cartridge in the barrel, separates the scouring stick and gives the cartridge five tucks.
Zh: ……然后掏出了一把轻型武器。他把一卷火药纸扔进枪筒中，把擦洗棒拆开，对着火药纸捅了五下。

En: That's a Kiejl A9/90 Armistice mass-produced muzzleloader. Ascetic, frugal, one of the most common firearms in the world.
Zh: 那是一把停战前大量生产的基耶尔A9/90前膛枪。朴素无华，是世界上最常见的武器之一。

En: He then steps back and assumes the Falostesse position, taking aim. The corner of his eye twitches -- his finger is on the trigger...
Zh: 接着他后退一步，摆出一副法罗塞特斯的姿态，瞄准了目标。他的眼角抽搐了一下——手指移向了扳机……

En: Shoot that motherfucker down.
Zh: “把那个狗杂种射下来。”

En: Easy does it, partner.
Zh: “放轻松，兄弟。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: He's gonna fucking mi...
Zh: “他打算……”

En: The kid's voice is drowned in a shrill blast that echoes off the walls of the surrounding tenements. A cloud of smoke slowly parts in the air, as the lieutenant steps back and says to himself...
Zh: 孩子的声音被淹没在刺耳的爆裂声中，回荡在周围公寓的墙壁上。一团烟雾在空中慢慢散开，警督退后几步，自言自语道……

En: God damn it.
Zh: “真该死。”

En: He feels bad about it. About his eyes mostly, just having bad eyesight -- probably from a young age. Whatever you do, do not *console* him.
Zh: 他感到很难过，主要是因为他的视力不好——大概从很小的时候就这样了。无论你做什么，都不要试图*安慰*他。

En: A lot of things were wrong with that shot: the Falostesse was the wrong choice, his shoulders were raised, but above all -- he cannot trust his eyesight.
Zh: 那一枪有很多地方出了问题：选择法罗塞特斯式是错误的，他的肩膀抬得太高，但最重要的是——他无法相信自己的视力。

En: He feels bad about it. About his eyes mostly, just having bad eyesight -- probably from a young age. Whatever you do, do not *console* him.
Zh: 他感到很难过，主要是因为他的视力不好——大概从很小的时候就这样了。无论你做什么，都不要试图*安慰*他。

En: FUCKING IDIOT! Mulkkupää asshole!
Zh: “该死的白痴！混蛋！”

En: Cuno could've hit it easy, but then -- Cuno's not fucking handicapped, is he?
Zh: “坤诺就可以轻松打中，不过——坤诺并不是残疾人吧？”

En: It's okay, man.
Zh: “没关系的，兄弟。”

En: Can I have your gun? I should try but I don't want to use mine.
Zh: “能把你的枪给我吗？我也该来试试，但我不想用自己的枪。”

En: I can try to shoot him down myself.
Zh: “我可以自己试着把他打下来。”

En: God dammit, get your shit together!
Zh: “该死的，你认真一点！”

En: Try again, maybe?
Zh: “要不，再试一次吧？”

En: Cuno's sorry too. Cuno feels sorry for the binoclard.
Zh: “坤诺也很难过，坤诺真为那个小四眼感到难过。”

En: OH MY GOD, that was so many types of wrong -- who taught four-eyes to shoot?! Whip up a gruelling training regiment for him RIGHT NOW. Beat THE MAN into him, go go go!
Zh: 天呐，真是大错特错——是谁教那个四眼仔射击的？赶快替他报名训练班吧！激发出他的男子气概，快快快！

En: What THE HELL is wrong with you, Sally!? You need to square your shoulders!
Zh: “你这是什么毛病，你得挺直肩膀！”

En: I think you have your sports mixed up there, officer, squaring my shoulders has nothing to do with this.
Zh: “我想你是将射击和其它的运动弄混了，警官，我的肩膀是否挺直与此毫无关系。”

En: Bitches fighting.
Zh: “菜鸡互啄。”

En: The lieutenant doesn't say a word, just looks at the gun in his hand.
Zh: 警督一句话也没说，只是看着自己手中的枪。

En: No, we're lucky as it is. We didn't break anything -- and the victim remains uncompromised. He looks around, at the windows overlooking the yard.
Zh: “恰恰相反，我们很幸运。我们没有打坏任何东西——被害人也没有受到伤害。”他环顾四周，看向俯瞰院子的窗户。

En: Any more mistakes could put us in an unfortunate position with the locals. We have eyes on us. I didn't do us any favours with that.
Zh: “再犯一点错误，都会让我们在当地人面前处境不利，有很多双眼睛在盯着我们呢。所幸我没有火上浇油。”

En: Remain collegial, officer. It was an honest mistake -- and I will not repeat it.
Zh: “学术一点，警官。这只是个无心之失——我不会再犯了。”

En: This had better not be another *demonstration*... He pauses, then shrugs and proceeds to load the pistolette:
Zh: “这最好不是又一次*演习*……”他停顿了一下，耸了耸肩，继续装填着手枪：

En: It's bad as it is -- us shooting firearms like punks... He pauses, then shrugs and proceeds to load the pistolette...
Zh: “已经够糟糕的了——我们像混混一样开枪……”他停顿了一下，耸了耸肩，继续装填着手枪……

En: But... what *else* can we do?
Zh: “可是……我们*还能*做什么呢？”

En: What now? (Conclude.)
Zh: “现在怎么办？”（总结。）

En: I have to say, it's beginning to look unlikely we can get him down without *assistance*.
Zh: “我不得不说，看来我们不太有可能在没有*帮助*的情况下把他放下来了。”

En: This is the sorriest pair of pigs Cuno's ever seen.
Zh: “这是坤诺见过的最可怜的一对猪猡了。”

En: They only have ONE GUN!
Zh: “他们只有一把枪！”

En: Take the gun.
Zh: 拿起枪。

En: The cold piece of bakelite and gunmetal is surprisingly light. Your finger fits right through the guard, instinctively resting on the trigger.
Zh: 这块冰冷的胶木与炮铜真是出奇的轻盈。你的手指刚好穿过了保险栓，本能地搭在了扳机上。

En: You've held this -- an A9 Armistice -- before. At some point it probably used to be your choice of firearm. It still feels comfortable, like you never laid it down.
Zh: 你摸过它——P9止战者——很早之前。某种程度上它很可能曾经是你的首选武器，它的触感仍然很舒服，就像你从未将它放下来一样。

En: The fuck are you waiting for, Cuno?! Tell him to shoot himself in the mouth!
Zh: “你他妈在等什么呢，坤诺？！告诉他直接饮弹自尽算了！”

En: Cuno is silent. Aggression gathers in the air, the trigger feels delicate and ready to break under your finger.
Zh: 坤诺陷入了沉默。剑拔弩张的氛围在空气中凝结，脆弱的扳机随时都有可能在你的手指下折断。

En: Yeah, take it you fucking *banaanipoika*. Take it and shoot yourself in the mouth!
Zh: “对呀，动作快点你这该死的*基佬*。拿起来给自己的嘴巴来一枪吧！”

En: Feel the weight first.
Zh: 先感受重量。

En: Point the gun at the belt.
Zh: 把枪口对准绑带。

En: Give the gun back to Lieutenant Kitsuragi. (Don't shoot.)
Zh: 把枪还给曷城警督。（不要开枪。）

En: The buckle comes into focus in your sights. You stand with your feet planted firmly in the ground and your left hand supporting your gun arm...
Zh: 你的眼神聚焦在滑扣上，你的双脚坚定地踩在地面上，左手支撑着持枪的手臂……

En: Why don't you just shoot yourself in your f****t mouth? she hisses. "At least you won't miss."
Zh: “为什么不朝着自己该死的嘴巴开枪呢，”她发出嘘声。“至少这样你不会失手了。”

En: Say: "Shut up."
Zh: 说：“闭嘴。”

En: Pull the trigger.
Zh: 扣下扳机。

En: Responded to provocation.
Zh: 回应挑衅。

En: Branch movement analyzed.
Zh: 分析树枝的运动轨迹。

En: Breathing slowed.
Zh: 放缓呼吸。

En: Close your left eye first.
Zh: 首先闭上左眼。

En: Your field of view narrows. The branch slowly moves, becoming entirely two-dimensional. The metal buckle glimmers, catching the noon light as the corpse slowly rotates.
Zh: 你的视野变狭窄了。树枝轻轻颤动着，完全变成了二维的模样。随着尸体的缓慢旋转，金属滑扣捕捉到正午的阳光，从而闪烁着微光。

En: Or what? You gonna *fuck* me? Her voice is almost a whisper now: "You wanna fuck me, pig? Is that what this is about?"
Zh: “否则怎样？你要来*搞*我吗？”她的声音几乎变成了耳语：“你想要搞我吗，猪猡？都是因为这个吗？”

En: Point the gun at Cunoesse.
Zh: 把枪口对准坤妮斯。

En: Look, he's crying! You gonna cry now, fucking *fägäri*?
Zh: “看啊，他要哭了！你现在准备哭了吗，该死的*基佬*？”

En: Do it, f****t! SET ME FREE!
Zh: “来啊，混蛋！让我解脱吧！”

En: F****T! She's ripping her blazer open to bare her chest. "DO IT!"
Zh: “混蛋！”她将上衣扯开，露出胸口。“来啊！”

En: You feel as though it would be *dangerous* to set this creature free to roam about the cosmos.
Zh: 你感觉让这样一种生物解脱，任她遨游在宇宙中非常*危险*。

En: Don't. You feel the lieutenant's hand on your shoulder.
Zh: “住手。”你感到警督的手搭在了你的肩膀上。

En: Point the gun at the belt.
Zh: 把枪口对准绑带。

En: Pull the trigger.
Zh: 扣下扳机。

En: Hesitated for a moment.
Zh: 犹豫片刻。

En: Fuck you little shit!
Zh: “去死吧小杂种！”

En: Put it right here *mulkku*, she points to her heart. "RIGHT HERE!"
Zh: “就对着这里*混账*，”她指向自己的心脏。“对准了！”

En: Intense shit, copper.
Zh: “很刺激嘛，条子。”

En: I agree. The lieutenant holsters the weapon with a quick move. "Enough gunslinging for today."
Zh: “我同意。”警督迅速把枪套了起来。“今天的枪战到此为止。”

En: F****t waved his gun. She pants, breathless with excitement. "It wasn't intense, it was pathetic."
Zh: “混蛋在挥枪呢。”她兴奋地气喘吁吁。“那不叫刺激，是悲哀。”

En: The lieutenant takes the gun carefully from you and holsters it. His eyes are narrow and he doesn't comment on your antics.
Zh: 警督小心翼翼地从你手中取过枪并套了起来，他眯着眼睛，对你滑稽的行为并无任何评价。

En: Fucking pussy didn't shoot shit, did he? Didn't shoot me, didn't shoot himself in the mouth...
Zh: “该死的懦夫根本就没开枪，是不是？没有朝我开枪，也没有朝自己的嘴巴……”

En: Didn't shoot *me*. There is sincere disappointment on her face. This kid was expecting *pandemonium* and release from her mortal coil.
Zh: “没有朝*我*开枪。”她的脸上流露着发自内心的失望，那孩子正期待着一场*大混战*，并让她从尘世的烦恼中解脱出来。

En: The slow movement of the branch in the wind and your shoulders directing the gun sync up, dancing hypnotically.
Zh: 树枝随风摇曳，你的肩膀引着枪口随之转动，仿佛被催眠了似的舞动着。

En: As you wait, your breathing slows down. Some mosquito-like organism buzzes in your peripheral:
Zh: 等待的时间里，你的呼吸放缓了。某些蚊子似的小生物在你身边嗡嗡作响：

En: Can I have your gun? I should try, but I don't want to use mine.
Zh: “能把枪给我吗？我也该来试试，但我不想用自己的枪。

En: Can I have the gun? I should try.
Zh: “能把枪给我吗？我应该试试。”

En: Go ahead, I'm not stopping you. Just don't lose it. The piece shines in his outstretched hand.
Zh: “来吧，我不会阻止你的，别弄丢就好了。”手枪在他伸出的手中闪闪发光。

En: Go ahead, I'm not stopping you. Just don't lose it. The Armistice A9 shines in his outstretched hand.
Zh: “来吧，我不会阻止你的，别弄丢就好了。”止战者P9在他伸出的手中闪闪发光。

En: It's bad as it is -- us shooting firearms like punks here... He pauses, then shrugs: "Go ahead, I'm not stopping you. Just don't hit the victim."
Zh: “已经够糟糕的了——我们像混混一样开枪……”他停顿了一下，耸了耸肩：“来吧，我不会阻止你的，别击中被害人就好。”

En: I'm gonna need your gun for this.
Zh: “我需要你的枪。”

En: This is the sorriest pair of pigs Cuno's ever seen.
Zh: “这是坤诺见过的最可悲的一对猪猡了。”

En: They only have ONE GUN!
Zh: “他们只有一把枪！”

En: Officer, why *do* we only have one gun? he asks -- carefully loading his pistolette.
Zh: “警官，为什么我们只*有*一把枪？”他问道——同时小心翼翼地装填好手枪。

En: Remember when I told you I didn't have my badge or uniform with me when I woke up -- I didn't have my gun either.
Zh: “还记得我告诉过你，在我醒来的时候警徽和警服都不在身边了吗——我的配枪也不见了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: I think someone has stolen it from me, stolen my gun. Gun is stolen.
Zh: “我应该是被人偷了，偷了我的枪，我的枪被偷了。”

En: That is even *more* unfortunate than the badge. You need to contact your station about it as soon as possible. The piece shines in his outstretched hand. "Try not to lose this one, please."
Zh: “这甚至比警徽*更加*不幸了，你必须尽快跟你的警局联系。”手枪在他伸出的手中闪闪发光。“请务必不要把它也弄丢了。”

En: The lieutenant follows suit. He finishes loading the gun in silence, then hands it to you.
Zh: 金紧随其后。他沉默地把枪装好，然后递给了你。

En: *Extremely* unfortunate. You need to contact your station about it as soon as possible. The piece shines in his outstretched hand. "Try not to lose this one, please."
Zh: “*极其*不幸，你必须尽快跟你的警局联系。”手枪在他伸出的手中闪闪发光。“请务必不要把它也弄丢了。”

En: This isn't mere boundary pushing. There is a true suicidal rage in the kid as she's provoking you.
Zh: 这不仅是在挑战底线。当那孩子在挑衅你时，她真的有种想要自毁般的愤世嫉俗。

En: With your hand numb from the recoil, you look at the body slump down. For a moment the man appears to *kneel* in front of you.
Zh: 当你的手因为后坐力而麻木时，你眼看着尸体跌落下来。有那么一瞬间，那个男人似乎*跪*在了你面前。

En: You missed the belt, but hit the corpse straight in the chest. Bits of ribcage protrude from the skin. No blood, only a murky sludge dripping down his belly. The sudden stink makes your eyes water.
Zh: 你没击中绑带，反而正中了尸体的胸口。部分胸腔凸了出来，没有血液，只有浑浊的粘液顺着他的肚子滴落下来。突如其来的恶臭让你的眼睛充盈着泪水。

En: Looking straight at you. Helpless, trapped within itself.
Zh: 直直地盯着你，无助地困在这幅躯壳中。

En: Who killed you?
Zh: 是谁杀了你？

En: Communism.
Zh: 康米主义。

En: It takes a millisecond for the association to flash within your cortex. You have no idea *where* it's coming from -- only that it's *right*.
Zh: 你花了一毫秒的时间才让这个联想闪现在你的大脑皮层中。你完全不知道它是从哪里冒出来的——只知道它是*正确*的。

En: Then the *rigor* in his muscles gives up, and he smashes sideways into the spring mud, letting out a horrid stench.
Zh: 然后他的肌肉不再*僵硬*，便侧身摔倒在春天的泥土上，散发出一股可怕的恶臭。

En: Turn away.
Zh: 避开。

En: You've been policed.
Zh: “你被执法了。”

En: RCM, baby! RCM!
Zh: “RCM，宝贝！RCM！”

En: How did I *do* that?
Zh: “我是如何*做到*的？”

En: Are you sure we've finished the preliminary examination of the cadaver? We might miss some of these things once he's down. He stops to think, then checks his notes.
Zh: 他停下来思考，然后检查着自己的笔记。“你确定我们已经完成了对尸体的初步检验了吗？一旦他被放下来，我们可能会错过一些东西。”

En: We've been thorough. Do you have a plan for getting him down? (Proceed.)
Zh: “已经彻底检查过了。你有想过该如何放他下来吗？”（继续。）

En: Step back and have another look first.
Zh: 后退一步，再看一眼。

En: Who's laughing now, you little shit!?
Zh: “是谁笑到最后了，你这个小混蛋！？”

En: Oh my god, he's gonna fucking cry, I knew it! What a *mulkku*...
Zh: “天呐，他就要哭了，我就知道！真是个*混账*……”

En: Nice shot, though. Fucked him up good...
Zh: “好枪法！把他弄得一团糟……”

En: There's something wrong with your weapon, lieutenant. It keeps missing.
Zh: “你的武器有问题，警督，一直打不中。”

En: I don't wanna do this anymore, this is boring.
Zh: “我不想再继续了，这可太无聊了。”

En: Don't provoke her. The lieutenant takes his sidearm from you, then holsters it.
Zh: “别惹她。”警督从你手里夺过他的枪，然后把枪套上。

En: Shot him in the chest. The creature is still in disbelief. "What a FUCKING IDIOT."
Zh: “朝他的胸口开枪。”这个生物仍处于怀疑之中。“真是个白痴。”

En: Actually it was f****ted. Cuno just said that because he felt sorry for you, pig. It's not your fault you can't shoot -- it's your pig-hands.
Zh: “实际上你搞砸了。坤诺这么说只是因为他在可怜你，猪猡。不会开枪并不是你的错——都是因为你的猪蹄。”

En: Are you? Because it sort of feels like you *are*. Please don't cry.
Zh: 是吗？因为感觉上你*确实*如此，千万别哭啊。

En: Start crying -- you're sensitive.
Zh: 开始哭泣——你很敏感。

En: It feels dry. Nothing comes out of you as you stretch and bend your face into a crying shape. You're too furious to cry, apparently.
Zh: 有些干涩。即使将自己的脸扭曲成哭泣的模样，也没有一滴泪水落下。显然，你气得都哭不出来了。

En: I can't even cry... (Throw the gun in the mud.)
Zh: “我甚至都哭不出来……”（把枪扔到地上。）

En: Try harder. Try *really* hard to cry.
Zh: 再努力些，*更加*努力哭出来。

En: What did he say?
Zh: “他说什么？”

En: The f****t can't even cry.
Zh: “那个笨蛋甚至哭不出来。”

En: The lieutenant picks up his gun and then holsters it. "In the name of god, please get a hang of yourself."
Zh: 警督捡起他的枪，然后塞进了枪套。“看在老天的份上，请你冷静一点。”

En: He's definitely not impressed by the way you're handling it, but he doesn't hold it against you either. He's supportive like that.
Zh: 他明显不喜欢你处理问题的方式，但他也不会因此而责备你。他就是如此的支持你。

En: The lieutenant watches you jerk some tears, your shoulders shaking and your eyes dry. The gun begins to slip from your fingers.
Zh: 警督眼看着你挤出了几滴泪水，你的肩膀颤抖着，眼睛却很干涩。手枪渐渐从你的手中滑落。

En: *Jumalauta*! The f****t can't even cry.
Zh: “*该死*！那个混账甚至哭不出来。”

En: (Give the gun back to Kim.) "What now?"
Zh: （把枪还给金。）“然后呢？”

En: You hear that, Sally? They're *laughing* at you. Are you gonna let it stand? Are you, Sally? Or are you gonna CRY LIKE A BEAST?! Like a BEAAAST!
Zh: 你听到了吗，萨莉？他们在*嘲笑*你呢。你就这么无动于衷吗？是这样吗，萨莉？还是你会像头畜生一样哭鼻子？像畜生一样！

En: What was that?
Zh: 那是什么？

En: I'll do it, coach!
Zh: 我会的，教练！

En: Don't take coach Physical Instrument's advice. It's bad advice.
Zh: 不要听强身健体教练的建议，那是个坏主意。

En: *DO IT!*
Zh: *快啊！*

En: The lieutenant gently picks his gun from your hand and holsters it.
Zh: 警督轻柔地从你手中拿过枪，塞进了枪套中。

En: A tiny tear forms on your duct. The corpse stinks from the blackened bullet hole in his chest. A single drop of decay liquid runs out of it, then down his stomach...
Zh: 胸腔的导管上形成了一道裂痕。从尸体胸部发黑的弹孔中散发出阵阵恶臭，一滴腐烂的粘液从里面流出，然后从他的腹部顺流而下……

En: The lieutenant gently picks his gun from your hand and holsters it.
Zh: 警督轻柔地从你手中拿过枪，塞进了枪套中。

En: We try something else.
Zh: “我们试试别的方法。”

En: The *Armistice* is sufficiently precise, officer. Especially at close range. He takes his sidearm from you and holsters it.
Zh: “这把*止战者*非常精准，警官，尤其是近距离射击。”他从你手里取过手枪塞进了枪套中。

En: It's not the gun's fault you can't shoot. It's your pig-hands.
Zh: “你瞄不准可不是枪的错，都是因为你的猪蹄。”

En: Pigs don't have hands. They have, like, fucking hoofs or something.
Zh: “猪才没有手呢。它们只有，好像，蹄子之类的玩意吧。”

En: Cuno has hands. He displays his little fingers, they're pink. "Cuno can shoot that shit down for you."
Zh: “坤诺有手呀。”他展示着自己幼嫩的粉色手指。“坤诺可以帮你把那玩意射下来。”

En: No, Cuno. If I can't shoot him down, no one will.
Zh: “不，坤诺。如果我不能把他射下来，那就没有人可以。”

En: Lieutenant, we should entrust Cuno with your service weapon. He says he can shoot it down.
Zh: “警督，我们可以放心把你的武器交给坤诺。他说他可以把尸体射下来。”

En: That's preposterous. We're not giving you the gun, kid.
Zh: “这真是太荒谬了。我们不会把枪给你的，孩子。”

En: The lieutenant does not dignify that with an answer. He snaps the button on his holster and says...
Zh: 那个男人沉默以对，他啪的一声按下枪套上的按钮说……

En: Cop's bitter cos' he fucked that corpse up, Cuno. What a fucking *kyrpelö*.
Zh: “警察们正因为他们破坏了那具尸体而蛋疼着呢，坤诺。真是一堆该死的*蠢蛋*。”

En: *"Mulkku*, *vittupää*? What is that?! What the hell kind of words are those?!"
Zh: *“混账*，*混蛋*？那都是什么？！是什么鬼话？！”

En: It's the song of my people, asshole!
Zh: “是我们族人的歌，混蛋！”

En: I agree. Police work is overrated. It is trying and stressful. However, it is still our job to get the dead body down from the tree. He takes his sidearm from you and holsters it.
Zh: “我同意，警察的工作确实被高估了，更像是一种压力和考验。然而，把尸体从树上弄下来仍旧是我们的职责。”他从你手里取过手枪塞进了枪套中。

En: Oh my god, what a fucking boring cop.
Zh: “天呐，真是个无聊透顶的警察。”

En: That's *preposterous*, she whines mockingly. "What are you, a fucking earl?"
Zh: “真是*荒谬*，”她嬉笑着抱怨，”你是什么东西，一位伯爵吗？”

En: He doesn't mean it in earnest. It's a cruel jest. He's going to say as much too, just you wait:
Zh: 他并非真心的，只是个残忍的玩笑。他还会这么说的，你等着瞧吧：

En: Can you reload it, please? I need another shot.
Zh: “能请你再填上些弹药吗？我得再来一枪。”

En: Ask for another shot. You'll get it with the next one, the god damn light reflected off some window, surely...
Zh: 请求再开一枪。下一枪一定能击中的，肯定是那该死的窗户反光干扰了你……

En: The cadaver is already compromised bad enough. We don't have to make it *worse*.
Zh: “尸体已经被损坏得够严重了，我们没必要再*火上浇油*了。”

En: It came out wrong, you made it sound like you're some sort of roulette addict looking for a fix, ask again, more controlled this time:
Zh: 语气有点问题，听上去你就好像个沉迷轮盘的赌徒。再问一遍吧，这次克制一些：

En: Please, Kim.
Zh: “拜托了，金。”

En: Swallow defeat.
Zh: 咽下失败。

En: There is silence.
Zh: 陷入沉默。

En: *Vittupää* wants to fuck up again, bad.
Zh: “*混蛋*又想把事情搞砸了。”

En: Feels like shit man, how did this happen? Hit him straight in the chest... how horrible, you're so sorry.
Zh: 感觉糟透了伙计，怎么会这样呢？正中他的胸口……太可怕了，你真的很抱歉。

En: We still need to get him down *somehow*. His tone is growing tired now -- the stench makes him turn away from the corpse.
Zh: “我们还是得想*办法*把他弄下来。”他的语气愈发疲惫——-那股恶臭使他转身背对着尸体。

En: Can't someone else do it?
Zh: “其他人不能做吗？”

En: I don't really want to pull his head off.
Zh: 我真的不想把他的脑袋扯下来。

En: Just pull his rotting head off. The meat's cooked anyway, give the boot another yank and it's done.
Zh: 干脆把他腐烂的脑袋扯下来吧。反正他整个人都烂透了，再用力拉一把靴子，万事搞定。

En: How do you plan to get him down then? With social sensibility? Are you going to *educate* him down?
Zh: 那你打算怎么把他弄下来？靠他的社会责任感？你是打算*感化*他下来吗？

En: Someone else? He pauses. "You mean like... *the police*?"
Zh: “其他人？”他顿了顿。“你是指……*警察*吗？”

En: You hear an explosion within the weapon in your hands. The branches of the pinetree behind the girl shake ever so lightly. Your ears are still ringing...
Zh: 你听到手中武器内部的爆裂声，女孩身后的松树枝桠轻轻摇晃着，你的耳朵还在嗡嗡作响……

En: A plume of smoke has erupted from the barrel. The little monstrosity stands in the cloud. Her face is petrified, then it begins to twist, demonically.
Zh: 一股烟从枪筒中冒出来。那个小怪物站在烟雾之中，她的表情石化了，然后如恶魔一般，开始扭曲。

En: She is disappointed, *trapped*, entangled in this world -- all because of the continuously *appalling* marksmanship of law officials.
Zh: 她很失望，被*困住*，纠缠在这个世界上——皆是因为执法人员不间断的令人震惊的枪法。

En: YOU FUCKING F****TS! she wails. "LEARN TO SHOOT."
Zh: “你真他妈是个混蛋！”她大哭了起来，“去学学射击吧！”

En: Whoah, C... Cuno's voice is low. "Pig tried to shoot you. You're *alive*."
Zh: “哇哦，C……”坤诺的声音很低沉。“那头猪想开枪射你。你还*活着*。”

En: Yes, Cuno, I CAN SEE THAT.
Zh: “是的，坤诺，我发现了。”

En: You sense nylon moving somewhere to your left. There's motion in your peripherals, then it's already too late...
Zh: 你感到尼龙在向你的左边移动，你的身侧有些动静，但一切已经太迟了……

En: The gun has left your hand, still numb from the recoil. The lieutenant holsters it under his arm.
Zh: 枪支脱离了你的手，尚处于后坐力带来的麻木之中。警督把枪套上后夹在了腋下。

En: Fucking die! (Throw the empty gun at her.)
Zh: “去死吧！”（把空枪扔向她。）

En: Kim, I need you to do your reload-thing, the beast is still alive.
Zh: “金，我需要你继续装填弹药，那头野兽还活着。”

En: Oh my god, why did I just try to shoot a kid?
Zh: “天呐，为什么我会试图向孩子开枪？”

En: My liege, it may be possible to *spin* this as some sort of, uhm... pedagogical exercise.
Zh: 大人，或许可以把它*归结*为某种，呃……教育活动。

En: But I wasn't trying to teach her a lesson. I was trying to *kill* her.
Zh: 但我不是想给她一个教训，我是想杀了她。

En: Well, then maybe you shouldn't have missed. This is merely a suggestion. If you want to be a *failed* child murderer, go with that.
Zh: 好吧，那你就不应该失手了。这只是一个建议。如果你想成为一个*失败的*儿童杀手，那就这样吧。

En: Yes, of course -- attempted child murder, that's not *me*.
Zh: 是的，当然——谋杀儿童未遂，那可不像*我*。

En: Not you at all, no-no-no!
Zh: 根本不像你，不-不-不！

En: I was merely teaching her a lesson, of course.
Zh: “我只是想给她一个教训，当然。”

En: And what kind of lesson is that? Just like that the gun has left your hand. The lieutenant holsters it.
Zh: 枪支就这样离开了你的手中，警督把枪套上。“具体是什么样的教训呢？”

En: You sense nylon moving somewhere to your left. There's motion in your peripherals, then it's already too late...
Zh: 你感到尼龙在向你的左边移动，你的身侧有些动静，但一切已经太迟了……

En: That he's a fucking f****t who misses.
Zh: “他就是个瞄不准的傻逼。”

En: I think you're underestimating the gravity of this situation, officer. We need to take a breather from this scene. He points at you. "Now."
Zh: “我想你是低估了目前形势的严重性，警官。我们需要缓口气。”他指着你。“现在就要。”

En: What are you Kim, a martial artist? Where'd the gun go?
Zh: “你以为自己是谁，金，武术家吗？枪去哪儿了。”

En: Yeah, a lesson about respecting the law.
Zh: “是啊，一个关于要尊重法律的教训。”

En: It was merely a jest. A light-hearted jab!
Zh: “这只是个玩笑，不痛不痒的。”

En: That is not important right now.
Zh: “现在已经不重要了。”

En: Can't the boys from Processing take care of this?
Zh: “不能让分析部门的孩子们接手吗？”

En: You have a point there.
Zh: “你说得有道理。”

En: What was that about *Processing*, then -- weren't they supposed to take care of the *boots*? Why don't they help?
Zh: 那么，*分析部门*是什么意思呢——他们不应该去检验那双*靴子*吗？为什么不帮忙？

En: No.
Zh: “不行。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: Okay then.
Zh: “那好吧。”

En: Think of the *boys from Processing* as murderers. Only instead of people they murder crime scenes. Processing is a wrecking crew. They know how to commission off items and how to work the incinerator in the morgue.
Zh: “就把*分析部门的小伙子*当成是杀人犯吧，不过他们的谋杀对象不是人类而是犯罪现场而已。分析部门就是拆迁队，只会指手画脚，或是在停尸房焚烧炉里工作。”

En: I mean someone who's *below* detective.
Zh: “我是指警探的*手下*。”

En: Yes, exactly -- call the police.
Zh: “是的，没错——报警吧。”

En: Someone like a paid garbage man? Or a cleaning crew? I have bad news for you -- that *is* a detective.
Zh: “比如收费的垃圾处理人员？还是一支清洁团队？我有个坏消息要告诉你——那*就是*警探的工作。”

En: I know it's hard. But I assure you -- the others won't come to help us. And we have a *growing* sanitary concern here. We need to get him down, fast.
Zh: “我知道这很困难。但我向你保证——不会有其他人过来帮忙的。而且这里的卫生问题*愈发*严重了，我们必须尽快把他弄下来。”

En: Someone already did. You know who came? We did. Because we have made bad life decisions.
Zh: “已经有人这么做了。你知道是谁过来了吗？就是我们，因为我们都做了错误的人生决定。”

En: Sadly, yes. The whole RCM is out there right now, doing the exact same thing we are. Are we in a rush to help them? Not with this on our hands... He covers his nose.
Zh: “很不幸，是的。整个RCM全体出动了，做着跟我们一样的事情。我们要急着去帮助他们吗？至少在处理完手上的案子前不行……”他捂住了自己的鼻子。

En: The gun has left your hand, still numb from the recoil. The lieutenant holsters it under his arm.
Zh: 枪支脱离了你的手，尚处于后坐力带来的麻木之中。警督把枪套上后夹在了腋下。

En: You sense nylon moving somewhere to your left. There's motion in your peripherals, then it's already too late...
Zh: 你感到尼龙在向你的左边移动，你的身侧有些动静，但一切已经太迟了……

En: At least you understand the gravity of the situation, detective. We need to take a breather from this scene. He points at you. "Now."
Zh: “至少你已经明白了目前形势的严重性，警官。我们得缓口气。”他指着你。“现在就要。”

En: Which was it that killed you, then -- love or communism?
Zh: 是什么杀了你——爱还是康米主义？

En: Huh?
Zh: 哈？

En: You said love killed you, but when you fell down you said it was communism.
Zh: 你说过是爱杀了你，但当你倒下的时候，又说是康米主义的错。

En: You're misquoting me, Roonie. I said communism killed me. Love *DID ME IN*.
Zh: 你误解我了，罗尼。我的意思是康米主义杀了我，而爱情使我*沦落至此*。

En: Ace's high! The lieutenant raises his right hand, waiting for you to slap it.
Zh: “高击掌！”警督举起右手，等着你拍过来

En: By being a damn good shot -- Ace's High! The lieutenant raises his right hand, waiting for you to slap it.
Zh: “好枪法——高击掌！”警督举起右手，等着你拍过来。

En: Fuck you, *kyrpäle*! Despite her words, her tone seems celebratory.
Zh: “去死吧，*蠢蛋*！”忽略内容，她的语气仿佛在庆祝似的。

En: Slap it.
Zh: 击掌。

En: Leave him hanging.
Zh: 让他举着手。

En: Okay, he mumbles for a second, then lowers his hand. "Maybe it's not customary in the 41st to perform an Ace's High. In the 57th we've carried over the custom from the Revolution. Maybe it's bad luck... anyway."
Zh: “好吧，”他咕哝了片刻，便放下了手。“可能高击掌不是41辖区的习惯吧，我们57辖区沿袭了革命时期的传统。不过……这会招来厄运也说不定。”

En: You *left him hanging* on an Ace's High?! What is wrong with you, you sharpshooting asshole?
Zh: 你就让他的手*空等在那里*？你是有什么毛病吗，神枪手？

En: The lieutenant takes a little hop to perform the customary salutation. Your palm hurts from the slap. It's precise and down to the point.
Zh: 警督微微跃起，按照惯例行了个礼。你的手掌被扇得生疼，那是非常精准且直中要害的一击。

En: You could add an Ace's Low to it, if you like. By turning your back after the High and waiting for another.
Zh: 如果你愿意，完全可以加上一次低击掌。只要在完成击掌后转过身去，等待下一次击掌就可以了。

En: The Ace's High -- a custom invented by the aerostatic brigades during the Revolution -- is used to celebrate success in Revachol. Especially in sports. The gesture has spread across the world, despite the defeat of the revolutionaries themselves.
Zh: 高击掌——航空部队在革命期间产生的一种习俗——以此庆祝在瑞瓦肖取得的胜利，尤其是运动方面的胜利。尽管革命者已然失败，但这一姿势已经传遍了全世界。

En: Slap it and wait for an Ace's Low.
Zh: 击掌，然后等待低击掌。

En: The High arrives with a sharp slap. As you turn around there is a moment of doubt -- feels like that Low ain't gonna *connect*...
Zh: 高击掌带来了一记响亮的击掌。当你转过身去时，突然有了片刻的怀疑——感觉低击掌可能无法*相通*……

En: I knew these guys were f****ts. His voice is deeply approving.
Zh: “我就知道那些家伙是傻逼。”他的声音里充满了赞许。

En: But then it does -- and with furious precision! The lieutenant is not one to leave an Ace's Low *hanging*.
Zh: 但它确实来了——而且非常精准！警督不是那种会让低击掌*空等*的人。

En: What now?
Zh: “接下来呢？”

En: We will perform a field autopsy and determine the cause of death -- but before... excuse me. He needs to turn away from the corpse.
Zh: “我们准备进行现场尸检并确定死因——但在此之前……抱歉。”他需要背对尸体一段时间。

En: Slumped on the ground like that has not *improved* the way the corpse smells -- the fall seems to have released something deep inside it...
Zh: 像那样瘫倒在地上并没有*改善*尸体的气味——刚刚的坠落似乎释放了某些隐藏其中的东西……

En: It looks like *I* feel like taking a break from the stench. I'm sorry to interrupt the jubilations here -- just a little breather before we do the autopsy.
Zh: “看起来*我*需要从恶臭中喘口气。抱歉要打断一下欢庆——我需要在尸检前稍作休息。”

En: Wait -- field *autopsy*?!
Zh: “等等——现场*验尸*？！”

En: All right. (Give him back his gun.)
Zh: “好吧。”（把枪还给他。）

En: No, we're *not*!
Zh: “不，我们*不是*！”

En: Yeah, we're f****ts, got a problem with it?!
Zh: “对啊，我们就是傻逼，有什么不满吗？！”

En: Officer, let's keep our attention on the victim.
Zh: “警官，还是重点关注一下被害人吧。”

En: Cuno cracks with laughter -- sounds like someone strangling a seagull. It's clear he enjoys himself.
Zh: 坤诺大笑了起来——那笑声就好像有人试图勒死一只海鸥。显然他非常愉悦。

En: I knew they suck each other off.
Zh: “我就知道他们会相互吹捧。”

En: This makes getting him down much more problematic than I had assumed...
Zh: “这样一来，把他弄下来比我想象中要困难许多……”

En: The stench is getting bad. I'm sorry to interrupt the jubilations here -- I need a little breather before we do the autopsy.
Zh: “这股味道越来越难闻了，抱歉打断了你们的庆祝——我需要在尸检前稍作休息一会儿。”

En: Yes. He covers his nose. "The four phases of a murder scene. One: Investigation of the scene."
Zh: “是的。”他捂住了自己的鼻子。“犯罪现场的四个步骤。一：现场勘查。”

En: I can't say I'm satisfied with that part yet. We should search for prints maybe... He stops to look at the spring mud. "And the trash container too..."
Zh: “我对这部分不太满意。或许我们应该再调查一下足迹……”他停下来看着春泥。“还有垃圾箱……”

En: I can't say I'm *fully* satisfied with that part. We should search for prints maybe... He points to the spring mud around you.
Zh: “我对这部分不是*很*满意。或许我们应该再调查一下足迹……”他指向你周围的春泥。

En: I'm satisfied with that part. The trash container, the prints -- we've been thorough enough.
Zh: “我对这部分很满意。垃圾箱，还有脚印——我们调查得非常周密了。”

En: I can't say I'm satisfied with that part yet. I still want to know what's in that trash container... He points to it.
Zh: “我对这部分还不是很满意，我仍然很好奇那个垃圾箱里装了什么……”他指向它。
