En: Two: Initial examination of the victim. We were not exhaustive in our efforts, should have looked at the tattoos maybe? But what's done is done.
Zh: “二：对被害人进行初步检验。我们并没有竭尽全力，或许还应该调查一下他的纹身？但过去的都已经过去了。”

En: Two: Initial examination of the victim. We were *exhaustive* in our efforts there. Good job.
Zh: “二：对被害人进行初步检验。我们已经*竭尽所能*了，干得好。”

En: Two: Initial examination of the victim. We were pretty good in our efforts -- and the autopsy will provide another chance.
Zh: “二：对被害人进行初步检验。我们做得不错——之后的尸检也会提供新的可能。”

En: Three: Field autopsy. This will *not* be pleasant. Or easy. And it *will* have to be performed on the scene.
Zh: “三：现场尸检。这可*不是*什么好差事，也不简单。而且*将会*在犯罪现场进行。”

En: The fuck are they on about?
Zh: “他们在搞什么鬼？”

En: Cops are gonna cut his shit up next.
Zh: “条子们等会儿要把他切开了。”

En: Four and final: transport of the coroner's case to the district morgue. I'll do that. The lieutenant stops. "God, he stinks."
Zh: “第四步，也是最后一步：“把验尸官的材料送往本地的太平间，这个我来做。”警督顿了一下。“天呐，他可真臭。”

En: Don't we have someone else for the autopsy -- like a doctor?
Zh: “难道没有其他人能来验尸吗——医生之类的？”

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白。”

En: No. You and I are *detectives*. The honorary rank of 'detective' signifies our ability to handle *the entire incident chain*. From crime scene, to autopsy, to clean-up. We do it all.
Zh: “没有。你跟我都是*警探*。*警探*的荣誉头衔意味着我们有能力处理*整起案件*——从调查犯罪现场，到尸检，以及清理现场。全部都要自己来。”

En: Honorary rank?
Zh: “荣誉头衔？”

En: I don't think I'm a detective.
Zh: “我不觉得自己是个警探。”

En: Yes, I feel like a detective.
Zh: “是的，我感觉自己好像是个警探。”

En: Your station would not have assigned you the case otherwise. This case is *important*.
Zh: “否则你的警局不会指派你过来处理这个案子，这起案件很*重要*。”

En: An honour -- and a burden. Attached to your rank. Mine is *lieutenant-detective* -- unfortunately. I can't tell you how tired I am of this corpse...
Zh: “一份荣耀——也是一种负担，都跟你的头衔有关。我本人是一名*警督*——很不幸。我无法告诉你我是有多么的厌倦尸体了……”

En: He is growing truly tired of it, it's not merely moodiness. His tolerance limit is near.
Zh: 他是真的觉得厌烦，不仅仅是情绪的问题，他的忍耐已经快到极限了。

En: Good.
Zh: “很好。”

En: In the meantime, we should interview Evrart Claire, the leader of the Union. Harbour property was clearly used in the hanging... He points to the belt. "The harbour is just east of here and we've already found a way in."
Zh: “与此同时，我们应该去拜访艾弗拉特・克莱尔，工会主席。这场绞刑明显使用了港口的财物……”他指着绑带。“港口就在这里往东，而且我们已经找到了进去的方法。”

En: In the meantime, we should try to interview Evrart Claire, the leader of the Union. Harbour property was clearly used in the hanging... He points to the belt. "The harbour just east of here. Getting *in* might prove a challenge, though..."
Zh: “与此同时，我们应该试着去拜访艾弗拉特·克莱尔，工会主席。这场绞刑明显使用了港口的财物……”他指着绑带。“港口就在东边，如何*进入*或许是个挑战，不过……”

En:
Is this the famous *list of initial interviews*?	“这就是传说中的*初次走访名单*吗？”
0x0100000400011B64	Dialogue Text	YARD / HANGED MAN	Kim Kitsuragi	Yes..." He nods slowly. "And those were the interviewees. Let's go."
Zh: “是的……”他缓缓地点头。“然后那些就是受访者了，我们走吧。”

En: Let's go then. [Leave.]
Zh: 那走吧。[离开。]

En: I would also suggest we interview Joyce, the Wild Pines representative, but we've already done that. So -- good for us. One down, one to go.
Zh: “我建议同时也去拜访一下野松公司的代表乔，不过我们已经问过话了。所以——方便了我们。搞定了一个，还剩一个了。”

En: Yup. Seems like a lynching to me.
Zh: “是啊，对我来说确实很像私刑。”

En: Everything here seems to corroborate that assumption. But we should still get him *down* before assigning a probable cause of death.
Zh: “这里的一切似乎都证实了这一假设。但在确定可能的死亡原因之前，我们还是得先把他弄*下来*。”

En: At the autopsy, you said you have ancient mysteries.
Zh: 验尸过程中你说自己有一些古老的秘密。

En: In silence, the lieutenant gives you a meaningful glance. As if to say: *case in point*.
Zh: 沉默之中，警督意味深长地看了你一眼。好像在说：*恰当的例子*。

En: How did they even get him up there?
Zh: “他们是怎么把他弄上去的？”

En: The risk of... acrobatic failure is one we cannot take. We must not become *comedy* for the locals.
Zh: “杂技失败的……风险是我们无法承受的，我们可不能成为当地人眼中的*笑话*。”

En: The fuck have you got against comedy, pigs?
Zh: “你他妈对笑话有什么意见吗，猪猡？”

En: This is a bad time for a drink, right?
Zh: “现在可不是喝酒的好时机，对吧？”

En: Extremely.
Zh: “非常不合适。”

En: Fucking talkin' about underpants...
Zh: “他妈的说什么内裤……”

En: Clearly Cuno would like to interject something here, but there's not enough for him to hold on to.
Zh: 显然坤诺想在此刻插上几句话，可他目前也没什么可说的。

En: Try what exactly?
Zh: “具体要尝试什么呢？”

En: I wanted to see what happens.
Zh: “我想知道会发生什么。”

En: I might have wanted to experiment. You know, get an exploratory jive going.
Zh: “我有点想做个实验，你懂的，来个实验性的牛仔舞。”

En: I have no idea why. I just do things. There's no underlying reason.
Zh: “我也不知道为什么，就是这么做了，并没有什么原因。”

En: If you can't experiment, how will you learn?
Zh: 如果你不能做实验的话，又该如何学习呢？

En: I see what you mean, but even so... The lieutenant taps on the boot.
Zh: “我明白你的意思，但即便如此……”警督轻敲着靴子。

En: Run your finger over the lines.
Zh: 用手指划过线条。

En: What does this remind me of?
Zh: 这让我想起了什么呢？

En: Not interested.
Zh: 没兴趣。

En: If trees were made of porcelain, this is what their cross-sections would look like.
Zh: 如果树木是用瓷器做的，这就是它们横截面的模样。

En: I am so disappointed in you. What *are* you interested in, then?
Zh: 我对你太失望了，那你究竟对*什么*感兴趣呢？

En: The whorls are in the shape of a letter and number combination: E50.100.1000.
Zh: 纹路的形状是字母与数字的组合：E50.100.1000

En: The smooth glossy surface fractures into ever more intricate interconnections, peaking on the right sabaton, where you notice...
Zh: 光洁平滑的表面断裂成了更加错综复杂的纹路，汇聚在右脚铠靴上，你注意到……

En: Yes. Let's run it as I said.
Zh: “是的。就按照我说的查吧。”

En: Actually, I don't know why I lied. The number is: E50.100.1000.
Zh: “说实话，我不明白为什么要说谎。数字是：E50.100.1000”

En: This had better not turn into another *demonstration* of yours.
Zh: “这最好不要变成你的另一个*范例*。”

En: There's the Frittte nearby -- maybe they have some in their apothecary? If they don't... He points to the greenhouse.
Zh: “附近有家弗利多——或许它们药库里有一些？如果没有的话……”他指向了温室。

En: Hah! The clown lips on the corpse appear to smile.
Zh: 哈！尸体滑稽的嘴唇似乎扬起了微笑。

En: Didn't that young woman, the gardener, mention using salts for the smell? Or maybe they sell ammonia at the local grocery store...
Zh: “那位年轻女士，那位园丁，不是说过可以用盐来掩盖味道吗？或者在当地杂货店里有卖氨的呢？……”

En: You can feel it coming up again. The corpse twirls in the wind.
Zh: 那种感觉再次袭来，尸体在风中旋转着。

En: Hmm... pretty clever.
Zh: 嗯……挺机灵的。

En: Until a pool of vomit lies under your feet -- and your throat stings from the stomach acid.
Zh: 直到你的脚下出现了一滩呕吐物——你的喉咙被胃酸刺痛了。

En: You've gained a Thought! When this dialogue is over, go to your THOUGHT CABINET and INTERNALIZE it for special bonuses and effects.
Zh: 你获得了一项思维！在本次对话结束后，前往你的思维阁并开始内化新思维吧，这将给你带来加成效果。

En: You've gained a Thought! When this dialogue is over, go to your THOUGHT CABINET and INTERNALIZE it for special bonuses and effects.
Zh: 你获得了一项思维！在本次对话结束后，前往你的思维阁并开始内化新思维吧，这将给你带来加成效果。

En: You can open this White Check again by going to your CHARACTER SHEET and spending a skill point to upgrade your Endurance. Gain new skill points by exploring and completing side-tasks.
Zh: 你可以在角色信息界面中消耗技能点来升级钢筋铁骨，从而再次开启该白色检定。你可以通过探索地图和完成支线任务来获得技能点。

En: ...even though I got my shit compressed!? How is it possible?
Zh: “……即使我会被压扁？这怎么可能呢？”

En: I am at a loss as well. I could *swear* your shit was together, detective.
Zh: “我也很迷茫。不过我可以*发誓*你不会有事的，警探。”

En: Sometimes even that is not enough... Life is unfair.
Zh: 有时候即便如此也还是不够……生活是不公平的。

En: The man before you is naked, but for a pair of underpants and enameled boots. His skin is greenish, marbled with decaying veins and blotched by lividity. A fading web of tattoos covers his chest and shoulders. The cargo belt used to fasten him to the branch above appears industrial in strength.
Zh: 你面前的男人除了一条内裤和一双珐琅质靴子之外未着一物。他的皮肤呈绿色，满是脂肪纹路的皮肤上布满了腐烂的血管与尸斑，胸口和肩膀上有褪色的纹身。那根将他固定在上方树枝上的运货带，从强度上看来应该是工业级的。

En: Could be. The shape of the branch supports the theory.
Zh: 可能是吧，树枝的形状证实了这一观点。

En: Ignore. Say no more. This is about the corpse and inspecting it.
Zh: 忽略它，不必多说。现在的重点是检查这具尸体。

En: He worked for Krenel -- like Joyce told us. This is military equipment, provided by a wealthy security contractor. With state ties. *Way* above what we have.
Zh: “他在为科奈尔工作——正如乔告诉我们的那样。这些是由一位富有的安保承包商提供的军用装备，有国家背景。*远*在我们之上。”

En: Looks like he worked for a private contractor -- like Evrart told us. Wealthy. With state ties. This is *way* above what we have.
Zh: “看来他是在为一个私人承包商工作——正如艾弗拉特告诉我们的那样。非常富有，还有国家背景。*远*在我们之上。”

En: That *is* what ka-ching means, yes.
Zh: “那*就是*钞能力的意思了，是的。”

En: Mhm. It means 'disciplinary hearing'. The lieutenant nods, wiping his finger on his sleeve.
Zh: “嗯。是*纪律听证会*的意思。警督点点头，用袖子擦着手指。”

En: Like the scales of some ancient white monster, cracked and pearly.
Zh: 如同某种古老的白色怪物的鳞甲，碎裂了，就像珍珠一般。

En: Decay is creeping on the tattoo, already most of the canvas that's holding it has darkened, now it disintegrates slowly, letting out a stink...
Zh: 纹身逐渐腐烂，大部分的图案已经变得黯淡，慢慢开始分解，散发出一阵恶臭……

En: Here -- a souvenir. He hands you the piece of rolled up photo paper. It's no larger than a pack of cigarettes. "Don't lose it."
Zh: “拿着——留作纪念。”他把那张卷起来的相纸递给你，不比一包香烟大了多少。”别弄丢了。”

En: The fish-lips stay silent in response. You must be losing your mind, asking him that over and over...
Zh: 鱼唇沉默以对，你一定是疯了才会一遍又一遍地问他这个问题……

En: No reply. How ever many times you keep asking.
Zh: 没有回答，无论你问了多少次。

En: Please stop trying to perform a *necromantic ritual*. We have other things to do.
Zh: “请不要再继续*亡灵仪式*了，我们还有别的事呢。”

En: You really need to learn the difference, buddy. This is embarrassing.
Zh: 你真的得去学学其中的差别了，伙计。这真是令人尴尬。

En: Roonie is not who I am. I'm Harry.
Zh: 我可不是罗尼，我是哈里。

En: No, you're not a Harry either. You're a motherfucker. That's who you are, Roonie the Motherfucker.
Zh: 不，你也不是哈里。你是个狗杂种。那就是你，罗尼，狗杂种。

En: That's right. Stupid, boring Harry. Harry the Motherfucker.
Zh: 没错。愚蠢又无聊的哈里。狗杂种哈里。

En: Because he told me to. (Nod toward Kim.)
Zh: 因为他告诉我的。（朝金点头。）

En: You're a lying sack of shit, coppo. You're doing this because there is *nothing* else to do. Everything else is over. It's just *me* now.
Zh: 你这个谎话连篇的混蛋，小警察。你就*闲得发慌*才会这么做，一切都结束了，现在只有*我*了。

En: *I* think he's dead -- and about to explode.
Zh: *我*想他是死了——就快要爆炸了。

En: No, of course not -- this time we shoot someone *else*!
Zh: 不，当然不是——这次我们*换个人*开枪！

En: No, of course not -- this time we shoot someone *else*!
Zh: 不，当然不是——这次我们*换个人*开枪！

En: Oh yeah! Do this shit! Fuck it up!
Zh: “噢耶！快点，来吧！”

En: Securing it in place.
Zh: 把它固定在合适的位置。

En: Your field of view narrows. The branch slowly moves, becoming entirely two-dimensional. The metal buckle glimmers, catching the morning light as the corpse slowly rotates.
Zh: 你的视野变狭窄了。树枝轻轻颤动着，完全变成了二维的模样。随着尸体的缓慢旋转，金属滑扣捕捉到清晨的阳光，从而闪烁着微光。

En: Your field of view narrows. The branch slowly moves, becoming entirely two-dimensional. The metal buckle glimmers as the corpse slowly rotates.
Zh: 你的视野变狭窄了。树枝轻轻颤动着，完全变成了二维的模样。随着尸体的缓慢旋转，金属滑扣闪烁着微光。

En: Your field of view narrows. The branch slowly moves, becoming entirely two-dimensional. The metal buckle glimmers, catching the evening light as the corpse slowly rotates.
Zh: 你的视野变狭窄了。树枝轻轻颤动着，完全变成了二维的模样。随着尸体的缓慢旋转，金属滑扣捕捉到傍晚的夕阳，从而闪烁着微光。

En: We need to go to the harbour and ask the leader of the Union to have it taken down. They have the tools -- and they have men.
Zh: “我们得去港口，请工会的主席来帮忙。他们有工具——也有人手。”

En: We need to access the harbour and ask the leader of the Union to have it taken down. They have the tools -- and they have men.
Zh: “我们得进入港口，请工会的主席来帮忙。他们有工具——也有人手。”

En: But what about the body?
Zh: “那尸体怎么办？”

En: Why didn't we just *begin* with that?
Zh: “为什么我们不从这里*入手*呢？”

En: Okay. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧。”[离开。]

En: I did not want us to be indebted to a man like Evrart Claire. I wanted us to be able to deal with it ourselves. That is *clearly* not the case. We need help.
Zh: “我也不想欠艾弗拉特・克莱尔那种人的情，所以我更希望能亲自处理这件事。但这*明显*行不通，我们需要帮助。”

En: I did not want us to be indebted to Evrart Claire. I wanted us to be able to deal with it ourselves. That is *clearly* not the case. We need help.
Zh: “我也不想欠艾弗拉特·克莱尔人情，所以我更希望能亲自处理这件事。但这*明显*行不通，我们需要帮助。”

En: You need to SUCK MY DICK! Why the fuck am I still *alive?!*
Zh: “过来谢罪吧！我他妈怎么还*活着*？！”

En: I suggest we go to the harbour and ask the leader of the Union to help us with the body -- I would have preferred us not to owe the Union anything, but we clearly cannot do this ourselves. Let's go.
Zh: “我的建议是到港口去，请工会的主席帮我们处理尸体——我本不愿欠工会任何人情的，但显然凭我们自己是搞不定了。走吧。”

En: The *bad* way -- the way I didn't want us to. He sighs. "By asking the harbour for help. They have the tools and the men. If they put him up there -- they can take him down too."
Zh: “*下下策*——是我最不情愿的一条路。”他叹了口气。“去请港口的人帮忙把。他们有工具也有人手，如果是他们把他吊上去的——也能放他下来。”

En: But *how*?
Zh: “但要*怎么做*呢？”

En: How dangerous can it be?
Zh: “能有多危险呢？”

En: But -- won't it be dangerous?
Zh: “但——不会很危险吗？”

En: How do we get *inside* the harbour?
Zh: “我们该如何*进入*港口呢？”

En: Good job we already found a way *in* the harbour.
Zh: “庆幸的是我们已经找到港口的*入口*了。”

En: Let's get to it then. [Leave.]
Zh: “那就行动起来吧。”[离开。]

En: Hooray for us.
Zh: “万岁。”

En: From the gates -- by negotiating or fighting. I'm *unenthusiastic* about fighting... He looks around. "Or we can try to find some secret third path -- it's unlikely, though."
Zh: “从正门——通过协商或者直接打进去。我对打斗实在*缺乏热情*……”他环顾四周。“或者我们可以试着找到第三条路——可能性不大就是了。”

En: To the gates! Let's fight, I say!
Zh: 前往正门！暴力突围！

En: To the gates! Let's negotiate!
Zh: 前往正门！和平协商！

En: Secret path, secret path, secret path!
Zh: 秘密之路，秘密之路，秘密之路！

En: To the gates! But we *negotiate*!
Zh: 前往正门，但我们要与人*协商*！

En: To ask the suspects for help with the victim's body? To be indebted to Evrart Claire? Very much, yes. He nods. "Which is why I would've preferred us to accomplish this feat ourselves."
Zh: “请嫌疑人帮忙处理被害者的尸体？欠下艾弗拉特・克莱尔的人情？非常危险，没错。”他点点头。“所以我更希望能亲自处理这件大事。”

En: To ask the *suspects* for help with the victim's body? To be indebted to Evrart Claire? Very much, yes -- which is why I would have preferred us to handle this ourselves.
Zh: “请*嫌疑人*帮忙处理被害人的尸体？欠下艾弗拉特·克莱尔的人情？非常危险，没错——所以我更希望能亲自处理这件事。”

En: Clearly we can't.
Zh: “显然我们办不到。”

En: Suck my dick, bitches!
Zh: “认输吧，菜鸡们！”

En: Who's Evrart Claire?
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔是谁？”

En: The leader of the Union. A dangerous and corrupt man, from what I hear -- you don't want to owe him much.
Zh: “工会主席，一个危险又腐败的男人，据我所知——你绝不会想欠他太多情。”

En: Yeah -- don't go bein' someone else's bitches. You're *Cuno's* bitches.
Zh: “是呀——别去跪舔别人。你们可都是*坤诺的*手下。”

En: Chill. It's *gonna* connect.
Zh: 放轻松，*会*连上的。

En: In the meantime, we should locate the representative of the logistics company. My preliminary information suggests the Wild Pines have sent a strike negotiator to wrangle back control from the Union.
Zh: “与此同时，我们应该去打听一下物流公司的代表。初步调查显示野松公司派了某个罢工谈判专家想从工会手中夺回控制权。”

En: I bet she has a *fresh* perspective to offer us. Since we've already talked to Evrart in the harbour -- that interview is done. For better or for worse.
Zh: “我敢说她会给我们提供一个*全新*的视角。既然我们已经在港口和艾弗拉特有过交流——他的走访就算是结束了，无论好坏。”

En: He holsters it. "I *would* suggest we interview Evrart and Joyce -- the leader of the Union and the Wild Pines rep -- but we've already done that. So... it's up to you detective. Take us where we need to be."
Zh: 他套上枪。“*我*原本的建议是去走访艾弗拉特和乔——工会主席和野松公司的代表——但我们已经完成了。所以……这就取决于你了警探。去我们该去的地方吧。”

En: He trusts you have plenty of things in the task chain, lined up.
Zh: 他认为你的任务清单上还排着一堆工作呢。

En: You could ask the Gardener for directions!
Zh: 你可以向园丁问路！

En: With shooting like that -- of course you are. Your station would not have assigned you to this case otherwise.
Zh: “以你那样的枪法——你当然是个警探了。否则你的警局也不会指派你过来处理这个案子。”

En: No, no. I have mine too, I'm just an asshole who does things in a strange order.
Zh: “不，不。我也有的，我不过是个办事逻辑有些奇怪的怪人罢了。”

En: Fine, then. The piece shines in his outstretched hand.
Zh: “那好吧。”手枪在他伸出的手中闪闪发光。

En: I was really hoping we wouldn't -- the Union appear to be *suspects* in this case. It seems like a dangerous route to go down.
Zh: “我真希望答案是否定的——这起案件里，工会有很大*嫌疑*。感觉前路充满了危险。”

En: They can get him down too.
Zh: “他们也能放他下来。”

En: He sighs. "Okay. Let's do it the lousy, dangerous way."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“好吧，就用这个糟心又危险的方法吧。”

En: Maybe we can ask for help from the harbour?
Zh: “或者我们可以去请港口的人来帮忙？”

En: But what *else* can we do? (Commit to it.)
Zh: “但我们*还能*做什么呢？”（决定下来。）

En: Okay... He looks at the cargo belt. "They *do* have the tools and the men. And since it looks like they *put* him there..."
Zh: “好吧……”他看着运货带。”他们*确实*有工具和人手。而且，既然看起来是他们把人*放在*那儿的……”

En: Yeah, wait -- let's reconsider. (Consider other options first.)
Zh: “是啊，等等——我们重新考虑一下。”（先考虑其它选项。）

En: Confirmed. It's unsafe.
Zh: 确认，这不安全。

En: But *what* other options? The corpse twists on the belt, like chicken on a skewer...
Zh: 但*还有*其它选项吗？尸体随着绑带转动着，如同串在烤肉串上的鸡肉……

En: Acquiring Ammonia will provide a MODIFIER to the White Check. MODIFIERS make checks easier -- and allow you to retry them.
Zh: 获得的氨可以提供白色检定的修正。修正可以降低检定的难度，并使你能够重试检定。

En: We should keep a lookout for these pieces. The armour could yield information. He nods toward Cuno, who's eyeing you suspiciously. "This is one thing he might actually know..."
Zh: “我们最好多留心这套盔甲，它们会带来线索的。”他朝用怀疑的眼光盯着你看的坤诺点点头。“关于这件事，他或许真的知道点什么……”

En: We should keep a lookout for these pieces. The armour could yield information. He nods toward the red-haired boy eyeing you suspiciously. "This is one thing he might actually know..."
Zh: “我们最好多留心这套盔甲，它们会带来线索的。”他朝那个用怀疑的眼光盯着你看的红发男孩点点头。“关于这件事，他或许真的知道点什么……”

En: Nice. That makes sense.
Zh: 很好，这样就说得通了。

En: Yes. He agrees. "The debrief we got from the Wild Pines representative appears to be solid too. A lynching is what this looks like. Our internal RCM brief said the same..." He looks at you.
Zh: “是的。”他赞同道。“我们从野松代表那里问出的消息似乎也证实了这一点，这就是一场私刑，这也与我们RCM的内部简报如出一辙……”他看着你。

En: By the way, when you get the chance -- *perhaps* you should ask me to share the RCM brief you. I'm pretty sure you've *forgotten* it.
Zh: “另外，如果有机会的话——*或许*可以让我与你分享一下RCM的简报，我敢说你一定是*忘记*了。”

En: An intricate web of blue lines stretches across the torso. If you want to re-examine the tattoos, looking at the photo would give you a better, more detailed view.
Zh: 一张错综复杂的蓝色线网蔓延了整具躯干。如果你想重新检查一次纹身，这张相片可以为你提供一个更好更详尽的视角。

En: The cadaver hangs from the cargo belt --limbs limp and torso covered in tattoos.
Zh: 尸体被吊在一根运货带上——四肢瘫软，躯干上布满了纹身。

En: I would really prefer if there was another way. These people might have an agenda.
Zh: 我真希望还能有别的办法，这些人可能有计划表。

En: That's it. We have to ask for help from the harbour. (Commit to it.)
Zh: “就这样吧，我们不得不向港口求助了。”（提交。）

En: This is years of experience talking. He's seen career after career ruined by careless media strategies.
Zh: 这是多年的经验之谈，他目睹过一人又一人的职业生涯被粗糙的媒体策略毁掉了。

En: You know, you don't feel like too bad of a shot yourself...
Zh: 要知道，你看起来就不像枪法很差的样子……

En: Take his gun and show him how to use it.
Zh: 拿起他的枪，教他如何使用。

En: Your field of view narrows. The branch slowly moves, becoming entirely two-dimensional. The metal buckle glimmers, slick with the falling rain. The corpse slowly rotates...
Zh: 你的视野变狭窄了。树枝轻轻颤动着，完全变成了二维的模样。金属滑扣闪着微光，被雨点打的锃亮。尸体慢慢地旋转着……

En: From the gates -- by negotiating or fighting. He looks up to the east. "Or we can try the secret route we found. Where your cloak is. It looked doable."
Zh: “从正门——通过协商或者直接打进去。”他朝着东方望去。”或者可以试试我们找到的秘密线路，你的斗篷那里。应该可行。”

En: I can't get the damn compartment in my ledger open.
Zh: 我弄不开手册里的那一格。

En: The blue heart? Oh, that's good shit, you'll love it. Just press down and *fuck* it open like you always do.
Zh: 蓝色之心？哦，那狗屁玩意不错，你会爱死的。只要跟你一直做的那样，按下去，然后*他妈的*打开它。

En: Fuck it hard, Copopo the Clown.
Zh: 快打开吧，小丑警察。

En: He means force. It'll work.
Zh: 他的意思是使用暴力，值得一试。

En: Just a small gulp away, my beloved Cobo. A small gulp away.
Zh: 就差那么一口气的距离，我亲爱的小警察。就一小口。

En: How do you like it in the fridge?
Zh: 在冰箱里感觉怎么样？

En: I like it a *lot* brother. This really is your finest hour! You're a genius -- a regular Coppolangelo!
Zh: 我*非常*喜欢，兄弟。这可真是你的闪光时刻了！你是个天才——一位合格的警察察！

En: Of course not. The name is Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: 当然不是。我的名字是拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。

En: Listen to yourself... You're not a Raphaël anything. You're probably just Harry or something. That's right... *Harry*.
Zh: 听听你自己……你才不是什么拉斐尔，充其量只是个哈里吧。没错……*哈里*。

En: You might be on to something there.
Zh: 你可算说到点子上了。

En: We're not off to a good start. This missing gun business is making your hand tremble.
Zh: 我们并没有一个很好的开端，这起枪支丢失事故已经让你手足无措了。

En: It *seems* unclimbable, cause the ladder is treacherously rotten, but you could use your hands and feet to reach around the tree, then hang from the branch...
Zh: 看起来*似乎*爬不上去，因为梯子都腐烂了，很危险的样子，不过你可以把手脚伸到树周围，然后挂在树枝上……

En: Even Cuno thinks it's unclimbable, cause the ladder is treacherously rotten, but you could use your hands and feet to reach around the tree, then hang from the branch...
Zh: 就连坤诺都觉得爬不上去，因为梯子都腐烂了，很危险的样子，不过你可以把手脚伸到树周围，然后挂在树枝上……

En: No, he says, shaking his head, "that ladder is not climbable and I don't really see another good way up there."
Zh: “不，”他摇摇头说道，“不能爬那个梯子，而且我真看不出还有什么上去的好办法。”

En: *Or* we could talk to the representative of the logistics company again. I know we already met Joyce, but that didn't count as an interview. We need to ask her how the strike and lynching are *connected*.
Zh: “*或者*我们可以再跟物流公司的代表聊聊。我知道，我们已经见过乔了，不过那根本算不上是一次走访。我们需要问问她罢工和私刑之间有什么*联系*。”

En: *Or* we could ask around for the representative of the logistics company. My initial information says the Wild Pines have sent some sort of *strike negotiator* to wrangle control back from Evrart.
Zh: “*或者*我们可以打听一下物流公司的代表。根据我得到的初步信息，野松公司派出了某种*罢工交涉人*，打算从艾弗拉特手中夺回控制权。”

En: We know he wasn't hung, so I'd say he was treated. These markings were produced.
Zh: “我们知道他并不是被绞死的，所以我认为他被动过手脚，这些痕迹也是故意制造的。”

En: To purposefully mislead us. He nods. "They must've hung him fast after that bullet hit. This is well done. It would have fooled *me*."
Zh: “为了故意误导我们。”他点点头。“子弹击中他后，他们一定是非常迅速地把他吊了起来。干得不错，差点就蒙住*我*了。”

En: There is only one ampoule left. Use it wisely.
Zh: 只剩一个安瓿瓶了，省着点。

En: It's tempting. But decapitating him *might* be against regulations.
Zh: “是个主意，但将他斩首的话很*可能*违反规定。”

En: We're assuming dockworkers from the harbour did it?
Zh: “我们怀疑是港口的工人们干的？”

En: Yes. He agrees. "The debrief we got from the Wild Pines representative appears to be solid too. A lynching is what this looks like."
Zh: “是的。”他赞同道。“我们从野松代表那里问出的消息似乎也证实了这一点，这就是一场私刑。”

En: By *they* do you mean the Hardie boys?
Zh: “你口中的*他们*是指哈迪兄弟吗？”

En: He nods. "Right now everything seems to fit their confession."
Zh: 他点点头。”目前一切似乎都与他们的供词相吻合。”

En: Oops...
Zh: 哎呀……

En: Who's Evrart Claire?
Zh: “艾弗拉特·克莱尔是谁？”

En: What's wrong with being indebted to Evrart Claire?
Zh: “欠艾弗拉特·克莱尔的人情有什么问题吗？”

En: The leader of the Union. A dangerous and corrupt man. One you don't want to be *indebted* to.
Zh: “工会主席，一个危险又腐败的男人。一个你不想*欠他人情*的人。”

En: He's a dangerous and corrupt man, and we cannot predict what he will want from us in return.
Zh: “他是个危险又腐败的男人，我们无法预测他会想从我们这里得到怎样的回报。”

En: He's a dangerous and corrupt man, and we cannot predict what he will want from us in return.
Zh: “他是个危险又腐败的男人，我们无法预测他会想从我们这里得到怎样的回报。”

En: What's wrong with being indebted to Evrart Claire?
Zh: “欠艾弗拉特·克莱尔的人情有什么问题吗？”

En: Relax. It's okay if you don't make it today -- the bloated corpse isn't going anywhere.
Zh: 放松。如果今天不行也没问题——反正这具浮肿的尸体也跑不到哪里去。

En: Maybe it's okay if you don't make it *today*? The dead man isn't going anywhere -- and there's always other things to do...
Zh: 也许如果*今天*不行也可以呢？死人也不会跑到哪里去——而且总是有其他事要做……

En: No, I think he had something precious underneath the clothes. They had to remove the *jeans and shirt* we found to get to it. And this kind of armour is often worn *under* fabrics.
Zh: “不，我认为他的衣服里面一定藏着某种贵重的东西，所以他们不得不把我们在垃圾箱里找到的*牛仔裤和衬衫*脱掉。这类盔甲往往是穿在衣服*下面*的。”

En: No, I think he had something precious underneath his clothes. They had to remove the *jeans* we found in the trash to get to it. This kind of armour is often worn *under* fabric.
Zh: “不，我认为他的衣服里面一定藏着某种贵重的东西，所以他们不得不把我们在垃圾箱里找到的*牛仔裤*脱掉。而这类盔甲往往是穿在衣服*下面*的。”

En: He could have been walking around naked -- just like this for all we know.
Zh: “或许他本就是在裸奔呢——正如我们看到的这样。”

En: A good point. He could have been intoxicated. Or something we cannot yet imagine... I shouldn't have assumed so much just from the clothes.
Zh: “有道理。也有可能他是喝醉了，或是因为一些我们无法想象的理由……我不应该仅是看到衣服就妄加猜测。”

En: What was that? Was the lieutenant just impressed by your unintuitive guess?
Zh: 发生什么了？刚刚警督是被你的天马行空说动了吗？

En: He was. Why else would he call it a very good point? Does this mean you're a *very* good detective? Possibly.
Zh: 没错。否则他怎么会说有道理呢？这是否意味着你是一个*非常*厉害的警探呢？有可能。

En: We should keep a lookout for these pieces. The armour could yield information. He nods toward Cuno. "Maybe he'll know something."
Zh: “我们最好多留心这套盔甲，它们会带来线索的。”他朝坤诺点点头。“也许他知道点什么。”

En: We should keep a lookout for these pieces. The armour could yield information. He nods toward the red-haired boy eyeing you suspiciously. "Maybe he'll know something."
Zh: “我们最好多留心这套盔甲，它们会带来线索的。”他朝那个用怀疑的眼光盯着你看的红发男孩点点头。“也许他知道点什么。”

En: If you wear those pieces, it will help me protect your mortal coil.
Zh: 如果你穿上了那副盔甲，它会帮助我保护你的躯壳。

En: Why, does my mortal coil need protecting?
Zh: 为什么？难道我的躯壳需要保护吗？

En: Yes. Bullets will fly, they always do. And the coil is fleshy and mush and permeable. Cast it in ceramic shell. Resist death.
Zh: 是的，子弹无情，一向如此。但躯壳却有血有肉，既柔软又可以被轻易穿透。那就在外铸造起陶瓷的外壳，抵御死亡。

En: I'm not squishy and permeable. I'm bark, sap and steel. Cast me in ceramic shell, but only for *ritual purposes*. It would adorn me well... bring me to attention.
Zh: 我既不软弱也不会被穿透，反而拥有钢筋铁骨。将陶瓷当成我的外壳吧，但只是为了*仪式需要*。它会为我增色不少……更加引人注目。

En: The noose acted like a tourniquet, keeping the blood in his head. The hypostasis here is in tune with a hanging. That's what I think.
Zh: “绞索就如同止血带，让血液积蓄在他的脑袋里。这里的尸斑也证明了这一点，我是这样想的。”

En: Oh yes, Cobo mi Lobo! In the gift horse's mouth, tracts and wakes and waterways! Ancient materials buried...
Zh: 哦，是的，大灰狼警察！在礼品马的嘴巴里，高速公路，航迹还有水路！古老的材料埋葬的地方……

En: Yeah, but to *where*, brother?
Zh: 是啊，但是在*哪里*呢，兄弟？

En: Alright.
Zh: 好吧。

En: It's *all* right.
Zh: *没*关系。

En: The hangover is clearly making this worse for you. You could use some ammonia -- to clear your head.
Zh: “宿醉明显让你的状态变得更糟了。可以用些氨——帮你清醒一下。”

En: Okay -- where do we get ammonia from?
Zh: “好吧——我们要去哪儿找氨呢？”

En: But not you?
Zh: “你不用吗？”

En: I can't handle the headache.
Zh: “我受不了那种头痛。”

En: It's more likely he can handle the smell. Unlike you.
Zh: 更像是他能够忍受这股恶臭，而你不行。

En: If she doesn't have any, there might be some in the Frittte store nearby.
Zh: “如果她那里没有，附近的弗利多商店可能会有一些。”

En: Bitch-fight, C. Bitches are at it.
Zh: “菜鸡互啄，坤妮。菜鸡们又开始了。”

En: Mhm, bitches about to kill each other, I think.
Zh: “哼，感觉他们又在互相残杀了。”

En: The lieutenant pays no heed: "We should go and do something else. Give it half an hour. Then come back, when you've gotten your act together."
Zh: 警督丝毫不在意：“我们应该去做点别的，差不多半个小时，等你把事情安排妥当再回来。”

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Come back later, coppo! Amuse yourself with my frank manners and my *memento mori* features. If possible -- also see me in your dreams.
Zh: 下次再来，小警察！尽情嘲笑我坦率的举止与透露着*死亡气息*的面容吧。有可能的话——还可以在你的梦中相会呢。

En: You can come back and look into this face any time you want. Humour yourself with my harlequin features. Ask me your little questions, freshen your memory. Create associations, remind yourself of your *mortality*, Coppolopo.
Zh: 你随时都能回来看着这张脸，尽情嘲笑我的丑态。用我来满足你的好奇心，唤醒你的记忆。培养一下感情，来提醒自己*必死的命运*，小察察。

En: You can come back and look into this face any time you want. Ask me your little questions, freshen your memory. Create associations, remind yourself of your *mortality*, Coppolopo.
Zh: 你随时都能回来看着这张脸，用我来满足你的好奇心，唤醒你的记忆。培养一下感情，来提醒自己*必死的命运*，阿察。

En: We've looked at the belt, now how do we get him down?
Zh: “我们检查过了绑带，现在要怎么把他弄下来呢？”

En: Yup. The preliminary examination is done -- let's get him down from there.
Zh: “是啊。初步检查已经完成了——我们把他放下来吧。”

En: Yes, we do. The lieutenant inspects the belt, his forehead is lined with worry.
Zh: “确实。”警督检查着绑带，他的前额写满了忧虑。

En: We need to talk about getting him down again.
Zh: “我们又得讨论一遍该如何放他下来了。”

En: He breathes out heavily.
Zh: 他喘着粗气。

En: Yeah, you should shoot him down. No other way.
Zh: “是啊，你得开枪把他打下来，没别的办法了。”

En: You crack open the ammonia ampoule and breathe in. The odour of death is still stronger. It's a spell of the mind telling you to run -- and your stomach to wring itself empty. With your eyes squinting you stand in it.
Zh: 你打开安瓿瓶，深吸了一口。死亡的气息仍然很浓烈，仿佛一种心灵暗示告诉你赶快离开——你的胃快要把自己榨干了。你眯着眼睛站在那里。

En: The sabatons dangle off the man's decaying form, ageless and synthetic.
Zh: 铠靴悬挂在那个男人腐败的身体上，成为一种人为的永恒。

En: I hear you, f****t! Talking shit 'bout the Cuno. Come here and say it to Cuno's face! the boy shrieks at the top of his lungs.
Zh: “我可听到了，该死的条子，又在对坤诺叽叽歪歪。有种过来当着坤诺的面说啊！”男孩声嘶力竭地尖叫。

En: The smell is repulsive. It pushes in from your mouth, more instant and more familiar than anything you'd expected; more fever than odour. It fills your mind, flushing you from within.
Zh: 这股气味令人作呕。它闯进你的嘴里，比预期的更加迅速熟悉；与其说气味，不如说像一场高烧。它充斥了你的思想，从内部占据了你。

En: The ammonia only makes it worse! The combination forces tears out of your ducts. You manage to keep it in once...
Zh: 氨让一切变得更糟了！这种组合令人作呕，你设法忍住了……

En: Ha-ha-ha! AGAIN! The corpse laughs at you, puss dripping from its mouth. You will *never* be able to hold it in. It's *always* too much...
Zh: 哈哈哈！又来了！尸体嘲笑着你，嘴里流下了腐水。你*永远*不可能控制得住，*实在*太夸张了……

En: The corpse appears to laugh at you, black froth bubbling on it's lips. Every molecule in this body reeks of death. It's *still* too much, you feel it coming up again...
Zh: 尸体似乎是在嘲笑你，嘴唇上冒出了黑色的泡沫。这具身躯上下每个分子都散发着死亡的气息。*还是*太过了，你感觉胃部再次翻涌起来……

En: His personality is no longer a part of the world.
Zh: 他在这个世界已经不复存在。

En: A plume of smoke erupts from the barrel. Your hand goes numb from the explosion. With your ears still ringing you lower the weapon to see what happened.
Zh: 一缕轻烟从枪管中喷了出来，你的手因为爆炸略感麻木。在耳朵仍在嗡嗡作响的同时，你放下了武器一探究竟。

En: The buckle explodes into tiny pieces, coming loose with a *whirr*.
Zh: 滑扣炸裂成细小的碎片，*呼*地一声松开了。

En: The sound of the explosion from your gun echoes over all of Martinaise. The bullet found its way to the heart of a kid.
Zh: 枪支的爆裂声回荡在整个马丁内斯。子弹射入了一个孩子的心脏。

En: I agree. A sudden ring fills the air as the lieutenant pulls down the zipper of his black jacket.
I agree. A sudden ring fills the air as the lieutenant pulls down the zipper of his orange jacket.
So am I. A sudden ringing fills the air as the lieutenant pulls down the zipper of his black jacket.
So am I. A sudden ringing fills the air as the lieutenant pulls down the zipper of his orange jacket.
1. External examination -- now that we have the chaincutters, let's cut the belt.
Zh: “1.体外检查——既然我们已经拿到了切链钳，就可以把绑带切断了。”

En: 2. Internal examination -- where were we?
Zh: “2.体内检查——我们做到哪一步了？”

En: 3. Description of injuries -- now that we've fully examined the body.
Zh: “3.创伤描述——我们已经彻底检查过这具尸体了。”

En: The man is decomposing visibly now. Every hour he looks less like a creature and more like a pile of intestines...
Zh: 男人的腐烂程度肉眼可见。每过一小时，他就更趋近于一堆肠子，而非一个生物……

En: The rotting man lies on his side with his eyes looking straight through you. Bits of cargo belt lie on the ground around him, his body is supine and open to intrusion by autopsy.
Zh: 这个腐烂的男人侧卧在地上，眼睛直直地盯着你。他周围的地上散落着运货带的碎片，身体平躺，准备迎接尸检。

En: The rotting man lies on his side with his eyes looking straight through you. His body is supine and open to intrusion by autopsy.
Zh: 这个腐烂的男人侧卧在地上，眼睛直直地盯着你。他的身体平躺，准备迎接尸检。

En: The bloated man is stuffed into the fridge like a rotten piece of meat. It looks disgusting among all the ice cream wrappers.
Zh: 这个肿胀的男人如同一块腐肉似的被塞进冰箱，与所有的冰淇淋包装挤在一起，看起来很恶心。

En: The rotting man lies on his side with his eyes looking straight through you. The belt is still around his neck, his body is supine and open to intrusion by autopsy.
Zh: 这具仍在腐烂的尸体侧卧在地上，双眼直勾勾地瞪着你。那根绑带仍然系在他的脖子上，你把尸体放平，准备进行尸检。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: First, what exactly is a *field autopsy*?
Zh: “首先，*现场尸检*究竟是什么？”

En: Tell me something, dead man.
Zh: 跟我聊聊吧，死者。

En: Shoot, Loonie Roonie!
Zh: 放马过来吧，鲁尼如尼！

En: Unfortunately, no.
Zh: “很遗憾，没有。”

En: I need a pair then... Can we return to this shortly? We have glove-hunting to do.
Zh: “我需要一副手套……能过会儿再来处理这个吗？咱们得先找到手套再说。”

En: OK, I'll take it like a man.
Zh: “好吧，我会像纯爷们一样赤手空拳上阵的。“

En: Unhygienically?
Zh: “你就这么不讲卫生？”

En: Come on, officer. He pulls on a pair of  latex gloves. "You know what a field autopsy is. You've done a hundred of them."
Zh: “别开玩笑了，警官。”他戴上了一双橡胶手套。“你当然知道什么是现场尸检。你都经历过上百次了。”

En: What you *do* know is: at 18.9 km, the dormant shield volcano *Corpus Mundi* is the world's highest summit; and the failure of the '38 single 'Et Puis Du Sang' to crack the Top 20 was the death knell of disco. But -- what a *field autopsy* is? You have no idea.
Zh: 你*确确实实*知道的事包括：海拔18.9千米的休眠盾形火山*蒙迪之躯*是世界最高峰；38年的单曲*鲜血流淌*未能闯入金曲榜前二十名的惨状敲响了迪斯科的丧钟。但是——*现场尸检*是什么意思？你对此一窍不通。

En: Why don't you know? What use are you?
Zh: 你怎么会不知道？你还有什么用？

En: Actually... it appears I have *forgotten* what a field autopsy is.
Zh: “其实……我好像*忘掉了*现场尸检的意思。”

En: Do you have another pair of gloves?
Zh: “你还有多余的手套吗？”

En: (Clap your gloved hands.) "Let's get in there."
Zh: （戴着手套拍手。）“咱们动手吧。”

En: You must have me confused with the *Copopedia*.
Zh: 你肯定是把我误认为是*警务大全*了。

En: Who's the Copopedia? I think I need to talk to him.
Zh: 警务大全是哪位？我得和他好好聊聊。

En: *You*, sir -- you are the Copopedia.
Zh: 是*您*，先生——您就是警务大全。

En: Yes.
Zh: “没错。”

En: Fine, the lieutenant sighs. "It's a three-part form to be filled out on the scene, by the detectives responsible. One takes notes, the other dictates. The goal is to establish cause of death."
Zh: “好吧，”警督叹了口气。“这张表格分为三部分，由现场负责的警探填写。一人负责记录，一人负责口述。目的是确认死因。”

En: Do we need a scalpel for it?
Zh: “我们会用到手术刀吗？”

En: A scalpel is not always required. I hope this is one of those cases... Latex gloves are, however.
Zh: “不一定用得上。我希望这次不用动刀……但橡胶手套还是有必要的。”

En: Let's not go *glove-hunting*, please. Let's use division of labour -- I perform the anatomical side, while you take notes.
Zh: “咱们还是别去*找手套*了，拜托。还是分工合作吧——我负责做尸检，你负责做记录。”

En: Okay. Where should I take these *notes*?
Zh: “行啊。那么我该在哪儿做这种*记录*呢？”

En: You're being *handled*. If you resist now he will turn on his heavy duty authority mode. You'll get your chance to dig in -- later.
Zh: 他正在*指挥*你。如果你敢抵抗，他那股争强好胜的劲头又会上来了。你还是有机会亲自发掘的——以后再说吧。

En: In your paperwork, officer. Just fill out the field autopsy form. He sighs, already expecting the answer.
Zh: “记录在你的工作报告里，警官。你只需要填写那张尸检记录表。”他叹了口气，已经预见到你的回答了。

En: There truly is a time for everything -- even for yellow gardening gloves. He nods approvingly. "However, they are lacking hygienically. I suggest you *get in there* in limited capacity."
Zh: “果然是万物皆有出头之日——就连一双黄色的园艺手套也不例外。”他赞许地点着头。“但是，这手套不够卫生。我建议你在*接触尸体时*量力而行。”

En: Alright.
Zh: “你说了算。”

En: I don't know. What do you mean?
Zh: “我不懂。你是什么意思？”

En: I mean when I need you to. Until then -- I should handle physical contact and you should take notes.
Zh: “我的意思是，在我不需要你动手帮我的情况下——我负责接触尸体，而你负责做文字记录。”

En: I'll ask you when I need you to. For the most part, maybe I should handle the contact and you take notes?
Zh: “当我需要你动手帮我的时候，我会叫你的。在大多数情况下，最好还是由我来接触尸体，而你负责文字记录。”

En: I suggest *I* handle the physical part of the autopsy -- and you take notes.
Zh: “我建议由*我*来负责与尸体接触——而你只要做好记录就行。”

En: I can give you my paperwork -- there's an autopsy form there. Several actually. But *only* if it helps move things along.
Zh: “我可以把我的工作报告给你——那里有尸检记录表。而且有好几张。*前提是*这样能推动案件进展。”

En: I should have just brought a dictaphone, he mumbles.
Zh: “我真该直接带个录音机来的。”他嘟囔着。

En: I'm sorry I lost everything.
Zh: “抱歉，我把一切都弄丢了。”

En: Yes... well. The lieutenant doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “唉……好吧。”警督彻底无语了。

En: It's okay to cry, *runkkari*.
Zh: “想哭就哭吧，*怂货*。”

En: I'm sure it's somewhere.
Zh: “肯定是丢在某个地方了。”

En: Somewhere *else*?
Zh: “*某个*地方？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: No.
Zh: “不是。”

En: Maybe.
Zh: “也许吧。”

En: As you say the words you find yourself peering from the corner of your eye... to where? To the West!
Zh: 你一边说着，一边发现自己正不由自主地瞥向……哪里？西边！

En: As the words leave your mouth -- there it is again! That strange feeling, like there's something *in* there...
Zh: 就在这句话即将脱口而出的瞬间——那种奇怪的感觉再次涌上心头！仿佛*有什么东西*在那儿……

En: Begone foul sensation of worry!
Zh: 退散吧！这股忧心忡忡的杂念！

En: What? What's there?
Zh: 什么？那边是什么？

En: A trash container. Yellow and capsule-like, lying in the corner of the yard.
Zh: 一个胶囊状的黄色垃圾箱，躺在院子的角落里。

En: Good call! Never mind that *old trash container* lurching in the corner of this yard, full of  old news...
Zh: 说得好！千万别在意院子角落里那个东倒西歪的*破旧垃圾箱*，那里满是过时的消息……

En: I guess you've got to start finding at least *some* of the things you've lost, before they hurt someone... He sighs.
Zh: “我觉得你至少需要动身去找*某些*弄丢的东西，趁它们还没对别人造成伤害……”他叹了口气。

En: If we must, then we must. Police paperwork is confidential material, officer... He sighs.
Zh: “如果不得不找，那就必须得找。警察的工作报告可都是机密啊，警官……”他叹了口气。

En: Where?
Zh: 在哪儿？

En: Begone strange feeling.
Zh: 退散吧，奇怪的感觉。

En: The trash container. THE TRASH CONTAINER OF BAD MEMORIES.
Zh: 垃圾箱。装满糟糕回忆的垃圾箱。

En: Good call! Never mind that *old trash container*lurching there, full of bad old news...
Zh: 说得好！千万别在意在那边东倒西歪的*破旧垃圾箱*，那里满是过时的消息……

En: Open your ledger at the field autopsy form.
Zh: 打开你的手册，翻到尸检记录表。

En: We should start the postmortem.
Zh: “我们该开始尸检了。”

En: Trying not to bring the incident up any more.
Zh: 尽量不要再提这起这件意外。

En: The assistant has a colour photograph of the markings -- to be added to the case files as document A1. The photo is taken on the scene, using a Trigat Mini.
Zh: “助手拍摄了纹身图案的彩色照片——命名为文件A1，补充至案件档案中。照片是在现场拍摄的，设备是特里盖特迷你型。”

En: The upper torso is covered in a single, continuous tattoo resembling a microelectronical circuit board. It reaches from the right shoulder to the heart. The ink is blue and white.
Zh: “上半身覆盖着一个类似微电子电路板模样的纹身，从右肩开始直至心脏，用的是蓝色与白色的墨水。”

En: The upper torso is covered in a single, continuous tattoo resembling a national pattern. It reaches from the right shoulder to the heart. The ink is blue and white.
Zh: “上半身覆盖着一个类似国家形状的单一纹身，从右肩开始直至心脏，用的是蓝色与白色的墨水。”

En: The upper torso is covered in a single, continuous tattoo resembling a map of the night sky. It reaches from the right shoulder to the heart. The ink is blue and white.
Zh: “上半身覆盖着一个类似夜空地图状的单一纹身，从右肩开始直至心脏，用的是蓝色与白色的墨水。”

En: The upper torso is covered in a single tattoo, beginning on the right shoulder and reaching around the heart. The shape is abstract. The ink is blue and white.
Zh: “上半身纹着一整张图案，始于右肩，终于心脏。形状很抽象。采用了蓝色与白色的墨水。”

En: Document A1. The detectives have taken a colour photograph of the markings. Photograph was produced on scene.
Zh: “文件A1。警探在现场拍摄了一张纹身图案的照片。”

En: The corpse lies on the ground among the remains of an absolutely *demolished* pinewood branch. It's gently laid on one side.
Zh: 尸体躺到了地上，一根被彻底*摧毁*的松树枝残片温柔地躺在他身旁。

En: Beautiful. (Wipe a tear from your eye.)
Zh: “真漂亮。”（拭去眼中的泪水。）

En: Well -- it's down. (Celebrate in a more reserved manner.)
Zh: “瞧吧——解下来了。”（以更含蓄的方式庆祝一下。）

En: Great job, wise race mentor.
Zh: “干得漂亮，睿智的种族导师。”

En: Mr. Measurehead has done a good job. The lieutenant nods approvingly. "Nothing is *too* broken -- or compromised. The victim is ready for a field autopsy."
Zh: “测颅先生做得很好。”警督赞许地点着头。“没造成*严重*损坏——也没暴露在危险之中。尸体已经就绪，可以开始现场尸检了。”

En: A field *autopsy*?
Zh: “现场*尸检*？”

En: Yes. He covers his nose and lists: "One, investigation of the scene. Two, inital examination of the victim. Three, field autopsy. Four, transportation of the body to the morgue. We're on number three."
Zh: “没错。”他捂住鼻子，开始解释：“第一，调查现场。第二，对尸体进行初步检查。第三，现场尸检。第四，将尸体运送至停尸房。我们已经进行到第三步了。”

En: Yes, I feel like a detective.
Zh: “好的，我感觉自己像个警探了。”

En: Don't we have someone else for this? A doctor?
Zh: “我们就不能派专人做这种事吗？比如医生？”

En: An honour -- and a burden -- attached to your rank once you've proven yourself able, usually after five to eight years of field work. Mine is *lieutenant-detective*.
Zh: “这是荣誉——也是负担——只有在五到八年的实地工作中证明自己称职之后，才能获得这种军衔。我的头衔是*中尉-警探*。”

En: You are. Your station would not have assigned you on this case if you weren't. Now, the way I see it... He looks at the corpse with some disgust...
Zh: “你就是，否则你的警局不会指派你过来处理这个案子。那么，在我看来……”他有些厌恶地看着那具尸体……

En: You are. Your station would not have assigned you on this case if you weren't. Now...
Zh: “你就是专人。如果你不是的话，你所属的警察局就不会派你负责这个案子了。那么……”

En: Got it.
Zh: “明白了。”

En: Wait -- honorary rank of detective?
Zh: “等等——警探还有荣誉军衔？”

En: The fuck are they on about?
Zh: “他们在搞什么？”

En: I don't think I'm a detective.
Zh: “我不觉得自己是个警探。”

En: Cops gonna cut his shit up.
Zh: “条子要把他大卸八块了。”

En: No. You and I are *detectives*. The honorary rank of 'detective' signifies our ability to handle *the entire incident chain* -- from autopsy to clean-up, to social work. Everything.
Zh: “不，你我都是*警探*。被授予荣誉军衔的‘警探’意味着我们有能力处理*整个事件*——从尸检到收拾残局，再到社会工作。涉及到案件的方方面面。”

En: Don't we have someone else to *cut his shit open*?
Zh: “我们就不能派专人来*把他大卸八块*吗？”

En: It's not about cutting... He squints at the corpse. "And no -- you and I are *detectives*. The honorary rank of 'detective' signifies our ability to handle *the entire incident chain* -- from autopsy to clean-up. To social work..."
Zh: “这不是分尸……”他眯起眼睛看着尸体。“以及，没有其他人手了——你我都是*警探*。被授予荣誉军衔的‘警探’意味着我们有能力处理*整个事件*——从尸检到收拾残局，再到社会工作……”

En: It's either this, *or* we complete the initial interviews. We already met Evrart *and* Joyce -- but didn't *quite* get Joyce's interview done. For that we have to ask her about the *lynching* -- not just talk to her.
Zh: “要么做这个，*要么*去走访。我们已经见过艾弗拉特*和*乔伊斯——但没有*详细*询问乔伊斯。我们得好好盘问她有关*私刑*的事——而非单纯的谈话。”

En: It's either this, *or* we complete the initial interviews. We already met Evrart -- that only leaves the Wild Pines representative. There's one in town, negotiating the strike. She could have information.
Zh: “我们要么先做尸检，*要么*先去完成初步走访。我们已经见过艾弗拉特了——还需要去会一会野松公司的代表。有位代表就在城里就罢工进行谈判。她可能掌握了一些情报。”

En: It's either this, *or* we complete the initial interviews. We already met Evrart but we didn't *quite* get his interview done -- for that we would have to ask him about the lynching. Similarly, we met Joyce but did not ask her about the lynching either.
Zh: “要么做这个，*要么*去走访。我们已经见过艾弗拉特，但没有*详细*询问他——我们得好好盘问他有关私刑的事。同样的，我们见过了乔伊斯，却没有问她私刑的事。”

En: It's either this, *or* we complete the initial interviews. We already met Evrart, but didn't *quite* get his interview done -- for that we have to ask him about the lynching. There's also a Wild Pines representative in town. She could have some information.
Zh: “我们要么先做尸检，*要么*先去完成初步走访。我们已经见过艾弗拉特了，但还没和他进行*深入*的交流——我们至少得问问关于这场私刑的事。城里还有一位野松公司的代表。她可能掌握了一些情报。”

En: It's either this, *or* we complete the initial interviews. We already met Evrart, but didn't *quite* get his interview done -- for that we would have to ask him about the *lynching*. Like we did with Joyce.
Zh: “我们要么先做尸检，*要么*先去完成初步走访。我们已经见过艾弗拉特了，但还没和他进行*深入*的交流——我们至少得问问关于这场*私刑*的事。就像我们和乔伊斯谈过的那样。”

En: No need for a distraction. Let's do this.
Zh: “没必要分心了。先做尸检吧。”

En: Yes. *Interview*. We can always do the *autopsy* later. [Leave.]
Zh: “好的。*走访*吧。*尸检*的事就留到以后再说。”[离开。]

En: Not much later, I hope. He looks at the man's rotting remains. "He's about to... *melt away* soon."
Zh: “希望你别留到太靠后。”他望着仍在腐烂的尸体。“他很快就要……*融解*了。”

En: Fuck are you talking to, clown?
Zh: “你他妈在跟谁说话呢，小丑？”

En: Talkin' to the dead man. Psycho cop.
Zh: “他正跟死人对话呢。通灵的条子。”

En: Somewhere, Lieutenant Kitsuragi is preparing a report. He should be here for this, you realise. You have to return later.
Zh: 曷城警督正在某个地方准备工作报告。你意识到自己需要他在场才能继续。只能留到以后再说了。

En: Good. Now, the way I see it... He looks at the corpse with some disgust...
Zh: “很好。那么，在我看来……”他看着尸体的眼神带着一丝厌恶……

En: The RCM's four-phase murder scene processing manual.
Zh: RCM谋杀现场四步处理手册。

En: Okay. 1. Assistant:
Zh: “好的。1. 助手：”

En: Ellis Kortenaer -- we have a *full* name to provide here... He seems satisifed.
Zh: “埃利斯·科顿艾尔——我们可以在这里填上*全名*了……”他看上去非常满意。

En: Write: Ellis Kortenaer.
Zh: 写下：埃利斯·科顿艾尔。

En: What's next -- date of birth?
Zh: “接下来是——出生日期？”

En: 4. Date of birth. (Nod.)
Zh: “4. 出生日期。”（点头）

En: Write: Lely (nom de guerre).
Zh: 写下：莱利（化名）

En: I'm not seeing anything yet -- but we *know* there's tampering here. He thinks. "Write down: Yes. Non-visual. Corroborated by witness (see report)."
Zh: “我还看不出究竟——但我们*知道*有人破坏了现场。”他思考着。“这么写吧：“有。不可见。由目击者证实（见报告）。”

En: Write down: Yes. Non-visual. Corroborated by witness (see report).
Zh: 写下：有。不可见。由目击者证实（见报告）。

En: Good... The lieutenant falls silent. He places his gloved hand on the corpse's chest, as if in preparation...
Zh: “很好……”警督陷入了沉默。他把戴着手套的手放到了死者的胸口处，就像是在做着准备……

En: When *am* I gonna get them?
Zh: 我*什么时候*才能拿到这个？

En: Patience. After the autopsy, before the body is taken away. There will be a *window* of opportunity, after the lieutenant has gone to sleep. I hope this has helped you, my liege.
Zh: 耐心点。在尸检完成之后，尸体被运走之前。趁警督睡着，你*大显身手*的时机就到了。希望这能帮上您的忙，陛下。

En: Mhm. He shows you the point on the man's neck where belt cuts into the flesh. "Cut with as much *precision* as you can, please."
Zh: “嗯。”他为你指出死者脖颈上被绑带紧紧勒住的位置。“请尽量*精准地*切开这里。”

En: Oh, yeah -- I'm *prepared*. (Pet the chaincutters.)
Zh: 哦耶——我*时刻准备着*。（抚摸切链钳。）

En: The hanged man lets out a joyous little bubble of rot from his nose...
Zh: 吊着的尸体从鼻子里挤出了一个腐臭而欢乐的气泡……

En: You got just the right tool for that -- the chaincutters.
Zh: 你有专门为此准备的工具——切链钳。

En: *Snap!* The knot is slashed. Another cut and the belt falls apart like a flower bouquet, revealing the dead man's neck -- and the dark red ligature mark around it.
Zh: *啪！*死结应声而断。在下一钳之后，绑带就如同花束一般散落在地，暴露出死者的脖颈——以及一圈暗红色的勒痕。

En: Yes. That's much better form than you had.
Zh: 很好。比以前强多了。

En: This scarring is extensive. Way more than a law official's.
Zh: “到处都是伤疤。数量远超一般执法人员。”

En: This is the body of a soldier. A mercenary.
Zh: “这是士兵的尸体。一个雇佣兵。”

En: Mhm, he nods. "We have a real museum here. Of battles, wars..."
Zh: “唔，”他点着头。“这是一座货真价实的博物馆。记录着战斗与战争……”

En: Not particularly -- him being *dead* when they carried him over...
Zh: “精确到——他被扛过来时已经*死了*……”

En: It is very hard to struggle when you're dead. Death is the *end* of struggle.
Zh: 死人是不会挣扎的。死亡是挣扎的*终点*。

En: Not from where I look, no. But you're right. Your hunch was right. He was having sex when he died.
Zh: “虽然我看不出来。但你是对的。你的直觉很准。他死的时候正在性交。”

En: Talkin' about sex. He's gonna suck him off now, I know it...
Zh: “说起性交。他这就要给他口交了，我就知道……”

En: Well done -- sounds like it would throw us off if we didn't know...
Zh: “干得好——我们差点就被误导了，幸好我们事先知道……”

En: ...that he was dead. Yes. They moved fast. This is a good treatment.
Zh: “……他已经死了。是的。他们动作很快。处理得非常彻底。”

En: Mute silence as always.
Zh: 一如既往的沉默。

En: This will do.
Zh: 这样就行了。

En: ...is what it would look like, but we know better. We *know* the ligature mark is a treatment. He awaits your call.
Zh: “……虽然看似如此，但我们都知道真相。我们*知道*勒痕是在死后做的伪装。”他等待着你的判断。

En: That's right. No clawing marks on the neck, no signs of struggle on the wrists. A witness statement from the perpetrators confirms: wound is post-mortem. Treatment.
Zh: “就是这样。脖子上没有抓痕，手腕也没有挣扎的迹象，以及嫌犯的目击证词也证实了这一点:伤口是死后造成的，被处理过。”

En: That's right. No clawing marks on the neck, no signs of struggle on the crime scene, faint lividity. A witness statement from the perpetrators confirms: wound is post-mortem. Treatment.
Zh: “就是这样。脖子上没有抓痕，犯罪现场也没有挣扎的迹象，略有尸斑，以及嫌犯的目击证词也证实了这一点:伤口是死后造成的，被处理过。”

En: That's right. No clawing marks on the neck, no signs of struggle on the wrists, faint lividity. A witness statement from the perpetrators confirms: wound is post-mortem. Treatment.
Zh: “就是这样。脖子上没有抓痕，手腕也没有挣扎的迹象，略有尸斑，以及嫌犯的目击证词也证实了这一点:伤口是死后造成的，被处理过。”

En: That's right. No clawing marks on the neck, no signs of struggle on the crime scene. And a witness statement from the perpetrators all confirm: wound is post-mortem. Treatment.
Zh: “正是如此。颈部没有抓痕，犯罪现场没有挣扎的痕迹。肇事者中有位证人已经证实：伤口是死后造成的，故意伪造。”

En: That was some sick shit, *sekopää*.
Zh: “你可真恶心，*癫子*。”

En: Lividity appears consistent with hanging -- albeit *faint*, ergo: postmortem. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head and chest, consistent with stones thrown; low velocity...
Zh: “尸斑看上去符合绞死的特征——有些微弱，因此为死后所致。头部充血。头部、胸部以及大腿处有挫伤——说明发生过石刑；低速度……”

En: Lividity *appears* consistent with hanging -- but is not. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head and chest -- consistent with stones thrown postmortem; low velocity...
Zh: “尸斑*看似*符合绞死的特征——但并非如此。头部充血，头部和胸部有多处挫伤——符合死后被石头砸过的现实；低速……”

En: Khm. A cough. "Lividity appears consistent with hanging -- albeit *faint*, ergo: postmortem. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head and chest, consistent with stones thrown; low velocity..."
Zh: “咳，”一声咳嗽。“尸斑看上去符合绞死的特征——有些微弱，因此为死后所致。头部充血。头部、胸部以及大腿处有挫伤——说明发生过石刑；低速度……”

En: Khm. A cough. "Lividity *appears* consistent with hanging -- but is not. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head and chest, consistent with stones thrown; low velocity..."
Zh: 他咳了一声。“尸斑*看似*符合绞死的特征——但并非如此。头部充血，头部和胸部有多处挫伤——符合死后被石头砸过的现实；低速……”

En: That's it. Field autopsy done, detective. All that remains is to see if the alleged cause of death -- the bullet -- is truly lodged in his brain. I'll leave that to you, colleague. *Or* to Processing -- I won't force you in there...
Zh: “结束了。我们完成了现场尸检，警探。剩下的工作只有检查尚未证实的死因——那颗子弹——是否进入了死者的大脑。这就交给你了，同事。*也可以*留给分析部门——我不会强迫你检查死者的大脑的……”

En: Oh yeah -- finally. Let's get *in there*.
Zh: 哦耶——总算是到了这一刻。咱们*进去*瞧瞧吧。

En: Don't let him ask twice! DIG IN, SON.
Zh: 别等他再提一次了！钻进去吧，小子。

En: Please get yourself together, the lieutenant from Precinct 57 thinks.
Zh: 请振作起来，第57分局的警督是这么想的。

En: Hmm hmm hmm... I'm sure we didn't get *everything*. There's always *something*...
Zh: 嗯哼哼……我肯定咱们做得还不够*彻底*。一定是有*漏网之鱼*……

En: Seriously -- I can just bag him and drive him to the morgue any time you tell me to. No need for heroics -- unless you want to...
Zh: “说真的——只要你开口，我随时都可以为他收尸，并开車把尸体运到停尸房去。你没必要逞英雄——除非你特别想……”

En: ...still, you can make out a case with a curious title: THE MAN WITH THE HOLE IN HIS HEAD.
Zh: 不过，你还是勉强辨认出了一个命名诡异的案件：脑子有洞的男人。

En: He looks around -- at the dark and lonely yard behind the hostel-cafeteria. "*Or* we could call it a day. It incredibly late."
Zh: 他环顾四周——你们身处咖啡厅旅馆那黑暗且孤寂的后院中。“*或许*我们可以就此收工。实在是太晚了。”

En: Yes, let's do it tomorrow. [Leave.]
Zh: “是啊，明天再继续吧。”[离开。]

En: Good call.
Zh: “好主意。”

En: Yeah, we got this. We're *smart*.
Zh: 很好。我们搞定了。我们*真聪明*。

En: The corpse is indifferent to time of the Dead Light and Great Culling, just lies there. The next box says:
Zh: 尸体对死亡之光与大淘汰的时刻漠不关心，死气沉沉地躺在地上。接下来的框里写着：

En: A strange word... *treatment*.
Zh: 真是个奇怪的词……*处理*。

En: Teeming with opportunistic micro-organisms, letting out a foul-smelling diamine compound. Your eyes turn watery...
Zh: 释放出一股恶臭的二胺化合物，还充满了条件性病原微生物。你的双眼瞬间就充满了泪水……

En: Don't overdo it. It's okay to be unsure.
Zh: 别做过头了。没把握也没关系的。

En: Stroke his hair -- gently.
Zh: 抚摸他的头发——温柔地。

En: As it ought to. This is where its grip on the curdled meat is gentlest, pulling up...
Zh: 理应如此。这就是绑带在尸体上绑得最松的位置，就这样将尸体吊起……

En: Of course.
Zh: “当然。”

En: You don't even have a joke.
Zh: 你甚至说不出一个笑话。

En: He's really *doing* it -- the dead man's teeth cut into his gloved hands.
Zh: 他真的*动手*了——死人的牙齿咬住了他戴着手套的双手。

En: Be *thorough* if you want maximum results.
Zh: 如果你想取得最全面的成果，就要做得*细致入微*。

En: What was that about no clawing around the neck? You'd be clawing for your life...
Zh: 脖子上怎么会没有抓痕？换成是你的话，一定会拼命挣扎的……

En: Honestly, I'm not sure there *weren't* marks on his wrists. That part got blurry for me. The *stench...* He covers his mouth. "But you're right. There should be signs of struggle. Let's leave the cause of death empty for the time being."
Zh: “说实在的，我无法确认他的腕部*到底*有没有伤痕。我对那个部位的印象非常模糊。这股*尸臭*……”他捂住嘴。“但你说得对。尸体上应该有挣扎的痕迹。关于死因，咱们还是暂时保留意见吧。”

En: Try to remove the dead man's boots again.
Zh: 再次尝试脱掉死者的靴子。

En: Okay, Kim. Take him away. (The lieutenant takes the body away -- work alone for the rest of the day.)
Zh: “好吧，金。送他上路吧。”（警督会把尸体运走，今天你只能孤军奋战了。）

En: Write: Harry Du Bois.
Zh: 写下：哈里·杜博阿

En: Clothes, he begins. "The deceased..." He blinks, staring at the body. "The deceased *used* to wear a pair of armoured boots -- they're gone now. Interesting."
Zh: “衣物，”他突然开口。“死者……”他眨了眨眼，盯着尸体。“死者*曾*穿着一双装甲靴——现在那双靴子不见了。有意思。”

En: But he's still wearing his white briefs -- thank god for that. The make of the briefs is 'Babroudine' I think. Let's see... He turns the body onto its side to check the underwear label.
Zh: “但他还穿着那条白色内裤——谢天谢地。我觉得内裤的牌子大概是‘巴布鲁戴恩’。我找找……”他翻转尸体，以检查内裤的标签。

En: He doesn't actually think it's 'interesting' that the boots have disappeared -- it's just sad, sad and unprofessional.
Zh: 他并不真的认为靴子的失踪“有意思”——他觉得这件事实在是太悲哀了。既悲哀，又不专业。

En: The rest of the clothes have been removed postmortem by scavengers. In order to get to the ceramic armour. Officers are in search of the missing pieces.
Zh: “剩下的衣物都是死后被拾荒者们捡走的。为了取回陶瓷盔甲，警方正在寻找丢失的碎片。”

En: The boots were ceramic, vitreous enamel. They were fused to his skin from blood flowing downward postmortem. They were removed postmortem by scavengers.
Zh: “靴子是陶瓷的，搪瓷涂层。因死后血液向下流动，靴子已经与双脚皮肤牢牢粘在一起。之后被拾荒者脱掉捡走了。”

En: Fortunately we already wrote down the serial number -- it begins with X5415. Let's move on.
Zh: “好在我们已经记录了靴子上的编号——以X5415开头。继续吧。”

En: Oops. A bad feeling comes over you...
Zh: 唉呀，一股不怎么样的滋味涌上心头……

En: It was merely a *jest* -- horseplay! But now it looks like you're a total *psychopath*, jeopardizing the crime scene...
Zh: 那只是*开玩笑*——恶作剧而已！但如今的你看起来完全就是个破坏犯罪现场的*心理变态*……

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Actually, I told you the wrong serial number. It was something else.
Zh: “其实，我告诉你的编号是假的。真的编号不是那个。”

En: Fortunately we already wrote down the serial number -- E50.100.1000. Let's move on.
Zh: “好在我们已经记录了靴子上的编号——E50.100.1000。继续吧。”

En: You told me the wrong serial number. The lieutenant grows very still. "Why would you do that? Why would you jeopardize your own crime scene? And now the boots are gone, too. This could have been a lead."
Zh: “你告诉我的编号是假的。”他的表情逐渐消沉。“为什么要干这种事？为什么要破坏你负责的犯罪现场？现在这双靴子已经不见了。这本有可能成为一条线索的。”

En: Sorry.
Zh: “抱歉。”

En: Sorry doesn't cut it here. Let's move on.
Zh: “道歉也解决不了问题。换个话题吧。”

En: Let him move on.
Zh: 那就换个话题吧。

En: Actually, I was the one who took the boots. I can tell you the right serial number -- it's E50.100.1000.
Zh: “事实上，拿走那双靴子的就是我。我可以告诉你正确的编号——E50.100.1000。”

En: *You* took the boots? He grimaces, before writing down the serial number.
Zh: “拿走靴子的是*你*？”他愁眉苦脸地记下了那串编号。

En: Hm. The lieutenant furrows his brow. "Not just yet -- let's get what we know *it isn't* out of the way first."
Zh: 警督皱起眉头。“唔。还没到时候——咱们先把已知的*不相关*的事排除掉。”

En: Khm... The lieutenant scratches the back of his head. "We already did that. Remember? We started, but we need the *chaincutters* from my motor-carriage to finish it."
Zh: “嗯……”警督抓了抓后脑勺。“我们已经做过了，还记得吗？我们试过，但我们需要放在我車里的*切链钳*才能完成。”

En: Where should I take these notes? In my paperwork? (Take out your ledger.)
Zh: “我应该在哪儿做这种记录？在我的工作报告里吗？”（掏出你的手册。）

En: Yes, you see... He points to the bright red pages at the end. "The field autopsy form is the one on red copier paper."
Zh: “是的，看这里……”他指着手册最后几页红色的纸张。“尸检记录表就印在红色的复印纸上。”

En: You're not gonna believe this -- I think I've *lost* my paperwork.
Zh: “你可能会觉得难以置信——我认为我把自己的工作报告*弄丢了*。”

En: Say nothing, just nod.
Zh: 默默点头。

En: You've lost your paperwork, haven't you?
Zh: “你把工作报告弄丢了，对吧？”

En: Yes. I've lost it.
Zh: “对。我弄丢了。”

En: Keep nodding.
Zh: 继续点头。

En: Fucking *runkkari*! He's afraid to say it!
Zh: “这个*怂货*！他不敢承认！”

En: Man up, pig. You're embarrassing Cuno.
Zh: “有点男人的样子吧，猪猡。坤诺都为你感到难为情。”

En: Now they only have one paperwork too...
Zh: “如今他们就连工作报告都只剩一份了……”

En: Officer, what *haven't* you lost?
Zh: “警官，你还有*没弄丢*的东西吗？”

En: I still have my disco trousers. (Look at your pants.)
Zh: “我还留着自己的迪斯科裤子。”（注视自己的裤子。）

En: I haven't lost this satin shirt. (Smell your armpit.)
Zh: “我没弄丢这件缎子衬衫。”（嗅嗅自己的腋下。）

En: I still have my trusty blazer. It is yet to abandon me -- like everything else.
Zh: “我还留着这件可靠的外套。只有它对我矢志不渝——不像其他的一切。”

En: My shoes. I have both of them. I found the *other* one too!
Zh: “我的鞋还在。而且是一整双。我连*另一只*都找到了！”

En: Maybe you *should*? He looks at his blue notebook and sighs.
Zh: “也许那是你*应该做的*？”他望着自己的蓝色笔记本叹了口气。

En: Good. Otherwise you would only have *one* shoe, now... He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “很好。不然就连你的鞋也要变成*孤家寡人*了，言归正传……”他望着自己的笔记本。

En: My left shoe! I don't know where the right one is, though... (Look at your shoe.)
Zh: “我还留着左脚穿的鞋！不过我找不到右脚那只了……”（注视自己的鞋子。）

En: No need to be melodramatic, officer. He looks at his blue notebook and sighs.
Zh: “没必要说得这么夸张，警官。”他望着自己的蓝色笔记本叹了口气。

En: I've noticed that, yes. And I must say I find it *troubling*. He looks at his notebook and sighs.
Zh: “嗯，我已经发现了。而且这确实令我感到*困扰*。”他望着自己的笔记本叹了口气。

En: Yes. Barely. He looks at his blue notebook and sighs.
Zh: “是的。马马虎虎。”他望着自己的蓝色笔记本叹了口气。

En: Let's go find mine. We have to find it sooner or later.
Zh: “咱们先去找我的工作报告吧。反正迟早都是要找的。”

En: Let's work off yours for now.
Zh: “先用你的将就一下吧。”

En: Yeah, lets get back to this later. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，待会儿再来处理这个吧。”

En: I hope we do. He covers his nose. "It's getting *really* late for an autopsy..."
Zh: “希望我们还来得及。”他捂住鼻子。“现在才尸检已经*太晚了*……。”

En: We just fill this in, right? (Show him the red *field autopsy form* in your ledger.)
Zh: “我们只要填这个就行了吧？”（向他展示你手册中的*现场尸检记录表*。）

En: That's right, he nods.
Zh: “没错。”他点点头。

En: The corpse cannot feel Kim's hand on his chest. It no longer meaningfully interacts with its surroundings. A thicket of boxes and lists on red copier paper tries to answer *why*:
Zh: 这具尸体感受不到金放在胸口上的手。它早已无法与周围的环境进行任何有意义的互动。这张红色复印纸试图根据上面印着的条条框框解答它变成尸体的*原因*：

En: Right. The autopsy form is near the end. He hands you his notebook and his pen.
Zh: “好吧。尸检记录表就在最后几页。”他把笔记本和笔都交给了你。

En: Open the notebook at the field autopsy form.
Zh: 打开笔记本，翻到尸检记录表。

En: You *knew* it because you inspected your ledger! The lieutenant is relieved you know the protocol.
Zh: 幸亏你事先检查过自己的手册，才没有在这里*露怯*！你对工作流程的了解令警督如释重负。

En: We should locate your ledger, then hurry back to the autopsy as soon as possible.
Zh: “我们应该先找到你的手册，再尽快赶回来处理尸检。”

En: We should find it before someone else does, then hurry back to the autopsy as soon as possible.
Zh: “我们得赶在其他人发现之前找到你的手册，再尽快赶回来处理尸检。”

En: 1. External examination -- summary.
Zh: “1.外表检验——总结。”

En: 1. External examination -- summary.
Zh: “1.体外检查——总结。”

En: 2. Internal examination -- summary.
Zh: “2.体内检查——总结。”

En: 3. Description of injuries -- summary.
Zh: “3.创伤描述——总结。”

En: Hmm-hmm... (Take a peek at Kim's notes.)
Zh: “哼—唔……”（偷看一眼金的记录）

En: Let's pause this autopsy.
Zh: “咱们先暂停一下尸检。”

En: The corpse does not object, he lies slumped to the side stinking. The ground underneath him is saturated in purge liquids.
Zh: 尸体没有反对，他向一边瘫倒，散发着臭气。身下的地面布满腐臭的汁液。

En: Let's. The lieutenant nods.
Zh: “好的。”警督点点头。

En: You stand there with the ledger open like a lonesome idiot, realizing you have no idea how to conduct an autopsy without the lieutenant, who is out there doing something immeasurably serious.
Zh: 你翻开手册，像个孤立无援的白痴一样站在原地，这才意识到没有警督帮忙，你根本就不知道如何进行尸检，而警督正忙于至关重要的事务。

En: The dead man stares in silence as you crack open the ledger. The bright red paper is covered in boxes and lists -- describing the condition of his skin and organs in three parts.
Zh: 死者默默地盯着你翻开手册。鲜红色的纸张上满是条条框框——分为三个部分，描述着他的皮肤与内脏。

En: (Begin.) "1. Assistant:"
Let's jump ahead to the three part summary!
Zh: “咱们直接跳到三个总结部分吧。”

En: No, let's fill out the info form first.
Zh: “不行，先填好信息表。”

En: That's you.
Zh: “你就是助手。”

En: Only the first question and already I don't know the answer!
Zh: “我连第一个问题都不知道该怎么回答！”

En: Write: Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau.
Zh: 写下：拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托。

En: Write the initials: H.D.B.
Zh: 写下首字母：H.D.B。

En: Write: probably Harry-something.
Zh: 写下：可能是叫哈里——什么的。

En: Write: Tequila Sunset.
Zh: 写下：龙舌兰日落。

En: Write: Harrier Du Bois, lieutenant double-yefreitor.
Zh: 记录:哈里尔·杜博阿，双重荣誉警督。

En: Write: Harrier Du Bois.
Zh: 写下：哈里尔尔·杜博阿。

En: The corpse is indifferent to your scribblings, just lies there. The next box says:
Zh: 尸体对你的潦草笔迹漠不关心，死气沉沉地躺在地上。接下来的框里写着：

En: 2. Coroner's case no:
Zh: “2.验尸官案件编号：”

En: KK = Kim Kitsuragi; 57 = Precinct 57; followed by his date (08.03) and time of arrival (08:15) on the scene. He's indexed the case after himself, not you.
Zh: KK=金·曷城；57=第57分局；之后是他到达现场的日期（08.03）与时间（08：15）。案件编号采用了他的名字，而不是你的。

En: That's because he doesn't want to bring up the messy question of your *initials*.
Zh: 那是因为他不想提起关于你*姓名首字母*的糊涂问题。

En: Shouldn't we file the coroner's case under *me*? Technically, I arrived at the scene before you.
Zh: “咱们应该把验尸官写成*我*才对。严格来说，我比你先到达现场。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: And what would that make the alphanumeric?
Zh: “那样的话，字母和数字要怎么写？”

En: Lets say 13:00 -- a good time to start drinking.
Zh: 就写成13：00吧——适合喝酒的好时候。

En: 11:11. It's *meaningful*.
10:15. It's understated.
HDB41-0803... help me out with the time of day anyone?
Zh: HDB41-0803……谁帮我想想具体是什么时间？

En: HDB41-0803. (No time of arrival.)
Zh: “HDB41-0803。”（不写到场时间。）

En: I have no idea.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Good. Let's go with that.
Zh: “很好。就这样吧。”

En: The lieutenant is quite surprised you managed to come up with an adequate case number.
Zh: 你居然能写出精确的案件编号，这令警督惊诧不已。

En: Next?
Zh: “继续？”

En: 3. Name:
N/A -- next?
Zh: “没有——继续？”

En: 4. Date of birth:
Zh: “4.出生日期：”

En: 5. Age:
Hmh, the lieutenant corrects his glasses: "roughly 50."
Zh: “唔，”警督扶正了眼镜：“大约50岁。”

En: Try 40. The damage is so extensive it's better to err on the young side.
Zh: 写40吧。尸体的外伤太严重了，最好往年轻了写。

En: I'm gonna write: ≈42.
Zh: “我准备写：大约42岁。”

En: Write: ≈50.
Zh: 写下：大约50岁。

En: He nods.
Zh: 他点了点头。

En: The corpse looks ageless, like meat on a hook.
Zh: 尸体看不出年纪，就像一块挂在钩子上的肉。

En: 6. Race:
Mondial.
Zh: “蒙迪人。”

En: Mondial. Subgroup: Occidental -- as per your observation.
Zh: “蒙迪人。子类别：奥克西登特人——根据你的观察。”

En: Fair to olive skinned, from the isola of Mundi. This is as vague as it gets. You might as well say: white-ish.
Zh: 皮肤为白色或橄榄色，人种来自蒙迪洲。这种说法实在是过于模糊。你还不如直接说：是白人。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: The pudgy mess looks neither *Mondial* nor *Occidental*. It doesn't even look human. The next line says:
Zh: 这坨臃肿的物质既不像*蒙迪人*也不像*奥克西登特人*。它根本就不像人。下一行字写着：

En: The pudgy mess of curdled meat looks neither *Mondial* nor anything other.
Zh: 这坨臃肿的烂肉根本就不像*蒙迪人*或任何人种。

En: 7. Sex:
Fucky-fucky! the little monster exclaims, energetically.
Zh: “操了-操了！”这个小畜生兴高采烈地大喊大叫。

En: Pigs gonna have sex?
Zh: “两头猪猡要性交？”

En: Male.
Zh: “男性。”

En: Write: male.
Zh: 写下：男性。

En: Naively, the lieutenant does not double check your notes.
Zh: 警督还是太天真了，居然没有仔细检查你做的记录。

En: *El Bigo Mistako*, lieutenant!
Zh: *一失足成千古恨呐*，警督！

En: Nor does he look male, with his pregnant belly and indistinguishable face.
Zh: 他看起来也不像男性，瞧瞧那像怀孕一样鼓起的肚子，还有那张无法辨认容貌的脸。

En: 8. Date of death:
Zh: “8.死亡日期：”

En: N/A.
Zh: “无法确认。”

En: We're still going with March 4th, '51.
Zh: “暂时先写成51年3月4日吧。”

En: What else? He looks over your shoulder. "*9. Body identified by* is non-applicable. *10. Case number* is the same as the coroner's case."
Zh: “其他的呢？”警督的视线越过你的肩膀。“*9.尸体身份确认*只能写无法确认。*10.案件编号*与验尸官案件编号一致。”

En: 11. Evidence of treatment:
Zh: “11.处理痕迹：”

En: None -- at least not after the initial examination.
Zh: “没有——至少在初次检查之后没有。”

En: What exactly *is* treatment anyway?
Zh: “这个处理是*什么*意思？”

En: I agree. Lividity pointed to a lynching.
Zh: “我同意。尸斑表明死者经历了一场私刑。”

En: I'm not so sure. Didn't the footprints look like he was *carried* over?
Zh: “我不确定。附近的足迹显示死者是被*扛*过来的，不是吗？”

En: What's next? (No opinion.)
Zh: “接下来呢？”（不表达意见。）

En: Interfering with the body's position or wounds postmortem.
Zh: “改变尸体的位置，或是在死后造成的创伤。”

En: How did you not know that?! Aren't you a *cop*? You're leaving a weak impression here, say something sure handed!
Zh: 你怎么连这种事都不知道？！你难道不是*条子*吗？你现在一副怂样，快说点有把握的话！

En: Then again -- the right lividity is easy to produce, if you know what you're doing. He places his hand on the dead man's chest, as if in preparation...
Zh: “话说回来——只要有意去做，这种尸斑是很容易伪造的。”他把手放到了死者的胸口处，就像是在做着准备……

En: He places his gloved hand on the dead man's chest, as if in preparation...
Zh: 他把戴着手套的手放到了死者的胸口处，就像是在做着准备……

En: Turn the page.
Zh: 翻到下一页。

En: They'd *have* to have incapacitated and carried him over -- this man was more than a match for untrained dockworkers. He places his hand on the dead man's chest, as if in preparation...
Zh: “他们*肯定要*让他失去行动能力，再把他扛过来——受害者生前肯定比几个未经训练的码头工人厉害。”他把手放到了死者的胸口处，就像是在做着准备……

En: Not an energetic start to an autopsy, I agree.
Zh: “我同意，对尸检而言这不是什么良好的开端。”

En: Just leave it empty, officer.
Zh: “先空着吧，警官。”

En: Pig's messed up. Cuno feels pity.
Zh: “猪猡搞砸了。坤诺表示同情。”

En: Leave it empty.
Zh: 空着吧。

En: The corpse lies there, indifferent to your retrograde amnesia. The next box says:
Zh: 尸体对你的逆行性遗忘漠不关心，死气沉沉地躺在地上。接下来的框里写着：

En: Write: The Gloaming.
Zh: 写下：黄昏。

En: As you read it you think: that leaves out dark haired Mesque, Sao, Saramirizian, Kedreatic.... Lighter skinned Occidental means this man could be... Vespertine? Oranjese? Messinian. Those three are on the table.
Zh: 你边读边想：那就排除了黑发的梅斯克人，索奥人，萨拉米兹人，肯得里克人……浅肤色的奥克西登特人种意味着他可能是……维斯珀人？奥兰治人？梅西纳人。有这三种可能。

En: A solid guess, Mr. Racial Profiler!
Zh: 合理的推测，种族分析师先生！

En: Also of note -- your unconscious racial profiling machine ruled out Messinian before. Despite all the *copporoonies* and *cobos*. That leaves Vespertine or Oranjese. Continue.
Zh: 另请注意：你潜意识中的种族分析器已经排除了梅西纳人的可能性。虽然你们交流了那么多带着梅西纳口音的“警茬筒子”和“条之”。也就是说只可能是维斯珀人或奥兰治人了。继续吧。

En: Above those -- an eleven field info form needs filling out first. It begins with:
Zh: 在这些之上——还要先填一个分成十一部分的信息表，首先是：

En: Agreed -- no treatment.
Zh: “同意——无处理痕迹。”

En: I'm not so sure.
Zh: “我不确定。”

En: A silent nod. The lieutenant places his gloved hand on the corpse's chest -- as if in preparation...
Zh: 警督默默地点了点头，把戴着手套的手放到了死者的胸口处，就像是在做着准备……

En: Your central nervous system recognizes this gesture. It's the *Stations of the Breath* -- ecclesiastic, religious in nature. A holdout from Pre-Dolorian burial rites. It takes him two seconds to perform, then...
Zh: 你的中枢神经系统认出了这个姿势。这是*呼吸之圣像*——宗教性的表现。亦是前德洛莉丝时代葬礼的衍生仪式。他用两秒钟摆出了这个姿势，此外……

En: Somewhere in Jamrock North, a small wood shed behind Rozenkrantz Row, Lieutenant Nick Feuerbach puts his hand to the chest of a small corpse, no larger than a monkey. It's raining outside, light drizzle. There is darkness in the shed.
Zh: 在加姆洛克北部某处，罗赞克兰兹街后面的小木棚中。尼克·费尔巴哈警督把手放在一具体积比猴子大不了多少的尸体胸口。外面细雨蒙蒙，木棚里一片漆黑。

En: Elsewhere yet, an obese female sits in a wicker chair, her silhouette ball-like against the window. Outside: Grand Couron. The day is turning dim for Sergeant Mack Torson. Hand extended, he approaches. To make sure she *is* dead, more than anything else...
Zh: 在另一个地方，一位肥胖的女性坐在一张藤椅上，她在窗上留下了球状的剪影。窗外是大科戎。一片暮色中，麦克·托尔森警长向她伸出手。这不是为了别的，而是为了确认她*已经*死了。

En: ...and so all across Jamrock, Coal City, G.R.I.H. Forty two deceased persons found today -- forty two Stations of Breath.
Zh: ……在加姆洛克，煤城，大瑞瓦肖工业港区，今天共发现了四十二具尸体——四十二次呼吸之圣像的仪式。

En: The building is tall, seven stories wind-wrapped in solitude; most of the apartments are unoccupied. This was a suicide. The other an accident -- the small one.
Zh: 这是一栋高大的建筑，七层楼孤零零地在风中耸立，大部分房间无人居住。死者是自杀的。至于另一个——体型较小的，则死于一场事故。

En: The corpse lays slouched to the side, oblivious to its surroundings. The field autopsy form describes his state in three parts:
Zh: 尸体无精打采地瘫倒在地，对周围的一切视而不见。尸检记录表以三个部分描述了他的状态：

En: Clothes, he begins. "The deceased wears armoured boots and white briefs. The make of the briefs is Babroudine I think. Let's see..." He turns the body onto its side to check the underwear label.
Zh: “衣着，”警督开口了。“死者穿着一双装甲靴，以及白色内裤。我觉得内裤的品牌是巴布鲁戴恩。我找找……”他翻转尸体，以检查内裤的标签。

En: C, it's happening!
Zh: “C，他要搞基了！”

En: The disappointment is palpable -- the red haired thing was expecting something more lurid.
Zh: 显而易见，她非常失望——这个红发的小怪物原本期待着你们会做出更耸人听闻的事。

En: Babroudine, yes. Inexpensive. Size M. Colour: white.
Zh: “是的，巴布鲁戴恩。便宜货。中码。颜色：白色。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: The rest of the clothes have been removed postmortem by scavengers. In order to get to the victim's ceramic armour. Officers are in search of the missing pieces -- removal of the boots is left for Processing.
Zh: “受害人剩下的衣物都在死后被拾荒者扒走了，就是为了拿走他的陶瓷轻甲。警方还在寻找丢失的部件——脱去靴子的工作尚在进行中。”

En: The boots are ceramic, vitreous enamel. They're fused to his skin from blood flowing downward postmortem. Removal of the boots is left for Processing.
Zh: “靴子是陶瓷的，搪瓷涂层。因死后血液向下流动，靴子已经与双脚皮肤牢牢粘在一起。移除靴子的工作交由分析部门处理。”

En: It would be *clever* of you to omit the boots altogether, sire. If you are to *keep* them for yourself -- as you ought to. You have deserved them more than anyone else!
Zh: *睿智*如您，应当省略掉关于这双靴子的记录，陛下。如果您打算将这双靴子*据为己有*的话——这是您应得的。您比任何人都更配穿上这双靴子！

En: Omit the boots.
Zh: 省略掉关于靴子的事。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Note. The boot has a hidden serial number on it. He crouches, twisting the dead man's foot: "It's E50.100.1000..."
Zh: “注意。靴子上藏着一行编号。”他俯身扭动死者的脚：“编号是E50.100.1000……”

En: The boot has a serial number. He twists the dead man's foot: "It's E50.100.1000. The lines between the plates are in the shape of the alphanumerical. The number is purposefully concealed by the design."
Zh: “靴子上有一串编号。”他扭动死者的脚：“编号是E50.100.1000。装甲板之间的线条呈现出了字母数字组合。盔甲的设计有意掩盖了这串编号。”

En: Oops. A bad feeling comes over you...
Zh: 唉呀，一股不怎么样的滋味涌上心头……

En: Which the assistant detective *inexplicably* reported to me as X5415- something. Why was that, assistant detective?
Zh: “有位担任助手的警探*莫名其妙地*向我报了一串X51415之类的编号。为什么要那么做呢，警探助手？”

En: It was merely a *jest* -- horseplay! The impression of you being *a total psychopath* is wrong.
Zh: 那只是*开玩笑*——恶作剧而已！把你当成*谎话连篇的心理变态*真是天大的误会。

En: A light-hearted jest -- locker room banter. Boys will be boys.
Zh: “那只是毫无恶意的玩笑——就像是在更衣室里扯淡。调皮捣蛋毕竟是爷们儿的天性嘛。”

En: I wanted to see what happens if I say it *wrong*.
Zh: “我想知道如果我说*错*了会怎样。”

En: I'm a total psychopath, you see. I lie all the time.
Zh: “我根本就是心理变态，你也看出来了。我总是谎话连篇。”

En: It *doesn't* say XBL47GH7456990JKLL303... (continue making up random numbers and letters)?! But lieutenant, this is impossible!
Zh: “编号*难道*不是XBL47GH7456990JKLL303……（继续编造随机的字母与数字组合）？！警督，这不可能啊！”

En: Yes -- and now this happened. I am perplexed. Officer, please write down the right serial number: E50.100.1000.
Zh: “是啊——但这就是现实。我感到非常困惑。警官，请记录正确的编号：E50.100.1000。”

En: Bitches are at it. This pleases Cuno.
Zh: “贱人们开始内讧了。坤诺就喜欢这一出。”

En: I fail to see *any* humour in it. Officer, please write down the right serial number: E50.100.1000.
Zh: “这*一点*都不好笑。警官，请如实记录正确的编号：E50.100.1000。”

En: That's wonderful, officer. My opinion of you has lowered. Go ahead, write down a stupid nonsensical number in the autopsy form too -- instead of E50.100.1000.
Zh: “很好，警官。我对你的看法变得更糟糕了。请便吧，在尸检记录表里填上这串荒谬愚蠢的数字——而不是E50.100.1000。”

En: It's quite possible. You lied to me for no good reason. Please write down the right serial number: E50.100.1000.
Zh: “很有可能。毕竟你毫无缘由地对我说谎了。请记录正确的编号：E50.100.1000。”

En: Write: AO5577789RHG9999...
Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Tattoos. He stands up, feet planted on either side of the body.
Zh: “纹身。”他站起身，脚跨到了尸体的两侧。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: The deceased has a cargo lashing belt around his neck, tied with a hangman's knot. Colour -- yellow; length -- three metres. There is a buckle on the other end.
Zh: “死者的脖子上系着一根货物系固带，打着绞刑结。颜色——黄；长度——三米。另一端有个搭扣。”

En: The deceased has a cargo lashing belt around his neck, tied with a hangman's knot. Colour -- yellow; length -- three metres. *Vermillion spirits* is printed on the side.
Zh: “死者的脖子上系着一根货物系固带，打着绞刑结。颜色——黄；长度——三米。*弗米利恩烈酒*被印在侧面。”

En: The deceased has a belt for airlifting cargo around his neck, tied with a hangman's knot. Colour -- yellow; length -- three metres. There is a buckle on the other end.
Zh: “死者的脖子上系着一根空运货带，打着绞刑结。颜色——黄；长度——三米。另一端有个搭扣。”

En: The deceased has a belt for airlifting cargo around his neck, tied with a hangman's knot. Colour -- yellow; length -- three metres. *Vermillion spirits* is printed on the side.
Zh: “死者的脖子上系着一根空运货带，打着绞刑结。颜色——黄；长度——三米。另一端有个搭扣。”

En: The deceased has a steel reinforced cargo belt around his neck, tied with a hangman's knot. Colour -- yellow; length -- three metres. There is a buckle on the other end.
Zh: “死者的脖子上缠着一根带钢筋的货物绑带，打着绞刑结。颜色——黄色；长度——三米。另一端有搭扣。”

En: Write it down -- underline *Vermillion*.
Zh: 记录下来——下划线*弗米利恩*。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: He produces a measuring tape: "Well nourished, athletically built measuring 1.80 metres. Generally consistent with age -- about 50. Preservation is good, ambient temperature below freezing."
Zh: 他拿出卷尺:“发育良好，体格健壮，一米八零。年龄大概在――五十岁。保存完好，常温低于冰点。”

En: He produces a measuring tape: "Well nourished, athletically built measuring 1.80 metres. Generally consistent with age 42. Preservation is good, ambient temperature below freezing."
Zh: 他拿出卷尺：“发育良好，体格健壮，身高一米八零。外观特征基本符合四十二岁。尸体保存良好，环境温度低于冰点。”

En: The spine is bent more than the lieutenant compensated for. This buster's actual height is about 1.85. And no, you could not take him *straight on*.
Zh: 警督低估了尸体脊椎的弯曲程度。这个壮汉的身高约为一米八五。不用试，你是无法把他*掰直*的。

En: I'm going to write 1.85 metres lieutenant.
Zh: “我打算写一米八五，警督。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Of course, I under-compensated. He pockets the tape.
Zh: “可以，我没算准。”他把卷尺塞回了口袋里。

En: Body hair is light brown, distribution is consistent with age. He kneels in to get a better look: "The deceased had male pattern baldness. Hair is combed back, short."
Zh: “体毛为浅棕色，毛发分布情况与年龄相符。”他跪到地上仔细观察：“死者有男性型脱发迹象。头发向后梳起，短发。”

En: Touch the corpse's hair before moving on.
Zh: 在进一步调查之前，先摸一下死者的头发。

En: Write it down: Short, light brown, male pattern baldness.
Zh: 写下：短发，浅棕色，男性型脱发。

En: The hair under your latex fingers feels cold to touch, wet.
Zh: 隔着一层橡胶手套，你仍能感到尸体的头发冰冷且潮湿。

En: His hair feels wet, soaked with rain, cold to touch. Not that different from a living person -- after a swim.
Zh: 他的头发湿漉漉的，早已被雨水淋透，触感冰冷。摸起来与活人——在游泳之后，没多大区别。

En: The stench is suffocating. Strands of dark brown hair start sticking to the latex of the glove -- like thread off a rag doll's head.
Zh: 这股恶臭令人窒息。几缕已经变成深棕色的头发正粘在橡胶手套上——如同从布娃娃头顶脱落的线。

En: Stroke his hair.
Zh: 抚摸他的头发。

En: The stench is suffocating. Strands of dark brown hair start sticking to your hand, like thread off a rag doll's head.
Zh: 这股恶臭令人窒息。几缕已经变成深棕色的头发正粘在你的手上——如同从布娃娃头顶脱落的线。

En: There's brilliantine in there. He's combed his hair back with oil.
Zh: 他抹了润发油。他是在抹油之后才向后梳起了头发。

En: There must be brilliantine in there. He's combed his hair back with oil.
Zh: 他一定抹了润发油。他是在抹油之后才向后梳起了头发。

En: Keep petting him.
Zh: 继续抚摸。

En: There there, baby.
Zh: “乖乖，宝贝儿。”

En: The lieutenant watches you, in silence.
Zh: 警督望着你，一言不发。

En: More hair sticks to the glove -- hair off the rain soaked head of a dead man. There are bumps and dips on the skull below. An alien landscape.
Zh: 更多头发粘到了手套上——来自一具尸体被雨淋透的头部。在这副皮囊之下，是他坑坑洼洼的头骨。真是一幅异世般的景象。

En: More hair sticks to your hand -- hair off the rain soaked head of a dead man. There are bumps and dips on the skull. An alien landscape.
Zh: 更多头发粘到了你的手上——来自一具尸体被雨淋透的头部。在这副皮囊之下，是他坑坑洼洼的头骨。真是一幅异世般的景象。

En: There-there dead man, you were someone's child. It's all over now.
Zh: 好了，好了。死者，你也曾是父母爱的结晶啊。一切都过去了。

En: Write it down, adding the brilliantine.
Zh: 就这么写，补充上润发油的部分。

En: That was some sick shit, *sekopää*.
Zh: “你可真恶心，*癫子*。”

En: Khm, he coughs. "Lividity is consistent with hanging -- albeit *faint*, as noted. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head, chest, and thighs -- indicating stones thrown; low velocity..."
Zh: “咳，”他咳嗽着。“尸斑符合绞死的特征——尽管很微弱，如前所述。头部充血。头部、胸部以及大腿处有挫伤——说明发生过石刑；低速度……”

En: Khm, he coughs. "Lividity is consistent with *hanging*. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head, chest, and thighs -- consistent with stones thrown; low velocity..."
Zh: “咳，”他干咳了一声。“尸斑符合*绞死*的特征。头部充血。头部、胸部和大腿处有多处挫伤——符合被石头砸过的表征；低速……”

En: Write down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Fucking *low* velocity!? The kid explodes. "You think Cuno doesn't know what you're talkin' bout? Velocity was FUCKING MAX!"
Zh: “去你妈的*低*速！？”这孩子突然爆发了。“你以为坤诺不知道你在说什么吗？那速度已经到！极！限！了！”

En: In addition there are contusion marks on the face, scalp, and chest. Consistent with predation. He remarks to you: "We have those now, for part 3."
Zh: “此外，面部、头部及胸部都有咬痕，与捕食者相吻合。”他对你说道:“关于第三部分，我们已经得出了这些结论。”

En: The lieutenant pays no heed. "In addition, there are bitemarks on the face, scalp, and chest. Consistent with predation."
Zh: 警督没理会他。“此外，脸上、头皮及胸部有多处咬痕，符合遭遇动物掠食的表征。”

En: Talkin' shit about Cuno's velocity...
Zh: “居然敢喷坤诺的速度……”

En: Write down, but amend for *high* velocity.
Zh: 就这么写，但是改成*高*速。

En: The steel wiring... he concedes -- breathless, "there's too much of it. We *need* to remove the belt so we can get to the ligature mark."
Zh: 警督在熏天的臭气中努力了半天，但绑带的材料显然无法被切断。“这些钢丝……”他屏住呼吸，宣告放弃，“太密集了。我们*需要*解开这根绑带，才能看到勒痕。”

En: Ligature mark... The lieutenant produces a small folding knife. With the other hand pulling on the belt, he starts cutting into the polyester. The stench is horrid. After a while, it's obvious the material cannot be cut.
Zh: “勒痕……”警督掏出一把小折刀。他一手拽着绑带，一手尝试切割外层的聚酯纤维。

En: May I try?
Zh: “能让我试试吗？”

En: Let's go get them. [Leave.]
Zh: “咱们去拿吧。”[离开。]

En: It's no use. We should get *chaincutters* -- you can try with them. I have a pair in the toolbox of my Kineema.
Zh: “刀是没用的。我们得去拿一把*切链钳*——你就等拿到它再试吧。我在锐影的工具箱里放了一把。”

En: What happens now?
Zh: “现在怎么办？”

En: We get chaincutters, then try with those. I have a pair in the toolbox of my Kineema.
Zh: “我们先去拿切链钳，再试试它的威力。我在锐影的工具箱里放了一把。”

En: Damn corpse. We had a good rhythm going... The lieutenant gets up and brushes his pants.
Zh: “倒霉的尸体。原本调查的节奏很顺利的……”金起身掸了掸裤子。

En: We need to cut the belt to see the ligature mark below. Carefully -- with as much *precision* as you can.
Zh: “我们得切断绑带才能看到下面的勒痕。认真点——尽可能做得*干净利落*一些。”

En: Cut the belt off.
Zh: 切断绑带。

En: Stupid argument with Cuno.
Zh: 和坤诺的愚蠢争论。

En: Visual Calculus suggests knot.
Zh: 见微知著暗示看看打结方式。

En: Interfacing gave instructions.
Zh: 能工巧匠做了说明。

En: Agreement with Cuno.
Zh: 赞同坤诺。

En: Look for a good spot to cut.
Zh: 寻找理想的下手位置。

En: After some deliberation you sink the cutters into the knot tying the belt together. You squeeze the rubber handles together, sweat forming on your brow...
Zh: 经过一番考虑，你把切链钳塞到了系着绑带的死结下方，用力紧握橡胶把手，汗水逐渐渗出额头……

En: You jam the cutters right under the knot -- that seems like a smart idea. Yeah, *somewhere there*. Already they're buried deep in the man's flesh. Then you rotate them to get a better hold. Then...
Zh: 你把切链钳塞到死结下方——应该是个不错的主意。是啊，*反正总要用个动手的地方*。绑带已经深深陷入死者的脖颈。你转动着工具，试图找一个舒适的握持姿势。接着……

En: C, my pig is gonna fuck his head off!
Zh: “C，我的猪猡要把他的脑袋切掉了！”

En: No he ain't. C looks blasé. "Your pig's a boring fuck."
Zh: “他才不会那么干呢。”C一脸不以为然。“你的猪猡无聊透了。”

En: I'm not your pig, Cuno.
Zh: “我不是你的猪猡，坤诺。”

En: You are, he says with calm certainty. "You're Cuno's pig."
Zh: “你已经是了，”他气定神闲，胸有成竹。“你就是坤诺的猪猡。”

En: The belt is equally tight around the whole circumference of his neck, swelling over the edges like white bread, rising from the yeast.
Zh: 死者的整个脖颈都被绑带紧紧缠绕，绑带边缘的腐肉就像发酵的白面包一样肿胀着。

En: Would it be a good idea to cut the knot, instead of the belt? Seems the more *conservative* approach...
Zh: 与切断绑带相比，切断死结似乎更明智一些？这是更加*保守*的做法……

En: The knot is the weak spot. The chaincutters fit in there. Steady now, like a flower arranger -- two cuts and it should come loose.
Zh: 这个死结即是弱点。切链钳可以塞到下面。像插花一样稳准——只需两钳即可解开。

En: Concentrate on the belt, not on who is  whose pig. There is no pig. There are only the chaincutters -- and the belt.
Zh: 把注意力集中在绑带上，这不是讨论谁是谁的猪猡的时候。这里根本就没有猪，这里只有切链钳——还有这根绑带。

En: *Snap!* The knot is slashed. Another cut and the belt falls apart like a flower bouquet, revealing the dead man's neck -- and the dark red ligature mark around it.
Zh: *啪！*死结应声而断。在下一钳之后，绑带就如同花束一般散落在地，暴露出死者的脖颈——以及一圈暗红色的勒痕。

En: Press down...
Zh: 向下按……

En: Rotate some more!
Zh: 再转一转！

En: Enough, cut some!
Zh: 好了，动手吧！

En: Fuckady-fuck...
Zh: “我就操了……”

En: The lieutenant looks by, somewhat worried as you summon power words to your aid.
Zh: 警督看着你，你为鼓劲而喊出的狠话多少有些令他担忧。

En: I should have a go first -- I think I have a strategy. He steps in and takes the chaincutters from you.
Zh: 他从你手中拿过切链钳。“我应该先上的——我有个办法。”

En: Yeah, fuck him! FUCK THAT FÄGÄRI! Corpse fucking time!
Zh: “耶，干他！干那个死基佬！奸尸现场！”

En: Told you my pig was hard core.
Zh: “我跟你说过我的猪猡特别硬核。”

En: A really, really bad smell is coming from there now. And... some kind of cracking sound.
Zh: 那里突然传来一股非常，非常糟糕的味道。还有……某种裂开的声音。

En: Let him work.
Zh: 让他上吧。

En: No, give them back, I was just about to cut it off.
Zh: “不行，还给我，我马上就要弄断了。”

En: His head, yes -- you were about to cut off his head. He sinks the cutters into the knot, preparing to perform the cuts -- with his elbow to his knee for precision.
Zh: “弄断他的头吗，是啊——你差点就要让他掉脑袋了。”他把钳子塞进了死结下面，准备动手——为求精确，他把手肘压在了膝盖上。

En: He sinks the cutters into the knot, preparing to perform the cuts -- with his elbow to his knee for precision.
Zh: 他把钳子塞进了死结下面，准备动手——为求精确，他把手肘压在了膝盖上。

En: The rope rises to a point, leaving a *gap* in the ligature mark. The suspension point is in the back of the neck. The lieutenant has kneeled closer, running his finger along the dark red groove -- until there's a gap.
Zh: 警督跪到了离尸体更近的地方，手指摸索着暗红色的痕迹——直到一处缺口。“绳子升到一定高度后，在勒痕中留下了一个*缺口*。绞刑的悬挂点就在脖子后面。”
