En: Haemorrhaging is observed on the skin above and below the ligature mark. It is well pronounced, consistent with a drop from 1 or 1.5 metres. He wipes his forehead. "That's a visual on the injury for part 3."
Zh: “勒痕下方的皮肤有大量出血，痕迹非常明显，与一米至一米五的落差相吻合。”他擦着前额。“这就是第三部分的伤口验视情况。”

En: Haemorrhaging is observed on the skin above and below the ligature mark. The mark is well pronounced, consistent with a drop from 1 or 1.5 metres.
Zh: “勒痕上下方的皮肤都发生了大出血。痕迹非常明显，符合一米或一米五的落差。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Chest is intact. He presses down on it. "Normal contour. Abdomen is protuberant, pelvis intact. Genitalia..." He pulls down the man's underpants.
Zh: “胸部完整。”他按着那里。“轮廓正常。下腹部隆起，骨盆无伤。生殖器……”他拉下了死者的内裤。

En: *NOW* IT'S GONNA HAPPEN! C!!!
Zh: “他们*终于*要搞基了！C！！！”

En: I fuckin' knew it!
Zh: “老娘就知道！”

En: This is clearly what they've been waiting for. Ever since the autopsy began. The lieutenant is trying to make it as boring as possible.
Zh: 显然，自从尸检开始，这就是他们一直在期待的场面。警督正在尽量轻描淡写此事，争取能一笔带过。

En: Genitalia is male and unremarkable. No evidence of injury.
Zh: “男性生殖器，无明显特征。无伤痕。”

En: Inspect the genitals.
Zh: 检查尸体的生殖器。

En: Write it down -- add semen sample.
Zh: 记录下来——添加精液样本。

En: Write it down -- amend toxicology for a semen sample.
Zh: 记录下来——为精液样本做毒理测试。

En: Write it down and move on.
Zh: 就这么写，赶紧进行下一步。

En: The dead man's penis is average-sized, congested from the downward collection of blood. The testicles are uneven in length, hanging underneath. The genitalia is greenish; marbling is present around the crotch.
Zh: 死者的阴茎尺寸中等，和整个下体一样都在充血。挂在下面的睾丸大小不一致。生殖器的颜色泛绿；股间出现了大理石状纹理。

En: Does it look like he was *enjoying* his moment of death?
Zh: “他看上去像不像*享受过*死亡的一刻？”

En: Ah, yes -- your hunch, before. We can have a semen analysis requested -- from Processing -- if that's what you meant.
Zh: “啊，对了——你之前提到过这种直觉。我们可以请求进行一次精液检测——那是分析部门的职责——如果你是那个意思的话。”

En: Ah, yes -- your hunch, before. He takes a moment to breathe. "We could have a semen analysis requested from Processing, but we're already requesting toxicology. There's a one test limit."
Zh: “啊，对了——你之前提到过这种直觉。”警督长出了一口气。“我们可以请求分析部门进行一次精液检测，但我们已经请求进行毒理学检测了，只能进行一种检测。”

En: Mhm. Just write down that we request an analysis.
Zh: “唔。就写我们请求进行一次检测吧。”

En: Yes. Processing. Science. That's what I meant.
Zh: “没错，分析部门，科学。我就是这个意思。”

En: No, I was trying to get a *psychic* read.
Zh: “不，我当时是在尝试*通灵*。”

En: No you weren't, officer -- that would be preposterous. He shakes his head. "Just write down that we request a semen, vaginal, and anal fluids analysis."
Zh: “没那回事，警官——那也未免太荒唐了。”他摇了摇头。“就写我们请求进行一次精液，鞘膜及肛门体液检测。”

En: Touch it.
Zh: 摸一摸。

En: Doesn't feel like anything. A cold spot under your gardening gloves, unremarkable and un-alive.
Zh: 毫无感触。你透过园艺手套只能感受到了一片冷淡，死气沉沉，不值一提。

En: Doesn't feel like anything. Just cold flesh under your finger, unremarkable and un-alive.
Zh: 毫无感触。你的手指只摸到了一块冰冷的肉而已，死气沉沉，不值一提。

En: My pig's so *FUCKIN' ILL* right now... His voice swells with pride.
Zh: “瞧吧，我的猪猡*真他妈病得不轻*……”他的声音充满了骄傲。

En: Ya pig's pretty sick, I gotta say...
Zh: “我服了，你的猪猡确实有病……”

En: This wasn't necessary. What's going on here? Why are you doing that?
Zh: 这完全没必要。怎么回事？你为什么要做那种事？

En: I'm detecting. Police work. Doesn't have to be *solicited*.
Zh: 我在办案。这是警察的职责。没必要*征求意见*。

En: It's an experiment. To see what happens.
Zh: 这是一次实验。我想知道这么做会发生什么。

En: The lieutenant is unaware of what's happening in your head and why you're touching it. He merely expects you to write down his notes on the man's genitalia so you can move on.
Zh: 警督不清楚你的想法，也不理解你为什么要摸一摸。他原本只指望你能如实记录他口述的关于死者生殖器的内容，这样就行下一步了。

En: The lieutenant looks relieved. "Back is symmetrical and intact. Upper and lower extremities are intact -- but asymmetrical. There are old combat injuries on the right hand, thigh, and hip."
Zh: 金的表情如释重负：“背部匀称完整。四肢完整——但不对称。右手、大腿及臀部有战斗中留下的旧伤疤。”

En: Back is symmetrical and intact. He struggles to turn the corpse on his side. "Upper and lower extremities are intact, but asymmetrical. There are combat injuries on the right hand, thigh, and hip."
Zh: “背部匀称完整。”他用力翻转尸体。“四肢完整——但不对称。右手、大腿及臀部有战斗中留下的旧伤疤。”

En: Bullets have bitten little pieces out of him. It must have been excruciating, especially the hip... Before you is a temple of pain that knew little tenderness in life.
Zh: 枪林弹雨撕扯掉了他的血肉。每道伤口都伴随着阵阵剧痛，尤其是臀部上的那些……你面前是一座与世间温柔无缘的痛苦殿堂。

En: In addition, I see smaller, residual scars -- too numerous to count, covering about 30% of his skin.
Zh: “另外，我还看到了面积更小的旧伤疤——多到数不清，大约覆盖了整体皮肤30%的面积。”

En: Last item -- hands. He takes the man's right hand in his, inspects it -- then moves on to the other hand...
Zh: “最后一项——双手。”他握起死者的右手，观察了一会儿——接着又开始观察左手……”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: From wounds sustained over two, maybe more decades. Dispersal and accumulation indicates long and active combat duty.
Zh: 这些都是二十年前，甚至更久之前的旧伤。伤口的分布和积累情况显示他曾长期活跃于战场。

En: Pick up the hand.
Zh: 握起死者的手。

En: Let the lieutenant work alone.
Zh: 让警督独自检查。

En: Hands are clean. He inspects the wrist. "No sign of a recent struggle."
Zh: “双手都很干净。”他检查着手腕部位。“没有近期挣扎的迹象。”

En: The hand feels heavy, filled with decay liquids. Like it's ready to explode, if squeezed hard enough. You're suddenly repulsed -- so much so you feel compelled to drop it.
Zh: 死者的手相当沉重，里面充满了腐败的体液，感觉只要用力一挤就会爆开。你突然感到一阵恶心——强烈的冲动驱使你甩开了这只手。

En: Pleased to meet you. Where are you from and what's your name?
Zh: 很高兴见到你。你是哪里人？叫什么名字？

En: The hand is surprisingly heavy to lift, filled with decay liquids. Feels as though it could explode, if squeezed harder. You're suddenly repulsed -- so much so you feel compelled to drop it.
Zh: 死者的手出人意料地沉重，里面充满了腐败的体液，感觉只要再稍微用力一挤就会爆开。你突然感到一阵恶心——强烈的冲动驱使你甩开了这只手。

En: My name is...
Zh: 我的名字是……

En: I'm from...
Zh: 我来自……

En: What the...
Zh: “搞什么……”

En: I'm only fucking with you! I know where you're from -- from Coppodocia! And your name is *Il Cobo*. What can I do you for, Il Cobo di Coppodocia?
Zh: 我跟你逗着玩呢！我知道你是从哪儿来的——你是条之多西亚人！你的名字就叫*小条之*。有何贵干啊，条之多西亚的小条之？

En: Nothing. I got nothing.
Zh: 不，我无可奉告。

En: Pleased to meet you -- are you private military by any chance? Krenel, maybe? I just want to be sure...
Zh: 很高兴认识你——你会不会是私人雇佣兵呢？科奈尔的？我只想确认一下……

En: Pleased to meet you -- are you private military by any chance? I just want to be sure...
Zh: 很高兴见到你——你会不会碰巧是干私人佣兵这一行的？我只是想确认一下……

En: It's good to hold your hand -- did you like it when I stroked your hair?
Zh: 和你握手的感觉不错——抚摸你的头发让你感觉如何？

En: Nothing is as nothing does! This was pleasurable. I sincerely thank you for touching my hand.
Zh: 我看你不是无可奉告，而是大脑空空吧！这是一次愉快的会面。衷心感谢你握了我的手。

En: We've met before, I've touched your genitals.
Zh: 我们以前见过面的，我还摸过你的生殖器。

En: That you have, Cobo, that you have! We're real tight that way. In that special, special way...
Zh: 你确实摸过，条之，你确实摸了！我们的关系可真是亲密。还是非常特殊的那种亲密……

En: Do you need the assistance of private security services? Cobo, I'm afraid even *we* can't protect you from what's after you... The Great Shadow.
Zh: 你需要私人安保服务吗？条之，恐怕就连*我们*也保护不了你，毕竟追赶你的是……无尽的黑暗。

En: I did, Cobo, I did. Reminded me of when I was just a small boy. Before *this* happened to my face and my body. You did me a kindness there.
Zh: 是的，条之，正是如此。这让我想起了小时候。在我的脸和身体变成*这样*之前。你对我有恩。

En: Pleasure's all mine -- tell me, are you a temple of pain and a stranger to tenderness?
Zh: 这是我的荣幸——告诉我，你是一座痛苦的殿堂吗？你不知世间的温柔为何物吗？

En: We should do this more often. Be close like this, I mean.
Zh: 我们应该多做做这种事。我指的是亲近一下。

En: Hands are clean, the lieutenant concludes. The dead man's fingers slip from your hand, cold and sausage-like. "No sign of injury from struggling."
Zh: “双手都很干净。”警督总结道。死者的手指从你手中滑落，冰冷，肿得像香肠一样。“没有挣扎造成的伤痕。”

En: Hands are clean, the lieutenant says as five cold, sausage-like fingers slip from your hand. "No sign of injury from struggling."
Zh: “双手都很干净。”在警督总结的同时，五根冰冷，肿得像香肠一样的手指从你手中滑落。“没有挣扎造成的伤痕。”

En: Were we expecting any?
Zh: “我们难道还指望着能找到伤痕吗？”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Whooh... He turns to the side to breathe -- it's not enough. He buries his face in the sleeve of his jacket. You hear a muffled voice: "That's *all* for the external. Well done! What next?"
Zh: “呼……”他把头扭到一边才能呼吸——但这还不够。他还得把脸埋进夹克的袖子里。你听到他从袖子中发出沉闷的声音：“体外检查*结束*了。干得好！接下来呢？”

En: I was. Maybe I'm just not seeing them... Honestly, the stench is making it hard for me to think at the moment.
Zh: “我确实指望过。也许我只是没发现……说实在的，现场的这股恶臭让我很难思考。”

En: Working so close to the fumes coming from the corpse must be hard. You realize suddenly that the lieutenant has been barely keeping it together these past two items.
Zh: 在尸体散发的腐臭气息中工作着实艰难无比。你突然意识到警督是咬紧牙关才勉强坚持完成了这两项检查。

En: You *get* me, Cobo. I feel like you were once for tenderness and kisses yourself... but then shit went *south* and now you're ahead of even me on the pain front!
Zh: 你*理解*我，条之。我感觉你也曾向往那片温柔乡……但事与愿*违*，如今的你在痛苦的前线上冲得比我还靠前了！

En: Central nervous system, he says and then concludes abruptly: "I have nothing. Do you have anything on this man's central nervous system?"
Zh: “中枢神经系统。”他唐突地欲言又止：“我对此一无所知。关于死者的中枢神经系统，你有什么见解吗？”

En: I'll write N/A.
Zh: “我就写未检测吧。”

En: Of course: there is a moral to be drawn from it -- a moral to this story!
Zh: 当然：你要揭示其中的寓意——这是故事的寓意所在！

En: If I may add the *moral* of this story...
Zh: “请让我补充一下这个故事的*寓意*……”

En: What would that be? He looks at you inquisitively.
Zh: “怎样的寓意？”他好奇地望着你。

En: The dead man looks too, with barely contained excitement to hear the moral of his story.
Zh: 死者也望着你，他也迫切想知道自己的故事究竟有着怎样的寓意。

En: The brain is very vulnerable to compromises in its blood supply.
Zh: “大脑易因供血不足而受损。”

En: I think that may well be the moral of *every* story, officer. The lieutenant grins.
Zh: 警督咧着嘴笑了：“我认为天下*所有的*故事都应该有这层寓意，警官。”

En: Thank you for lightening his mood. It's hard down there.
Zh: 感谢你让他轻松了一点。眼下的工作实在是艰难。

En: Good. Muscoskeletal. Purge fluid is coming from the mouth. He gets close to the mouth-hole, eyes squinting from the stench: "Not injury related. Eyes and tongue protuberant. Hyoid bone... let's see."
Zh: “很好，骨骼肌。嘴里流出了尸液。”他靠近死者的嘴部，眼睛因为那股恶臭眯了起来：“没有损伤，眼睛和舌头凸出，舌骨……我看看。”

En: Muscoskeletal -- purge fluid is coming from the mouth. He gets close to the swollen mouth-hole, eyes squinting from the fumes: "Not injury related. Eyes and tongue protuberant. Hyoid bone... let's see."
Zh: “肌肉及骨骼——尸体的口中流出腐液。”他靠近尸体浮肿的嘴巴，被尸臭熏得睁不开眼：“与伤口无关。双眼及舌头凸起。舌骨……等我检查一下。”

En: With his eyes almost closed, the lieutenant puts his hand on the dead man's throat and begins to massage it, gently. A rotting smell erupts from the mouth. Purge fluid runs down his lips -- black and viscous.
Zh: 警督几乎闭上了双眼，把手伸进了死者的喉咙，轻轻按摩着那里。尸体口中喷出一股腐烂的恶臭。腐液随之顺着嘴唇流淌——乌黑粘稠。

En: You hear cracks as the lieutenant moves his sharp fingers inside the flesh. Like the creaking of an old house at night.
Zh: 当警督将尖细的手指插入尸体中时，你听到了某种东西破裂的声音。如同古老的房屋在夜晚吱嘎作响。

En: The hyoid bone is *fractured*, he says after a while. "The rest of the muscoskeletal system is intact, unremarkable."
Zh: “舌骨*折断了*，”他过了一会儿才说出这句话。“其他肌肉及骨骼系统完整，无明显特征。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Nope. Write: N/A.
Zh: “没有。”写下：未检测。

En: Yeah, jack that fucker off!
Zh: “耶，给他撸一管！”

En: Respiratory system... Back hunched -- as if in prayer -- he begins to pry open the dead man's jaws. He stops to exert more force. Both hands are used.
Zh: 他弯着腰——仿佛是在祈祷——接着敲开了死者的上下颚：“呼吸系统……”他暂停了一下，双手施加了更大的力量。

En: Oral cavity shows no lesions. The victim has received dental implants, possibly after a combat wound. Mouth swollen, haemorrhaging present in mucose of the lips and mouth.
Zh: “口腔未受损伤。死者曾接受过人工植牙，可能是因在战斗中受过的伤。嘴巴肿胀，嘴唇及口腔粘膜有出血迹象。”

En: Look inside the dead man's mouth.
Zh: 查看死者口腔内部。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: No scream, no sigh of relief rises from the darkness inside. It's humid there, sickly sweet air unlike anything living. You feel like you're about to throw up again, straight in that mouth of his...
Zh: 没有尖叫，黑暗中也没有升起一丝宽慰的叹息。口腔里面很潮湿，甜腻的气息没有任何生机。你感觉自己又想直接吐在他的嘴里了……

En: No scream, no sigh of relief rises from the darkness inside. It's humid there, sickly sweet air unlike anything living. You feel like throwing up, straight in that mouth...
Zh: 口腔深处的黑暗既没传出尖叫声，也没发出如释重负的叹息。那里仍然保持着湿润，散发出一股与活物无关的甜腻气息。你想吐，直接吐进这张嘴里……

En: His flesh is cold. Icy.
Zh: 他的血肉冰冷。

En: From here it looks as though the clown-faced man is screaming. The tendons of his jaw are torn apart, hyoid ripped from the force of the lieutenant's hands.
Zh: 从这个角度看，这个长着一张小丑面孔的男人仿佛正在尖叫。他下巴的肌腱已被撕裂，舌骨也被警督的双手弄断了。

En: Hepatobiliary. N/A. He wipes his brow.
Zh: 他擦了擦额头的汗。“肝胆。未检测。”

En: It's hard. Once more you taste stomach acid in the back of your throat. A contraction -- your throat pumps a little something from your stomach and into your mouth. You're forced to swallow, just to keep looking...
Zh: 太难了，你的喉咙再次泛起了胃酸的滋味。一阵收缩——有什么东西通过喉咙从胃部涌进了嘴巴。你不得不把它咽了下去，继续寻找着……

En: It's hard. You taste stomach acid in the back of your throat. A contraction -- your throat pumps a little something from your stomach and into your mouth. You're forced to swallow, just to keep looking...
Zh: 实在是太艰难了。你已经能感觉到胃酸涌上喉头。随着一阵收缩——胃里的某种东西直接涌进了嘴里。为了继续深入调查，你不得不强行咽下这一口……

En: Look deeper inside.
Zh: 看向更深处。

En: No, you don't. You can keep it in. You can keep *anything* in.
Zh: 不，你不会吐出来的。你能忍住。你能忍受*一切*，

En: You manage to suppress the contractions trying to empty your stomach -- all it takes is concentration. Through it, you see nothing but darkness. More meat and darkness...
Zh: 你尽量抑制住了正在试图通过收缩来清空内部的胃——只要专心致志就行。顺着死者的口腔，你只能看到一片黑暗。那里只有腐肉和黑暗……

En: Look deeper inside.
Zh: 看向更深处。

En: Inside you see darkness. Just a mess of meat and darkness.
Zh: 眼前一片漆黑。那里只有腐肉和黑暗。

En: THERE ARE ANCIENT MYSTERIES DOWN THERE, COBO... ASK ME LATER.
Zh: 远古的神秘深藏其中，条之……以后来问我吧。

En: Haemorrhaging present in mucous, the lieutenant repeats impatiently. He lets go of the jaws. The mouth snaps shut before you.
Zh: 警督松开了他的上下颚。死者在你面前闭紧了嘴巴。“粘膜有出血迹象。”他不耐烦地重复着。

En: Respiratory system, he pries the corpse's mouth back open. "Put it down as -- oral cavity shows no lesions, mouth swollen, haemorrhaging present in mucous of the lips and mouth."
Zh: “呼吸系统，”他撬开了死者的嘴。“就这么写吧——口腔未受损伤，嘴巴肿胀，嘴唇及口腔粘膜有出血迹象。”

En: Write: N/A.
Zh: 写下：未检测。

En: Why? Don't we have anything?
Zh: “为什么？我们不检测了吗？”

En: Are you a hepatobiliary expert? He looks at the corpse's stomach with a mixture of tiredness and disgust.
Zh: 他满脸厌倦地望着尸体的肚子。“你是肝胆方面的专家吗？”

En: *Hepato-* means liver and *-biliary* the gallbladder and bile ducts.
Zh: *肝*指的是肝脏，而*胆*指的是胆囊及胆管。

En: Am I an expert in those things?
Zh: 我是那些玩意儿的专家吗？

En: I don't think so.
Zh: “我觉得自己不是。”

En: Nothing in your alcohol-soaked memory directs to having forensic expertise on either one.
Zh: 你那浸泡在酒精中的记忆与这些器官的司法鉴定无缘。

En: Neither am I.
Zh: “我也不是。”

En: And that's it?
Zh: “那就这样？”

En: That's it.
Zh: “就这样吧。”

En: Same for toxicology and serology - N/A.
Zh: “毒理学和血清学检测也这么写吧——未检测。”

En: *Both*?
Zh: “*都*这么写？”

En: Write: N/A
Zh: 写下：未检测。

En: Unless you have untapped reservoirs of knowledge there?
Zh: “除非你在这些方面还有什么尚未派上用场的知识储备？”

En: *Reservoirs*? No -- but do they take obscure trivia and odd titbits?
Zh: “*储备*？没有——但鲜为人知的小知识和奇闻异事会不会用得上？”

En: Would obscure titbits and odd trivia fit the fields?
Zh: “鲜为人知的小知识和奇闻轶事会不会适用于这一领域？”

En: The completionist in me wonders if there's something we could still do...
Zh: “身为完美主义者，我总觉得好像还有未竟之事……”

En: No.
Zh: “没戏。”

En: We only have one test, as per regulation -- and we already requested semen.
Zh: “按照规定，我们只能申请一次检测——而我们申请的是精液检测。”

En: Pigs requestin' semen like it's no big deal.
Zh: “猪猡申请精液就像家常便饭一样。”

En: I'm not even interested in these boring *mulkkus* anymore. Haven't sucked him off or anything...
Zh: “我对这俩*傻货*已经失去兴趣了。反正他们还没给尸体口一管什么的……”

En: Like a toxicology screening? He looks at the monster. "At this stage, I doubt Processing will find anything, even if he was brimming with cocaine. But still... you should add a request."
Zh: “比如毒理学筛查？”他盯着这个怪物。“到了现在这种阶段，就算他体内充满可卡因，我觉得他们也分析不出什么东西了。但总之……你应该补充一项申请。”

En: Can we change it to toxicology? Seems prudent.
Zh: “我们能把申请换成毒理学吗？慎重起见。”

En: Yes, better to leave it at that. More important.
Zh: “很好，最好保持这样。这更重要。”

En: Add a request then. We'll know if drugs or poisons remain in his blood... He looks at the monster. "At this stage, I doubt Processing will find anything, even if he was *brimming* with it."
Zh: “那就补充一项申请吧。这样就能知道他的血液中有没有毒素或毒品了……”他盯着这个怪物。“到了现在这种阶段，就算他体内*充满*那些东西，我觉得他们也分析不出什么东西了。”

En: Leave it *N/A* then.
Zh: “那就写*未检测*吧。”

En: Write: N/A and add *toxicology requested*.
Zh: 写下：未检测。顺便补充*申请毒理学检测*。

En: Mmm... *brimming* with cocaine.
Zh: 唔……*充满*可卡因。

En: Is he touching it again? What is *wrong* with these *mulkkus*?
Zh: “他又摸起来了？这俩*傻货*到底有什么*毛病*？”

En:
Cardiovascular. The body exhibits heavy lividity. Blood has gathered in the hands, feet, and neck. Hypostasis is visually consistent with a hanging.	“心血管。尸体下肢有尸斑。血液集中在手部、足部和脖子上。尸斑与目测的悬吊特征吻合。
0x0100004500004587	Alternate2	YARD / HANGED MAN AUTOPSY	Kim Kitsuragi	Cardiovascular. The body exhibits some lividity. Blood has gathered in the hands, feet, and neck. Hypostasis is visually consistent with a hanging. Note: lividity is not very well pronounced."
Zh: “心血管。尸体下肢有尸斑。血液集中在手部、足部和脖子上。尸斑与目测的悬吊特征吻合。注意：尸斑不是非常明显。”

En: Cardiovascular. The body exhibits lividity in the lower extremities -- feet, hands, and neck -- visually consistent with a hanging.
Zh: “心血管。尸体的下肢存在大量尸斑——以及双足，双手及颈部——外观符合被吊死的特征。”

En: Let's change it to that, seems more important.
Zh: “还是换成那个吧，感觉更重要。”

En: Right. Let's keep the drug screening then. More info there.
Zh: “好吧，还是保留毒品筛查吧。这样能获得更多情报。”

En: The corpse with his pants down does not have an opinion on the subject. All he has is genitals and a deathly odour.
Zh: 被扒掉裤子的尸体没有参与这一话题的讨论。他通过裸露的生殖器和散发出的腐臭味表达了自己的意见。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Gastrointestinal, he breathes a sigh of approaching relief -- this is the last field on the list. He looks around -- to the ground, the pool of faeces there...
Zh: “胃肠道，”警督为即将解脱而长出一口气——这是表格上的最后一项内容。他环视四周——地面上有一滩粪便……

En: ... then: "Digested semisolid food in stomach -- voilà." He touches the corpse's bloated lower abdomen -- briefly.
Zh: ……接着，他触碰着尸体肿胀的下腹部——很快就松开了手——并说道：“胃中有已消化的半固体食物——瞧吧。”

En: Write it down, omit the *voilà*!
Zh: 就这么写，省略掉*瞧吧*！

En: Write it down, keep the *voilà*!
Zh: 就这么写，保留下*瞧吧*！

En: What's next on the list?
Zh: “下一项是什么？”

En: You find a moment, as the lieutenant inspects the dead man's fingernails. Just a few glances. The pages are filled with a bulky freehand that's nearly illegible...
Zh: 你趁着警督检查死者指甲的空当偷看了几眼。笔记本里满是大片难以辨认的潦草字迹……

En: Ha. (Check out THE MAN WITH THE HOLE IN HIS HEAD.)
Zh: “哈。”（查看“脑子有洞的男人”。）

En: Hmh... (Try to understand what the lieutenant has written about the case at hand.)
Zh: “唔……”（试图辨认警督关于本案的记录。）

En: Ha... no. (Search for something about *you*.)
Zh: “哈……不。”（从中搜寻关于*你*的记录。）

En: Oh, so here it is!
Zh: “啊，原来在这里！”

En: This appears to be a case investigated about three weeks ago -- a workplace accident. The titular character's wife tried to get *compensation*. The company was not forthcoming. It's hard to make out if the hole was fatal.
Zh: 这似乎是大约三周前调查完毕的一起案件——一场工地事故。事件主角的妻子试图索取*赔偿*。而相关企业没有回应。很难证明这个洞是致命的。

En: It's very hard to draw conclusions. All you can make out is that he is in a hurry to solve the case -- the tempo of the handwriting says as much -- and that there are *a lot* of notes in there. Most of them are made prior to arriving at the scene, in preparation.
Zh: 很难得出结论。你只能看出警督急于解决这个案子——他做笔记的速度充分说明了这一点——而且他还做了*大量的*笔记。大多数都是在到达现场之前的准备阶段就做完的。

En: Maybe you're imagining it, but there are some remarks in there -- and they're not all negative! You're referred to as 'the 41st' -- by your station number. And...
Zh: 这可能只是你的想象，但笔记里还有一些对你的评价——而且不都是负面的！你被称为*41号*——根据你的分局命名。还有……

En: What?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: Odd. There doesn't seem to be much about *you* in his notes. As if *you* aren't the sun around which the world revolves? Could it be that the case is not *about* you?
Zh: 怪了。他的笔记里没有多少关于*你*的内容。仿佛你不是被整个世界绕着转的太阳？难道说这个案子和你*无关*？

En: You're probably imagining it -- the notes are *very* hard to decipher at a glance -- so don't draw any conclusions from this, but...
Zh: 这可能只是你的想象——毕竟这些笔记*很难*一眼辨认出来——所以不要急于下结论，但是……

En: That's not good. Trust among officers is extremely important. If you don't improve your rapport, there could be consequences down the line...
Zh: 这可不妙。警官之间的信任是至关重要的，如果你不改善你们之间的关系，后果可能不堪设想……

En: What?
Zh: 怎么了？

En: Better not to know -- back to something else!
Zh: 有些事还是不知为妙——回头是岸！

En: So, where were we on this?
Zh: “对了，我们进行到哪儿了？”

En: It's not a glowing review, sorry to say. 'The 41st' -- as you're referred to -- comes across as somewhat less than competent. It's not *explicitly* stated that the lieutenant doesn't trust you, there's just... nothing positive in there.
Zh: 很遗憾，这些不是充满热情的表彰。“41号”——这是他对你的称呼——给人的印象基本上就是没能耐。虽然警督没有*明确*表达对你的不信任，但这里……根本没有任何正面的评价。

En: And what?
Zh: 还有吗？

En: And 'the 41st' comes off as a moderately competent detective.
Zh: 还有，“41号”看起来像是个还算称职的警探。

En: Maybe you *are* a moderately competent detective?
Zh: 也许你*还算*是个称职的警探？

En: It's not spelled out explicitly, but the lieutenant fears a tear is forming between the liberal-moralist establishment of Revachol -- and her populist citizenry. This populist force, a pot pourri of far right and left, is exemplified in the Débardeurs' Union.
Zh: 虽然没明说，但警督非常担心瑞瓦肖的自由—道德主义当权派与民粹主义民众的分歧日益加剧。这股民粹主义势力是极左与极右阵营调和之后的产物，已在码头工人的工会中崭露头角。

En: Their strike, if allowed to escalate into violence, adds to a growing sense of civil war in the city. He's extremely neutral, even in his notes, but avoiding this escalation is Kim's primary concern when dealing with the situation in Martinaise.
Zh: 如果放任他们的罢工逐步升级为暴动，就会加剧这座城市日益增长的内战冲动。警督就算是在笔记中都表现得极为中立，但在处理马丁内斯的局势时，金最优先考虑的是避免让矛盾升级。

En: Let's see. He tilts his head: "We have: bitemarks, contusions on the head and chest -- and a ligature mark encircling the neck. You'll need three fields. Leave a fourth one too..."
Zh: “来总结一下吧。”他歪着脑袋：“我们已经发现了：头部和胸部的咬痕及挫伤——以及环绕颈部的勒痕。你需要填上这三段。顺便留下第四段的位置……”

En: 1. Bite marks.
2. Contusions.
3. Ligature mark. (Finish the autopsy.)
Zh: “3.勒痕。”（完成尸检。）

En: Turn the page back.
Zh: 翻过这页。

En: What's the fourth injury field for?
Zh: “创伤描述的第四段要留给什么？”

En: Nothing. Just in case.
Zh: “没什么，只是以防万一。”

En: What about the injuries *we* have inflicted?
Zh: “那*我们*造成的伤害呢？”

En: Oh? He looks incredulous: "So *we* inflicted them?"
Zh: “哦？”他露出了难以置信的表情：“你的意思是，这是*我们*造成的伤害？”

En: This is a team effort.
Zh: “这是团队合作的成果。”

En: Okay, *I* have inflicted.
Zh: “好吧，是*我*造成的。”

En: You mean the spin-stabilized munition from a Kiejl A9/90 muzzleloader lodged in his lung?
Zh: “你是指他肺里那颗自旋稳定式子弹吗？从耶基尔A9/90前膛枪里射出去的？”

En: I agree. The waters are muddy enough.
Zh: “我同意，水已经够浑的了。”

En: But... isn't that sloppy?
Zh: “可是……那不是很草率吗？”

En: (Keep it minimal.) "So -- back to injuries?"
Zh: （不提了。）“咱们还是谈谈创伤的事吧。”

En: C, these pigs are fucking corrupt. The boy nods approvingly.
Zh: “C，这些猪猡太他妈腐败了。”男孩赞许地点着头。

En: Why don't you fuck them if you love them so much?
Zh: “你那么爱他们，为什么不和他们来一发呢？”

En: Injuries, he nods.
Zh: “谈创伤吧。”他点点头。

En: Be pedantic, if you like -- it doesn't matter. No one else is going to investigate this man's murder. And if they *do*, such details would only *confuse* them.
Zh: “如果你愿意的话，可以迂腐一些——这不要紧。没人会去调查他的死因。就算有人*调查*，这种细节也只会把他们*搞糊涂*。”

En: Now -- injuries.
Zh: “现在来谈谈创伤吧。”

En: Or the incision on the thorax -- from a chaincutter? Neither was a team effort. And I wouldn't mention them. Muddies the water.
Zh: “还是说你指的是胸口的割伤——由切链钳造成的？两者都不是团队合作的成果。我不打算在报告中提到这种事。这只会把水搅浑。”

En: You mean the incision on the thorax, from a chaincutter? That was not a team effort. And I wouldn't mention it. Muddies the waters.
Zh: “你指的是切链钳在胸口造成的割伤？那可不是团队合作的成果。我不打算在报告中提到这种事。这只会把水搅浑。”

En: That was not a team effort. I just *missed* my shot. Also, let's not mention it. Muddies the water.
Zh: “那可不是团队合作的成果。只是我*打偏了*。此外，咱们别在报告中提这件事了。这只会把水搅浑。”

En: Okay, so there's an incision on the thorax, from a chaincutter. There's a pause. "I wouldn't mention it. Better not to muddy the waters."
Zh: “好吧，胸口确实有一道切链钳造成的割伤。”他停顿了一下。“我不打算在报告中提到这种事。最好别把水搅浑。”

En: Okay. So there's a spin-stabilized munition -- from a Kiejl A9/90 muzzleloader -- lodged in his lung.
Zh: “好吧。有一颗自旋稳定式子弹——从耶基尔A9/90前膛枪里射出——射入了他的肺部。”

En: *And* an incision on the thorax, from a chaincutter. There's a pause. "I wouldn't mention those. Better not to muddy the waters."
Zh: “*以及*一道位于胸口的割伤，由切链钳造成。”他停顿了片刻。“我不打算在报告中提到这种事。最好别把水搅浑。”

En: There's a pause. "I don't think we should mention that. Better not to muddy the waters."
Zh: 他停顿了片刻。“我觉得还是别在报告里提到那件事了。最好别把水搅浑。”

En: I'm afraid we can only fill this in once the belt is removed from his neck and we have visual on the injury.
Zh: “恐怕我们只有在取下他脖子上的绑带，并亲眼看到伤口之后，才能填写这一项。”

En: We need to check the hyoid bone for damage before we can sign off on this. It's a potential fatal injury... Let's do part 2, external examination and then return.
Zh: “在签字提交报告之前，我们还得检查一下舌骨受到的损伤。那里也可能受致命伤……咱们先做体外检查，这个回头再说吧。”

En: You should touch it.
Zh: 你应该摸一摸。

En: He nods. "Head, chest, and scalp bite mark injuries. Predation by birds has caused damage to the body. Odontologist does not need to be consulted."
Zh: 他点点头。“头部，胸部及头皮都被咬伤了。是鸟类的捕食行为对尸体造成了损伤。没必要为此向牙科医生咨询。”

En: So -- the scalp bleeds from a postmortem head injury. A stone. The injury does not have the rim of an early inflammatory response. A perpetrator on the scene has confessed to causing it postmortem...
Zh: “也就是说——死后头部创伤导致了头皮出血。一块石头砸到了头。伤口没有出现早期炎症反应的痕迹。现场的肇事者承认他对尸体造成了伤害……”

En: A dark red abraded ligature mark encircling the neck, with a gap on the nape measuring... let's say seven centimetres. The hyoid bone is fractured, the cervical column intact.
Zh: “颈部皮肤磨损，环绕着一圈暗红色的勒痕，勒痕在后颈有一处缺口，长度……大约7厘米。舌骨折断，颈椎完整。”

En: A: Opinion -- fatal injury.
Zh: “A：观点——是致命伤。”

En: B: Non-fatal, post-mortem.
Zh: “B：非致命伤，死后受创。”

En: And your opinion, officer? Beneath the description there are two boxes waiting to be ticked:
Zh: “有何高见，警官？”描述下方有两个框等待勾选。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Fatal injury by seagulls?
Zh: “海鸥造成的致命伤？”

En: No -- the great skua.
Zh: “不——凶手是大贼鸥。”

En: This man was murdered by seagulls.
Zh: “海鸥才是凶手。”

En: Okay, it's not a fatal injury then.
Zh: “好吧，那就算不上是致命伤了。”

En: Yes, it's... He's expecting for you to finish the sentence.
Zh: “是的，这……”他期待着你把话说完。

En: I don't like this game.
Zh: “我不喜欢这个游戏。”

En: For god's sake, leave the box unticked then. What next?
Zh: “拜托，那就先空着别选了。下面还有什么？”

En: Between the stench of death and the neverending corpse-examination, he's under some stress there. Better not to poke.
Zh: 尸臭与没完没了的尸检让警督承受着巨大的压力。最好别惹他。

En: Agreed -- next injury?
Zh: “同意——下一处伤口？”

En: Non-fatal, post-mortem.
Zh: “非致命伤，死后受创。”

En: That's right. What's next?
Zh: “很好。接下来呢？”

En: Those are mean, yes. But not that mean.
Zh: “那种鸟确实不怎么样。但也到不了这种程度。”

En: No, he was not murdered by seagulls.
Zh: “不，海鸥不可能是凶手。”

En: At MAXIMUM velocity, fucko!
Zh: “老子扔的石头到达了极——限速度，你个白痴！”

En: Has confessed to causing it *at maximum velocity*.
Zh: “肇事者承认他以*极限速度*抛出石头造成了伤害。”

En: The lieutenant's admission has caused great gratitude in Cuno. He is silent with it.
Zh: 警督的认可给足了坤诺面子。他默不作声。

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Coagulated blood sticks to his scalp and chest -- where the countless stones have hit the dead man. Beneath the description of injury, two boxes:
Zh: 死者的胸口和头皮上沾满了凝固的血液——数不清的石头砸到了那些地方。创伤描述下面有两个框：

En: B: Non-fatal, post-mortem.
Zh: “B：非致命伤，死后受创。”

En: A: Opinion -- fatal injury.
Zh: “A：观点——是致命伤。”

En: Right. Next?
Zh: “很好，然后呢？”

En: No, it's not.
Zh: “不，不是致命伤。”

En: Fucking fatal as SHIT, *Seol-man*!
Zh: “特别他妈的致命，你个*首里佬*！”

En: Bitch saying your injuries weren't *fatal*, Cuno. You gonna let it stand?
Zh: “那个贱人说你造成的伤害不*致命*，坤诺。这你还能忍吗？”

En: I do what I want, C. He spits. "Learn it."
Zh: “我爱干嘛就干嘛，C。”他啐了一口。“学着点。”

En: I'll put it down as fatal.
Zh: “我会写成致命伤的。”

En: Okay. Non-fatal, post-mortem.
Zh: “好吧。非致命伤，死后受创。”

En: Don't.
Zh: “别这样。”

En: He's tired and weary and you are better not to test his patience.
Zh: 他筋疲力尽，心烦意乱，你最好别挑战他的耐心。

En: I see haemorrhaging on the skin above and below the ligature mark. Depth of the mark -- one centimetre. No signs of clawing on the neck.
Zh: “勒痕所在的皮肤表里都有出血。痕迹深度——1厘米。没有抓挠颈部的迹象。”

En: Write it down.
Zh: 就这么写。

En: Below the note, two customary boxes wait to be ticked. The man's head jerks to the side -- awaiting your judgement. The ring around his neck is visible.
Zh: 记录下方，有两个等待勾选的常规方框。死者的脑袋猛地歪向一边——等待你的裁判。他脖子上的勒痕清晰可见。

En: Below the note, two boxes wait to be ticked. The dead man's head jerks to the side -- awaiting your judgement. The ring around his neck is visible.
Zh: 记录下方有两个框有待勾选。死者的脑袋猛地歪向一边——等待着你的判断。他脖子上的勒痕清晰可见。

En: A: Opinion -- fatal injury.
Zh: “A：观点——是致命伤。”

En: B: Non-fatal, post-mortem.
Zh: “B：非致命伤，死后受创。”

En: That's it. He cracks an uneasy smile. "We have established cause of death. It's not much -- and it leaves much to be questioned -- but it's a *start*."
Zh: “就这样吧。”他勉强挤出了一丝微笑。“我们已经确定了死因。这算不上是多大的进展——而且还留下了更多谜团——但至少是个*起点*。”

En: Hmh... The lieutenant falls silent, abruptly.
Zh: “嗯……”警督突然陷入了沉默。

En: He is deep in thought, eyes fixed on the bright red ring around the dead man's neck.
Zh: 他正在沉思，目光锁定着死者颈部鲜红的勒痕。

En: A column of cold air encircles you, rising slowly upward in the shape of a courtyard; bordered by cracked plaster, windows, seagulls perched on air conditioning units.
Zh: 一股冷气环绕着你，沿着院子的轮廓缓缓上升；周围满是开裂的灰泥墙体，窗户，以及海鸥栖息的空调外机。

En: A small green enclosure in the middle of a corral of tenements: Capeside Apartments, Whirling-in-Rags and the fortress wall of Terminal B. Above it -- a thin blanket of coastal stratus clouds.
Zh: 在宛如畜栏的公寓楼之间，有一片面积狭窄的绿色围栏：圈起了岬岸公寓、褴褛飞旋和货运站B的围墙。海滨地区特有的海洋层云覆盖其上，如同一张薄毯。

En: Higher yet, Coalition airships guard over the twenty one cordons of the Zone of Control. The air is criss-crossed with radio transmissions, the hair on your neck rises...
Zh: 在云层之上的高空中，漂浮着联盟的飞空艇，那些武装镇守着特别行政区的二十一道警戒线。无线电信号在空气中纵横交错，令你脖子上的汗毛直竖……

En: Was I right?
Zh: 我当时的判断正确吗？

En: Rub your sides, close your eyes.
Zh: 紧闭双眼，摩擦身体两侧。

En: YOU WERE.
Zh: 非常正确。

En: Why do you say that? He tilts his head. "Is it the seagull-murder again, or are you serious?"
Zh: “你为什么这么说？”他歪着脑袋。“又一起海鸥凶杀案，认真的吗？”

En: Why do you say that? He tilts his head. "Is it like that *fatal* stone injury, or are you serious?"
Zh: “你为什么这么说？”他歪着脑袋。“就像那起*致命*的乱石砸伤，认真的吗？”

En: Why do you say that? He tilts his head.
Zh: “你为什么这么说？”他歪着脑袋。

En: I'm serious. I don't think this was the injury that killed him.
Zh: “我是认真的。我不认为这是导致他死亡的创伤。”

En: I just took a whack in the dark.
Zh: “我只是瞎猜的。”

En: Okay. *Why* don't you think it was fatal?
Zh: “好吧。你*为什么*不认为这是致命伤？”

En: Did you? His eyes narrow. "I'm not so sure. I *thought* we could establish the cause of death here, officer. Now I think we should leave it empty."
Zh: “你真是瞎猜的吗？”他眯起了眼睛。“我不太确定。我*本以为*我们这就能确定死因的，警官。可现在我觉得应该保留意见。”

En: I arrived at this conclusion through the *psychic arts*.
Zh: “我是通过*通灵之术*得出这个结论的。”

En: I can't shake the feeling he was doing something *else* when he died.
Zh: “我总感觉他在死亡的一刻肯定是在干*别的事情*。”

En: I don't like the colouration. The lividity did not convince me.
Zh: “我不喜欢这种‘上色’方式。这些尸斑说服不了我。”

En: There's something in me that tells me to be sceptical of appearances here. The footprints were too perfect.
Zh: “我总觉得要对这里的表象持怀疑态度。那片足迹未免太‘完美’了。”

En: Think for a moment. There's time, don't rush.
Zh: 深思熟虑吧。时间很充裕，不要急于下结论。

En: I agree.
Zh: “我同意。”

En: No, this is clearly a fatal injury. Lets mark it as such.
Zh: “不，这显然是致命伤。就这么标注吧。”

En: Now you're just undermining yourself for the sake of it. What's *strange* is -- I agree. I was sure we had our cause of death here, and now I think we should leave it empty. At least for the time being...
Zh: “你这样纯粹就是在故意损害自己的信誉。我同意——这里确实有*蹊跷*。我本以为已经可以确定死因了，可现在我觉得应该保留意见。至少暂时如此……”

En: There is no reason. It's just a random guess.
Zh: “没什么根据。我就是瞎猜的。”

En: I'm not usually one to go for these... hunches. But I think I *agree*. I thought we had our cause of death here, officer... He shakes his head. "Now I think we should leave it empty. At least for the time being."
Zh: “我通常不会相信这种……直觉。但这次我*同意*。我本以为已经可以确定死因了，警官……”他摇摇头。“可现在我觉得应该保留意见。至少暂时如此。”

En: Most everything *else* on the victim says otherwise... He pauses. "But I agree. I was ready to establish a cause of death here, officer, but now I think we should leave it empty. At least for the time being."
Zh: “死者身上*除此之外*的一切迹象都与你的观点相悖……”他就此打住。“但我同意。我本打算就此确认死因的，警官。可现在我觉得应该保留意见。至少暂时如此。”

En: Everything that's physically on the victim says otherwise -- but I agree. I was ready to establish a cause of death, officer -- now I think we should leave it empty. At least for the time being.
Zh: “受害者尸体上的一切迹象都与你的观点相悖——但我同意。我本打算就此确认死因的，警官——可现在我觉得应该保留意见。至少暂时如此。”

En: Still... there may be truth to this one. I was ready to call a probable cause of death here -- but now I think we should leave it empty. At least for the time being.
Zh: “确实……此案可能另有隐情。我本打算就此确定一个可能的死因——可现在我觉得应该保留意见。至少暂时如此。”

En: At least for the time being. He nods. "If we don't like it, I can always amend this later. In my paperwork."
Zh: “暂时如此吧。”他点点头。“如果我们对结论不满意的话，我随时都可以改。毕竟这是我的工作报告嘛。”

En: No-no officer, I'm afraid I disagree now. We should leave the question open -- at least for the time being. If necessary, I can always amend this later. In my paperwork.
Zh: “不-不，警官，恐怕我不能认同。我们应该搁置这个问题——至少暂时如此。如果有必要的话，我随时都可以再修改这些内容。毕竟这是我的工作报告。”

En: HDB41-0803.1111 listens, motionless -- with the cargo belt still around his neck. Only one box remains:
Zh: HDB41-0803.1111听着，一动不动——他的脖子上还挂着运货带。只剩下一个方框了:

En: HDB41-0803.1015 listens, motionless -- with the cargo belt still around his neck. Only one box remains:
Zh: HDB41-0803.1015听着，一动不动——他的脖子上还挂着运货带。只剩下一个方框了:

En: HDB41-0803.1300 listens, motionless -- with the cargo belt still around his neck. Only one box remains:
Zh: HDB41-0803.1300听着，一动不动——他的脖子上还挂着运货带。只剩下一个方框了:

En: HDB41-0803 listens, motionless -- with the cargo belt still around his neck. Only one box remains:
Zh: HDB41-0803听着，一动不动——他的脖子上还挂着运货带。只剩下一个方框了:

En: KK57-0503-0815 listens, motionless -- with the cargo belt still around his neck. Only one box remains:
Zh: 编号为KK57-0503-0815的尸体倾听着你们的对话，一动不动——货物绑带仍然勒着他的脖子。只剩一个框没填了：

En: Lividity is consistent with *hanging* -- albeit *faint*, as noted. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head, chest, and thighs -- consistent with stones thrown postmortem; low velocity...
Zh: “咳，”他咳嗽着。“尸斑符合绞死的特征——尽管很微弱，如前所述。头部充血。头部、胸部以及大腿处有挫伤——说明发生过石刑；低速度……”

En: Lividity is consistent with hanging. The head is congested. Contusions are present on the head, chest, and thighs -- consistent with stones thrown postmortem; low velocity...
Zh: “尸斑符合绞死的特征。头部充血。头部、胸部和大腿处有多处挫伤——符合死后被石头砸过的现实；低速……”

En: Yes, I'm Cuno's pig. I agree.
Zh: “没错，我就是坤诺的猪猡。我承认。”

En: You are, he says with calm certainty. "This pig is Cuno's."
Zh: “你就是，”他气定神闲，胸有成竹。“这头猪猡归坤诺所有。”

En: Being Cuno's pig has a steadying effect on your hand. Go with the flow, man...
Zh: 成为坤诺的猪猡有助于稳住局面。见风使舵吧，朋友……

En: First. How did it go?
Zh: “首先，进展如何？”

En: What now? (Give him back his notebook.)
Zh: “现在我们要做什么？”（将笔记本还给他。）

En: It was a... He looks for the right words. "An irregular field autopsy. We did not establish cause of death, which is supposed to be the goal of an autopsy. But... *personally*, I do not see this as a parameter for success."
Zh: “这是一场……”他琢磨了一下得体的说法。“一场不寻常的尸检。我们尚未确定死因，而这是尸检的目的。但……就*我个人*而言，我不认为这是衡量成功的标准。”

En: What else?
Zh: “还有呢？”

En: Then -- given the state of decay here, I consider establishing the victim's ethnicity a minor success.
Zh: “此外——考虑到尸体的腐败程度，我认为能确定死者的种族就已经能算是小小的成功了。”

En: We were thorough with the list of injuries too -- we described them all in detail. What is there to say? Given the circumstances, it was a professional field autopsy.
Zh: “我们还仔细检查了尸体的伤势——并按照表单做了细致的描述。怎么说呢？鉴于当前的情况，这已经是一次相当专业的现场尸检了。”

En: Well. We established probable cause of death -- some would say that's the *goal* of an autopsy.
Zh: “进展良好。我们已经确定了可能的死因——有人认为这就是尸检的*目的*。”

En: Oh yeah, well done master detective! Maybe a drink is in order?
Zh: 哦耶，干得漂亮，警探宗师！按规矩咱们该喝一杯了吧？

En: Now, you see -- that worries me. He wipes his forehead. "You will die if you drink -- you know that, don't you? You're proving a useful detective, the organization would miss you."
Zh: “说起这个，你瞧——我很担心你的酒瘾。”他擦着额头。“喝酒会要了你的命——你明知道的，不是吗？你充分证明了自己是一位优秀的警探，组织不想失去你这样的人材。”

En: Perhaps a drink is in order -- later, I mean?
Zh: “这种时候是不是该喝一杯——我的意思是，稍后再喝？”

En: We weren't *too* sloppy with the injuries. We did not describe the bitemarks. We should have provided an opinion there. But, given the circumstances -- it is excusable. They were clearly not fatal.
Zh: “我们做的创伤描述并不是*特别*草率。确实，我们没对咬痕进行描述。还应该那里标注上自己的观点。但是考虑到当时的情况——这是有情可原的。那些显然不是致命伤。”

En: We were not *too* sloppy with the injuries. We did not describe the contusions. We did not provide an opinion. But given the circumstances -- it is excusable. And they were clearly not fatal.
Zh: “我们做的创伤描述并不是*特别*草率。确实，我们没对挫伤进行描述。也没有在那里标注自己的观点。但是考虑到当时的情况——这是有情可原的。而且那些显然不是致命伤。”

En: To be honest, we were sloppy with the injuries. The victim has bite marks and contusions. We should have described them in detail, provided an opinion. But what's done is done -- and they're clearly not fatal.
Zh: “说实在的，我们做的创伤描述确实很草率。死者身上有咬痕和挫伤。我们应该对这些做细节描述，并且标注上自己的观点。但如今木已成舟——而且那些显然不是致命伤。”

En: Can't we go back and fill those out?
Zh: “我们不能回头查漏补缺吗？”

En: We can -- but I don't want to.
Zh: “能——但是我不想这样。”

En: The next time you encounter a list of topics with numbers on it -- start with 1. Then 2. Then 3. Lists are numbered for a reason. It is the *intended* order.
Zh: “当你下次遇到一串带编号的题目时——请按编号的顺序依次作答。出题者之所以给它们编号，就是为了让你*按指定顺序*填写。”

En: What if we *missed* something as a result?
Zh: “如果这样会导致我们*错过*什么呢？”

En: We don't always have to be so *original* about everything. Anyway...
Zh: “我们没必要总是如此追求*原创*。总之……”

En: We also requested a toxicological screening -- that was thorough. The results should arrive in a couple of weeks. If we're lucky. I would not hold my breath...
Zh: “我们还提交了毒理学筛查的申请——这是非常缜密的检测。如果我们走运的话，大概过几周就能收到结果。我不怎么指望这个……”

En: We requested a test to be run on the genitals -- that was *de règle*. The results should arrive in a couple of weeks, if we're lucky. I would not hold my breath...
Zh: “我们还提交了对生殖器进行检测的申请——非常*正规*的申请。如果我们走运的话，大概过几周就能收到结果。我不怎么指望这个……”

En: I'm not worried about that.
Zh: “我担心的不是那些。”

En: However, a piece of professional advice. The next time you encounter a list of topics with numbers on them -- start with 1. Then: 2. Then: 3. Lists are numbered for a reason.
Zh: “但我还是要提一条专业的意见。当你下次遇到一串带编号的题目时——请按编号的顺序作答。这些编号的存在是有原因的。”

En: He raises a finger: "It is the *intended* order. Anyway..."
Zh: 他竖起一根手指：“这是为了让人*按指定顺序*作答。总而言之……”

En: What now? (Conclude.)
Zh: “现在该干嘛了？”（结束。）

En: He's thinking: Did I miss something?
Zh: 他正在思考：我是不是错过了什么？

En: Now? He pockets it. "Now we put him in a body bag and I drive him to Faubourg -- for processing..."
Zh: “现在？”他把笔记本装了起来。“现在我们把尸体装进尸袋，我开車把他运到福布——交给分析部门……”

En: You tilt your head, also looking at the corpse.
Zh: 你歪着头，和他一同望着尸体。

En: Why didn't he claw at his neck when they hanged him? And why did they not tie his hands? A big man like this is dangerous.
Zh: “当他们把他吊起来的时候，他为什么没有抓挠自己的脖子？而且他们为什么不捆住他的手呢？像这样的大块头是很危险的。”

En: Why weren't his hands tied? A big man like this -- I would tie his hands when marching him to the gallows?
Zh: “为什么他的双手没被绑起来？像这样的大块头——换成是我把他送上绞刑架的话，肯定得把他的双手绑上吧？”

En: Honestly, I'm not sure there *weren't* marks on his wrists. That part got blurry for me. The *stench...* He covers his mouth. "But you're right. I was ready to call this -- now I think we should leave it empty. At least for the time being."
Zh: “说实在的，我无法确认他的腕部*到底*有没有伤痕。我对那个部位的印象非常模糊。这股*尸臭*……”他捂住嘴。“但你说得对。我本打算就此确定死因的——可现在我觉得应该保留意见。至少暂时如此。”

En: He produces a small black plastic roll from his jacket -- a body bag. "Let's wrap this up. I pronounce this field autopsy over."
Zh: 他从夹克里掏出了一卷黑色的塑料薄膜——那就是尸袋。“收尸吧。我宣布现场尸检结束。”

En: The lieutenant looks at the dead man one more time -- then at his notebook, then at the corpse again...
Zh: 警督又看了尸体一眼——接着又看了看他的笔记本，然后又看了看尸体……

En: ...for processing. He looks at the dead man one more time, then at the slip of red paper in his hand, then at the corpse again.
Zh: “……交给分析部门。”他又看了尸体一眼，接着又看了看手中红色的纸张，接着又看了看尸体。

En: I need a copy of that autopsy form, then I will drive him to Faubourg...
Zh: “我需要那张尸检记录表，之后开車把他运到福布……”

En: Rip out a copy of the autopsy pages.
Zh: 撕下一页尸检记录表。

En: For a fraction of a second you feel something. An irregularity -- but then it's gone, too minute to register in your mind.
Zh: 你在那一瞬间感受到了一丝异常——但这种感觉转瞬即逝，短暂到无法融入记忆之中。

En: What was that, a bump?
Zh: 那是什么，一个肿块？

En: What was what? Brilliantine? There's brilliantine in his hair.
Zh: 那是什么？润发油？他的头发上抹了润发油。

En: Write: 'fucky-fucky'.
Zh: 写下：‘操了-操了’。

En: Write: 'pigs gonna have sex'.
Zh: 写下：‘两头猪猡要性交’。

En: Everything! Here I stand -- completely topless, assaulted by the elements!
Zh: “一切！我立于此地——赤裸上身，承受着元素的侵袭！”

En: None of your business, Kim. Lay off my case.
Zh: “不关你的事，金。不要插手。”

En: Hmm, yes. I didn't want to mention it before, but you really should find your clothes. He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “唔，好吧。我本来不想提这件事的，但你确实应该找到你的衣服。”他盯着自己的笔记本。

En: Losing your paperwork is not a small issue. I'm sorry if I came off as patronizing. He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “弄丢工作报告可不是小事。如果我表现得像是在摆谱的话，抱歉，我并无此意。”他盯着自己的笔记本。

En: Very dramatic. Nevertheless, I would appreciate it if you'd just put your clothes back on. He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “非常激动人心。不过，你如果能把衣服穿上的话，我会感激不尽的。”他盯着自己的笔记本。

En: Very dramatic. Nevertheless, I would appreciate it if you'd at least put your jacket back on. He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “非常激动人心。不过，你如果能把夹克穿上的话，我会感激不尽的。”他盯着自己的笔记本。

En: Very dramatic. Nevertheless, I would appreciate it if you'd at least put your shirt back on. He looks at his notebook.
Zh: “非常激动人心。不过，你如果能把衬衫穿上的话，我会感激不尽的。”他盯着自己的笔记本。

En: We've heard a nom de guerre -- Lely. It's better than nothing. Write it down.
Zh: “我们听说了他的化名——莱利。聊胜于无。记下这个吧。”

En: Good thing we got these *chaincutters*. (Pull out the rubber gripped cutters.)
Zh: “幸好我们带了*切链钳*。”（抽出工具的橡胶把手。）

En: Always good to think ahead. Now... He points to the rope squeezing the dead man's neck.
Zh: “未雨绸缪总会有收获的。那么……”他指向勒着死者脖子的绳索。

En: Search the body *one more time*, thoroughly.
Zh: *再一次*彻底检查尸体。

En: Stop wasting time, you're smart!
Zh: 别再浪费时间了，聪明人！

En: Knows there's a bullet in there.
Zh: 知道里面有颗子弹。

En: Need reason to pull head off.
Zh: 需要个理由把头拔下来。

En: Look in the mouth!
Zh: 检查嘴巴里面！

En: Established probable cause.
Zh: 确认可能的原因。

En: Didn't establish probable cause.
Zh: 没有确认可能的原因。

En: Felt a little bump on the head.
Zh: 感觉头部有个小肿块。

En: Refrigerated the body.
Zh: 将尸体冷藏。

En: Titus mentioned a wound...
Zh: 提图斯提到了一处伤口……

En: Two officers must be present to conduct a field autopsy.
Zh: 现场验尸必须有两名警官在场。

En: Let's bag him. Take him away. (The lieutenant takes the body away -- you work alone for the rest of the day.)
Zh: “把他装起来带走吧。”(警督带走了尸体——剩下的时间你独自工作。)

En: Let's drag him to the Kineema. (You're finished with the corpse.)
Zh: “把他搬到锐影去吧。”(尸体处理完毕。)

En: Let's bag him. Take him away. (Let the lieutenant take the body away without further examination.)
Zh: “为他收尸吧。把他送走。”（不做进一步检查，让警督把尸体运走。）

En: I'm afraid we can't continue before we've gone through both the external *and* internal examination first. Thoroughly.
Zh: “恐怕我们得先彻底做完体外检查*和*体内检查，才能继续进行其他项目。”

En: Alright. Bitemarks, contusions on head and chest, and a ligature mark encircling the neck.
Zh: “好的。头部和胸部的咬痕及挫伤，以及环绕脖颈的一圈勒痕。”

En: Let's see, what did we find... He scratches his chin as he leafs through his notes. "We observed haemorrhaging on the skin above and below the ligature mark. Well pronounced, consistent with a drop from 1 or 1.5 metres."
Zh: “来总结一下我们的发现吧……”他一边翻看笔记，一边挠着下巴。“我们发现勒痕上下方的皮肤都发生了大出血。痕迹非常明显，符合一米或一米五的落差。”

En: The chest was intact, normal contours. Abdomen protuberant, pelvis intact. No injuries in genital region.
Zh: “胸部完整，轮廓正常，下腹部隆起，骨盆无伤。生殖器部位无伤痕。”

En: Back was symmetrical and intact, upper and lower extremities also -- but asymmetrical. The lieutenant nods along to his meticulous overview. "Old combat injuries on the right hand, thigh, and hip."
Zh: “背部匀称完整。四肢完整——但不对称。”警督一边陈述自己细致的观察结论，一边点着头。“右手、大腿及臀部有战斗中留下的旧伤疤。”

En: Also numerous residual scars, covering about 40% of his skin. Extensive scarring, to say the least. He coughs. "Hands were clean, no sign of injury from struggling. That's about it."
Zh: “此外还有面积更小的旧伤疤，大约覆盖了整体皮肤40%的面积。不提百分比的话，至少可以描述为大面积的伤疤。”他咳了几下。“双手都很干净，没有因挣扎造成的伤痕。大概就是这些。”

En: This, I remind you, also necessitates the removal of the belt from around his neck. So we have a visual on the injury.
Zh: “关于这个，我得提醒你，我们还有必要取下他脖子上的绑带。这样才能看到那里的伤痕。”

En: I have gardening gloves, maybe they are enough?
Zh: “我有一副园艺手套，差不多能用吧？”

En: They're better than nothing... The lieutenant looks at the gloves. "Tell you what: I perform the anatomical side of things, while you will take notes."
Zh: “总比没有强……”警督看了看你的手套。“还是这样吧：我负责做尸检，你负责做记录。”

En: Try to remove the dead man's boots.
Zh: 试着脱掉死者的靴子。

En: Here, he hands you the chaincutters back and then kneels closer to the body -- running his finger along the dark red groove. Until he comes to a gap...
Zh: “给你，”他把切链钳还给了你，然后跪到了离尸体更近的地方——他的手指摸索着暗红色的痕迹。直到一处缺口……

En: The rope rises to a point, he says, "leaving a *gap* in the ligature mark. The suspension point is in the back of the neck, on the nape."
Zh: “绳子升到一定高度后，”他讲解着，“在勒痕中留下了一个*缺口*。绞刑的悬挂点就在脖子后面，颈背的位置。”

En: The lieutenant adjusts his glasses and takes a deep breath.
Zh: 警督扶正了眼镜，深吸了一口气。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Damn it, this is turning into some sort of mega-puzzle.
Zh: 该死的，情况变得越来越令人费解了。

En: Okay, so you can *sneak* out of your room maybe, after he's gone asleep...
Zh: 好吧，也许你可以偷偷*溜出*你的房间，趁他睡着之后……

En: Yeah, fuck it to pieces for us. Liar cop!
Zh: “干得漂亮，让我们见识一下你是怎么把它大卸八块的。骗子条子！”

En: Just relax. This is easy. This is *happening*.
Zh: 放松。这很简单。*很快*就能搞定了。

En: Damn, you're out of shape... In your defence, the corpse *was* quite slippery.
Zh: 见鬼，你的状态不佳……你还是能为这种失态做出辩解的，毕竟尸体*实在是*太滑了。

En: You may want to slow down. Don't tear into it like that.
Zh: 你可能得把动作放慢点儿，别这么粗暴。

En: Yes. These absolutely need to be washed before. The stink is incredible.
Zh: 没错，这靴子必须好好洗洗才能穿。这股臭味简直熏死人。

En: Yes. These absolutely need to be washed before. The stink is incredible.
Zh: 没错，这靴子必须好好洗洗才能穿。这股臭味简直熏死人。

En: Didn't the Whirling have a kitchen?
Zh: 褴褛飞旋不是有间厨房吗？

En: Didn't the Whirling have a kitchen?
Zh: 褴褛飞旋不是有间厨房吗？

En: See? The corpse doesn't care if you try to take his boots. He doesn't need them. He's probably *glad* to get rid of them.
Zh: 你瞧？尸体根本不介意你脱掉他的靴子。反正他也不需要这种东西了。他没准还*很高兴*能摆脱它们呢。

En: Adjust his seating position first.
Zh: 先调整他的落座位置。

En: He's too heavy to move by yourself -- a great mass of dead muscles, with hoarfrost covering his milky eyes.
Zh: 他太重了，你无法独自搬动这具尸体——虽然毫无生气，但这仍是一块体积庞大的肌肉，他浑浊的双眼上正覆盖着一层白霜。

En: Is that an accusatory glance he gave you? No... no, he's cool.
Zh: 他的眼神难道是在谴责你吗？不……不可能的，他不会在意的。

En: Before you can grab hold of the boot, you hear a voice coming out of the dead man's mouth. It's the voice of Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi, telling you to stop whatever you're doing.
Zh: 在你抓住这双靴子之前，你听到死者口中发出了声音。那是曷城警督的声音，在告诫着你立刻住手不要继续下去。

En: You're sabotaging the crime scene, and yet you haven't even finished the field autopsy. Come back later when you're done with the investigation.
Zh: 你这是在破坏犯罪现场，而且是在尚未完成现场尸检的情况下搞破坏。等调查完成之后再说吧。

En: I thought we decided to leave it to Processing. The lieutenant shakes his head. "Let's not turn this into some kind of circus."
Zh: “我记得我们已经决定把剩下的工作交给分析部门了。”警督摇着头。“别在这里继续胡闹了。”

En: The body is reluctant to let go of the boots -- as though they were its last bit of dignity.
Zh: 这具尸体不愿脱下靴子——仿佛这是它残存的最后一丝尊严。

En: Pull on the right boot.
Zh: 拽右脚的靴子。

En: It's not completely useless. You're able to reveal a little more of the putrid polymer sock beneath the boot.
Zh: 你的努力不是毫无成果。至少让穿在靴子下的恶臭尼龙袜稍微露出来了一点。

En: Pick up the body and try to shake the boots off.
Zh: 抬起尸体，尝试甩下靴子。

En: Try to twist the boots off one by one.
Zh: 尝试把靴子分别拧下来。

En: Step on the corpse as you pull on the boots.
Zh: 踩在尸体上猛拽靴子。

En: You're going to need to summon all your strength for this undertaking.
Zh: 你要为这艰巨的使命用上全部的力量。

En: It's heavier than you'd think. You're forced to drop it back down immediately. The corpse lands on the ground with a nauseating thud, looking even sadder than before.
Zh: 这远比你想的更沉重。你不得不把它立刻放回原地。尸体砸到地面的瞬间发出了令人恶心的闷响，模样变得比之前更悲惨了。

En: This is the right method. It takes some time, but eventually the boots come off -- with nasty slices of polymer sock stuck to even bigger pieces of skin and rotting flesh.
Zh: 这是正确的方案。虽然花了不少时间，但你总算是把这双靴子脱下来了——连带着一双恶心的尼龙袜，以及大片腐肉与皮肤。

En: Something cracks in the left leg -- it really is about to come off. The leg, I mean. Not the boot.
Zh: 左腿的某个部位断裂了——这玩意真要脱落了。我指的是腿，而不是靴子。

En: Take stock of the damage.
Zh: 估量造成的损伤。

En: The legs look like they've been clawed at by wild animals. As for the boots -- you can't possibly do anything with them until you have cleaned and *disinfected* them thoroughly.
Zh: 尸体的双腿看上去就像是被野生动物撕扯过。至于这双靴子——在全面清洗和*消毒*之前，你对它们无可奈何。

En: Pull on the left boot.
Zh: 拽左脚的靴子。

En: It feels like the leg is going to come off along with the boot, but you are able to get the boot to move a centimetre or two.
Zh: 感觉整条腿都快要随着靴子一起被扯下来了，不过你至少让靴子移动了一两厘米。

En: Whatever you're planning to do, you need to do it before you catch pneumonia in the cold draft coming from deep inside the fridge.
Zh: 无论你打算做什么，你需要在因从冰箱深处吹出的冷风染上肺炎之前搞定。

En: Whoah. Cuno's little *sneak-pig's* back for the booty...
Zh: “嚯。坤诺的*贼兮兮猪猡*回来拿战利品了……”

En: Look -- he's skinning the fucker up good. *Puukko* style.
Zh: “瞧啊——他正给那孙子剥皮呢。*朴寇短刀*的调调。”

En: Not good. Let's pin this on the bear -- the ice bear tore up his legs with its claws. As for the boots -- you can't possibly do anything with them until you have cleaned and *disinfected* them thoroughly.
Zh: 这可不好。咱们还是把这个事故现场嫁祸给熊吧——这头冰熊用利爪撕烂了他的双腿。至于这双靴子——在全面清洗和*消毒*之前，你对它们无可奈何。

En: Hell. Keep at it, pig!
Zh: “妈的。使劲儿啊，猪猡！”

En: The inside of the head feels cold and smooth like a glove as you pull out, sweat dripping down your brow -- careful not to lose the prize between your fingers...
Zh: 当你抽出手来的时候，脑袋内部感觉冰冷湿滑，就像手套似的，汗水从你的额头上滴了下来——小心别把你手指间的战利品弄丢了……

En: What about that giant ice bear fridge in the Doomed Commercial Area?
Zh: 诅咒商业区的那个巨型冰熊冰箱呢？

En: The corpse looks *enormous* with its bloated belly. Was the ice bear fridge really that giant? You should go and measure it, just to be sure.
Zh: 肿胀的腹部让尸体看上去*硕大无朋*。冰熊冰箱真的有这么大吗？你应该过去测量一下，以防万一。

En: Klaasje told us the bullet hit him in the *mouth* -- try putting your hand there...
Zh: “卡拉洁告诉我们子弹射进了他的*嘴里*——不如把你的手伸进去……”

En: Why... yes there was.
Zh: “为什么……的确如此。”

En: So that's what the *ice bear shaped* refrigerator is for!
Zh: “所以这就是那个*冰熊形状*的冰箱的用途了！”

En: An absolutely colossal fridge -- still plugged in -- *literally* in the shape of an ice bear!
Zh: “一个绝对巨大的冰箱——还通着电——*完全*就是冰熊的形状！”

En: Yes -- now. I've rarely been disappointed by the size of a *giant* ice bear fridge, but I think we should still take a look at it first. Make sure it's big *enough* before we carry him over.
Zh: “是的——现在。我很少会对一个*巨型*冰熊冰箱的尺寸感到失望，但我想我们还是得提前看一眼。在我们把他抬过去之前，确保它确实*足够*大。”

En: He closes his notebook and cracks his neck. "Let's move. With every hour, whatever we're looking for in the deceased will become harder to find."
Zh: 他合上笔记本，扭了扭脖子。“行动起来吧。随着时间的推移，我们想在死者体内寻找的东西也会变得越来越难找。”

En: Now that's an overstatement. It's not actually *for* storing cadavers, or at least I hope so. I think we should take a look at it first. Make sure it's big *enough* before we carry him over.
Zh: “这么说就有些夸张了。实际上它不是*用来*储存尸体的，至少我希望如此。我想我们还是得提前看一眼，在我们把他抬过去之前，确保它确实*足够*大。”

En: Why yes there was. It was massive. Red eyes glowing in the dark.
Zh: 为什么的确如此。它非常巨大，红色的眼睛在黑暗中闪闪发光。

En: Only if *all else* fails, he stresses.
Zh: “除非是*其它办法*都行不通了。”他强调着。

En: Fuck are you looking at bino-man? You wanna piece of the Cuno? Wanna get *fucked*?
Zh: “该死的你在看什么，小四眼？你想尝尝坤诺的怒火吗？想*挨揍*吗？”

En: I need to take another look at the body first.
Zh: “我需要先去看一眼尸体。”

En: Hurry. This is one task we *cannot* sideline. With every hour, whatever we're looking for will become harder to find.
Zh: “尽快。这是我们唯一*不能*耽搁的任务。随着时间的推移，我们想找的东西也会变得越来越难找。”

En: Soon you'll be looking for clues in a pile of sludge and bones.
Zh: 很快你就要在一堆腐肉和骨头之间寻找线索了。

En: You touch the dead man's body. His skin is cold, light blue and silvery in the light of the fridge. You *still* have no idea where to begin -- or what to even do with him...
Zh: 你触摸着死者的身体。他的皮肤很冷，在冰箱灯光下交织成浅蓝与银灰色。你*仍然*不知该从何下手——甚至要怎样处理他……

En: You really ought to wait until the cadaver is *refrigerated*. Digging around in this mess now is proving hopelessly difficult.
Zh: 你真的应该等到尸体被*冷藏*的。事实证明，想在这片混乱中找东西真是极其困难。

En: Klaasje told us the bullet hit him in the mouth -- try putting your hand there...
Zh: “卡拉洁告诉我们子弹射进了他的嘴里——不如把你的手伸进去……”

En: If the Hardies are to be trusted, there should be a bullet in there... The lieutenant looks at you. "Try putting your hand in his mouth and probing for the bullet in his head."
Zh: “如果哈迪兄弟的话可信，里面应该有颗子弹才对……”警督看着你。“不如你把手伸进他的嘴巴，去他脑袋里找出子弹。”

En: The lieutenant rubs his side for warmth. "It'll come to you sooner or later. At least he's safe here until then."
Zh: 警督摩擦着身侧取暖。“你迟早会有所发现的，至少在那之前他在这里很安全。”

En: The lieutenant should *clearly* be here for this. You're lost.
Zh: 警督*显然*应该在这里坐镇，你输了。

En: You're on the right track.
Zh: 你走对了路。

En: This is what he used to regulate his emotions with...
Zh: 这就是他用来控制情绪的东西……

En: This means Lieutenant Kitsuragi will leave the party until tomorrow morning. You're free to do side-tasks and even the main case -- though it might be more difficult.
Zh: 这意味着直到明天早上，曷城警探都不会在队伍中了。你可以去做些日常杂事，或者调查这起案件——虽然可能会棘手许多。

En: Brain stem?
Zh: 脑干？

En: Yes. That's what this part is called.
Zh: 没错，那就是这块地方的名字。

En: You've seen the drawings. You've studied them.
Zh: 你曾看过那些画，也研究过他们。

En: The *funk soul brother* at the back of his head has gone dark. Forever.
Zh: 他后脑勺的那位*放克灵魂兄弟*已经永远的消失了。

En: The ululations of the limbic system have ended. All is quiet.
Zh: 边缘系统的嚎叫已经结束了，陷入了安静之中。

En: He's proposing the bullet was the real cause of death -- and the hanging an attempt to *conceal* this fact.
Zh: 他认为子弹才是真正的死亡原因——而绞死只是在试图*掩盖*这一事实。

En: We put him in a bag and carry him to the holding pen of my Kineema. The bodybag should contain the odour for the duration of the transport...
Zh: “我们把他装进袋子里然后搬到我的锐影車厢上吧。裹尸袋在运送期间应该会有气味残留……”

En: We bag the corpse and carry him to the holding pen of my Kineema. I can transport him to Processing myself, but I *will* be gone for the rest of the day.
Zh: “我们把尸体装进袋子里然后搬到我的锐影車厢上吧。我可以送他去分析部门，但今天剩下的时间我*将*无法奉陪了。”

En: You'll be gone? What should I do in the meanwhile?
Zh: “你要离开？那在此期间我该做什么呢？”

En: Work on the case, tend to personal matters? Try not to do anything too dangerous. An officer needs backup in a neighbourhood like this.
Zh: “继续办案，处理私事？但尽量不要以身*犯险*。在这种地方警官们都需要自己的搭档。”

En: After you bag the corpse, Lieutenant Kitsuragi will leave the party until tomorrow morning. You can do side-tasks and even the main case, but it might be more difficult. Plan his exit accordingly.
Zh: 在你打包尸体后，金警督已离开小队，他会在明天早上与你会和。在此之前，你可以进行支线任务，甚至也可以进行主线任务。好好计划一下他离开时该做什么吧。

En: I'll leave that choice to you. And one more thing... He looks you in the eye. "Great work, detective."
Zh: “我把选择权交给你……还有一件事——“他看着你的眼睛。“做得好，警探。”

En: The word lingers in the air of the basement, far away ice cream makers are buzzing and the sea wind blows outside...
Zh: 这句话在地下室的空气里回荡着，远处的冰淇淋店里人声鼎沸，海风阵阵……

En: *Detective*.
Zh: *警探*。

En: The word lingers in the air of the yard, far away dogs are barking, further yet the sound of motor traffic...
Zh: 这句话在院子的空气里回荡着，远处有狗在吠叫，还有汽車的轰鸣从更远处飘来……

En: *Detective*.
Zh: *警探*。

En: One more thing -- this was *really* good work, detective.
Zh: “还有一件事——干得*真*不错，警探。”

En: I *would* drive him to Processing, but it's too late to do that today... I'll do it first thing tomorrow. No problem.
Zh: “我*可以*开車送他去分析部门，但今天过去太迟了……明天一早我就过去，没问题。”

En: The word lingers in the air of the yard, as far away dogs are barking, further yet the sound of motor traffic...
Zh: 这句话在院子的空气里回荡着，远处有狗在吠叫，还有汽車的轰鸣从更远处飘来……

En: The word lingers in the air of basement, far away ice cream makers are buzzing and the sea wind blows outside...
Zh: 这句话在地下室的空气里回荡着，远处的冰淇淋店里人声鼎沸，海风阵阵……

En: *Detective*.
Zh: *警探*。

En: *Detective*.
Zh: *警探*。

En: Bag the corpse and drag it to the motor carriage. [Leave.]
Zh: 把尸体装袋并拖进車厢。[离开。]

En: Bag the corpse and drag it to the motor carriage. [Leave.]
Zh: 把尸体装袋并拖进車厢。[离开。]

En: All right. He takes out a shiny black body bag and starts pulling the plastic over the dead man's face.
Zh: “好吧。”他拿出一个闪闪发光的黑色裹尸袋，开始用塑料布盖住死者的脸。

En: I will need a little help carrying him -- you take the hands, I'll take the legs.
Zh: “我需要有人帮我抬着他——你来抬他的手，我负责抬脚。”

En: There go those beautiful enamel boots. May they rest in Processing...
Zh: 再见了那双漂亮的珐琅质靴子，在分析部门安息吧……

En: Oh well -- in another lifetime.
Zh: 哦好吧——来生再见。

En: If you have nothing to do, read books or sit on the bench on the plaza.
Zh: 如果你实在无事可做，不如去看书或者坐在广场的长椅上。

En: There go those beautiful enamel boots. May they rest in Processing...
Zh: 再见了那双漂亮的珐琅质靴子，在分析部门安息吧……

En: Oh well -- in another lifetime.
Zh: 哦好吧——来生再见。

En: As I said, treatment is an attempt to manipulate the body after death -- to hide the real cause with a *false* cause. In this case...
Zh: “正如我说的，被处理过就是指一种在死者死后试图操纵尸体的行为——用*伪造*的死因掩盖真正的死亡原因。若是如此……”

En: We almost fell for it, he thinks. *Almost*.
Zh: 我们差点就上当了，他想。*差一点*。

En: We didn't fall for it, he thinks. There's pride in there.
Zh: 我们并没有上当，他想。非常值得骄傲。

En: This little thing could reveal much about the weapon that shot it.
Zh: 这个小东西可以揭示出更多关于凶器的信息。

En: If you have nothing to do, read books or sit on the bench on the plaza.
Zh: 如果你实在无事可做，不如去看书或者坐在广场的长椅上。

En: This means Lieutenant Kitsuragi will leave until tomorrow morning. Meanwhile, you're free to do side-tasks and even the main case -- though it might be more difficult.
Zh: 这意味着直到明天早上，曷城警探都都不在场了。你可以去做些日常杂事，或者调查这起案件——虽然可能会棘手许多。

En: All right. He takes out a shiny black body bag and starts pulling the plastic over the dead man's face.
Zh: “好吧。”他拿出一个闪闪发光的黑色裹尸袋，开始用塑料布盖住死者的脸。

En: I can drive him to Processing today, no problem. Since we stalled with this... But this *does* mean I will be gone for the rest of the day.
Zh: “我今天就可以开車送他去分析部门，没问题。反正什么事也做不了……但这*的确*意味着我在今天余下的时间里都无法奉陪了。”

En: Work on the case, tend to personal matters? Try not to do anything *too* dangerous. An officer needs backup in a neighbourhood like this.
Zh: “继续办案，处理私事？但尽量不要以身*犯险*。在这种地方警官们都需要自己的搭档。”

En: You'll be gone? What should I do in the meanwhile?
Zh: “你要离开？那在此期间我该做什么呢？”

En: Fish it out.
Zh: 掏出来。

En: Precise spot of exit wound.
Zh: 贯穿伤的精确位置。

En: Don't let down Cunoesse!
Zh: 别让坤妮斯失望！

En: Punched a hole.
Zh: 打一个洞。

En: There go those beautiful enamel boots. You will never own the full set now. May they rest in Processing...
Zh: 再见了那双漂亮的珐琅质靴子。现在你永远也没办法拥有一整套了。在分析部门安息吧……

En: There go those beautiful enamel boots. You will never own the full set now. May they rest in Processing...
Zh: 再见了那双漂亮的珐琅质靴子。现在你永远也没办法拥有一整套了。在分析部门安息吧……

En: Your arm reaches out and your eyes close -- as if by their own volition. It's dark all around. You feel cold, dead flesh through the latex glove. It's right under the palm of your hand.
Zh: 你闭着眼睛伸出手臂――仿佛它们生出了自己的意志。四周陷入黑暗，透过乳胶手套，你感受到了冰冷的肉体，就在你的手掌之下。

En: Your arm reaches out and your eyes close, as if by their own volition. It's dark all around. You feel cold, slippery flesh -- first with your fingertips, then under the palm of your hand.
Zh: 你闭着眼睛伸出手臂，仿佛它们生出了自己的意志。四周陷入黑暗，你感受到了冰冷又滑腻的肉体——先是指尖，紧接着是你的整个手掌。

En: You run your  hands over the cold-cold body of the victim: his limbs, swollen organs... there's a bullet in here, somewhere.
Zh: 你的手抚摸过被害者冰冷的身体：他的四肢、肿胀的器官……在尸体的某处，藏着一颗子弹。

En: You run your hands over the victim's cold body: his limbs, his torso with its swollen organs... maybe you should be more *thorough*?
Zh: 你的手指抚摸过被害人冰冷的身体：他的四肢、躯干以及肿胀的器官……或许你应该再*彻底*一些？

En: Here we go.
Zh: “在这儿。”

En: What is this?
Zh: 这是什么？

En: His face. His cheeks, his nose, his fat swollen lips. Like a rubber spider your gloved hand crawls on his features. Everything is silent all around.
Zh: 他的脸、双颊、鼻子以及肥胖肿胀的嘴唇。你的手如橡胶蜘蛛一般在他的五官上爬行，全世界都陷入了静默之中。

En: His face. His cheeks, his nose, his fat swollen lips. Like a spider, your hand crawls over his features. Everything is silent.
Zh: 他的脸、双颊、鼻子以及肥胖肿胀的嘴唇。你的手如蜘蛛一般在他的五官上爬行，全世界都陷入了静默之中。

En: Crawl up his nostrils.
Zh: 触摸他的鼻孔。

En: Put your fingers in his mouth.
Zh: 将手指伸进他的嘴巴。

En: They are swollen shut -- you need to really *push* to get in.
Zh: 它们肿胀到近乎闭合了——你得全力*推*进去。

En: Push your fingers in his nose.
Zh: 把你的手指伸进他的鼻子。

En: Only the little one fits. The flesh changes shape as you bore in, searching for something in the cartilage.
Zh: 只有小指头才伸得进去。当你钻开软骨寻找某样东西的时候，肌肉也会改变形状。

En: The fuck is he doing? You hear a voice squeak. It sounds very far away.
Zh: “他他妈的在搞什么？”你听见一阵吱吱的声音，听起来非常遥远。

En: The oral cavity is cold and moist. A ball-like tongue attaches itself to the base of the mouth, lolling around like a scallop...
Zh: 口腔内部又湿又冷，一条球状的舌头蛰伏在嘴巴底部，像只扇贝似的懒洋洋地耸拉着……

En: Play with it -- this feels right.
Zh: 玩弄它——感觉好极了。

En: The tongue moves freely in the cavity. The mucose of the mouth is slippery, fragile even through the latex. From the soft meat -- teeth are budding; hard pearls of bone in the gums. And in the back of the mouth...
Zh: 舌头在口腔内自由地游移着，口腔黏膜很滑腻，即便透过乳胶手套也能感受到那份脆弱。软肉中——牙齿崭露了头角；是牙龈上最坚硬的骨头。至于口腔后方……

En: The tongue moves freely in the cavity. The mucous of the mouth is slippery, delicate to touch. From the soft meat -- teeth are budding; hard pearls of bone in the gums. And in the back of the mouth...
Zh: 舌头在口腔内自由地游移着，口腔黏膜很滑腻，感觉很脆弱。软肉中——牙齿崭露了头角；是牙龈上最坚硬的骨头。至于口腔后方……

En: Open your eyes and look.
Zh: 睁开眼睛观察。

En: A vision of black and dark red death: pried open by your hands and studded with teeth. Looks like he's laughing, death fumes rising from his throat. And there, in the back of his mouth, above the bell of the uvula, right in the soft palate...
Zh: 一副红与黑交融的死亡景象：被你赤裸的双手撑开，嵌满了牙齿。他好像在笑着，喉咙里喷出死亡的气息。在那里，他口腔的后方，腭垂的上面，就在软腭那儿……

En: A vision of black and dark red death, pried open by your naked hands and studded with teeth. Looks like he's laughing, death fumes rising from the throat. And there, in the back of his mouth, above the bell of the uvula, right in the soft palate...
Zh: 一副红与黑交融的死亡景象，被你赤裸的双手撑开，嵌满了牙齿。他好像在笑着，喉咙里喷出死亡的气息。在那里，他口腔的后方，腭垂的上面，就在软腭那儿……

En: Touch it with your finger, gently. (Proceed.)
Zh: 用你的手指轻轻地触摸它。（继续。）

En: A black trickle of liquid runs into his throat from the wound...
Zh: 一股黑色的液体从伤处流进了他的喉咙……

En: Put your finger in.
Zh: 把手指伸进去。

En: ...you see a hole, barely visible to the human eye. It is swollen shut, almost vanished, no larger than 0.4 cm in radius. The edges appear darkened.
Zh: ……你看到一个小洞，肉眼很难看见。它肿胀到近乎闭合，几乎要消失了，半径不超过0.4厘米。边缘部分变得越来越暗淡。

En: An *abrasion collar*! THIS is what we're after.
Zh: 一个*射伤轮*！这正是我们想找的。

En: Say: "Abrasion collar."
Zh: 说“射伤轮“。

En: This is it.
Zh: “就是这个。”

En: Say: "Fuck yeah."
Zh: 说：“牛逼！”

En: Abrasion collar... The lieutenant looks in. There's a pen in his hand. His notebook is open at the copy paper.
Zh: “射伤轮……”金望过去，手中握着一支钢笔，他的笔记本翻开至复写纸处。

En: I see it... The lieutenant looks in. There's a pen in his hand. His notebook is open at the red pages.
Zh: “看出来了……”金望过去，手中握着一支钢笔，他的笔记本翻开至红页处。

En: Mhm, he agrees. "Keep going."
Zh: “嗯，“他赞同道。”继续。”

En: Cuno is silent.
Zh: 坤诺陷入了沉默。

En: Cuno nods too. He takes a step closer.
Zh: 坤诺同样点了点头，他走近了一步。

En: Your index fits right in there. A tight tunnel of flesh opens up -- tissue damage -- wide enough for two fingers. As you push both in, you reach through his mouth, right into his brain stem.
Zh: 你的食指正好可以伸进去，一条狭窄的血肉通道敞开了——组织损伤——足有两指宽。你的两根指头并驾齐驱，穿过他的嘴巴，进入了他的脑干。

En: The fuck is happening?!
Zh: “这他妈是怎么回事儿？！”

En: Oh shit, C...
Push deeper.
Zh: 继续深入。

En: Feel around first.
Zh: 先摸索四周。

En: The basal ganglia feels clumpy -- the bullet has torn apart his reptilian complex.
Zh: 基底神经节处呈块状——一颗子弹撕裂了他的爬行动物脑。

En: The basal ganglia feels clumpy -- what entered here has torn apart his reptilian complex.
Zh: 基底神经节处呈块状——打进里面的东西撕裂了他的爬虫复合体。

En: Your yellow fingers slide into the remains of the limbic system. There is no resistance. Its gelatinous. The slug-like structures are damaged too, the tearing extends deep into both hemispheres.
Zh: 你黄色的手指滑进他残存的边缘系统，没受到任何阻力。其呈现凝胶状，鼻涕虫状的结构也被破坏，裂口延伸至两个脑半球。

En: Your fingers slide into the remains of his limbic system. There is no resistance. It's gelatinous. The slug-like structures are damaged too, the tearing extends deep into both hemispheres.
Zh: 你的手指滑进他残存的边缘系统，没受到任何阻力。其呈现凝胶状，鼻涕虫状的结构也被破坏，裂口延伸至两个脑半球。

En: The cavity is here, cut right between the hemispheres.
Zh: “弹孔在这儿，正好穿过了两个脑半球。”

En: There's a cavity... cut right between the hemispheres.
Zh: “这里有个弹孔……正好穿过了两个脑半球。”

En: Push deeper.
Zh: 继续深入。

En: Fuckin' *CAVITY* C... Cuno's voice is hushed.
Zh: “该死的*弹孔*坤……”坤诺的声音戛然而止。

En: The lieutenant answers with the sound of his pen on paper.
Zh: 警督用纸笔的声音作为回答。

En: Your fingers are all the way in now, reaching toward the inside of his skull. The cavity goes further, but the entry wound isn't wide enough for the rest of your hand to follow...
Zh: 你的手指一直伸进了他的头骨。弹孔还在更深处，但裂口的宽度还不足以让你手掌的其余部分伸进去……

En: All the muscles in your body harden. Time to *enter* him.
Zh: 你全身上下所有的肌肉都僵硬起来，是时候*进入*他了。

En: Wriggle in...
Zh: 蜿蜒行进……

En: Quivering with awe.
Zh: 敬畏地颤抖着。

En: Punch a hole through his mouth.
Zh: 在他的嘴里打一个洞。

En: Your fingers reach toward his skull. His cerebral cortex feels like jelly -- cold jelly -- strange fluid streams down your wrist as you push deeper. Until you feel it, on the tip of your finger...
Zh: 你的手指伸向了他的头骨，他的大脑皮层如同果冻一样——冰冷的果冻——当你继续深入的时候，奇怪的液体顺着你的手腕流了下来。直到你的指尖，感觉到了它……

En: Sharp, serrated material. The edges cut right into your skin. This must be the *bullet*.
Zh: 锋利的锯齿状材质，边缘直接割进了你的皮肤。一定是那颗*子弹*了。

En: Ice cold, serrated metal. Its edges cut right through the latex and into your finger.
Zh: 冰冷的锯齿状金属，边缘直接穿透乳胶手套，割进了你的手指。

En: Sharp, serrated material. The edges cut right into your skin.
Zh: 锋利的锯齿状材质，边缘直接割进了你的皮肤。

En: I feel a solid object, right under the skull.
Zh: “我摸到一个固体，就在头骨下方。”

En: No, this is too much. (Pull your hand out.)
Zh: 不，这太过头了。（抽出手来。）

En: The pain is barely noticeable under the adrenaline rush.
Zh: 在肾上腺素的刺激下，疼痛几乎无从察觉。

En: The cartilage gives in with a crack, like some fruit. Just like that your hand is in his head. Fluid streams down your wrist -- and then you finally feel it, with the tip of your finger...\n
Zh: 软骨就像水果似的咔嚓裂开，你的手就这样插进了他的脑袋。液体顺着你的手腕流了下来——然后你的指尖，感受到了它……

En: The cartilage gives in with a crack, like some fruit. Just like that your hand is in his head. Strange fluid streams down your wrist -- and then you feel it, with the tip of your finger...
Zh: 软骨就像水果似的咔嚓裂开，你的手就这样插进了他的脑袋。奇怪的液体顺着你的手腕流了下来——然后你的指尖，感受到了它……

En: Fish it out.
Zh: 掏出来。

En: Precise spot of exit wound.
Zh: 贯穿伤的精确位置。

En: Don't let down Cunoesse!
Zh: 别让坤妮斯失望！

En: Punched a hole.
Zh: 打一个洞。

En: Touch something else.
Zh: 摸摸别的。

En: You're not far, it'll come to you, keep crawling...
Zh: 不远了，它会来找你的，继续爬下去……

En: No. Touch something else.
Zh: 不，摸摸别的。

En: Can you feel it? You're so close! Rip his jaws open now, look in...
Zh: 你感觉到了吗？就差一点了！快把他的下巴掀开，朝里面看看吧……

En: Inspect the skull first.
Zh: 先检查头骨。

En: The thing you're looking for -- it's not here either. Crawl out, spider...
Zh: 你想找的东西——也不在这儿。爬下来吧，小蜘蛛……

En: The thing you're looking for -- it's not there. Crawl out, spider...
Zh: 你想找的东西——不在这儿。爬下来吧，小蜘蛛……

En: Can you... can you get to it? He searches his pockets for something.
Zh: “你……能拿到它吗？”他在口袋里翻找着。

En: There's a tiny crack, a protrusion in the cranium, right in the back of his head. Your finger must be pointing straight at it. From the *inside*.
Zh: 他的后脑勺上有一条细缝和一处凸起。你的手指一定是顶到那里了，从头骨*内部*。

En: We have the makings of a... very small exit wound here. The lieutenant leans closer.
Zh: “看起来这是一条……非常小的贯穿伤。”警督靠了过来。

En: Forget about the *fucking exit wound*, bino! THE PIG IS WEARING HIM LIKE A FUCK PUPPET!
Zh: “别管那*该死的贯穿伤*了死四眼！那头猪把他当成了一个该死的人偶！”

En: Her voice is absolutely sizzling with excitement.
Zh: 她激动得声音嘶哑。

En: You pick the sharp, metal bullet between your index and middle finger. With your face twisting from pain and concentration, all you need to do is just...
Zh: 你用食指与中指夹起了那颗锋利的金属子弹。你的脸庞因为疼痛和专注而扭曲着，唯一要做的就是……

En: You pick the object between your index and middle finger. Feels sharp, like metal. You press your teeth together, your jaw is clenched. All you need to do is just...
Zh: 你用食指与中指夹起了那个东西，摸上去很锋利，就像金属。你咬紧牙关，唯一要做的就是……

En: You pick the object between your index and middle finger. It feels sharp, like metal. With your face twisting from pain and concentration, all you need to do is just...
Zh: 你用食指与中指夹起了那个东西，摸上去很锋利，就像金属。你的脸庞因为疼痛和专注而扭曲着，唯一要做的就是……

En: You push your hand further in. The bullet keeps rolling between your fingers as you beckon it to come out.\n
Zh: 你的手继续深入。只要你试图把它掏出来，它就会不停地在你的手指间滚动。

En: You push your hand further in. Whatever is in there keeps rolling between your fingers as you beckon it to come out.
Zh: 你的手继续深入。无论里面藏了什么，只要你试图把它掏出来，它就会不停地在你的手指间滚动。

En: Slowly pull your fingers out...
Zh: 慢慢地抽出手指……

En: Whisper: "I got it."
Zh: 低语：“找到了。”

En: My pig's fucking *got* it!
Zh: “我的猪猡他妈的*找到*了！”

En: He's watching his old man get the big prize at the claw game.
Zh: 他看着自己的老爸在抓握游戏中拔得头筹。

En: What?! WHAT is it?
Zh: “什么？！是什么*东西？”

En: A bullet... The lieutenant puts a small bag marked *evidence* under it.
Zh: “一颗子弹……”警督把一个标着*证物*的小袋放在它下面。

En: With a *plop*, your hand emerges from the mouth, covered in blood up to the wrist. Between your fingers -- a small flower, a blossom made of lead!
Zh: *扑通*一声你的手从他的嘴里抽了出来，鲜血一路流至手腕。你的两指之间——是一朵用铅做成的，小花。

En: With a *plop*, your hand emerges from the mouth. The garden glove is covered in blood right up to the wrist. Between your fingers -- a small flower, a blossom made of lead!
Zh: *扑通*一声你的手从他的嘴里抽了出来，园艺手套上充斥的鲜血一路流至手腕。你的两指之间——是一朵用铅做成的，小花！

En: With a *plop*, your hand emerges from the mouth, covered in blood up to the wrist. Between your fingers -- a small flower, a blossom made of lead!
Zh: *扑通*一声你的手从他的嘴里抽了出来，鲜血一路流至手腕。你的两指之间——是一朵用铅做成的，小花。

En: Drop it in.
Zh: 放进去。

En: The bullet falls in the bag leaving a smattering of blood on the plastic. He raises the bag under his eyes and says:
Zh: 子弹落入证物袋中，在塑料上留下了一丝血迹。他把袋子举至眼前说道：

En: Of course. You deserve it. The lieutenant drops the bag in your hand. It feels light.
Zh: “当然，这是你应得的。”警督把袋子扔到你血淋淋的手里，感觉很轻。

En: Of course. You've *earned* it. The lieutenant drops the bag in your bloody hand. It feels light.
Zh: “当然。这是你*应得*的。”警督把袋子扔在你血淋淋的手上，感觉很轻。

En: Fucking shit.
Zh: “真该死。”

En: A little help, lieutenant?
Zh: “帮个忙吧，警督？”

En: I can't get it...
Zh: “我拿不到……”

En: Out comes his small folding knife -- the lieutenant moves its blade across the man's skull, searching for the exit wound...
Zh: 他的折叠刀出场了——警督用刀片划过那人的头骨，寻找着贯穿伤……

En: Mhm. Withdraw your hand 4 centimetres please.
Zh: “嗯。请把手往回收4厘米。”

En: There. (Nod toward the protrusion.)
Zh: “好了。”（向着凸起的地方点头。）

En: Oh yeah...
Pull your fingers back and nod.
Zh: 缩回手指然后点头。

En: With a *crack* he punches the knife in the skull. Then one more time -- and one more -- until the bone comes loose like eggshell. Inside you feel the jello move from his poking. The bullet is still under your finger.
Zh: 他*啪*的一声把刀刺进头骨中，一次——又一次——直到骨头如同蛋壳般松动。头骨中的你的手指感觉随着他的动作，果冻状的物体也在随之移动。子弹仍在你的手指下方。

En: With a *crack* he punches the knife in the skull. Then one more time -- and one more -- until the bone comes loose like eggshell. Inside you feel the jelly move from his poking. The object is still under your finger.
Zh: 他*啪*的一声把刀刺进头骨中，一次——又一次——直到骨头如同蛋壳般松动。头骨中你的手指感觉随着他的动作，果冻状的物体也在随之移动。那个东西仍在你的手指下方。

En: Can you push it out?
Zh: “你能把它推出来吗？”

En: Push.
Zh: 推。

En: A fractured piece of lead, like a flower, blossoms from the man's head -- followed by your finger.
Zh: 一个碎裂的铅块，如同花朵一样，在男人的脑袋上绽放开来——随后是你的手指。

En: A small flower of metal blossoms from the man's head, followed by your yellow finger.
Zh: 一朵小小的金属花朵在男人的脑袋上绽放开来，随后是你黄色的手指。

En: A small flower of metal blossoms from the man's head, followed by your finger.
Zh: 一朵小小的金属花朵在男人的脑袋上绽放开来，随后是你的手指。

En: A bullet. The lieutenant picks it up between his fingers. "Unknown calibre. Rifled... you may remove your hand from the victim's head now, officer -- well done."
Zh: “一颗子弹。”警督用手指将其夹了起来。“未知口径，步枪……你可以把手从被害人的头里抽出来了，警官——-干得漂亮。”

En: Here's your prize. He drops the bullet in an evidence bag and puts it in your hand. It feels light.
Zh: “拿好你的奖励。”他把子弹放进证物袋里，然后放在你的手上，感觉很轻。

En: Here's your prize. He drops the bullet in an evidence bag and puts it in your hand -- your other hand, the one *not* covered in blood and cerebral cortex.
Zh: “拿好你的奖励。”他把子弹放进证物袋里，然后放在你的手上——另一只手，另一只*没有*被血液及大脑皮层覆盖的手上。

En: Wait, detective! Just as you start to slide your fingers out...
Zh: “等等，警探！”就在你准备抽出手指的时候……

En: Can I have it?
Zh: “我能留着它吗？”

En: Keep it, lieutenant. As a gift.
Zh: “留着吧，警督。就当是一个礼物。”

En: Unknown calibre. Rifled. Some kind of brittle alloy, fractured on impact...
Zh: “未知口径，步枪。某种易碎的合金，撞击后断裂……”

En: Keep it, lieutenant. Also, I'm sorry for calling you a binoclard.
Zh: “留着吧，警督。还有，很抱歉我叫你四眼仔了。”

En: Don't be silly. A smile comes across his face. "You've earned it. Consider the binoclard episode forgotten." He drops the bag in your hand.
Zh: “别傻了。”他的脸上浮现出了笑容。“它是你应得的。至于四眼仔的事情就忘了吧。”他把袋子扔到你的手里。

En: Don't be silly. A smile comes across his face. "You've earned it. Consider the binoclard episode forgotten." The lieutenant drops the bag in your bloody hand. It feels light.
Zh: 他的脸上浮现出了笑容：“别傻了。这是你应得的。四眼仔这篇就算翻过去了。”警督把袋子扔到你血淋淋的手里，感觉很轻。

En: No-no, you deserve it. We can log it later. The lieutenant drops the bag in your hand. It feels light.
Zh: 不-不，它是你应得的。我们可以稍后再归档。警督把袋子扔到你的手里，感觉很轻。

En: No-no, you deserve it. We can log it later. The lieutenant drops the bag in your bloody hand. It feels light.
Zh: “不不，这是你应得的。我们可以稍后再记录。”警督把袋子扔到你血淋淋的手里，感觉很轻。

En: Looks like we need to add one more item to your injury list... He takes out his notebook, *click* goes the pen. "Injury #4. Oval entry wound with an abrasion collar. Soft palate, back of mouth."
Zh: 接着他拿出自己的笔记本：“看来我们得在你的伤情列表中添上一笔了……”——*咔哒*按下了笔——“伤情#4。带有射伤轮的椭圆贯穿伤。软腭，口腔后方。”

En: We need to add an item to the injury list. He turns to his notebook. "Injury #4: Oval entry wound with an abrasion collar. Soft palate, back of mouth."
Zh: 他转而看向自己的笔记本：“我们得在伤情列表里加上这条：伤情#4：带有射伤轮的椭圆贯穿伤。软腭，口腔后方。”

En: High velocity, temporary cavity in brain tissue. Small exit wound on the occiput. He underlines the injury, forcefully. "How does that sound?"
Zh: “速度很快，脑组织中有瞬时空腔。枕骨上有小型贯穿伤。”他着重在这处损伤下划了几笔。”听起来如何？”

En: Sounds like heaven.
Zh: “太美妙了。”

En: Sounds about right.
Zh: “差不多吧。”

En: Say nothing, just nod.
Zh: 沉默不语，点头就好。

En: Opinion -- fatal injury.
Zh: “意见——致命伤。”

En: God damn right.
Zh: “太他妈对了。”

En: Agreed.
Zh: “同意。”

En: Wasn't that what we thought the last time?
Zh: “我们之前不就是这么想的吗？”

En: Wasn't that what we thought the last time? (Shake the gloves clean.)
Zh: “我们之前不就是这么想的吗？”(把手套甩干净。)

En: Wasn't that what we thought the last time? (Clean your hand in the handkerchief.)
Zh: “我们之前不就是这么想的吗？”(用手帕把你的手擦干净。)

En: Wasn't that what we thought the last time? (Clean your hand in the snow.)
Zh: “我们之前不就是这么想的吗？”（用雪把你的手擦干净。）

En: And one last thing. *Click-click* goes the pen. "We should amend injury #3: ligament mark. *Treatment*. It's obvious the Hardie boys tampered with it."
Zh: *咔哒-咔哒*按下了笔:“还有最后一件事。我们应该修正一下伤情#3:勒痕。*被处理过*。很明显是哈迪兄弟在混淆视听。”

En: This goes very, very well with #3: ligament mark. *Treatment*. *Click-click* goes the pen. "We have the tampering -- and real cause of death. Very neat."
Zh: *咔哒-咔哒*按下了笔:“还有最后一件事。我们应该修正一下伤情#3:勒痕。*被处理过*。我们有了被篡改过的——以及真正的死亡原因。非常明了。”

En: And one last thing. *Click-click* goes the pen. "We should amend injury #3: ligament mark. New opinion -- non-fatal, post-mortem: *treatment*."
Zh: *咔哒-咔哒*按下了笔：“还有最后一件事。我们应该修正一下伤情#3：勒痕。新意见——非致命，死后造成：*被处理过*。”

En: And one last thing. *Click-click* goes the pen. "We can now fill in injury #3: ligament mark. Opinion -- non-fatal, post-mortem: *treatment*."
Zh: *咔哒-咔哒*按下了笔：“还有最后一件事。我们应该修正一下伤情#3：勒痕。新意见——非致命，死后造成：*被处理过*。”

En: That was then. This time we have extensive tissue destruction away from the wound track. This time we have a *bullet*.
Zh: “那是之前。这次我们发现了远离伤口轨道的组织损伤，这次我们还发现了*子弹*。”

En: Good-good... The fridge in the background buzzes with excitement.
Zh: “很好-很好……”背景中的冰箱因为兴奋而嗡嗡作响。

En: Fucking *beautiful...*
Zh: “真他妈的*漂亮……*”

En: I'm *still* not sure. (Look at your hand, quizzically.)
Zh: “我*还是*不确定。”（困惑地看着你的手。）

En: We have *extensive* tissue destruction away from the wound track -- we have a *bullet*.
Zh: “我们发现了*远离*伤口轨道的组织损伤——我们还发现了*子弹*。”

En: Beautiful...
Zh: “漂亮啊……”

En: Suddenly, you recall: Two officers must be present for a field autopsy. You take your hand from the dead man's face and open your eyes. You will still need to come back with the lieutenant.
Zh: 你突然想起：现场尸检必须有两名警官在场。你把手从死者的脸上拿开，睁开了眼睛。你还是得跟警督一起回来。

En: Squeeze his nose.
Zh: 捏他的鼻子。

En: You pick it between your fingers. The mass is even larger than *your* nose. Very *porous* somehow. Like it's about to pop. Keep crawling, lest you break it.
Zh: 你用手指捏起它，这团东西甚至比你的*鼻子*还要大。还有很多孔，就像要爆炸了一样。继续前进，以免把它弄坏。

En: You pick it between your fingers. The mass feels large, very porous somehow. Keep crawling, lest you break it...
Zh: 你用手指捏起它。这团东西感觉有些分量，还有很多孔。继续前进，以免把它弄坏……

En: This man will never sleep again. Never wake.
Zh: 这个人再也无法沉睡，永远不会醒来了。

En: The object that is in there stopped just short of the skull, in the encephalus, knocking this tiny fracture into the cranium...
Zh: 里面的东西在距离头骨不远处停了下来，就在大脑底端，把颅骨敲开了一条细缝……

En: This is the *irregularity* you felt before. The one that was too tiny to register consciously.
Zh: 这正是你之前感觉到的*异样*，因为太小而被人下意识的忽略了。

En: Treatment?
Zh: “处理过？”

En: The ligament mark, the fractured hyoid bone, it was all treatment...
Zh: “勒痕、被折断的舌骨，都是被动过手脚的……”

En: He's proposing the bullet was the real cause of death -- and the hanging an attempt to *conceal* this fact.
Zh: 他认为子弹才是真正的死因——而上吊不过是在试图*掩盖*这一事实。

En: Treatment, officer, is an attempt to manipulate the body after death -- to hide the real cause with a *false* cause. In this case...
Zh: “被处理过，警官，试图在被害人死后对尸体动手脚——用*伪造*的死因掩盖真正的死亡原因。若是如此……”

En: ...*this* injury here. He points to the mark encircling the corpse's neck.
Zh: 他指着尸体脖子上的痕迹“……*这*圈伤痕。”

En: You think so?
Zh: “你这么觉得？”

En: Agreed.
Zh: “同意。”

En: Aren't we jumping to conclusions?
Zh: “我们现在下结论是不是为时过早了？”

En: I've had this doubt since you showed me the tracks, officer. We could not account for why they *carried* him over -- why not march him? There was no satisfying explanation.
Zh: “自从你让我看过那些脚印之后，我就一直心存疑虑，警官。我们无法解释他们为何要把他*背*过来——为什么不让他自己过来？一直没有令人满意的解释。”

En: I've had this doubt since you pointed out the lividity was faint. They know we do it -- analyze hypostasis. I have had perpetrators manipulate the body position before.
Zh: “自从你指出尸斑很暗淡之后，我就一直心存疑虑。他们知道我们会——分析尸斑。我也曾遇到过会摆弄尸体姿势的犯罪者。”

En: I've had this doubt since you had your *hunch*, officer. That he was doing something *else* at the time of his death. I don't have that kind of intuition myself -- and I don't usually trust it. But it made me... doubt.
Zh: “自从你有*预感*之后，我就一直心存疑虑。在他临死前，正在做*别的*事儿。我自己是没有那种直觉的——一般也不相信它。但它让我……产生了怀疑。”

En: I have had this doubt since I inspected his hands, officer -- there were no signs of struggle. No claw marks on his neck. Why? Why didn't he fight for his life?
Zh: “自从我检查过他的双手之后就一直心存疑虑，警官——根本没有挣扎的痕迹。他的脖子上也没有抓痕，为什么？为什么他不挣扎求生呢？”

En: Yes. And the belt around his neck; the hanging; even dragging him to the yard... *all* of it was done after this man was already dead.
Zh: “是的。以及他脖子上的绑带；上吊；甚至把他拖到院子里……*一切*都是这个男人死后才做的。”

En: Dead, daba-doop-doop.
Zh: “死咯-死噜-死透啦。”

En: Deadady-dead-dead.
Zh: “死咯-死噜-死透啦。”

En: There have been other signs too, small details. Everything is too neatly designed for us to assign probable cause here. As we did, foolhardily. Well -- no more.
Zh: “还有其它的迹象，一些小细节。一切都被精心设计成我们可以找出大致死因的样子，就像我们之前那样鲁莽。不过——不会再继续下去了。”

En: There have been other signs too, small things. We were right not to assign hanging as cause of death. As the perpetrators *expected* we would -- no such luck for them.
Zh: “还有其它的迹象，一些小细节。没有把上吊作为死亡原因是对的，正如犯罪者原本*期望*的那样——他们不会继续得逞了。”

En: I have had my doubts since you showed me the tracks. Why did they *carry* him over -- why not march him, I thought. There was no satisfying explanation.
Zh: 他点点头。“自从你让我看过那些脚印之后我就一直心存疑虑。他们为何要把他*背*过来——为什么不让他自己过来？我是这么想的，没有一个令人满意的解释。”

En: I have had my doubts since you pointed out the lividity was faint. They know we analyze hypostasis. I have had perpetrators manipulate the body position before.
Zh: 他点点头。“自从你指出尸斑很暗淡之后我就一直心存疑虑。他们知道我们会分析尸斑，我也曾遇到过会摆弄尸体姿势的犯罪者。”

En: I have had my doubts about this since you told me he was doing something *else*. At the time of his death. I don't have that kind of intuition -- I don't usually trust it. But it made me... doubt.
