Zh: “自从你告诉我他在做*别的*事之后我就一直心存疑虑，在他临死之前。我自己是没有那种直觉的——一般也不相信它。但它让我……产生了怀疑。”

En: I have had my doubts for a while now. Since I saw there were no signs of struggle on his hands. And no claw marks on his neck...
Zh: 他点点头。“我已经心存疑虑很久了。自从我看到他的手上没有挣扎的痕迹，脖子上也没有抓痕……”

En: I did not *just* come up with this. I've had my doubts since you showed me the tracks, officer. Why did they *carry* him over -- why not march him? There is no satisfactory explanation.
Zh: “我并非是*灵光一闪*。自从你让我看过那些脚印后我就一直心存疑虑。他们为何要把他*背*过来——为什么不让他自己过来？没有一个令人满意的解释。”

En: I did not *just* come up with this. I've had my doubts since you said the lividity was faint. *They* know we do it -- analyze hypostasis. I have had perpetrators manipulate the body position before.
Zh: “我并非是*灵光一闪*。自从你指出尸斑很暗淡之后我就一直心存疑虑。*他们*知道我们会——分析尸斑。我也曾遇到过会摆弄尸体姿势的犯罪者。”

En: I did not *just* come up with this. I've had my doubts since you told me about that hunch, officer. Him doing something *else* when he died. I don't have intuition -- I don't usually trust it. But it made me doubt.
Zh: “我并非是*灵光一闪*。自从你告诉我了那个预感之后我就一直心存疑虑，警官。他在临死之前正在做*别的*事。我自己是没有那种直觉的——一般也不相信它。但它让我产生了怀疑。”

En: I did not *just* come up with this. I've had my doubts since we found no signs of struggle on his wrists. No claw marks on his neck -- why? Why didn't he fight for his life?
Zh: “我并非是*灵光一闪*。自从我们在他的手腕上没有找到任何挣扎痕迹时我就一直心存疑虑，脖子上也没有抓痕——为什么？为什么他不挣扎求生呢？”

En: Who would do this?
Zh: “谁会这么做呢？”

En: Why would anyone do this?
Zh: “为什么会有人这么做？”

En: I think I need to wash myself.
Zh: “我得去清理一下自己。”

En: What happens next? (Proceed.)
Zh: “然后呢？”（继续。）

En: That's for us to find out. But *this* -- it will make finding them just a little easier.
Zh: “那就等着我们去发现了。但有了*这个*——就让寻找的过程简单多了。”

En: To hide something. The real killer? The real motivation? What really happened here.
Zh: “为了隐藏某样东西。真正的凶手？真正的动机？真实发生的一切。”

En: There is of course the very real possibility he was both shot *and* hanged.
Zh: 当然，他也很有可能是*同时*被枪杀和吊死的。

En: Maybe they just shot him *while* they hanged him?
Zh: “或许他们是在他被吊起来的*期间*开的枪？”

En: To put him out of his misery? He thinks. "It's possible, but it does not explain all the other dubious things here -- lack of struggle primarily. I may be intellectually sloppy, but I prefer one theory at a time. And this just smacks of treatment to me."
Zh: “帮他从痛苦中解脱？”他思索着。“有可能，但这不能解释其余的疑点——主要是缺少挣扎痕迹。可能我头脑方面有些随意，但我更倾向于这条推测。在我看来绝对动了手脚。”

En: Oh. He nods. "You *really, really* do. I am glad to hear you say that. Your room in the Whirling-in-Rags should come with a bathroom. Be sure to make use of it in the evening."
Zh: “哦，”他点点头。“你*的确*需要，我很高兴你这么说了。你在褴褛飞旋订的房间应该有浴室，晚上务必要利用起来。”

En: Maybe the bullet holds more answers?
Zh: “也许这颗子弹藏着更多答案？”

En: Look in his pants again.
Zh: 再次查看他的内裤。

En: Look under his fingernails, like Kim already did.
Zh: 查看他的指甲下面，正如金之前做过的那样。

En: Give up.
Zh: 放弃。

En: The genitals in his breeches continue to be unnoteworthy -- you see the penis of a dead man. If you've seen it once, you already got the picture.
Zh: 他内裤下的生殖器官依然不值得注意——你看见的是一个死者的阴茎。只要看过一次，你的脑海中就有了画面。

En: You pull down his breeches. Marbling is present around the crotch. The penis has shrunk, crawling half way back inside him. There is nothing mysterious or noteworthy here.
Zh: 你扯下他的马裤，胯下满是肥胖的细纹。阴茎萎缩着，只有小半部分暴露在外。这里并没有什么神秘或值得注意的东西。

En: Maybe if I touch it again...
Zh: 如果我再摸摸它……

En: I can't believe the pig is stroking him *again*...
Zh: “真不敢相信那头猪猡*又*在摸他了……”

En: Don't touch it again.
Zh: 别再摸它了。

En: Fucking pig's probably thinking about touching it again...
Zh: “那头该死的猪猡很可能又想摸了……”

En: There is no need. We have been thorough with the genitals... The lieutenant looks worried.
Zh: “没有这么做的必要了，我们已经全面调查了生殖器……”警督看上去很焦虑。

En: His fingernails have turned dark. They're chipped, and quite long. There is dirt under them. Surprise-surprise. No bullet here.
Zh: 他指甲的颜色变深了。坑坑洼洼的而且很长。里面还有些污垢。令人惊奇的是，并没有子弹藏在里面。

En: His fingernails have turned dark. They're chipped and quite long. There is dirt under them. That's all.
Zh: 他指甲的颜色变深了，坑坑洼洼的而且很长。里面还有些污垢，就是这样。

En: F****ts can't get enough of that dick...
Zh: “那混蛋对那根丁丁爱不释手了……”

En: Do you think we missed something?
Zh: “你认为我们错过了什么吗？”

En: Yes. There's something we're not seeing.
Zh: “没错，我们忽视了某样东西。”

En: No, let's just leave it to the Processing guys.
Zh: “没有，还是把他交给分析部门的人吧。”

En: Okay, well... We're in livor mortis here. He's disintegrating. We need to *refrigerate* the body, if we want to conduct another examination -- and we need to do it fast.
Zh: “那好吧……我们目前在检查尸斑，他已经开始分解了。如果还想做另一项检查的话，我们需要把尸体*冷藏*起来——而且速度要快。”

En: He shakes his head. "I worked as one of these *Processing guys* for a year. They are butchers and clowns. I once saw twenty CODs go misidentified in one week. Chances are slim to none that they'll find anything useful in processing."
Zh: 他摇摇头。“我在*分析部门*工作过一年，里面就是一群屠夫和小丑。我曾经在一周内看过20起死因误判事件，想让他们分析出什么有用的东西根本就是天方夜谭。”

En: I've got a gut feeling that there's more to this corpse.
Zh: “我的直觉告诉我这具尸体还藏着更多秘密。”

En: You can't shake the feeling that there are more secrets concealed in the flesh before you.
Zh: 你无法摆脱那种面前的这具肉体还隐藏着更多秘密的感觉。

En: The corpse seems to yawn contentedly, as though its glad you've run out of steam.
Zh: 尸体似乎在心满意足地打着哈欠，好像很高兴你已经失去了动力。

En: In short, you and I are the *last* line of detection. If you're not sure we got everything, we need to look *again*. And for that -- we need to refrigerate the corpse.
Zh: “简而言之，你和我就是最后一道侦查线了。如果你不确定我们采集到了全部信息，我们就得*再*看一遍。因此——我们需要把尸体冷藏起来。”

En: Mhm. He gets up. "This is one task we *cannot* sideline. With every hour, whatever we're looking for will become harder to find."
Zh: “嗯。”他站起身。“这是我们唯一*不能*耽搁的任务。随着时间的推移，我们想找的东西也会变得越来越难找。”

En: Okay -- where do we find a fridge for the body?
Zh: “好吧——我们要去哪儿找能够存放尸体的冰箱呢？”

En: Let's hurry then. [Leave.]
Zh: “那就赶紧吧。”[离开。]

En: Can't he just... hang out here for a while longer?
Zh: “他就不能……在这里多待一会儿吗？”

En: If you want for this task to slowly become impossible, then yes. Otherwise -- no. He's been *hanging out* far too long already. We need to ice him.
Zh: “如果你想使这个案子渐渐变得无法侦破，那就这样吧。否则——不行。他已经*挂在这*太久了，必须得冷藏起来。”

En: Not to mention the damage that would be dealt to the force's reputation by the corpse just rotting here like this...
Zh: 更别说放任这具尸体就这么烂在这里会对警队的声誉造成多大损害了……

En: It would have to be *industrial* in size. Let's start by asking Garte at the Whirling-in-Rags, and the Frittte store down at the gates. If they don't know... His voice trails off -- and his gaze settles on *Cuno*. "But only if *all else* fails."
Zh: “它得是*工业级别*的大小。我们先去飞旋褴褛问问加尔特，再去正门前的弗利多试试吧。如果他们都不知道……”他的声音渐渐低沉下来——目光落在了*坤诺*身上。“除非是*其它办法*都行不通了。”

En: It would have to be *industrial* in size. Let's start by asking the proprietor of the Whirling-in-Rags, then the Frittte store down at the gates. And if neither has one... His voice trails off -- and his gaze settles on *Cuno*.
Zh: “它得是*工业级别*的大小。我们先去问问褴褛飞旋的老板，再去大门前的弗利多试试吧。如果两个地方都没有……”他的声音渐渐低沉下来——目光落在了*坤诺*身上。

En: If you think so, officer. He nods. "I am not going to object."
Zh: “如果你这么想的话，警官。”他点点头。“我也不会持反对意见。”

En: Hmm... Cuno looks like he gets around. *Knows* Martinaise. And its fridges too probably...
Zh: 嗯……坤诺看上去路子很多，对马丁内斯*了如指掌*，可能对冰箱也……

En: Or -- remember the buttons on that intercom over there, on the side of the building next to the Whirling? One was for "Revachol *Ice* City"? Perhaps it's a... clue?
Zh: 或者——还记得吗对讲机上的按钮吗，就在褴褛飞旋旁边的大楼那儿？有一个叫*瑞瓦肖冰城*的？可能也算一条……线索？

En: Hey... A wide smile appears on his face. "Wasn't there a *giant ice bear sarcophagus* below that building?" He points toward the commercial area.
Zh: “嘿……”他的脸上露出了灿烂的笑容。“那栋楼下面不是有个*巨型冰熊石棺*吗？”他指着那片即将消失的商业区。

En: Okay. He unrolls the plastic. "I need a little help for carrying him to the holding pen of my Kineema. I can take care of the rest."
Zh: “好吧。”他摊开塑料布。“我需要人帮我把他扛到我的锐影車厢上，剩下的我来处理就好。”

En: Continue working here. The locals, the case, or tend to your own business? I will meet you downstairs in the Whirling-in-Rags, tomorrow morning -- 7:30 sharp.
Zh: “留下来继续工作。走访当地人，这起案子或者办点自己的私事？我们之后在褴褛飞旋的楼下，明天早上——7：30准时集合。”

En: What should I do in the meanwhile?
Zh: “在此期间我要做什么呢？”

En: You can almost see the body decomposing before your very eyes. You should drop everything else and look for a way to preserve it so you can conduct your examination better.
Zh: 你面前的尸体几乎是肉眼可见的腐烂着。你最好暂时搁置其余的工作，想办法将其保存起来。这样你就可以更好的进行尸检了。

En: He unrolls the plastic. "I need a little help for carrying him to the holding pen of my Kineema. I'll take it from there."
Zh: 他摊开塑料布。“我需要人帮我把他扛到我的锐影車厢上，我就从这里接手吧。”

En: Okay. He unrolls the plastic. "I need a little help for carrying him to the holding pen of my Kineema. I can take care of the rest."
Zh: “好吧。”他摊开塑料布。“我需要人帮我把他扛到我的锐影車厢上，剩下的我来处理就好。”

En: All right. He takes out a shiny black body bag and starts pulling the plastic over the dead man's face.
Zh: “好吧。”他拿出一个闪闪发光的黑色裹尸袋，开始用塑料布盖住死者的脸。

En: All right. He takes out a shiny black body bag and starts pulling the plastic over the dead man's face.
Zh: “好吧。”他拿出一个闪闪发光的黑色裹尸袋，开始用塑料布盖住死者的脸。

En: This concurs with the Hardie boys story -- and Klaasje's.
Zh: “这跟哈迪兄弟的说法——以及卡拉洁的相吻合。”

En: This concurs with the Hardie boys story.
Zh: “这跟哈迪兄弟的说法吻合。”

En: Correct.
Zh: “正确。”

En: We should have known.
Zh: “我们早该知道的。”

En: He nods. "I had my doubts --  there were no signs of a struggle on his hands. No claw marks on his neck. But still..."
Zh: 他点点头。“我也有些疑虑——他的手上没有挣扎迹象，脖子上也没有抓痕。但至少……”

En: His brow furrows while his eyes glaze over. The lieutenant looks regretful for not figuring this out before.
Zh: 他的眉头紧锁，目光呆滞。警督看上去很后悔之前没有把这件事弄清楚。

En: Meanwhile, the lieutenant is sitting behind his desk, hunched over a stack of paperwork. His help would be invaluable right now.
Zh: 与此同时，警督坐在他的办公桌后面，背靠着成堆的文件。他的帮助目前至关重要。

En: We still haven't found the *bullet*.
Zh: “我们还没有找到*子弹*。”

En: And, if we are to believe Hardie boys, it's somewhere in there...
Zh: “另外，如果我们决定相信哈迪兄弟的说法，它一定就在那里……”

En: He frowns, thinking. "Try putting your hand in his mouth and probing for the bullet in his *head*."
Zh: 他皱着眉头思考。“不如你把手伸进他的嘴巴，去他*脑袋*里摸索看看。”

En: It seems he is leaving you no choice in the matter.
Zh: 他似乎是让你别无选择了。

En: But where?
Zh: “但在哪里呢？”

En: It's too late to take him into Processing today. I'll take him first thing tomorrow. He pulls the black plastic over the dead man's face.
Zh: “今天送他去做分析已经太迟了，明早我会直接带他过去。”他把黑色塑料布盖到了被害人的脸上。

En: Then what?
Zh: “然后呢？”

En: Yes. We should take a closer look at it. I am certain it has more to tell us.
Zh: “是的，我们是得仔细看看。我敢肯定它还有更多事情要告诉我们。”

En: He unrolls the plastic. "I need a little help for carrying him to the holding pen of my Kineema. I'll take it from there."
Zh: 他摊开塑料布。“我需要人帮我把他扛到我的锐影車厢上，我就从这里接手吧。”

En: Now, detective... I've rarely been disappointed by the size of a *giant* ice bear fridge, but I think we should still take a look at it first. Make sure it's big *enough* before we carry him over.
Zh: “现在，警探……我很少会对一个*巨型*冰熊冰箱的尺寸感到失望，但我想我们还是得提前看一眼。在我们把他抬过去之前，确保它确实*足够*大。”

En: You can almost see the body decomposing before your very eyes. You should drop everything else and look for a way to preserve it so you can conduct your examination better.
Zh: 你面前的尸体几乎是肉眼可见的腐烂着。你最好暂时搁置其余的工作，想办法将其保存起来，以便更好的进行尸检。

En: That's charming.
Zh: “真有意思。”

En: It reveals no secrets at this time.
Zh: “原来这就是坤诺提到的棚屋……我们进去看看吧。”

En: Discard the feeling.
Zh: 把这种感觉抛在脑后。

En: What feeling?
Zh: 什么感觉？

En: Hard to say, it's gone now. Feelings pass, you see. Especially the small ones.
Zh: 很难说，那种感觉已经消失了。你也知道，感觉总是转瞬即逝的。尤其是这种微妙的感觉。

En: What is this?
Zh: 这是什么?

En: Why am I looking at this pile of the roofing material?
Zh: 我为什么要盯着这堆屋顶建材?

En: Cuno told you about his shack.
Zh: 坤诺向你提起了他的棚屋。

En: Inland Empire said come back.
Zh: 本心在召唤。

En: Drama said take another look.
Zh: 戏精说再去看一眼。

En: Perception is suspicious.
Zh: 洞察力很多疑。

En: It's nothing. Someone just left some roofing material slanted against an old shack.
Zh: 这不重要，只是有人在这间旧棚屋旁堆了一些屋顶建材。

En: Glad you asked. When junior researcher Õlari Taal invented eternite in the Värtna Polytechnic Institute, some thirty odd years ago, he thought it would last forever -- hence the name eternite. Sadly the only lasting thing turned out to be the material's highly carcinogenic effect.
Zh: 这个问题真令我感到欣慰。三十多年前，瓦塔那理工学院的次级研究员奥拉瑞· 塔尔发明了亘石，他本以为这种东西能够永存——所以才起了亘石这个名字。可惜结果证明这玩意唯一持久的性质只有致癌效果。

En: Because it's nice and orderly. Well laid pallet. Easy on the eyes.
Zh: 因为这堆建材整齐有序。铺设平整。赏心悦目。

En: Rhythmic pattern -- calms your mind. Mammals like this stuff.
Zh: 工整的布局——这令你感到安稳，哺乳动物喜欢这样的。

En: No, there's more to this. You get this strange *feeling*.
Zh: 不，没那么简单。你有一种奇怪的*感觉*。

En: There it is! You see a shabby little door.
Zh: 因为亘石板后藏着一扇暗门。这就是它们码放得*过于*整齐的原因。

En: Because unless Cuno lied, you should just be able to pull the panels aside.
Zh: 因为除非坤诺说了谎，你应该可以把嵌板拉开。

En: Because there's a secret door hidden behind the panels of eternite. That's why they're *too* orderly.
Zh: 因为如果坤诺没说谎的话，你应该可以把这些板子挪开。

En: Pull the panels aside.
Zh: 把这些板子挪开。

En: So this is the shack Cuno mentioned... Let's take a look inside, then.
Zh: “所以这就是坤诺提到的棚屋……我们进去看看吧。”

En: What is this, then? A tool shed? He peeks inside. "Let's investigate."
Zh: “这是什么？工具棚吗？他瞧了瞧里面。“我们去调查一下吧。”

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The pile of eternite looks stranger now that Cuno told you about his... pad.
Zh: 自从坤诺跟你提过了他的……设备之后这堆亘石看起来愈发奇怪了。

En: An inconspicuous pile of the roofing material, eternite.
Zh: 一堆不起眼的屋面材料，亘石。

En: No matter how strong you think you are, this is really not something you should be attempting to do with your bare hands.
Zh: 无论你自以为有多强壮，这都不是你应该赤手空拳去尝试的事。

En: This trash container is locked. The sliding lid has a padlock that says "Whirling-in-Rags."
Zh: 垃圾箱被锁住了。滑盖的挂锁上写着“褴褛飞旋”。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: You force open the sealed metal lid with your goddamn *bare hands*. It is open now. It could not resist your supra-human strength.
Zh: 你天生神力，仅凭*赤手空拳*就强行打开了锁住的金属盖。垃圾箱向你敞开。它无法抵抗你超人的力量。

En: Some items, such as the ledger you found, are interactable. Go to your INVENTORY and select the INTERACT tab to read your paperwork.
Zh: 你得到了一个可交互物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: He might also have information -- this is better than the prybar idea.
Zh: 他也许还知道什么消息——这比用撬棍的主意好多了。

En: The lock says Whirling-in-Rags. Maybe the proprietor has the key? Who knows what secrets wait inside...
Zh: 锁上写着褴褛飞旋。也许那里的老板会有钥匙？谁知道里面藏着怎样的秘密……

En: Your fingers start to itch just looking at that padlock...
Zh: 光是看着那把锁，你的手指就开始蠢蠢欲动……

En: Something slimy catches your eye...
Zh: 某种黏糊糊的东西吸引了你的视线……

En: Officer, the lieutenant adds, "This is sensitive information. You need to take this seriously."
Zh: “警官，”警督补充道，“你得严肃对待这些敏感的信息。”

En: We could try using a prybar -- the one you took from my motor carriage. Or...
Zh: “我们可以试试撬棍——你从我的車里拿到的那根。或者……”

En: An interesting little clue. Let's see where this goes. Clues have a way of magically connecting to other clues -- down the road...
Zh: 一条微妙的线索。调查一下这条线索会引向何方吧。线索总会以奇妙的方式彼此相连——只要这样追查下去……

En: Interesting. The lieutenant looks at the mug. "Reminds me of the mug collection we saw in our weasel's apartment. Maybe he or she has been dumping their garbage here?"
Zh: “有意思。”警督盯着马克杯。“这让我想起了我们在黄鼠狼的公寓里看到的马克杯收藏。也许那家伙也一直在这里倒垃圾？”

En: Yes! The one in the apartment you broke into. Interesting little clue. Surely this will yield something -- down the road...
Zh: 对了！就在你闯入的那间公寓里。真是条微妙的线索。这必定能引出什么——只要这样追查下去……

En: You betcha. You know -- this awfully *colonial* mug reminds you of another one, doesn't it? Anyway...
Zh: 你猜对了。你是知道的——这个充满*殖民地风格*的马克杯让你想起了另一个有着相同情调的家伙，对吧？总而言之……

En: Or it may just be a coincidence. Either way, something to keep in mind. What else do you see?
Zh: “也可能只是巧合。但无论如何，这都是值得留意的事。你还发现什么了？”

En: And... what's this?
Zh: 呃……这是什么？

En: Open the padlock with the key.
Zh: 用钥匙开锁。

En: Force time -- jam the tool under the lid and pull!
Zh: 该动手了——用工具撬开盖子！

En: Cuno said to aim for the staple.
Zh: 坤诺说过要瞄准目标。

En: Tool not equipped.
Zh: 没有装备工具。

En: Kvalsund multitool.
Zh: 库瓦桑德多用工具。

En: Dresscode: Rummager.
Zh: 着装要求：翻查者。

En: Force time isn't happening. You've jammed the prybar under the lid but it just isn't bending.
Zh: 暴力时刻结束了。你把撬棍插进盖子下方，盖子纹丝不动。

En: You stick the prybar into the fissure beneath the lid and push down. It doesn't take much force -- with a satisfying *crack,* the metal gives way. You can open the lid now.
Zh: 你把撬棍插进盖子下的裂缝然后用力下压。并没有耗多大力气——伴随着一声令人欣慰的*咔哒*，你战胜了这块金属。现在可以打开盖子了。

En: WHAT IS THIS? Your triceps ache for the metal to bend, but it resists... this calls for a potent curse to help break it's resistance. Utter the POWER WORDS!
Zh: 这算什么？你的肱三头肌都疼了，盖子仍然纹丝不动……看来得用几句恶咒来粉碎它的意志了。感受一下言灵的力量吧！

En: The FUCK kind of metal is this, this is fucking SHIT!
Zh: “这该死的盖子，真他妈的该死！”

En: Kick the container.
Zh: 踢垃圾箱。

En: Hang from the prybar with all your weight.
Zh: 用全身重量压下撬棍。

En: That's it, I can't pry it open.
Zh: “没戏了，我撬不开这玩意。”

En: The quality of metal is not the problem. From what I see, it's sturdy enough.
Zh: “金属的质量没有问题。在我看来，它很结实。”

En: Quit pushin' the blame on the metal piggo. Metal's fine. It's your arm that's shit.
Zh: “别再埋怨金属了，猪猡。金属没问题，你的胳膊才是一坨屎呢。”

En: He's enjoying your little failure. He finds it amusing, he's revelling in the sweaty rage on your face.
Zh: 他正在享受你遭遇小小挫折的难堪模样，乐此不疲。你恼羞成怒，大汗淋漓的面孔令他陶醉不已。

En: Unsatisfying. Hurtful. You hear a ringing sound, but not quite hollow. The can seems to be full...
Zh: 令人不爽。有损自尊。你听到一声铃响，并非沉闷的回声。垃圾箱似乎已经满了……

En: *Full* of itself. Content, happy you failed. This snickering trash container is having a grand old time.
Zh: 是“自满”。它为你的失败感到满足和愉悦。这垃圾箱正在幸灾乐祸，窃笑不止。

En: There's a creaking sound as the prybar slips under the lid. You hang from it, like a moron, huffing and puffing. Clearly the technique is wrong -- but nothing else comes to mind.
Zh: 撬棍滑进盖子下面吱吱作响。你像个白痴一样，气喘吁吁地挂在撬棍上。很明显，你撬的方式不对——但你也想不出别的办法了。

En: The strange thing is -- we don't have to. He smiles. "We have the *key* remember. The manager gave you the key."
Zh: “奇怪的是——我们没必要这样。”他微笑着，“我们拿到*钥匙*了，记得吧。经理已经把钥匙交给你了。”

En: Give up.
Zh: 放弃。

En: Cuno's gotta say, man, that was unimpressive. The fuck were you trying to do?
Zh: “坤诺不得不说了，伙计，你也忒掉链子了。你本来是想干什么来着？”

En: No he don't! He fuckin' lost it! The ape's lost the key too.
Zh: “不，他可不记得！他失去记忆了！这个人猿肯定也把钥匙也弄丢了。”

En: Unreal.
Zh: “奇了怪了。”

En: We should find his gun and kill someone with it.
Zh: “我们应该去找他的枪，再用那把枪打死个人。”

En: Let's ask the manager for the key, this is clearly not working.
Zh: “我们去找经理拿钥匙吧，这显然是撬不开的。”

En: But I wanted to use the prybar... I feel like I'm losing out on something not using the prybar.
Zh: “但我就是想用撬棍……我感觉不用撬棍的话自己就输了。”

En: Let it go.
Zh: 就这样吧。

En: The dumpster is goading you. It thinks you're weak.
Zh: 这个垃圾箱在挑衅你。它认为你很软弱。

En: You are. The prybar is quite satisfying and handy. He nods, without the slightest hint of irony. "But you are just not good enough at prying. We should get the keys."
Zh: “我看得出来。那是一把很好用的撬棍。”他点点头，语气中毫无嘲讽之意。“但你并不擅长撬东西。我们还是去拿钥匙吧。”

En: I was trying to open the trash.
Zh: “我刚才在试着打开垃圾箱。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: Fuck you, Cuno.
Zh: “操你大爷，坤诺。”

En: Yeah, I know but what were you trying to do pig?
Zh: “是啊，我知道，但你刚才是想干嘛呢，猪猡？”

En: Figures.
Zh: “看出来了。”

En: No you weren't. If you were you'd stick that shitto... He makes a move with his hands. "In that staple or some shit. That little ring that has the lock in it. Then twist that shit, it's the weak spot."
Zh: “你根本没用心啊。如果你真想撬开，你就得把那玩意捅进……”他伸手比划着。“捅进那个订书钉还是什么破烂形状的东西里。就是那个里面挂着锁的小圈。再转那个破棍子，那里是弱点。”

En: What are you his fuckin' sidekick now, Cun'? Don't listen to him pig, you were good.
Zh: “你他妈的成了他的跟班了吗，坤？别听他胡扯，猪猡，你之前干得不错。”

En: Another time maybe. [Leave.]
Zh: “下次再说吧。”[离开。]

En: Don't! Maybe you shouldn't...
Zh: 不行！也许你不该……

En: Didn't I just have a premonition that *there's something in there*?
Zh: 我刚才是不是有种*里面装着什么东西*的不祥预感？

En: There is. But you won't *like* it. Sweat forms on your brow, your hand is still on the lid...
Zh: 确实有东西。但不是你会*喜欢*的那种。你的手还放在盖子上，额头上已是大汗淋漓……

En: Open the lid.
Zh: 打开盖子。

En: The smell of rotten food rises to greet you. You see soggy cartons, dirty rags, and organic waste.
Zh: 食物腐败的气息扑面而来。你看到了湿漉漉的纸盒，脏兮兮的破布，以及各种厨余垃圾。

En: No-no. We need to investigate now. Just like that, he opens the lid.
Zh: “不——不行。我们需要立刻进行调查。”他一边说着，一边打开了盖子。

En: With a well-oiled crack the lock pops open. It should now be possible to simply raise the lid...
Zh: 这把锁已经屈服于娴熟的技艺。现在已经可以轻松地掀开盖子了……

En: Look under the boxes of carton.
Zh: 查看纸盒下方。

En: Pick at the rags.
Zh: 挑起那些破布。

En: Search the food waste.
Zh: 翻找厨余垃圾。

En: Dig in Hobocop-style -- for extra content.
Zh: 用流浪汉警察的方式翻个底朝天——为了找到更多东西。

En: We're just in time. The lieutenant peers in. "This hasn't been emptied for over a week."
Zh: “我们来得还算及时。”警督仔细看着里面。“这堆垃圾已经超过一周没清理过了。”

En: Close the lid.
Zh: 合上盖子。

En: The container sounds a muffled gong.
Zh: 垃圾箱发出一声沉闷的锣响。

En: We should return later, officer. I don't think we were thorough enough. There may be more in there.
Zh: “稍后再回来一趟吧，警官。我们的调查还不够彻底。里面可能还有什么重要的东西。”

En: That's one thing off the list... The lieutenant sounds relieved. "I think we got it all."
Zh: “这样就从待办事项清单里划掉了一项……”警督如释重负。“我想这里已经调查完毕了。”

En: The trash container stands in the spring snow.
Zh: 垃圾箱立于这场春雪之中。

En: 'Maybe you shouldn't...' Of course you should! This is your time to shine, Hobocop! Dive into that dumpster for extra content!
Zh: “也许你不该……”你当然应该！这可是你大显身手的时机，流浪汉警察！一头扎进那个垃圾箱里，多找点料吧！

En: Yeah! Crack it open Hobocop -- this is your time to shine. Dive into that dumpster for extra content!
Zh: 耶！打开它吧，流浪汉警察——这就是你大显身手的时机。一头扎进那个垃圾箱里，多找点料吧！

En: It's just organic waste, cold and slimy on your hands. Apple and potato peels mostly, unidentified sludge, and the occasional chicken bone thrown in for good measure. But hey...
Zh: 只有一些冰冷粘腻的厨余垃圾沾满了你的双手。大部分都是苹果和土豆的皮，还有一些难以辨认的残渣，外加几根鸡骨头偶尔客串一下。但是，嘿……

En: Wow, an Armistice calibre 50 nock cannon, half wrapped in paper tissues! So shiny...
Zh: 哇，一把止战者50口径箭扣炮，包在纸巾里，却露出了一半！闪闪发亮……

En: What's a nock cannon?
Zh: 箭扣炮是什么东西？

En: Where?! I can't see it.
Zh: 哪儿有？！我没看到。

En: It's a giant rifle and it's very expensive. Not as expensive as that fat string of pearls snaking among the rotten banana peels however...
Zh: 那是一种体积庞大，价格昂贵的来复枪。但和那堆混在香蕉皮里的整串大颗珍珠相比，也不算特别昂贵……

En: And is that a Cordon Electrics pre amp with Elektra f2 tubes? It is! That catches quite a price, we're talking 12,000 easy. Unless you're into hi-fi yourself?
Zh: 那边的难道是配置有厄勒克特拉f2真空管的科登电气前置放大器吗？还真是！那玩意的价格高昂，轻松就能卖到一万二以上。除非你是个Hi-Fi发烧友？

En: I am into hi-fi!
Zh: 我对Hi-Fi特别发烧！

En: I'll sell it.
Zh: 我决定卖掉它。

En: That's too bad, because none of those things are actually in there. There's just food waste and crisp wrappings.
Zh: 那真是太遗憾了，因为那些东西并不是真的。那里只有厨余垃圾和皱巴巴的包装纸而已。

En: No you won't, because none of those things are real. They're not actually in there. All you see is food waste and crisp wrappings.
Zh: 你做不到的，因为那些东西都不是真的。它们并不在垃圾箱里。你看到的只有厨余垃圾和皱巴巴的包装纸。

En: A cruel jest... there must be *something*...
Zh: 多么残忍的玩笑……那里一定有*什么东西*……

En: I knew it. (Withdraw from the dumpster's cavernous depths.)
Zh: 我就知道。（从垃圾箱的深渊中抽身。）

En: All you see is a broken mug with a racist depiction of *the yellow man* frolicking in saffron.
Zh: 引起你注意的只有一个破烂的马克杯，上面以种族歧视的调调画着*黄皮人*在番红花中嬉戏。

En: An antique?
Zh: 是古董吗？

En: (Take the mug.)
Zh: （拿起马克杯。）

En: Only in its social sensibility.
Zh: 唯一古老的只有它的社会敏感性吧。

En: Mhm... The lieutenant briefly glances at the mug, then returns his sight to the trash.
Zh: “唔……”他瞥了一眼马克杯，视线再次转回那堆垃圾里。

En: You betcha.
Zh: 这还用说。

En: Never mind, look at that fat string of Archipelagoan pearls snaking amidst the banana peels!
Zh: 别气馁，瞧瞧那串盘绕在香蕉皮里的大颗群岛珍珠吧！

En: Among the threadbare kitchen towels, something catches your eye -- a pair of denim trousers?
Zh: 在一堆破旧的厨房用毛巾里，你注意到了不寻常的东西——一条牛仔裤？

En: Are these the victim's clothes? The smell is not nearly as bad as the cadaver. These clothes could not have been in contact with the deceased for more than two days after his death.
Zh: 这是死者的衣物吗？上面的味道没有尸体散发出的那么糟糕。这些衣物在死者死后仍穿在身上的时间不会超过两天。

En: The victim's clothes? The lieutenant smells them. "Cadaverine odour is faint. If these belonged to the deceased, they were removed when he was still in the early stages of decay."
Zh: “受害人的衣服？”警督闻了闻。“尸胺的气味很微弱。如果这些属于死者，他们在死者腐烂初期就被脱下来了。”

En: Cadaverine odour is faint. The lieutenant smells them. "If these belonged to the deceased, they were removed when he was still in the early stages of decay."
Zh: 警督嗅了嗅。“只有微弱的尸臭。如果这些是死者的衣物，它们一定在尸体腐败初期就被脱掉了。”

En: Bag the trousers.
Zh: 将这条裤子装进袋子里。

En: *Guitar* mark blue jeans. Pockets empty. The lieutenant quickly searches the jeans. "Or *emptied*? He wore them with a belt, too, a wide belt -- the loops appear stretched, but..." He looks into the container.
Zh: 金迅速搜查了整条牛仔裤。“有*吉他*标志的蓝色牛仔裤。口袋是空的。也可能是*被掏空的*？死者穿这条裤子时曾系着一条裤腰带，那是一条宽腰带——裤袢有被拉扯过的痕迹，但是……

En: The belt is missing. That's it. Do you see anything else in there? I have another bag here...
Zh: 他望向垃圾箱：“这条裤腰带不见了。就这样。你在垃圾箱里看见其他东西了吗？我还有一个袋子……”

En: Reach for it.
Zh: 伸手拿袋子。

En: Nope.
Zh: “没了。”

En: The lieutenant nods, then looks back into the trash container.
Zh: 警督点了点头，又回头去调查垃圾箱了。

En: A drab long-sleeved shirt, olive-coloured, appears from the food waste, dripping with pus.
Zh: 一件土气的长袖衬衫，橄榄色，被厨余垃圾簇拥着，沾满脓液。

En: Bag the shirt.
Zh: 将衬衫装袋。

En: This is a military type over-garment. No label or serial number -- this is the kind of rib-knit shirt that's worn over light armour to conceal it in an urban scenario... He nods to himself. "Anything more?"
Zh: “一件军用外套。没有商标或编号——这是那种穿在轻型盔甲外，便于在都市环境中保持低调的罗纹衬衫……”他若有所思地点点头。“还有其他发现吗？”

En: The rest of the rags are just kitchen-variety waste. A yellow old mug that catches your eye. But other than that...
Zh: 剩下的破布就只是些厨房垃圾了，一个黄色的旧马克杯吸引了你的注意。但除此之外……

En: That's all, I think.
Zh: “就这么多了，我想。”

En: A thrown out towel, a mug. That's all.
Zh: “一条用过的毛巾，一个马克杯。就这些了。”

En: This feels like something you should return to with Lieutenant Kitsuragi present. Also, it makes you feel like an idiot for not having an evidence bag on you -- what will you do with a dead man's trousers?
Zh: 基苏拉吉警督在场时，这应该是你要做的事。同时，没带证物袋这件事让你感觉像个白痴似的——你要拿死者的裤子怎么办呢？

En: Alright. We should go to Garte again and ask if he knows who put the clothes in the trash. It could be as simple as someone from the hostel cleaning the yard, or *that* one... He nods toward the red-haired boy behind him. "I'd advise *against* confronting that force."
Zh: “很好。我们应该去问问加尔特，看他知不知道是谁把衣物扔进了垃圾里。可能只是旅馆里负责清洁院子的人，也可能是*那个*……他朝身后的红发男孩点了下头。“我建议你不要尝试和那股力量对峙。”

En: The kid said he didn't know anything about the clothes.
Zh: “那孩子说他不清楚衣服的事。”

En: Yeah, we need to ask the kids who put them here.
Zh: “没错，我们得去问问把东西放在这里的孩子们。”

En: Damn, this reminds me: I got side- tracked asking Cuno about the rags... The damned FALN pants got in the way. We should ask again, Kim.
Zh: “该死，这提醒了我：得暗示一下坤诺有关破布的事……被这该死的FALN裤子给耽搁了。我们得再去问问，金。”

En: Cuno can hear you, cop -- and Cuno don't know shit about the rags, okay? You don't have to come asking about them again like some bitch.
Zh: “坤诺听到咯，条子——而且坤诺根本不知道什么破布不破布的，好吗？不要再像个婊子一样叽叽歪歪了。”

En: See?
Zh: “看到了？”

En: Information from that *source* has truly been invaluable to the investigation. I cannot wait to hear more.
Zh: “消息*来源*提供的情报非常值得一查，我等不及要听详情了。”

En: You think someone from the Whirling might have been... involved maybe?
Zh: “你认为褴褛飞旋里有人或许会……牵扯其中吗？”

En: Okay. (Proceed.)
Zh: “好吧。”（继续。）

En: Not really. All we know is -- the victim's clothes are in the trash, the lid was locked, and this establishment had the key. It's just a *small* loose thread.
Zh: “不见得。我们只知道——垃圾箱里有被害人的衣服，盖子被锁上了，而这个酒吧掌握着钥匙。只是个*简单的*推理。”

En: Open the lid again.
Zh: 再次掀开盖子。

En: Nothing. It's nothing. Nothing more to see here.
Zh: 没有，什么都没有。这里面没什么可看的了。

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Elbows out, there's nothing more here.
Zh: 省省吧，里面没有东西了。

En: A blue piece of plastic sticks out from the apple peels. It's *shiny*. Looks like the corner of something...
Zh: 一片蓝色塑料从苹果皮中脱颖而出。十分*闪亮*，看上去像是某样东西的边角……

En: Pick it out.
Zh: 拉出来。

En: Something larger -- a clipboard! A blue plastic clipboard with moist papers hanging from it. They look badly damaged, but you can still make out forms and notes. Written in a *man's* handwriting...
Zh: 某种更大的东西——一个文件夹！这是个蓝色的塑料文件夹，上面挂着一堆湿漉漉的纸。看上去受损严重，但你还是能从中辨认出表格和记录。这些源自某个*男人*的笔迹……

En: Not just any man's. This dense cursive -- it's *yours*.
Zh: 不是某个路人的笔迹。这密集的草书——正是*你*亲手写的。

En: Officer, is that your... paperwork?
Zh: “警官，那是你的……工作报告吗？”

En: No. It can't be.
Zh: “不。不可能。”

En: I don't know *what* this is.
Zh: “我不知道这是*什么*。”

En: The handwriting -- it's mine.
Zh: “这笔迹——是我的没错。”

En: Yes it is, look. He points to it. "This plastic has the RCM street grid on it. You've even got an autopsy form..." A miserable looking slip of paper sticks to the board.
Zh: “这就是你的，瞧。”他指着文件夹。“这块塑料板上有RCM的街道网络。你甚至还有一张尸检记录表……”一张惨不忍睹的纸条粘在板子上。

En: There's a *lot* of it too. He nods approvingly. "*And* you've got an autopsy form in there -- in the back. That light pink copy paper." A miserable looking slip of paper sticks to the board.
Zh: “上面的内容还*真不少*。”他点头称是。“*而且*你还有一张尸检记录表——贴在背面。就是那张粉色的复印纸。”一张惨不忍睹的纸条粘在板子上。

En: It is, look... He points to the clipboard. "The plastic has the RCM street grid on it. You've even got an autopsy form in there..." A miserable looking slip of paper sticks to the board.
Zh: “这是你的，瞧……”他指着文件夹。“这块塑料板上有RCM的街道网络。你甚至还在这里贴着一张尸检记录表……”一张惨不忍睹的纸条粘在板子上。

En: What is this?
Zh: “这是什么？”

En: You askin' me now? It's some shit, copper. How should I know?
Zh: “你问我呢？是坨屎，烂条子。我怎么会知道。”

En: *Mine*? What is it doing in trash?
Zh: *我的*？它怎么会在垃圾里？

En: This makes a lot of sense.
Zh: 这真是合情合理。

En: You don't know. It *might* have been by your hand, though. Feels like that. Feels like you might have stuffed it somewhere. Not in this trash though. How it got here you don't know.
Zh: 你不清楚。但你*有可能*曾经持有过这东西。你能感觉到自己曾把它塞到某个地方。但肯定不是塞进垃圾箱里。你不知道它是怎么被扔进垃圾箱的。

En: It does? Maybe it does. It *might* have been thrown out by your hand. Feels like that anyway -- like *stuffed* it somewhere. Not in this trash though. How it got here you haven't a clue.
Zh: 真的吗？也许吧。*可能*是你亲手扔掉的。你能感觉到——自己把它*塞进了*某个地方。但绝对不是塞进了垃圾箱。你想不通它是怎么被扔到这儿的。

En: Hey, maybe it's your paperwork, officer?
Zh: 嘿，也许这是你的工作报告，警官？

En: If you don't mind my asking, how could you have let your paperwork end up in the trash?
Zh: “如果你不介意的话，你的工作报告是怎么沦落到垃圾箱里去的？”

En: If you don't mind me asking -- how did this get in the trash?
Zh: “如果你不介意的话，我想问问——它为什么会在垃圾箱里？”

En: I think I didn't want to be a cop anymore. That's why I tried to flush my cop life down the toilet.
Zh: “我觉得自己不想再当警察了，所以干脆把自己的警察生涯冲进下水道好了。”

En: I think I didn't want to be a cop anymore, so I threw it away.
Zh: “我不想再当警察了，所以就把它扔掉了。”

En: It must have been cramping my style.
Zh: “一定是因为这玩意阻碍了我的办案风格。”

En: Someone from the Whirling threw it in the trash? I don't know. I'm boring.
Zh: “也许是褴褛飞旋的人把它扔进了垃圾箱？我不知道，我实在是太无聊了。”

En: It has a foreboding quality to it. Maybe I *needed* to lose it, for the Great Bloodletting to begin?
Zh: “这玩意有股不祥的气息。也许我*有必要*失去它，才能让血流成河的惨剧拉开序幕？”

En: What are you *talking* about?
Zh: “你*胡言乱语*什么呢？”

En: I don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。”

En: I speaketh the tongue.
Zh: “吾之所言乃天意。”

En: Do-eth thou also thorough inventories? He points to the ruined notes. "You should take stock of your notes, make sure it's all there. Official notes contain informants' names. If some of it has fallen into the hands of the RCM's adversaries, bloodletting may well ensue."
Zh: “*汝*可查过自己的东西吗？”他指着破损的笔记。“你最好整理一下自己的笔记，确保没有遗漏。官方记录里会涵盖线人的姓名。如果他们的名字落入RCM的死敌手中，屠杀便会紧随其后。”

En: I don't know either... He points to the ruined notes. "You should take stock of your notes, make sure it's all there. Official notes contain informants' names. If some have fallen into the hands of the RCM's adversaries, bloodletting may well ensue."
Zh: “我也不知道……”他指着破损的笔记。“你应该整理一下自己的笔记。确保没有文件丢失。官方记录中含有线人的姓名。如果这些内容落入RCM的敌人手中，还真可能导致血流成河。”

En: Officer, this is an official piece of paperwork. It probably contains notes on *numerous* ongoing investigations -- and could even list undercover operatives. Informants. I suggest integrating it into your *style*. For all our sakes.
Zh: “警官，这些都是正式的工作报告。其中很可能记录了*很多*正在调查的案件——甚至可能列出了卧底的探员和线人。我建议你把这些信息融入你的*办案风格*，这样对大家都好。”

En: Well... He doesn't know what to say.
Zh: “这个嘛……”他无言以对。

En: Lucky we found it. You should take stock of what remains, just to be sure some has not made it into the hands of the RCM's adversaries. Organized crime and the like. There might have been police secrets in your notes.
Zh: “好在我们还能找到它。你应该整理一下残留的笔记，确保没有什么重要文件落到RCM的敌人手里。比如犯罪组织之类的。你的笔记里很可能含有警察的机密。”

En: Boring? Try *dangerous*. You should do a thorough inventory of that -- be sure some has not fallen into the hands of the RCM's enemies: organized crime, or worse... Official notes sometimes contain informants' names, even undercover operatives.
Zh: “无聊？不如说*危险*。你应该整理一下那份笔记——确保没有什么重要文件落入RCM的敌人手里：比如犯罪组织，甚至还有更糟糕的对手……有些官方记录会提到线人的姓名，甚至是卧底的探员。”

En: Grab them.
Zh: 拽出这条裤子。

En: As the legs of the slime-covered jeans begin to unspool from the garbage, a rank corpse smell fills the air.
Zh: 随着沾满粘液的牛仔裤裤腿逐渐与垃圾划清界限，空气中的尸臭味愈加浓重。

En: The garbage stinks in front of you, overflowing with soggy cartons, dirty rags, and organic waste.
Zh: 面前的垃圾箱中堆满了湿漉漉的纸盒，脏兮兮的破布，以及各种厨余垃圾，这些散发着同一股恶臭的气息。

En: By *early stages* you mean these were taken from him no more than two days after his death?
Zh: “*初期*是指这些衣物是在他死后两天之内脱掉的？”

En: Yes. He nods. "...if we're still assuming the clothes were taken off to get to the armour. They must have stripped him of it fast, the scavengers. In a matter of days."
Zh: “是的。”他点了点头。“……如果我们继续假设有人脱掉他的衣物是为了拿到他的盔甲。那些拾荒者肯定要尽快动手，就在死后数天之内。”

En: Yes. He nods. "I think the clothes were taken off to get to that armour he wore, then discarded. They must have stripped him of it fast, too, the scavengers -- in a matter of days."
Zh: “是的。”他点了点头。“我认为脱掉衣物是为了拿到他穿的盔甲，在得逞之后，衣物就被随手丢弃了。那些拾荒者肯定要尽快动手——就在死后数天之内。”

En: The fuck's he on about -- *kid*?! the one behind the fence yells. "You hear that, Cuno? He thinks you're an infant or some shit."
Zh: “他瞎逼逼什么呢——*孩子*？！”栅栏后面的熊孩子大声喊叫。”你听见了吗，坤诺？他觉得你是个没长毛的鸡巴婴儿。”

En: Did he? I must have drifted away... somewhere between the copious mentions of pigs and homo-sexuality.
Zh: “他说了吗？我一定是走神了……毕竟听了这么多关于猪猡和同性恋的事。”

En: Fuck yourself, binoclard!
Zh: “去你大爷的，*黄佬*！”

En: No, Cuno, it's just *bino!*
Zh: “不，坤诺，应该是*黄皮佬*！”

En: The fuck's he on about -- *kids*?! The one behind the fence yells. "You hear that, Cuno? He thinks you're an infant or something."
Zh: “他瞎逼逼什么呢——*孩子*？！”栅栏后面的熊孩子大声喊叫。”你听见了吗，坤诺？他觉得你是个没长毛的婴儿。”

En: Easier said than done -- how could this pathetic cabbage of copy paper and plastic ever become *très disco*?"
Zh: 说得容易——这坨惨不忍睹的复印纸和塑料板怎么可能跟得上你的*迪斯科节奏*？

En: Challenge accepted. You should be on the lookout for stylistic elements that elevate this cabbage to heights unforeseeable.
Zh: 接受挑战。你应该留意一些富有格调的元素，用来把这坨废纸抬高到不可预见的高度。

En: What I should do is get back to that garbage. (Decline.)
Zh: 我真正需要做的是回到那个垃圾箱里。（拒绝）

En: Alright then. Seems like a good idea. Right after the garbage, of course. (Accept.)
Zh: 好吧，似乎是个好主意。当然，我得先搞定垃圾的事。（接受）

En: I don't know, it seems foreboding to me somehow. Like there's something in there that's out to get me.
Zh: 我不知道，但这东西给我一种不祥的预感。好像里面有什么想抓住我。

En: What are you talking about? No one's out to get you -- get real man! Upgrade that cop-ledger!
Zh: 你胡说什么呢？哪儿有什么想抓你的东西——现实一点，兄弟！更新一下那本警察手册吧！

En: Soggy carton and some kind of food sludge welcome you back, uninvitingly. Smells like the wrong choice.
Zh: 湿漉漉的纸盒与某种食物残渣以毫无吸引力的姿态迎接着你的归来。这股味道昭示着一个错误的选择。

En: Good choice. Soggy carton and some kind of food sludge welcome you back, invitingly.
Zh: 明智的选择。湿漉漉的纸盒与一些食物的残渣正搔首弄姿地迎接你的归来。

En: Looks like we found your paperwork.
Zh: “看来我们找到你的工作报告了。”

En: Tell me, *see-eth* your bad ass more in there or are we done here? The lieutenant peers into the trash.
Zh: “请赐教，*汝*可在此窥见玄机，还是我们这就收工走人？”警督注视着这堆垃圾。

En: Okay, I'll do that.
Zh: “好吧，我会照办的。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 沉默不语。

En: I don't know, man. Sounds like an order. I don't take those.
Zh: “这可不好说，兄弟。你好像是在给我下令。而我可不接受命令。”

En: It would also not hurt to start taking notes on the case. He peers into the trash, where soggy cartons and rags stink, uninvitingly. "Now, tell me what your eagle eyes see. Or are we finished?"
Zh: “从现在开始对案情做些记录对你没有坏处。”他注视着这堆垃圾，湿漉漉的纸盒与破布正散发着令人反感的臭味。“告诉我吧，你那双慧眼都看到了什么。还是说我们可以收工走人了？”

En: It would also not hurt to start taking notes on the case. Anyway, where were we... The lieutenant peers into the trash container where soggy cartons and rags stink uninvitingly.
Zh: “从开始做案情记录对你也没有任何坏处。话说回来，我们刚才说到哪儿了……”警督注视着垃圾箱，那里湿漉漉的纸盒与破布正散发着臭气。

En: I see, yes. You're what we call a *bad ass*, aren't you? He makes little quotation marks around the words, indicating he is unsure of its actual badassery.
Zh: “好吧，我明白了。你就是那种*纯爷们儿*，对吧？”他为这几个字打上了小小的引号，表示他不确定这爷们儿到底有多纯。

En: Tell me, does your bad ass see more in there or are we done here? The lieutenant peers into the trash.
Zh: “告诉我吧，你那纯爷们儿的眼光发现别的东西了吗，还是说我们可以收工走人了？”警督注视着这堆垃圾。

En: Wow, went off script there, getting your ass handed to you. You shouldn't go picking fights if your rhetorical faculties haven't suggested it.
Zh: 哇，事情居然没按剧本发展，这下你可吃瘪了。如果你的遣词造句水平不达标，最好不要尝试和人打嘴仗。

En: There's nothing more of interest in this sludge of peels and meat by-product. Try the carton or those stinking rags.
Zh: 这些果皮与肉类加工副产品的残渣中已经没有什么值得翻找的东西了。在纸盒与那些发臭的破布里碰碰运气吧。

En: Not much to see here anymore. Just a burnt apron and some kitchen towels.
Zh: 没有什么值得一瞧的东西了。只剩一条烧焦的围裙和几条厨房里用的毛巾。

En: I'd rather not to talk about it right now.
Zh: “咱们暂时别提这事了。”

En: Understood. He nods, reverting his attention to the container, still stinking in front of you.
Zh: “明白。”他点点头，把注意力再次集中到了仍在散发着恶臭的垃圾箱里。

En: Be untroubled. If anything, this small moment has made him respect you more.
Zh: 一切顺利。要说有什么变化，那就是这件小事让他更尊重你了。

En: His eyes express a rare condolence. Then he picks it up:
Zh: 他的目光流露出了一丝罕见的哀悼之情。接着就伸手拿起了那个东西。

En: Drop them in here, officer. The lieutenant produces a black plastic bag marked *evidence* from his pocket.
Zh: 警督从口袋中掏出一个写着*证物*的黑色塑料袋。“放进来吧，警官。”

En: ...what's *this*?
Zh: ……*这*是什么？

En: The lieutenant averts his eyes as you crawl into the trash container.
Zh: 当你爬进垃圾箱的时候，警督把视线移开了。

En: His gaze is focused on something nebulous in the far distance. On nothing in particular.
Zh: 他的目光聚焦在了远方。却并不是什么特别的东西上。

En: [Close the lid.]
Zh: [关上盖子。]

En: Night, Kim.
Zh: “晚安，金。”

En: With effort, he looks at you.
Zh: 他费力地看向你。

En: Sorry it had to be this way.
Zh: “抱歉，只能这样了。”

En: See you in the morning.
Zh: “明早见。”

En: He puts the cigarette back, and says: "Me too."
Zh: 他把烟放了回去，说道：“我也是。”

En: He is as silent as the night.
Zh: 他如同这个夜晚一样沉默。

En: No! Not like this! (Crawl out of the trash.)
Zh: 不！不是这样的！（爬出垃圾箱。）

En: The smell of rotten food rises up at you as you climb inside the trash container.
Zh: 当你爬进垃圾箱时，腐烂食物味道扑面而来。

En: Darkness engulfs you. The stink of rot nearly knocks you out. For a minute you can smell cigarette smoke wafting in from outside.
Zh: 黑暗将你吞没，腐臭味几乎将你熏晕。片刻间，你闻到了从外面飘进来的烟味。

En: Darkness engulfs you. The stink of rot nearly knocks you out. For a minute you can smell cigarette smoke wafting in from outside -- then it is gone, and you hear footsteps walking away.
Zh: 黑暗将你吞没，腐臭味几乎将你熏晕。片刻间，你闻到了从外面飘进来的烟味——随后烟味消失，你只听到离去的脚步声。

En: Good night, detective, he says, with more gentleness than you've heard from him before.
Zh: “晚安，警探，”他的语气前所未有地亲切。

En: He nods. Absent-mindedly, he reaches for a cigarette; no sooner has it left his pocket than he catches himself.
Zh: 他点点头，心不在焉地准备去拿烟。可是他刚把烟从口袋里掏出来，就后悔了。

En: The morning will never come.
Zh: 早晨再也不会到来了。

En: Whatever he may feel in this moment is locked away deep inside him.
Zh: 他把此刻的感受都埋藏在心里。

En: A few words also come through the metal: "Good night, detective." But they dissipate around you like the smoke; you hear the lieutenant's footfall carry him away.
Zh: 你隔着金属箱子也听到了这句话：“晚安，警探。”但声音很快如烟雾般消散。你听见警督离去的脚步声。

En: Time for bed again. Crawl inside for real...
Zh: 又到了睡觉的时间。真的爬进去……

En: You crawl out of the trash container. The lid clangs shut. The fresh air rushes into your lungs, but does not clear them of the garbage stink.
Zh: 你从垃圾箱里爬了出来，盖子哐当一声盖住了。新鲜空气立刻袭入你的肺里，但却清除不了那股垃圾的臭味。

En: The lieutenant greets you with a small nod, as if you'd just taken a momentary pause to tie a shoelace.
Zh: 警督微笑着对你点点头，就好像你只是突然停下来系鞋带一般。

En: The container remains as inviting as a coffin. It is ready whenever you are.
Zh: 垃圾箱像一具棺材般邀请你进去。它正时刻准备着迎接你。

En: This is where you belong...
Zh: 这就是你的归宿……

En: The smell of rotten food rises up at you as you climb inside the trash container.
Zh: 当你爬进垃圾箱时，腐烂的食物味道扑面而来。

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: You've acquired an interactable item. Investigate this item further by going to the INTERACT tab in your INVENTORY.
Zh: 你得到了一个可互动物品。前往物品栏中与之互动吧。

En: Well it is a *container*. Maybe you're prioritizing it?
Zh: 这毕竟是个*容器*。也许你应该优先考虑这里？

En: He's being sarcastic. Do *not* ask what the Remote Viewers Division is.
Zh: 他是在挖苦你。*不要*问他遥视者部门是什么。

En: I agree. We should get someone from the Remote Viewers Division here.
Zh: “同意。我们应该请遥视者部门派人来支援。”

En: No, because they don't exist.
Zh: “不能，因为他们根本不存在。”

En: Wow! Could we get them here?
Zh: “哇！我们能把他们请过来吗？”

En: What do you mean *feel*?
Zh: “你说的*感觉*是什么意思？”

En: I should get you open, trash container.
Zh: 我应该打开你，垃圾箱。

En: We should still access this container. There may be evidence in there.
Zh: “我们还是得把这个垃圾箱打开。里面可能会有证物。”

En: Beware. They're very *painful* secrets.
Zh: 小心点。这些秘密非常*沉痛*。

En: There isn't -- but we should still access this container.
Zh: “不存在——但我们还是得把这个垃圾箱打开。”

En: The lock says Whirling-in-Rags. Maybe the proprietor has the key? Who knows what secrets wait inside...
Zh: 锁上写着褴褛飞旋。也许那里的老板会有钥匙？谁知道里面藏着怎样的秘密……

En: I'm so disappointed right now. I thought there was a Remote Viewers Division.
Zh: “我特别失望。我还以为真有遥视者部门呢。”

En: It's remarkable how he can keep a straight face. Take note -- the lieutenant is well versed in dead-panning. Many of the things he says could be meant in jest.
Zh: 他还真是擅长面无表情地讲笑话。记好了——警督精通冷面笑匠的技艺。虽然总是一脸正经，但他说的某些话其实是玩笑话。

En: All the detectives from all the Precincts who experience extrasensory perception go to the Remote Viewers Division. Their work is invaluable to the force.
Zh: “所有分局中每个具有超感官知觉的警探都会被调入遥视者部门。他们的能力对警队至关重要。”

En: What is the Remote Viewers Division?
Zh: “遥视者部门是什么？”

En: Mhm. He leans in to inspect the lock.
Zh: “唔。”他靠过来近距离观察着锁。

En: It's just a hunch. Maybe someone threw something in there?
Zh: “直觉而已。也许有人往里扔了什么东西？”

En: Oh. Okay.
Zh: “哦，好吧。”

En: There is no Remote Viewers Division, is there?
Zh: “遥视者部门根本就不存在，对吧？”

En: The body is downwind from here. Maybe you prefer the smell of garbage to the smell of death?
Zh: 尸体位于这里的顺风侧。也许比起尸臭，你更喜欢垃圾的味道？

En: *Just* a trash container? There is no such thing.
Zh: *只是*个垃圾箱而已？天底下可没有仅此而已的东西。

En: There isn't.
Zh: “不存在。”

En: Lieutenant, what do you think could be in there?
Zh: “警督，你觉得里面有什么？”

En: Oh. He pauses. "It *might* also be evidence. The mob could have disposed of something in there. We should get it open."
Zh: “哦。”他停顿了一下。“*可能*会有证物。暴徒也许把处理掉的东西扔在里面了。我们得把它打开。”

En: There is no need for sarcasm. I just feel strongly about it, that's all. Maybe it's not connected to the case, but still.
Zh: “没必要挖苦我。我只是特别喜欢这个话题而已。也许这和案件无关，但爱好就是爱好。”

En: Keep the hunch to yourself.
Zh: 绝口不提自己的直觉。

En: The lieutenant leans in to inspect the lock.
Zh: 警督靠过来近距离观察着锁。

En: It's extrasensory perception. Whatever is in there holds a special *significance*.
Zh: “这是超感官知觉。里面的东西一定*意义非凡*。”

En: Trash? Food waste from the cafeteria? They lock these containers to keep the derelicts from flocking in. Could be evidence too.
Zh: “垃圾？旅店扔出来的残羹剩饭？人们把垃圾箱锁住，是为了防止流浪汉蜂拥而至。里面也可能有证物。”

En: Yes, I *feel* like there's something in there.
Zh: “是的，我*感觉*里面有某种东西。”

En: Why am I looking at you, trash container? You're just a trash container.
Zh: 我为什么会盯着你看呢，垃圾箱？你只是个垃圾箱而已。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么都别说。

En: Nothing.
Zh: “当我什么都没说。”

En: What kind of extra-natural abilities do they possess?
Zh: “他们拥有哪些超自然能力？”

En: None. Because they don't exist.
Zh: “没有能力。因为他们并不存在。”

En: We could try using a prybar -- there's one in my motor carriage. Or...
Zh: “我们可以试试撬棍——我的車里正好有一根。或者……”

En: Did someone say *prybar*?!
Zh: 刚才有人提到*撬棍*了？！

En: How do we get the lock open?
Zh: “我们怎么才能打开这把锁？”

En: Fuck yeah! Prybar!
Zh: 牛逼！撬棍！

En: What's a prybar?
Zh: 撬棍是什么？

En: Forget about the prybar.
Zh: 忘了撬棍这回事吧。

En: Prybar, crowbar, leverage displacement tool of the gods. Your hands have held its paint-flaked metal one hundred thousand times.
Zh: 撬棍，撬棒，诸神钦定的利用杠杆原理造成位移的工具。你的双手已经紧握过十万次那油漆斑驳的金属了。

En: Or we could ask for a key from the manager of the Whirling-in-Rags. He probably has one.
Zh: “或者我们可以向褴褛飞旋的经理要钥匙。他应该有一把。”

En: Prybar, prybar, your palms yearn it's cold touch. To grasp it once more, as you've done so many times...
Zh: 撬棍，撬棍，你的手掌渴望着它冰冷的触感。渴望着再次握紧它，就如同之前无数次的接触那样亲密无间……

En: *Forget* about it? Like you forgot about your life? No! You cannot forget your own body, and your body cannot forget...
Zh: *忘了*它？就像忘掉你的一生那样？不行！你无法忘掉自己的躯体，而你的躯体也无法忘掉……

En: THE PRYBAR!
Zh: 撬棍！

En: Really? Your muscles are down with either one.
Zh: 真的吗？你的肌肉觉得两者皆可。

En: No *or*. Prybar!
Zh: “没有*或者*。就用撬棍吧！”

En: *Or*, lieutenant?
Zh: “*或者*怎样，警督？”

En: It's not a *pry*bar, it's a crowbar.
Zh: 这不是*撬棍*，这是撬棒。

En: We *might* want to ask for a key from the manager of the Whirling-in-Rags. He probably has one. And he also has *information*.
Zh: “我们*也许*该向褴褛飞旋的经理要钥匙。他可能有一把。我们还能顺便跟他打听些*消息*。”

En: Time for bed, Hobocop. Crawl inside...
Zh: 是时候睡觉了，流浪警探。爬进去……然后合上盖子。

En: Time for bed. Crawl inside...
Zh: 就寝时间到啦。钻进垃圾箱……然后关上盖子吧。

En: This is where you belong...
Zh: 当你爬进垃圾箱时，腐烂的食物味道扑面而来。这就是你的归宿……

En: The trash container has *somehow* been unlocked. It's all for you, Harry. The smell of rotten food rises up as you climb inside. This is where you belong...
Zh: 垃圾箱*不知何故*已经打开了。这都是为你准备的，哈里。当你爬进去时，腐烂的食物味道扑面而来。这就是你的归宿……

En: *Still* not force time. You're just not getting there... the prybar is primed, but you lack the ability.
Zh: 动手*仍未*成功。你还没掌握窍门……撬棍已经准备就绪，但你缺乏驾驭它的能力。

En: Soggy cartons and rags look back at him. "Oh yes -- and it would also be appropriate to start taking notes on the case. It's what cops do."
Zh: 湿漉漉的纸盒与破布回应着他的注视。“哦对了——你可以开始为本案做些记录了。这是警察的职责。”

En: You said you didn't want to talk about the ledger, however... now that you have it back, it would not hurt to start taking notes on the case. Officially.
Zh: “你曾经说过不想再提手册的事了，不过……既然你已经把它拿回来了，不妨正式开始做案件记录吧。”

En: Officer... the lieutenant says. "Now that you have your ledger back, it would not hurt to start taking notes on the case. Officially."
Zh: “警官……”他开了口。“既然你已经取回了自己的手册，不妨开始为本案做些正式的记录吧。”

En: Now that your ledger is back in your possession, it feels like you should do something... *important*.
Zh: “虽然你说过不想再提手册的事……但既然已经物归原主，不妨为本案做些正式记录吧。”

En: What am I supposed to do?
Zh: 我该做什么？

En: I'm not going to do anything.
Zh: 我可不打算做什么。

En: I should... start taking proper notes on the case?
Zh: 我应该……开始为本案做些正式记录？

En: Oh, yes you are. It's *fundamental* to proper police procedure to start keeping notes on the case at hand. Open your official investigation.
Zh: 是的，这是你的职责所在。为手头的案件做记录是警察工作的*基础*流程。正式展开调查吧。

En: Exactly. Open your official investigation.
Zh: 正是如此。正式展开调查吧。

En: You have to start taking proper notes on the case. Open your official investigation.
Zh: 你必须开始为本案做记录了。正式展开调查吧。

En: A box falls into pieces in your hands: Batiste *Soleil* cereal. There are plastic pasta packages below, and *Turbo* noodles. Nothing of note, however.
Zh: 一个包装盒在你的手中支离破碎：巴蒂斯特*太阳*麦片。下面还有些通心粉的塑料包装，以及*吞勃*面条。但没有什么值得留意的东西。

En: Used to what -- dumpster diving?
Zh: 习惯什么——淘垃圾吗？

En: No, searching for evidence in the trash.
Zh: 不，习惯于从垃圾里搜寻证物。

En: You see: milk, an egg-rest with one broken egg in it, some pasta wrapper... Picking up the soggy packages somehow feels familiar.
Zh: 你看到了：牛奶，摆着一枚破掉鸡蛋的鸡蛋托盘，一些通心粉包装纸……捡起这些湿漉漉的包装盒令你感到莫名地熟悉。

En: Dive further...
Zh: 继续深入挖掘……

En: Good practice for dumpster diving.
Zh: 对于深挖垃圾桶这门本事，这是一次绝佳的练习。

En: You've done this before. The movements are recorded in your elbows, the methodology in your fingers... you're used to this.
Zh: 你干过这种事。你的肘关节记录着一系列相关动作，整套方法铭刻在你的手指上……你早已习惯这种事了。

En: Ask *the manager*? Bullshit! Go straight for the prybar and pry this baby wide open!
Zh: 去找*经理*？别扯了！直接去拿撬棍，把这个宝贝儿撬开！

En: The mug, I'm getting that mug too.
Zh: 马克杯，那个马克杯我也要拿走。

En: You pick out a broken mug, with an oddly racist depiction of *the yellow man* frolicking in saffron.
Zh: 你捡起了一个已经破了的马克杯，上面以阴阳怪气的种族歧视调调描绘着*黄皮人*在番红花中嬉戏的场景。

En: The rest of the rags are just kitchen-variety waste. Just garbage.
Zh: 这堆破布里只剩各种各样的厨余垃圾了。

En: What's this?
Zh: 这是什么？

En: Just a feeling. A warning from some part of you.
Zh: 只是一种感觉。由你的某个部分发出的警告。

En: Seems like a reasonable assumption.
Zh: “似乎是个合理的推测。”

En: Then we should open it immediately!
Zh: “那我们就该立刻打开！”

En: There's something in there. Not necessarily connected to the case -- but still...
Zh: 里面有某种东西。不一定与眼下的案件有关——但毕竟……
